  deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Security deposit amount collected for this account-premise relationship. Null if no deposit was required. Expressed in local currency.']
  deposit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a security deposit was required for this account-premise relationship based on credit classification and service territory regulations.']
  der_enrolled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account-premise relationship is enrolled in a Distributed Energy Resource (DER) program such as demand response, virtual power plant, or distributed generation program.']
  final_bill_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a final bill has been issued for this account-premise relationship upon termination. Used in move-out and service disconnection workflows.']
  link_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account-premise link record was first created in the Customer Information System (CIS). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  link_end_date date [note: 'Date when the account-premise relationship was terminated. Represents move-out date or service disconnection date. Null for active relationships.']
  link_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account-premise link record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking in customer relationship management.']
  link_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments regarding this account-premise relationship. Used by customer service representatives to document special circumstances, arrangements, or historical context.']
  link_start_date date [note: 'Date when the account-premise relationship became effective. Represents move-in date or service initiation date for the account at this premise.']
  link_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the account-premise relationship. Indicates whether the link is currently in effect or has been terminated.']
  link_termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for termination of the account-premise relationship. Provides business context for why the link was ended. Null for active relationships.']
  link_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the relationship between the account and premise. Defines the role and responsibility of the account holder at the service location.']
  master_metered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this relationship involves a master-metered configuration where a single meter serves multiple tenant accounts. Common in multi-family residential and commercial properties.']
  nem_participant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account-premise relationship involves a Net Energy Metering (NEM) participant with Behind the Meter (BTM) generation. Used to identify customers with distributed energy resources.']
  notification_authorization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account holder has authorized receipt of outage notifications and service alerts for this premise. Used by Outage Management System (OMS) for customer communication.']
  occupancy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the occupancy status for this account-premise relationship. Indicates whether the account holder is an owner, tenant, or if the premise is vacant.']
  priority_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of this account-premise link when multiple accounts are associated with a single premise. Used to determine primary contact for outage notifications and field operations.']
  property_management_company_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the property management company responsible for this premise when the link type is property manager. Used in commercial and multi-family property management scenarios.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record that originated this account-premise link. Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'Original primary key or unique identifier from the source system. Used for data reconciliation and bidirectional synchronization with operational systems.']
  vacancy_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vacancy billing is active for this account-premise relationship. When true, the landlord or property manager is billed for usage during tenant vacancy periods.']

  Note: 'Association entity and SSOT for all relationships between customer accounts and premises, including active service relationships, landlord-tenant relationships, and property management roles. Stores link start date, link end date, link type (primary service, secondary service, landlord-of-record, property manager, master-metered tenant), landlord account reference, tenant account reference, move-in/move-out order references, vacancy billing flag, notification authorization flag, and link status. Enables historical tracking of which accounts have been served at a given premise, supports premise-to-account resolution for outage notifications and field operations, manages multi-family residential and commercial property management scenarios, and tracks landlord-tenant billing and notification workflows. SSOT for all account-to-premise and landlord-tenant relationship data.'
}

Table customer.credit_profile {
  credit_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the credit profile record. Primary key for the credit profile entity.']
  primary_credit_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account to which this credit profile applies. Links credit assessment to the billing account in CIS (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B).']
  to_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Credit classification is assessed per account. credit_profile.account_id → account.account_id.']
  average_monthly_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average monthly payment amount over the past 12 months. Used for deposit calculation and payment arrangement sizing.']
  bankruptcy_discharge_date date [note: 'Date the bankruptcy was discharged or dismissed. Credit profile may be eligible for upgrade after discharge and demonstration of payment performance.']
  bankruptcy_filing_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent bankruptcy filing. Used to determine if bankruptcy is still active and impacts credit classification duration.']
  bankruptcy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has an active or recent bankruptcy filing. Impacts collections strategy and may trigger automatic deposit requirement per PUC rules.']
  broken_arrangement_count varchar [note: 'Number of payment arrangements that were broken (customer failed to make scheduled payments). Impacts future payment arrangement eligibility and credit classification.']
  collections_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the collections process for past-due balances. Drives automated dunning notices, disconnection scheduling, and external collections referral. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: current|reminder|final_notice|disconnection_pending|disconnected|write_off|legal_action — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credit profile record was first created in the CIS. Required for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_class_code varchar [note: 'Credit classification code assigned to the customer account based on payment history, credit score, and risk assessment. Determines deposit requirements and collection strategies.']
  credit_freeze_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has placed a credit freeze with the credit bureaus, preventing external credit checks. Requires manual credit assessment process.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding balance allowed before service disconnection or additional deposit requirement is triggered. Based on credit class and deposit on file.']
  credit_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the credit profile record. Active profiles are used for deposit and collections decisions; under_review profiles are pending credit reassessment.']
  credit_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent credit review or reassessment. Credit profiles are typically reviewed annually or upon significant payment events (late payments, disconnection, bankruptcy).']
  credit_score_band varchar [note: 'Credit score range band obtained from external credit bureau (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion). Used for initial credit classification and deposit determination.']
  deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Security deposit amount required from the customer, typically calculated as 2-3 months of estimated billing. Null if no deposit is required.']
  deposit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a security deposit is required for this account based on credit classification and PUC regulations.']
  deposit_type varchar [note: 'Type of security deposit instrument held for the account. Cash deposits are held in trust accounts; surety bonds and letters of credit are third-party guarantees.']
  deposit_waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for waiving the security deposit requirement. Regulatory exemptions include low-income program enrollment, medical necessity, and senior citizen status per PUC rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: excellent_credit|existing_customer|low_income_program|senior_citizen|medical_necessity|regulatory_exemption|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disconnection_history_count varchar [note: 'Total number of service disconnections for non-payment in the account history. Indicates chronic payment issues and impacts credit classification.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date this credit profile became effective. Credit profiles are versioned; this date marks the start of this version\'s validity period.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date this credit profile version expires or is superseded by a new version. Null for the current active credit profile.']
  external_credit_bureau varchar [note: 'Credit bureau used to obtain the credit score and credit report for this customer. Required for FCRA compliance and dispute resolution.']
  external_credit_check_date date [note: 'Date the external credit bureau check was performed. Credit checks are typically performed at account establishment and periodically for high-risk accounts.']
  highest_past_due_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Highest past-due balance amount in the account history. Indicates maximum payment risk exposure and informs deposit amount calculation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this credit profile record. Tracks credit reassessments, manual overrides, and system updates.']
  late_payment_count_12m varchar [note: 'Number of late payments (payments received after due date) in the rolling 12-month period. Key metric for credit class downgrade and disconnection eligibility.']
  low_income_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is enrolled in a low-income assistance program (LIHEAP, state-specific programs). Enrollment may waive deposit requirements and provide disconnection protections.']
  manual_override_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the credit management staff member who performed the manual override. Required for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  manual_override_date date [note: 'Date the manual credit classification override was performed. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance.']
  manual_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credit classification was manually overridden by credit management staff. Overrides require supervisor approval and documented justification.']
  manual_override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text justification for manual credit classification override. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic credit review. Typically set 12 months from the last review date or earlier if triggered by payment issues.']
  payment_arrangement_count varchar [note: 'Total number of payment arrangements or installment plans established for this account. Excessive arrangements may indicate chronic payment issues and impact credit classification.']
  payment_arrangement_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is eligible for a payment arrangement or installment plan based on credit class, payment history, and PUC regulations.']
  returned_payment_count_12m varchar [note: 'Number of returned payments (NSF checks, ACH returns, declined credit cards) in the rolling 12-month period. Indicates payment reliability issues and may trigger deposit requirement.']

  Note: 'Master entity capturing the credit classification and financial risk attributes of a customer account as managed within the CIS. Stores credit class code (excellent, good, fair, poor, new), credit score band, deposit requirement flag, deposit amount, deposit type (cash, surety bond, letter of credit), deposit waiver reason, credit review date, number of late payments in rolling 12 months, disconnection history count, bankruptcy flag, low-income program enrollment flag, and collections stage. Sourced from SAP IS-U FICA credit management and Oracle CC&B credit configuration. Used by billing and collections domains for deposit calculation, disconnection eligibility, and payment arrangement qualification.'
}

Table customer.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for segment']
  account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Segments classify accounts for rate design, DR targeting, and regulatory reporting. Required for tariff application and program eligibility determination. Note: this may be a many-to-many via an impli']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Customer segments are defined within control area boundaries for load forecasting, demand response program design, and regulatory reporting. Grid planners use segment-level load data scoped to a contr']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_program. Business justification: DR program eligibility targeting by customer segment: utilities map segments (residential, C&I, industrial) to eligible DR programs for enrollment campaigns and regulatory reporting. A demand-side pla']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Customer segment metrics (annual_energy_sales_mwh, average_peak_demand_kw, der_adoption_flag, demand_response_eligible_flag) must be aggregated by forecast zone for IRP filings, load forecasting, and ']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: Customer segments are defined within service territory boundaries for load forecasting, demand response program capacity management, and regulatory reporting. Segment analytics (annual_energy_sales_mw']
  account_count varchar [note: 'Current number of active customer accounts assigned to this segment. Updated periodically from CIS systems for segment size tracking and program capacity planning.']
  ami_penetration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment has significant AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure) deployment, enabling interval data collection and time-of-use rate programs. True if AMI penetration exceeds utility-defined threshold (typically 75%+).']
  annual_energy_sales_mwh decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual energy sales in megawatt-hours for all accounts in this segment. Key metric for load forecasting, resource planning (IRP), and regulatory sales reporting.']
  annual_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual billed revenue in US dollars for all accounts in this segment. Used for revenue forecasting, rate design impact analysis, and regulatory reporting. Aggregated from billing system revenue data.']
  average_monthly_consumption_kwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Typical average monthly electricity consumption in kilowatt-hours for accounts in this segment. Used for load forecasting, rate design analysis, and program targeting. Calculated from historical MDM interval and register data.']
  average_peak_demand_kw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Typical peak demand in kilowatts for accounts in this segment. Relevant for commercial and industrial segments with demand-based rate components. Null for residential segments without demand metering.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment classification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_risk_tier varchar [note: 'Aggregate credit risk classification for accounts in this segment based on historical payment performance, deposit requirements, and collections activity. Used for deposit policy and collections strategy segmentation.']
  critical_infrastructure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment includes critical infrastructure customers (hospitals, emergency services, water treatment) requiring priority restoration during outages and enhanced service reliability.']
  demand_response_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts in this segment are eligible for demand response programs (CPP, TOU, direct load control). Eligibility based on load characteristics, metering capability, and regulatory program rules.']
  der_adoption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment has notable DER (Distributed Energy Resource) adoption including solar PV, battery storage, or other behind-the-meter generation. Used to identify segments eligible for NEM (Net Energy Metering) and interconnection programs.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this segment classification expires or is superseded. Null for currently active segments. Supports historical segment analysis and regulatory reporting for prior periods.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this segment classification becomes effective for customer account assignment and rate application. Supports temporal segmentation changes driven by rate case approvals or program launches.']
  ev_ownership_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment has significant electric vehicle ownership, making them candidates for EV-specific time-of-use rates and managed charging programs.']
  interruptible_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts in this segment are on interruptible rate schedules, allowing the utility to curtail service during peak demand or emergency conditions in exchange for discounted rates.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment classification record was last updated. Tracks changes to segment definitions, eligibility rules, or statistical attributes.']
  load_factor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average load factor (ratio of average load to peak load) for this segment, expressed as percentage. Indicates load shape characteristics and efficiency of capacity utilization. Higher values indicate flatter, more efficient load profiles.']
  load_profile_class varchar [note: 'Load profile classification code used for non-interval meter customers to estimate hourly consumption patterns. References standard load shapes maintained in MDM systems for settlement and forecasting purposes.']
  low_income_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment represents low-income customers eligible for special rate assistance programs, payment plans, or energy efficiency subsidies as defined by state PUC income thresholds.']
  marketing_program_eligibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of marketing and customer program codes that accounts in this segment are eligible for (e.g., energy efficiency rebates, solar incentives, EV charging programs). Used for targeted campaign management.']
  net_metering_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts in this segment are eligible for NEM (Net Energy Metering) programs allowing customer-owned generation to offset consumption. Subject to state interconnection rules and capacity caps.']
  rate_schedule_category varchar [note: 'High-level rate schedule category that this segment typically maps to for tariff application. Used by billing systems to determine applicable rate structures and riders. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential|general_service|large_power|industrial|agricultural|municipal|lighting|standby|interruptible — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_reporting_class varchar [note: 'Standardized customer class code used for FERC Form 1, EIA-861, and state PUC regulatory reporting. Maps segment to required reporting categories for revenue, sales, and customer count statistics.']
  rps_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts in this segment are subject to state RPS (Renewable Portfolio Standard) requirements or eligible for voluntary green power programs. Used for renewable energy allocation and REC (Renewable Energy Certificate) tracking.']
  seasonal_variation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment exhibits significant seasonal consumption variation (e.g., residential cooling load in summer, agricultural irrigation in growing season). Used for seasonal rate design and forecasting adjustments.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the segment classification (e.g., RES_LOW, COM_MED, IND_LARGE). Used as business identifier in CIS systems (SAP IS-U, Oracle CC&B) for rate schedule assignment and regulatory reporting.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the segment characteristics, typical customer profiles, usage patterns, and program applicability. Provides context for business users and supports segment definition documentation.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the customer segment (e.g., Residential Low-Use, Small Commercial, Large Industrial). Human-readable label for business users and customer-facing communications.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment classification. Active segments are available for customer assignment; pending segments await regulatory approval; retired segments are maintained for historical reporting only.']
  segment_tier varchar [note: 'Primary market segmentation tier defining the customer class and usage profile. Drives rate schedule eligibility, demand response program targeting, and regulatory reporting breakdowns for FERC and state PUC filings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential_low_use|residential_high_use|small_commercial|medium_commercial|large_commercial|industrial|agricultural|municipal|street_lighting|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record that originated or maintains this segment classification (e.g., SAP IS-U, Oracle CC&B, custom segmentation engine). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  time_of_use_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts in this segment are eligible for TOU (Time of Use) rate schedules with differentiated pricing by time period. Requires AMI metering capability and regulatory approval.']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining market segmentation classifications applied to customer accounts for rate design, demand response targeting, regulatory reporting, and marketing programs. Captures segment code, segment name, segment tier (residential low-use, residential high-use, small commercial, medium commercial, large commercial, industrial, agricultural, municipal), load profile class, AMI penetration flag, DER adoption flag, EV ownership flag, segment effective date, and segment expiration date. Used by billing, demand response, and regulatory reporting domains to classify accounts for tariff application, program eligibility, and FERC/PUC reporting breakdowns.'
}

Table customer.customer_service_request {
  customer_service_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service request. Primary key for the service request entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with this service request. Links to the account master data in CIS.']
  adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.adjustment. Business justification: Service requests for meter re-reads, estimated bill corrections, and billing disputes result in billing adjustments. Linking the service request to the resulting adjustment enables SLA tracking (was t']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Service request has embedded contact fields (contact_name, contact_phone, contact_email) that should be normalized to reference the contact table. This establishes which contact point the service requ']
  customer_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_service_agreement. Business justification: Service request should reference the specific service agreement it pertains to. Currently service_request has account_id and premise_id but no service_agreement_id. This FK establishes which service a']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Service requests for DER installation, maintenance, inspection, and troubleshooting require direct linkage to the specific DER asset for work order management, compliance tracking, and customer servic']
  distribution_transformer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.transformer. Business justification: Transformer-specific service requests (load investigations, failure analysis, maintenance) require direct transformer reference for asset work order integration, capacity planning, and reliability tra']
  dr_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.demand_dr_enrollment. Business justification: DR enrollment service request handling: CSR agents handling enrollment changes, opt-outs, and device issues must reference the specific DR enrollment record. Direct FK enables agents to pull enrollmen']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_event. Business justification: DR event operational response tracking: service requests are created in response to specific DR events (device failure during event, missed notification, curtailment dispute). Linking CSR to dr_event ']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_program. Business justification: DR program operations SLA tracking: service requests for enrollment, device installation, and program changes must be attributed to a specific DR program. Program managers track CSR volumes and SLA co']
  failure_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.failure_event. Business justification: Customer service requests reporting equipment failures (burning smell, sparking lines) are often the first notification of an asset failure event. Linking CSR to failure_event supports NERC reportable']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.feeder. Business justification: Field service dispatch and crew routing require direct feeder context for switching order coordination, outage correlation, and SCADA-based fault location. Feeder-level assignment is operationally dis']
  grid_reliability_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.grid_reliability_event. Business justification: When a NERC-reportable grid reliability event occurs, CSRs are created to track customer-reported impacts. Linking CSR to grid_reliability_event supports customer impact reporting for NERC OE-417 fili']
  incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.incentive_program. Business justification: Service requests are routinely filed for incentive program eligibility checks, compliance inspections, and payment disputes. CIS systems must link service requests to the specific incentive program to']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Service requests for DER system modifications, disconnections, export limit changes, or metering troubleshooting must reference the interconnection application for system specifications, approval hist']
  interconnection_queue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.interconnection_queue. Business justification: Customers file service requests for interconnection queue status updates, technical review scheduling, and study cost disputes. Linking service requests directly to the renewable interconnection queue']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter. Business justification: Field service requests for meter testing, replacement, or investigation require direct meter reference. Supports work order dispatch, asset lifecycle management, and customer complaint resolution work']
  nem_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.nem_account. Business justification: NEM billing disputes and true-up inquiries are among the highest-volume service request types for utilities with solar customers. A direct FK to nem_account allows CIS to surface NEM billing details d']
  outage_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.event. Business justification: CSRs are routinely created in response to active OMS outage events (no-power calls, restoration verification). Linking CSR to outage event enables outage-driven SLA tracking, regulatory complaint corr']
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: CSRs are created to manage customer inquiries and appointment scheduling around planned maintenance outages. Linking CSR to planned_outage_window enables tracking of planned-outage-driven service requ']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise where the requested service will be performed. Links to premise master data.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Service requests often target specific customer-visible assets (streetlight, meter socket, service drop, pole guy wire). Essential for field service dispatch, asset maintenance triggering, and custome']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event. Business justification: Post-storm CSR volumes (restoration inquiries, damage reports, billing adjustments) must be attributed to the triggering storm event for OE-417 regulatory reporting, post-storm after-action reviews, a']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field work order created to fulfill this service request. Links to work management system for field execution tracking.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred to complete the service request. Populated after work completion for cost tracking and billing.']
  appointment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service request requires scheduling a customer appointment for premise access. True for inside-meter work.']
  appointment_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the scheduled appointment window for customer availability. Defines the time range for field technician arrival.']
  appointment_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the scheduled appointment window for customer availability. Used for field dispatch coordination.']
  assigned_to varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the CSR, team, or field technician assigned to handle this service request.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service request will result in a charge to the customer. True for billable services, false for no-charge requests.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why the service request was cancelled. Null for non-cancelled requests.']
  completed_date date [note: 'Date when the service request was fully resolved and closed. Null for requests still in progress.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service request record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Audit trail for data ingestion.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Customer satisfaction rating provided after service completion, typically on a 1-5 or 1-10 scale. Used for CRM and quality tracking.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to fulfill the service request, including labor, materials, and equipment. Used for customer notification and approval.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the service request based on urgency, customer class, or regulatory requirements. Drives work order scheduling.']
  request_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer initiated the service request. Tracks customer engagement interface for multi-channel analytics.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the service request was submitted by the customer or created in the CIS system.']
  request_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the service request provided by the customer or CSR. Free-text field capturing specific customer needs or issues.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the service request, displayed to customers and CSRs. Externally visible request tracking number.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service request. Tracks progression from initiation through resolution or cancellation.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the service request was created in the system. Principal business event timestamp for the transaction.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Category of service request initiated by the customer. Defines the nature of the requested action or inquiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: start_service|stop_service|transfer_service|meter_reread|high_bill_inquiry|payment_arrangement|medical_baseline|der_interconnection|service_upgrade|outage_report|billing_dispute|name_change|other — promote to reference product]']
  requested_effective_date date [note: 'Date requested by the customer for the service action to take effect. For start/stop/transfer requests, this is the desired service date.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Notes documenting how the service request was resolved, actions taken, or reasons for cancellation. Populated upon request closure.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Date scheduled by the utility for field work or service action. May differ from requested effective date based on operational constraints.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service request was completed within the SLA target timeframe. Used for performance reporting.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Target number of hours for service request resolution as defined by utility SLA or regulatory requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the service request record. Identifies the CIS or channel system of record.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the service request in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service request record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Tracks data refresh cycles.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing customer-initiated service requests processed through CIS channels including start service, stop service, transfer service, meter re-read, high bill inquiry, payment arrangement request, medical baseline application, and DER interconnection inquiry. Stores request type, request channel (web, IVR, call center, mobile app, walk-in), request date and time, requested effective date, assigned work order reference, resolution status, resolution date, and resolution notes. Sourced from Oracle CC&B Service Request and SAP IS-U Service Order.'
}

Table customer.interaction {
  interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each customer interaction record in the CIS/CRM platform. Primary key for the interaction entity.']
  complaint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.complaint. Business justification: Interaction currently has complaint_flag (BOOLEAN) and regulatory_complaint_reference (STRING) which are denormalized. This should be replaced with a proper FK to complaint. Many interactions can refe']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Customer interactions occur via specific contact channels and methods. Linking interaction to the contact record used enables tracking which contact method (phone, email, SMS) was engaged for each int']
  customer_service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_request. Business justification: Interaction currently has service_request_number (STRING) which is a denormalized reference. This should be replaced with a proper FK to service_request. Many interactions can reference one service re']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_event. Business justification: Post-DR-event customer experience measurement: customers call during and after events to report non-receipt of notification or dispute curtailment. Linking interaction to dr_event enables event-level ']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_program. Business justification: DR program contact center analytics: interactions about enrollment inquiries, opt-out requests, and incentive questions must be attributed to a specific program. interaction already has demand_respons']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: Contact centers link customer service interactions to specific outage events for ETR inquiries, complaint tracking, and regulatory reporting on outage communication quality. The existing outage_event_']
  grid_reliability_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.grid_reliability_event. Business justification: Customer call-center interactions spike during grid reliability events (outages, voltage excursions). Linking interaction to grid_reliability_event enables customer impact analysis per NERC-reportable']
  interaction_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility service account associated with this interaction. A customer may have multiple accounts (residential, commercial, NEM/BTM). Links to the account master.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Billing inquiries referencing a specific invoice are the #1 contact center driver in utilities. Contact center agents need to pull up the exact invoice during the interaction. This link enables first-']
  meter_read_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_read. Business justification: Billing inquiry and dispute resolution interactions require direct reference to the specific meter read being questioned. Call center agents handling high-bill complaints pull up the meter read record']
  payment_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_arrangement. Business justification: Payment arrangement setup and modification are distinct, high-volume contact center processes. interaction has payment_arrangement_flag confirming this scenario is tracked. Linking to the specific arr']
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: Contact center interactions spike around planned outage notifications. Linking interaction to planned_outage_window enables measurement of notification effectiveness, contact avoidance rates, and cust']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who initiated or was the subject of this interaction. Links to the customer master record in the Customer Information System (CIS).']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event. Business justification: Storm events drive massive contact center interaction surges. Linking interaction to storm_event enables storm-period contact center performance reporting, staffing analysis, and post-storm customer c']
  to_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Customer interactions are associated with an account for CRM tracking. interaction.account_id → account.account_id.']
  agent_queue varchar [note: 'The call center queue or skill group to which this interaction was routed (e.g., \'billing\', \'technical_support\', \'collections\', \'spanish_language\', \'commercial_accounts\'). Used for workforce management and routing optimization analytics.']
  callback_scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time scheduled for a return call to the customer when interaction_outcome is \'callback_scheduled\'. Null when no callback was arranged.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The communication channel through which the customer interaction occurred. Supports omni-channel CRM analytics and channel cost allocation. IVR (Interactive Voice Response) interactions are self-service completions without agent involvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inbound_call|outbound_call|web_chat|email|ivr|mobile_app|in_person — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  complaint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interaction was classified as a formal customer complaint. True = complaint. Drives regulatory complaint tracking and reporting to State Public Utility Commissions (PUCs) as required by retail rate regulation.']
  contact_center_site varchar [note: 'Identifier for the physical or virtual contact center site that handled this interaction (e.g., \'Phoenix_AZ\', \'Virtual_Home_Agent\', \'Offshore_Partner_Manila\'). Supports site-level performance benchmarking and cost allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interaction record was first created in the source CIS/CRM system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post-interaction customer satisfaction score collected via IVR survey, email survey, or SMS (typically 1–5 scale). Null when no survey response was received. Supports CRM analytics and PUC service quality reporting.']
  demand_response_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer enrolled in or modified a Demand Response (DR) or energy efficiency program during this interaction. True = DR enrollment action taken. Supports DERMS integration and regulatory DR program reporting.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the interaction was initiated by the customer (inbound) or by the utility (outbound, e.g., proactive outage notification, collections call, demand response enrollment outreach).']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the interaction in seconds from start to end. For calls, this is the total call handle time including hold time. Used for SLA compliance measurement and workforce management analytics.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer interaction concluded (call disconnected, chat session closed, email response sent, visit ended). Used to compute handle time and duration metrics.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interaction was escalated to a supervisor, specialist team, or regulatory complaint handler during or after the session. True = escalated. Supports regulatory complaint tracking required by PUC reporting.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for escalation when escalation_flag is True (e.g., \'customer_requested_supervisor\', \'regulatory_complaint\', \'unresolved_billing_dispute\'). Required for PUC complaint tracking and quality assurance.']
  first_contact_resolution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s issue was fully resolved during this single interaction without requiring a follow-up contact. True = resolved at first contact. Key CRM KPI and PUC service quality metric.']
  hold_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time in seconds the customer was placed on hold during a call interaction. Contributes to customer experience scoring and agent performance metrics. Applicable primarily to inbound and outbound call channels.']
  interaction_category varchar [note: 'High-level business category classifying the primary purpose of the interaction. \'outage_report\' links to OMS event tracking. \'meter_inquiry\' relates to AMI/MDM data. \'billing_inquiry\' is the most frequent category in utility CRM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing_inquiry|outage_report|payment|complaint|general_inquiry|service_request|meter_inquiry|demand_response|new_service|disconnect_request — promote to reference product]']
  interaction_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the interaction occurred, derived from interaction_start_timestamp. Stored as a separate DATE field to support efficient date-partitioned queries, daily reporting, and regulatory complaint date tracking.']
  interaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interaction record. \'abandoned\' applies to IVR or chat sessions where the customer disconnected before resolution. \'escalated\' indicates transfer to a higher-tier agent or supervisor.']
  ivr_containment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s need was fully resolved within the IVR self-service system without requiring transfer to a live agent. True = IVR-contained; False = transferred to agent or unresolved. Key metric for IVR optimization and cost-to-serve analysis.']
  ivr_menu_path varchar [note: 'Sequence of IVR menu selections made by the customer during the interaction (e.g., \'1>3>2\' representing the menu tree traversal). Used for IVR design optimization and self-service analytics.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code for the language used during the interaction (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'zh-TW\'). Supports multilingual service analytics and compliance with state language access requirements for utility customers.']
  nem_inquiry_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interaction involved a Net Energy Metering (NEM) inquiry, application, or billing question related to a customer\'s behind-the-meter (BTM) distributed energy resource (DER) such as rooftop solar. Supports NEM program analytics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the agent summarizing the interaction, customer concerns, actions taken, and follow-up commitments. Confidential as may contain sensitive account or personal context. Stored in Oracle CC&B or SAP IS-U CRM activity log.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'The result or disposition of the interaction at its conclusion. \'follow_up_required\' triggers a subsequent outbound contact. \'callback_scheduled\' indicates a scheduled return call was arranged. Used for first-contact resolution (FCR) measurement.']
  payment_arrangement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a payment arrangement or deferred payment plan was established during this interaction. True = payment arrangement created. Supports collections analytics and FICA (Financial Customer Accounting) integration in SAP IS-U.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric reference number assigned to this interaction, communicated to the customer as a confirmation or tracking number (e.g., from Oracle CC&B or SAP IS-U interaction log).']
  sentiment_classification varchar [note: 'Customer sentiment assessed during or after the interaction, derived from speech analytics, text analysis, or agent classification. Supports CRM analytics, churn risk modeling, and regulatory complaint identification. \'highly_negative\' may trigger proactive follow-up.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this interaction record originated (e.g., SAP IS-U CRM, Oracle CC&B, Genesys telephony platform). Supports data lineage and Silver layer reconciliation.']
  source_system_interaction_reference varchar [note: 'The native interaction identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Oracle CC&B interaction log ID, SAP IS-U activity number, Genesys call GUID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for audit and reconciliation.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer interaction began (call connected, chat session opened, email received, in-person visit commenced). Principal business event timestamp per TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Stored in ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Granular sub-classification of the interaction within its category (e.g., \'high_bill_dispute\' under billing_inquiry, \'payment_arrangement\' under payment, \'tree_contact\' under outage_report). Supports root-cause analytics and agent training.']
  survey_response_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer completed a post-interaction satisfaction survey. True = survey completed. Used to calculate survey response rates and validate CSAT score population.']
  tou_rate_inquiry_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interaction involved a Time-of-Use (TOU) or Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) rate plan inquiry, enrollment, or change request. Supports rate analytics and AMI-enabled pricing program adoption tracking.']
  transfer_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the interaction was transferred between agents, queues, or departments during a single session. High transfer counts indicate routing inefficiency and negatively impact customer satisfaction scores.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this interaction record in the source system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing all multi-channel customer engagement interactions including inbound calls, outbound calls, web chat sessions, email exchanges, IVR self-service completions, mobile app sessions, and in-person visits. Stores interaction date and time, channel type, interaction category (billing inquiry, outage report, payment, complaint, general inquiry), agent ID, call duration, IVR containment flag, sentiment classification, case reference, and interaction outcome. Supports CRM analytics and regulatory complaint tracking.'
}

Table customer.complaint {
  complaint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each formal customer complaint record in the Customer Information System (CIS). Primary key for the complaint data product.']
  complaint_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility service account associated with the complaint. A customer may have multiple accounts; this identifies the specific account under dispute.']
  customer_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service agreement (rate schedule, tariff contract, or NEM agreement) under which the complaint arises. Relevant for rate dispute, NEM, and DER-related complaints.']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Complaints about DER performance, interconnection issues, NEM billing disputes, or export credit discrepancies require tracking the specific DER asset for root cause analysis, regulatory reporting, an']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Complaints about DER export curtailment, NEM credit disputes, power quality issues, or interconnection performance require direct reference to the DER system for root cause analysis and CPUC regulator']
  dispute_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Billing disputes are the most common formal complaint type in utilities. PUC regulatory reporting requires tracking which invoice triggered a formal complaint. complaint has billing_dispute_amount con']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_substation. Business justification: Power quality and outage complaints are investigated at the substation level for root cause analysis and PUC regulatory reporting. Complaint clustering by substation identifies systemic infrastructure']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_event. Business justification: DR event dispute resolution: customers complain about specific events — unexpected curtailment, missed notifications, incorrect incentive payments. Linking complaint to the triggering dr_event enables']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_program. Business justification: PUC regulatory complaint tracking by DR program: customers file formal complaints about DR program participation, billing disputes, and forced curtailment. Regulators require complaint resolution repo']
  dunning_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.dunning_notice. Business justification: Customers frequently file formal complaints about disconnection/dunning notices. PUC regulations require utilities to track complaints triggered by specific dunning actions. Linking complaint to the t']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outage event in the Outage Management System (OMS) that is the subject of or contributed to the complaint. Populated for outage_response and service_quality complaint types to enable cross-domain analysis.']
  failure_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.failure_event. Business justification: Customer complaints about equipment failure (transformer explosion, cable fault) should reference the asset failure_event for root cause analysis and NERC/PUC regulatory reporting. This differs from c']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.feeder. Business justification: Power quality complaints, voltage deviation issues, and outage-related complaints are investigated and resolved at feeder level. Direct feeder link supports reliability root cause analysis, SAIDI/SAIF']
  grid_reliability_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.grid_reliability_event. Business justification: Formal customer complaints reference NERC-reportable reliability events for regulatory filings, customer communication, and claims processing. Direct operational link required for OE-417 reporting and']
  incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.incentive_program. Business justification: Utility complaint management systems track complaints filed against specific incentive programs (denied rebates, delayed payments, eligibility disputes). Regulatory reporting to PUCs requires complain']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.inspection. Business justification: Safety-related customer complaints (sparking equipment, gas odor, meter damage) trigger mandatory asset inspections. PUC regulations require utilities to document the inspection conducted in response ']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Complaints about interconnection process delays, queue position disputes, export limitations, or metering accuracy must reference the specific application for regulatory reporting (PUC case tracking) ']
  interconnection_queue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.interconnection_queue. Business justification: Interconnection delay complaints and queue position disputes are a formal regulatory complaint category filed with FERC and state PUCs. A direct FK to interconnection_queue enables complaint tracking ']
  interruption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.interruption. Business justification: Customers dispute specific interruptions for billing credits or file regulatory complaints about a particular outage duration. Linking complaint to the specific interruption record enables precise reg']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) meter associated with the complaint. Populated for meter accuracy, estimated read, or AMI-related complaints to enable cross-domain investigation with the MDM system.']
  nem_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.nem_account. Business justification: NEM true-up disputes and export credit errors are a significant formal complaint category tracked by state PUCs. Linking complaints directly to the NEM account enables regulatory complaint reporting b']
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: Customers file formal complaints about planned outages (insufficient advance notice, extended duration, repeated scheduling). Linking complaint to planned_outage_window enables PUC regulatory complain']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise (physical location) related to the complaint, such as the address experiencing a service quality issue or outage-related grievance.']
  prior_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the most recent prior complaint from the same customer on the same issue, when is_repeat_complaint is true. Enables complaint chain analysis and chronic issue identification.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who filed the complaint. Links to the customer master record in the CIS (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B).']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Customer complaints frequently reference specific problematic assets (noisy transformer, leaning pole, damaged streetlight). Critical for asset condition monitoring, failure pattern analysis, and regu']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Formal customer complaints (is_regulatory_formal=true) are directly associated with PUC/commission regulatory filings. Utilities must link formal complaints to docket proceedings for regulatory report']
  remediation_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.work_order. Business justification: PUC/regulatory complaint resolution requires documenting the remediation work order that addressed the equipment issue. Role-prefix \'remediation\' distinguishes this from complaint\'s existing registry_']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event. Business justification: Customers file formal complaints during and after storm events (restoration time, communication failures, repeated outages). Linking complaint to storm_event supports post-storm regulatory complaint a']
  to_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Complaints are filed by/about a customer account. complaint.account_id → account.account_id. Required for PUC regulatory reporting.']
  transmission_outage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_outage. Business justification: Customer complaints triggered by transmission outages must be linked for PUC/FERC regulatory reporting, root-cause analysis, and NERC reliability event documentation. Regulators require utilities to c']
  vee_exception_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.vee_exception. Business justification: Billing dispute resolution requires linking complaints to the VEE exceptions that caused erroneous reads or estimations. Regulatory complaint investigations and PUC case documentation depend on tracin']
  assigned_department varchar [note: 'Name or code of the internal department or business unit assigned ownership of the complaint resolution (e.g., Customer Service, Billing, Regulatory Affairs, Field Operations).']
  billing_dispute_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount in dispute for billing-related complaints, expressed in the local currency (USD). Populated only when complaint_type is billing_dispute or rate_dispute. Used for financial impact analysis and regulatory reporting on disputed revenue.']
  complaint_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative text of the complaint as submitted by the customer or regulatory body. Contains the customer\'s account of the issue, relevant dates, and requested remedy. Treated as confidential due to potential inclusion of sensitive account details.']
  complaint_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable complaint reference number assigned at intake. Used in all customer-facing correspondence, regulatory filings, and PUC/PSC docket cross-references. Format: CMP-YYYY-NNNNNNN.']
  complaint_source varchar [note: 'Channel or originating party through which the complaint was received. Distinguishes internally filed complaints from those escalated via regulatory bodies (PUC referral), third-party mediators (BBB), or legislative offices.']
  complaint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the complaint within the resolution workflow. Drives SLA monitoring, regulatory reporting, and workload management for the customer service and regulatory affairs teams. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|under_review|pending_customer|escalated|resolved|closed|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  complaint_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the complaint subject matter. Drives routing, regulatory reporting, and resolution workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing_dispute|service_quality|outage_response|disconnection|rate_dispute|discrimination|safety|meter_accuracy|net_energy_metering|demand_response|deposit_dispute|other — promote to reference product]']
  contact_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer submitted the complaint to the utility (distinct from complaint_source which identifies the originating party). Used for multi-channel engagement analytics and CRM reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the complaint record was first created in the CIS or data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and SOX internal controls. Distinct from filed_timestamp which captures the business event time.']
  credit_issued_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary credit applied to the customer\'s account as part of the complaint resolution, expressed in USD. Tracks financial remedies granted to customers and supports revenue adjustment reporting and SOX controls.']
  customer_satisfaction_outcome varchar [note: 'Categorical outcome of the customer\'s satisfaction assessment following complaint resolution. Derived from post-resolution survey or follow-up contact. Complements the numeric satisfaction score for reporting and segmentation.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post-resolution satisfaction rating provided by the customer, typically on a 1–5 or 1–10 scale. Captured via follow-up survey after complaint closure. Used for service quality analytics and CRM performance reporting.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Classification of the customer\'s service segment at the time of complaint filing. Used for regulatory reporting breakdowns (e.g., low-income customer complaints, NEM participant disputes) and service quality analytics by segment.']
  escalation_tier varchar [note: 'Escalation level of the complaint within the internal resolution hierarchy. Tier 1 is front-line customer service; Tier 2 is supervisor/specialist; Tier 3 is executive or legal; Regulatory indicates PUC/PSC or FERC involvement.']
  filed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the complaint was formally received and logged in the CIS. This is the principal business event timestamp used to calculate regulatory response deadlines and SLA compliance.']
  first_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the utility first acknowledged or responded to the complaint. Used to measure initial response time against regulatory and SLA targets.']
  is_regulatory_formal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complaint has been formally filed with or referred by a regulatory authority (PUC, PSC, FERC). Formal regulatory complaints trigger mandatory response timelines and are included in regulatory reporting submissions.']
  is_repeat_complaint boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this complaint is a repeat filing by the same customer for the same or substantially similar issue within a defined lookback period. Used to identify chronic service failures and customers at risk of regulatory escalation.']
  is_safety_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complaint involves a safety concern (e.g., downed power lines, gas leak, electrical hazard, unsafe disconnection). Safety-related complaints require immediate escalation and are subject to OSHA and NERC CIP reporting obligations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the complaint record in the CIS or data platform. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, audit trails, and SOX internal controls.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal case notes, follow-up actions, and investigative findings recorded by the assigned resolution owner throughout the complaint lifecycle. Not shared externally.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the complaint based on regulatory sensitivity, safety risk, customer segment, or escalation source. Drives SLA response time targets and resource allocation.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State or federal regulatory jurisdiction governing the complaint (e.g., state abbreviation for PUC/PSC jurisdiction, or \'FERC\' for federal wholesale market complaints). Required for multi-state utility regulatory reporting segmentation.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the actions taken and outcome achieved to resolve the complaint. Documents the remedy offered, root cause identified, and any corrective actions applied to the customer\'s account or service.']
  resolution_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the complaint must be fully resolved, as defined by regulatory mandate, internal SLA policy, or PUC/PSC order. Distinct from the initial response due date.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the complaint was formally resolved and closed. Used to calculate total resolution cycle time for regulatory reporting on complaint resolution rates and response times.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Outcome classification of the complaint resolution. Indicates whether the complaint was upheld in the customer\'s favor, dismissed, settled by mutual agreement, withdrawn by the customer, referred to another authority, or partially upheld.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the utility must provide an initial response to the complaint, as mandated by state PUC/PSC service standards or FERC regulations. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and regulatory reporting.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the underlying root cause of the complaint (e.g., billing system error, meter read failure, field crew delay, tariff misapplication). Used for trend analysis, process improvement, and regulatory root cause reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing_system_error|meter_read_failure|field_crew_delay|tariff_misapplication|estimated_read|disconnection_error|payment_processing|communication_failure|equipment_failure|other — promote to reference product]']
  sla_resolution_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the utility met the regulatory or internal SLA target for full complaint resolution. Derived at record closure from resolution_timestamp vs. resolution_due_date. Key metric for PUC/PSC complaint resolution rate reporting.']
  sla_response_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the utility met the regulatory or internal SLA target for initial response to the complaint. Derived at record closure from first_response_timestamp vs. response_due_date. Used for SLA compliance reporting to PUC/PSC.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief, structured subject line summarizing the nature of the complaint. Used for search, reporting, and initial triage by customer service representatives.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing formal customer complaints filed through regulatory channels (PUC/PSC), internal escalation paths, or third-party mediators. Stores complaint date, complaint type (billing dispute, service quality, outage response, disconnection, rate dispute, discrimination, safety), complaint source (customer direct, PUC referral, BBB, social media, legislative inquiry), assigned resolution owner, regulatory docket number, PUC/PSC case reference, resolution deadline, resolution date, resolution type (upheld, dismissed, settled, withdrawn), customer satisfaction outcome, and escalation tier. Required for FERC, PUC, and state PSC regulatory reporting on complaint volumes, resolution rates, and response times.'
}

Table customer.enrollment {
  enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each customer program enrollment record in the utility\'s Customer Information System (CIS). Primary key for the enrollment entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility service account under which the enrollment is registered. A customer may have multiple accounts (premises) with independent enrollments.']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.aggregator. Business justification: Aggregator-mediated enrollment tracking: when customers enroll through a third-party aggregator, the CIS enrollment record must reference the aggregator for payment reconciliation and regulatory compl']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.interconnection_agreement. Business justification: NEM and SGIP enrollment terms — export limits, NEM version, parallel operation allowance, and compensation rate — are governed by the interconnection agreement. Direct FK enables enrollment validation']
  customer_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_service_agreement. Business justification: Enrollment represents program participation and should reference the specific service agreement under which the customer is enrolled. Currently enrollment has account_id and premise_id but no service_']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'The unique registration identifier assigned to the customer\'s DER asset in the utility\'s Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS). Used for dispatch, telemetry, and FERC Order 2222 aggregation tracking.']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: NEM, SGIP, VPP, and demand response program enrollment requires direct reference to the DER system for dispatch authorization level, telemetry availability, inverter certification, and export capacity']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_program. Business justification: Customer DR program enrollment requires proper FK to dr_program for tracking enrollment terms, incentive structures, and regulatory compliance. Currently uses denormalized program_code/name. Essential']
  financial_assistance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.assistance_program. Business justification: Low-income and medical baseline enrollments must reference the authoritative financial assistance program in billing to ensure correct discount application, verify eligibility criteria match, and supp']
  incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.incentive_program. Business justification: Customer enrollments in solar, storage, and renewable incentive programs require direct linkage for payment processing, compliance verification, budget tracking, and regulatory reporting. Essential fo']
  installation_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.work_order. Business justification: DER/demand response/NEM program enrollments require a physical installation work order (smart thermostat, solar inverter, smart meter). Role-prefix \'installation\' distinguishes from existing interconn']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Customer DER program enrollments (NEM, demand response, VPP) originate from interconnection applications; tracking the originating application enables program eligibility verification, capacity valida']
  interconnection_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: DER enrollments must reference the interconnection asset (inverter, generation equipment, storage system) for NEM export tracking, Rule 21 compliance, and grid impact studies. Mandated by interconnect']
  interconnection_queue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.interconnection_queue. Business justification: Interconnection queue approval is a regulatory prerequisite for many DER incentive and NEM enrollments. Linking customer_enrollment to the interconnection_queue entry supports eligibility verification']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter. Business justification: NEM and DER program enrollments require specific meter configurations (bidirectional metering, interval data collection). Supports interconnection approval workflows, export credit calculations, and r']
  nem_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.nem_agreement. Business justification: NEM program enrollment processing requires direct reference to the nem_agreement for annual true-up scheduling, cumulative credit balance tracking, grandfathering status, and export compensation rate ']
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: Life support and medical baseline enrolled customers require advance notification before planned outages — a regulatory mandate in most jurisdictions. Linking enrollment to planned_outage_window enabl']
  ppa_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.ppa_contract. Business justification: Community solar and green tariff enrollments are directly tied to a specific PPA contract that supplies the subscribed renewable energy. Linking customer_enrollment to ppa_contract supports subscripti']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical service premise (metered location) associated with this enrollment. Required for NEM, DER, and demand response programs tied to a specific service point.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account holder enrolled in the program. Links to the master customer record in the CIS (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B).']
  pto_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.inspection. Business justification: NEM/DER/interconnection enrollments require a passed Permission-to-Operate (PTO) inspection before activation. Role-prefix \'pto\' names this specific regulatory inspection milestone. Interconnection ru']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.rate_schedule. Business justification: NEM, TOU, and demand response enrollments are tied to specific rate schedules that govern export compensation rates, time-of-use periods, and incentive calculations. customer_enrollment has nem_versio']
  tou_rate_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.tou_rate_program. Business justification: TOU enrollment management and billing rate application require the enrollment record to reference the specific TOU rate program definition. Regulatory reporting of TOU participation, program performan']
  vpp_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.vpp_configuration. Business justification: Customer DER enrollments in VPP programs require tracking which VPP configuration the asset participates in for dispatch authorization, performance incentives, capacity payments, and ISO/RTO settlemen']
  annual_true_up_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month (1–12) in which the customer\'s annual NEM true-up billing cycle is settled. Determines when net export credits are reconciled against consumption charges for the 12-month period.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the enrollment application was formally approved by the utility or regulatory authority. Relevant for NEM interconnection approvals, medical baseline certifications, and low-income program eligibility approvals.']
  arrearage_forgiveness_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of past-due balance forgiven under an arrearage management program. [Moved from assistance_program: Arrearage forgiveness is applied at enrollment time and varies by account based on outstanding balance, making it enrollment-specific.]']
  arrearage_forgiveness_date date [note: 'Date when the arrearage forgiveness was applied to the customer account. [Moved from assistance_program: The date arrearage forgiveness was applied is specific to each enrollment instance.]']
  arrearage_plan_balance decimal(12,2) [note: 'The outstanding balance (in USD) enrolled in the customer\'s Arrearage Management Plan (AMP). Tracks the deferred debt amount being managed under the structured repayment program. Null for non-AMP enrollments.']
  certification_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the medical baseline or life-support certification expires and must be renewed. Used to trigger renewal reminder notifications and manage disconnection protection eligibility.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the customer enrolled in the program. Supports multi-channel engagement analytics and channel effectiveness reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|ivr|call_center|field_agent|mail|third_party|auto_enrolled — promote to reference product]']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'The communication protocol used for telemetry and dispatch signaling between the utility/DERMS and the customer\'s DER device. Determines interoperability requirements for demand response and VPP dispatch.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the enrollment record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage, regulatory compliance, and SOX internal controls.']
  cumulative_export_credits decimal(12,4) [note: 'The running balance of net energy export credits (in kWh) accumulated by the customer under the NEM program since the last annual true-up. Used for billing reconciliation and NEM settlement calculations.']
  der_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Enrollment status of this account in a Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS) or Virtual Power Plant (VPP) program. Enrolled accounts may have their DER assets dispatched by the utility or grid operator for demand response, frequency regulation, or capacity services. [Moved from account: This status field indicates DER program enrollment status but belongs to the specific account-program enrollment relationship, not to the account itself. An account may be enrolled in multiple DER programs with different statuses. Should be captured as enrollment.status for DER program enrollments.]']
  der_technology_type varchar [note: 'The technology type of the customer\'s Distributed Energy Resource (DER) asset enrolled in the program. Used for DERMS registration, VPP aggregation, and FERC Order 2222 compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pv_solar|wind|chp|battery_storage|ev_charger|smart_thermostat|generator|fuel_cell — promote to reference product]']
  disconnection_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s account is protected from service disconnection due to medical baseline, life-support, or low-income program enrollment. True = disconnection protection active; False = no protection. Critical for collections and field operations.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the customer bill under the assistance program (e.g., 20.00 for 20% discount).
