[Moved from assistance_program: While programs may have standard discount rates, the actual discount applied can vary by account based on rate class, usage tier, or household size, making it enrollment-specific.]']
  dispatch_authorization_level varchar [note: 'The level of dispatch authority granted by the customer for their DER asset in demand response or VPP programs. \'full_auto\' = utility can dispatch without customer action; \'semi_auto\' = customer notified before dispatch; \'manual\' = customer initiates; \'opt_in_only\' = customer must opt in per event.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the enrollment ceases to be effective. Null for open-ended enrollments. Used for term-limited programs, certification expirations, and contract end dates.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the enrollment becomes operationally effective and the customer begins receiving program benefits, rates, or obligations. May differ from enrollment_date due to billing cycle alignment or approval processing.']
  eligibility_verification_date date [note: 'Date when customer eligibility was verified for the assistance program. [Moved from assistance_program: Verification date varies by enrollment instance based on when the customer submitted documentation.]']
  eligibility_verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the customer eligibility verification process for the assistance program. [Moved from assistance_program: Verification status is specific to each account\'s enrollment application and documentation submission.]']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the customer submitted or was enrolled in the program. Represents the principal business event date (application/enrollment action date), distinct from the effective start date when benefits begin.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the enrollment record. \'pending\' = application submitted, awaiting approval; \'active\' = currently participating; \'suspended\' = temporarily paused (e.g., medical review); \'cancelled\' = voluntarily withdrawn; \'expired\' = past effective end date; \'rejected\' = application denied.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment expires and requires re-certification or renewal. [Moved from assistance_program: Enrollment expiration is specific to each account-program enrollment, not to the program definition.]']
  export_compensation_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The per-kilowatt-hour ($/kWh) rate at which the utility compensates the customer for net energy exported to the grid under the NEM tariff. Rate is locked at enrollment for grandfathered NEM versions. Null for non-NEM enrollments.']
  green_energy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the customer\'s energy supply sourced from renewable energy under a green energy program enrollment (0–100%). Used for Renewable Energy Certificate (REC) allocation and Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance reporting.']
  household_size varchar [note: 'Number of individuals in the customer household used for eligibility determination. [Moved from assistance_program: Household size is captured at enrollment time for eligibility determination and may change between recertifications, making it enrollment-specific.]']
  income_verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer\'s income eligibility was last verified for low-income assistance programs (CARE, FERA, LIHEAP). Used for periodic re-certification compliance and audit trails.']
  installed_capacity_kw_dc decimal(10,3) [note: 'The nameplate DC capacity in kilowatts (kW-DC) of the customer\'s installed distributed generation or storage system. Used for NEM eligibility sizing, export limit calculations, and DERMS registration. Applicable to NEM and DER/VPP enrollments.']
  interconnection_application_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer submitted the interconnection application for a distributed generation system (e.g., rooftop solar, battery storage). Applicable to NEM and DER enrollments. Required for FERC Order 2222 and state PUC interconnection tracking.']
  inverter_type varchar [note: 'The type of inverter installed with the customer\'s solar PV or battery storage system. Relevant for NEM technical review, grid interconnection safety assessment, and DERMS telemetry compatibility.']
  last_recertification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent eligibility recertification completed by the customer. [Moved from assistance_program: Recertification history is specific to each account\'s enrollment, not to the program.]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the enrollment record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trails, and downstream incremental processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  life_support_equipment_type varchar [note: 'The type of electrically powered life-support medical equipment used by the customer or household member. Determines disconnection protection rules and priority restoration status during outages. Null for non-life-support enrollments.']
  low_income_program varchar [note: 'Code identifying the low-income assistance program in which this account is enrolled, such as CARE (California Alternate Rates for Energy), FERA (Family Electric Rate Assistance), LIHEAP, or utility-specific low-income discount programs. Drives rate discounts and disconnection protections. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: care|fera|liheap|utility_discount|none — promote to reference product] [Moved from account: This code identifies a single low-income program (CARE/FERA) but does not support enrollment in multiple assistance programs, nor does it capture enrollment date, eligibility verification date, tier assignment, or recertification schedule. Should be replaced by enrollment records.]']
  med_certification_type varchar [note: 'The type of medical program certification for the customer. \'life_support\' = customer or household member depends on electrically powered life-support equipment; \'medical_baseline_allowance\' = customer qualifies for additional baseline energy allowance due to medical condition. Null for non-medical enrollments.']
  medical_baseline boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account has been approved for a Medical Baseline Allowance, providing additional low-cost energy allocation to customers with qualifying medical conditions that require life-support equipment or climate control. Governs disconnection protections and rate adjustments per PUC rules. [Moved from account: This boolean flag indicates medical baseline approval but does not capture approval date, expiration date, recertification schedule, or participation level. Should be replaced by enrollment records linking to the Medical Baseline Program with full lifecycle attributes.]']
  monthly_credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly credit amount applied to the customer bill under the assistance program. [Moved from assistance_program: The monthly credit amount may vary by enrollment based on account characteristics, usage patterns, or tiered benefit structures.]']
  nem_participant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is enrolled in a Net Energy Metering (NEM) program, meaning the customer has behind-the-meter generation (typically solar PV) and receives bill credits for excess energy exported to the grid. Drives bidirectional metering requirements and NEM tariff application. [Moved from account: This boolean flag indicates NEM program participation but does not capture which NEM program, enrollment dates, or participation level. The flag should be replaced by enrollment records in the enrollment association that link to specific NEM program records with full lifecycle tracking.]']
  nem_version varchar [note: 'The version of the Net Energy Metering tariff under which the customer is enrolled. Determines export compensation rate structure and true-up methodology. Null for non-NEM enrollments.']
  opt_out_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer voluntarily opted out of the program. Null if the customer has not opted out. Distinct from effective_end_date which may be system-driven.']
  opt_out_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the customer\'s voluntary withdrawal from the program. Used for churn analysis, program improvement, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|moved|rate_change|financial_hardship|program_change|ineligible|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  permission_to_operate_date date [note: 'The date on which the utility granted Permission to Operate (PTO) for the customer\'s distributed energy resource (DER) system. Marks the official start of NEM or DER export eligibility.']
  physician_certification_date date [note: 'The date on which a licensed physician certified the customer\'s medical condition for the medical baseline allowance or life-support program. Required for PUC medical tariff compliance.']
  qualifying_condition varchar [note: 'The medical condition or diagnosis that qualifies the customer for the medical baseline allowance or life-support program. Protected Health Information (PHI) — classified as restricted. Null for non-medical enrollments.']
  recertification_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the customer must complete eligibility recertification to maintain program enrollment. [Moved from assistance_program: Recertification cycles are specific to each enrollment instance based on initial enrollment date and program rules.]']
  renewal_reminder_date date [note: 'The date on which the utility should send a renewal reminder notification to the customer for medical baseline or life-support certification renewal. Typically set a defined number of days before certification_expiration_date.']
  telemetry_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time telemetry data is available from the customer\'s DER device for DERMS monitoring and dispatch. True = telemetry enabled; False = no real-time telemetry (manual or estimated dispatch).']
  total_benefit_received_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative total benefit amount the customer has received to date under the current enrollment. [Moved from assistance_program: This is a cumulative metric specific to each account\'s enrollment, tracking total benefits received to date for this enrollment instance.]']
  tou_rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'The specific Time-of-Use (TOU) or Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) rate plan code assigned to the customer under this enrollment. Maps to the tariff schedule in the billing system. Null for non-rate-plan enrollments.']
  vpp_program_code varchar [note: 'The utility-assigned code for the Virtual Power Plant (VPP) aggregation program in which the customer\'s DER is enrolled. Used for dispatch scheduling, settlement, and FERC Order 2222 reporting.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity and SSOT for all customer program participation across the utility. Captures enrollments in demand response (DR), time-of-use (TOU) rate plans, critical peak pricing (CPP) tariffs, budget billing, auto-pay, paperless billing, Net Energy Metering (NEM 1.0/2.0/3.0/NEM-ST) including interconnection application date, approval date, permission-to-operate date, installed capacity (kW-DC), inverter type, technology type (PV, wind, CHP), export compensation rate, annual true-up month, and cumulative export credits. Captures DER/VPP aggregation program enrollments including DER asset type (rooftop solar, battery storage, EV charger, smart thermostat, generator), DER registration ID, DERMS system reference, aggregator name, VPP program code, telemetry availability flag, communication protocol (IEEE 2030.5, OpenADR, Modbus), and dispatch authorization level. Captures medical baseline allowance certifications including certification type (life support, medical baseline allowance), qualifying condition, physician certification date, certification expiration, renewal reminder date, approved additional baseline kWh per day, and life-support equipment type (oxygen concentrator, dialysis, ventilator). Captures low-income assistance program enrollments (LIHEAP, CARE, FERA), green energy programs, EV charging incentive programs, and arrearage management plans. Stores program code, enrollment date, enrollment channel, enrollment status, effective start and end dates, opt-out date and reason, and all program-specific eligibility and technical attributes. Supports FERC Order 2222 compliance, PUC medical baseline tariff compliance, disconnection protection rules, and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table customer.move_order {
  move_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the move order record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  customer_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_service_agreement. Business justification: Move order (move-in, move-out, transfer) should reference the service agreement being initiated or terminated. Currently move_order has account_id and premise_id but no service_agreement_id. This FK e']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Move-out orders for premises with DER assets require DER disposition decisions (transfer, decommission, re-registration). Utilities must track which DER is affected by a move order to trigger intercon']
  field_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field work order created in the asset/workforce management system (Ventyx Asset Suite) to execute the physical service connection, disconnection, or meter read associated with this move order. Links the CIS move order to the field operations domain.']
  final_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Move-out orders generate a final bill invoice that must be tracked for deposit reconciliation, forwarding address delivery, and collections initiation if unpaid. move_order has final_bill_amount confi']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the meter device installed at the service point at the time of the move order. Used to schedule move-in/move-out meter reads and link final read values to the correct device record in the Metering domain.']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service point (metering point / point of delivery) at the premise where service is being initiated or terminated. A premise may have multiple service points (e.g., electric and gas).']
  nem_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.nem_account. Business justification: Move orders for NEM customers require NEM account closure, final true-up calculation, and export credit disposition. A direct FK from move_order to nem_account is essential for the NEM move-out billin']
  primary_move_premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise (physical location) at which the move order is being executed. Central linkage to the premise master record for address, meter, and distribution asset context.']
  primary_move_prior_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the existing service account being closed or vacated as part of this move order. Populated for move-out and transfer order types. Used to trigger final bill generation and deposit disposition in the billing domain.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer party initiating the move order. Links to the customer master record in the Customer domain. For a transfer, this is the incoming customer (move-in party).']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: Move-in/move-out orders are processed and routed within service territory boundaries for crew dispatch, service activation sequencing, and regulatory compliance. Service territory determines applicabl']
  tertiary_move_prior_occupant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the account of the previous occupant at the premise, if known at the time of the move-in order. Used to coordinate final bill issuance for the departing customer and to identify any outstanding balance or deposit transfer obligations.']
  to_premise_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.premise.premise_id — Move orders reference the premise where service starts/stops. move_order.premise_id → premise.premise_id.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.work_order. Business justification: Service connect/disconnect/transfer move orders require a physical asset work order to energize or de-energize service at the meter. Utilities track this link for move-in/move-out completion confirmat']
  budget_billing_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is enrolled in a budget billing (levelized payment) plan at the time of the move order. When true, the final bill calculation must account for any budget billing true-up balance upon move-out.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason explaining why the move order was cancelled or rejected. Populated only when order_status is \'cancelled\' or \'rejected\'. Used for operational quality analysis and customer service improvement.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The customer engagement channel through which the move order was submitted. Supports multi-channel analytics and CRM reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_portal|mobile_app|call_center|walk_in|field_crew|ivr|third_party — promote to reference product]']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the move order was fully completed and all downstream actions (meter read, billing, account activation/deactivation) were confirmed. Distinct from effective_date (the business date of service change) and submitted_timestamp (when the order was placed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this move order record was first created in the Silver layer data store. Distinct from submitted_timestamp which reflects the business event time.']
  credit_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a credit check was required for this move-in or transfer order based on the customer\'s credit classification or account history. When true, the move order may be held pending credit review and deposit determination per SAP IS-U FICA or Oracle CC&B Collections rules.']
  credit_check_status varchar [note: 'The outcome of the credit check performed for this move order. Not_required: credit check was not triggered. Passed: customer met credit criteria. Failed: customer did not meet criteria (deposit required). Pending: check in progress. Waived: credit check bypassed per business rules (e.g., existing customer in good standing).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts on this move order (deposit_amount, final_bill_amount). Typically USD for North American utilities. Supports multi-currency operations for utilities with international operations.']
  demand_response_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer or premise is enrolled in a Demand Response (DR) program at the time of the move order. When true, the DR program administrator must be notified to update enrollment records and avoid erroneous dispatch signals.']
  deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the security deposit associated with the prior or new account at the time of the move order. Used to determine deposit disposition (refund, transfer, or application to final bill balance) upon move-out or transfer.']
  deposit_disposition varchar [note: 'The outcome or planned treatment of the security deposit upon move-out or transfer. Refund: returned to customer. Applied_to_balance: offset against outstanding final bill. Transferred: moved to new account. Forfeited: retained by utility per tariff terms. Pending: disposition not yet determined.']
  der_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise associated with this move order has an active Distributed Energy Resource (DER) enrollment (e.g., battery storage, EV charger, demand response program). When true, triggers DERMS notification and DER agreement review upon move-out or transfer.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the service change (move-in, move-out, or transfer) became effective at the premise. Used as the billing boundary date for final bill generation and new account activation. May differ from requested_date due to scheduling constraints or field execution timing.']
  final_read_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The meter register reading (in kWh or applicable unit) captured at the time of the move order. For move-out, this is the closing read used to calculate the final bill. For move-in, this is the opening read establishing the new billing baseline.']
  landlord_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property owner or landlord must be notified of the service change per tariff rules or regulatory requirements. Relevant for rental properties where the landlord has a standing notification agreement with the utility.']
  landlord_notified_date date [note: 'The date on which the landlord or property owner was notified of the service change associated with this move order. Populated only when landlord_notification_required is true. Used for regulatory compliance documentation.']
  medical_baseline boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has an approved medical baseline allowance (life support or medical necessity designation) that requires special handling during service disconnection or move-out. When true, additional notification and regulatory compliance steps are required before service termination.']
  meter_read_date date [note: 'The date on which the meter was physically or remotely read in connection with this move order. Used as the billing cut-off date for the final bill (move-out) or the start-of-service read date (move-in). Sourced from Oracle Utilities MDM or SAP IS-U Device Management.']
  nem_participant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer associated with this move order is enrolled in a Net Energy Metering (NEM) program, typically due to rooftop solar or other behind-the-meter (BTM) generation. When true, triggers specialized NEM billing and interconnection review processes upon move-in or move-out.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the move order, assigned by the source system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Used for customer-facing correspondence and cross-system reconciliation.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the move order workflow. Pending: submitted but not yet processed. In_progress: actively being executed (meter read scheduled, field crew dispatched). Completed: service change fully effected. Cancelled: withdrawn before execution. Rejected: failed validation or business rules.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the service order: move_in (new service activation at a premise), move_out (service termination at a premise), transfer (simultaneous move-out and move-in for a change of occupant), reconnect (restoration of previously disconnected service), or disconnect (non-payment or voluntary disconnection). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: move_in|move_out|transfer|reconnect|disconnect — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  rate_schedule_code varchar [note: 'The tariff rate schedule code assigned to the new service account upon move-in or transfer. Determines the billing rate class (e.g., residential, small commercial, large industrial, TOU, CPP). Sourced from the applicable PUC-approved tariff.']
  read_type varchar [note: 'Indicates how the move-order meter read was obtained: actual (field technician physical read), estimated (calculated based on usage history when actual read is unavailable), AMI_remote (automated read via Advanced Metering Infrastructure), or customer_self_read (customer-reported reading).']
  read_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the final_read_value. Typically kWh for electric meters, but may be therms, CCF, or MCF for gas service points. Ensures correct interpretation of the meter read value across commodity types.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or exceptions associated with this move order. May include field crew instructions, customer-specific handling notes, or escalation details entered by customer service representatives.']
  requested_date date [note: 'The date the customer requested the service change to take effect. This is the customer-stated desired effective date, which may differ from the actual effective date after scheduling and field execution.']
  scheduled_read_date date [note: 'The date on which the meter read associated with this move order is scheduled to be performed. May differ from meter_read_date (actual read date) if the read is rescheduled or delayed. Used for field crew scheduling and MDM read request management.']
  service_class varchar [note: 'Customer service classification at the time of the move order, determining applicable tariff, billing rules, and regulatory reporting category. Residential, small commercial, large commercial, industrial, agricultural, or lighting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this move order record was sourced. SAP_IS-U: SAP Industry Solution for Utilities. Oracle_CCB: Oracle Utilities Customer Care and Billing. Manual: entered directly into the data platform. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_order_reference varchar [note: 'The native primary key or order identifier from the originating operational system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Enables traceability back to the source system record for audit, reconciliation, and issue resolution.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the move order was first submitted into the CIS system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Represents the principal business event timestamp for order creation. Used for SLA tracking and order aging analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this move order record was most recently modified in the Silver layer data store. Used for change data capture and incremental processing.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing move-in, move-out, and transfer-of-service orders that initiate or terminate service at a premise. Stores order type (move-in, move-out, transfer), requested date, actual effective date, prior account reference, new account reference, premise reference, meter read date at move, final read value, deposit disposition, and order status. Sourced from SAP IS-U Move-In/Move-Out and Oracle CC&B Start/Stop Service. Triggers meter read scheduling and final bill generation in downstream domains.'
}

Table customer.field_order {
  field_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for field_order']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location where the field work is to be performed.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing account to which charges are applied.']
  customer_service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_service_request. Business justification: Field orders in the customer domain are dispatched in response to customer service requests (e.g., connect/disconnect, meter inspection, equipment service). Linking field_order to its originating cust']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Field orders are dispatched for DER inspections, installations, maintenance, and decommissioning. Field crews require direct reference to the DER registry entry for asset specifications, interconnecti']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Field orders for DER commissioning, witness testing, inspection, and decommissioning require direct reference to the DER system for work scope definition, safety requirements (IEEE 1547 compliance), a']
  follow_up_field_order_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on field_order (follow_up_field_order_id)']
  grid_switching_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.grid_switching_order. Business justification: Customer field orders (meter sets, disconnects, reconnects) require coordination with grid switching orders to ensure safe de-energization before crew arrival. This safety clearance coordination is a ']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Billable field orders (meter installations, service connections, reconnections) generate invoices for labor and material charges. field_order has is_billable, amount_gross, amount_net confirming billi']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the meter associated with the field order, if applicable.']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: Field orders for meter installation and maintenance require meter_point context (service voltage, phase configuration, interval data flags) not available from meter_id alone. Technicians need meter_po']
  outage_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.event. Business justification: Field orders are dispatched in direct response to outage events (service restoration verification, meter inspection post-outage, damage assessment). Linking field_order to outage event enables trackin']
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: Field orders for equipment work requiring de-energization must be coordinated with planned outage windows. Linking field_order to planned_outage_window ensures field work is scheduled within approved ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer associated with the field order.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the asset related to the field order.']
  warranty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.warranty. Business justification: Field orders for equipment repair or replacement must reference the asset warranty to determine coverage eligibility and initiate warranty claims. Utility field operations require technicians to check']
  actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time taken to complete the field work.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when field work was completed.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when field work commenced.']
  amount_adjustments decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of monetary adjustments (taxes, fees, discounts) applied to the field order.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross monetary amount before adjustments for the field order.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net monetary amount after adjustments for the field order.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the field order.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the field order was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the field order.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the field order complies with regulatory requirements.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center code for financial tracking of the field order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the field order record was first captured in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  dispatch_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispatch the field order to a crew.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time required to complete the field work.']
  external_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to an external order system identifier.']
  external_system_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from an external system linked to this field order.']
  field_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the field order.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the field order is billable to the customer.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Soft delete indicator for the field order record.']
  is_emergency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the field order is an emergency response.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes related to the field order.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the field order.']
  order_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the field order was created or placed.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the field order indicating urgency.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the field order.']
  required_permits varchar [note: 'List of permits required to perform the field work.']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed safety risk level for the field work.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date and time for the field work.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date and time for the field work.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of service to be performed in the field order.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the field order record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  work_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tasks to be performed.']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work order based on planning horizon.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for field_order. Referenced by field_order_id.'
}

Table transmission.line {
  line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for line']
  bes_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.bes_facility. Business justification: Transmission lines with nerc_bes_flag=true have a corresponding BES facility compliance record. Linking enables compliance teams to track BES designation status, inspection results, and compliance sta']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Transmission lines operate under a specific balancing authority/control area for AGC, ATC calculations, and NERC TOP-001 outage coordination. The rto_iso_region is a denormalized text reference; a dir']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Transmission lines are physically located within forecast zones. Zonal load flow studies, ATC calculations, and congestion analysis require mapping lines to forecast zones. A transmission planning eng']
  line_transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating (sending-end) substation for the transmission line. Used to define the line\'s position in the network topology for power flow models, ATC calculations, and NERC reliability studies. References the substation master record.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: BES-designated transmission lines (nerc_bes_flag=true) are subject to NERC FAC-001/FAC-002 facility rating obligations. Linking each line to its governing obligation supports compliance evidence colle']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Transmission lines are capital assets requiring lifecycle tracking, depreciation, maintenance planning, and FERC regulatory reporting. Every utility tracks transmission lines in their asset registry f']
  to_substation_transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the terminating (receiving-end) substation for the transmission line. Together with from_substation_id, defines the line\'s terminal nodes in the network model for power flow, contingency analysis, and OATT path definitions.']
  circuit_configuration varchar [note: 'Physical circuit arrangement of the transmission line structure. Single-circuit lines carry one three-phase circuit; double-circuit lines carry two three-phase circuits on the same tower/structure. Affects contingency analysis (N-1, N-2), right-of-way utilization, and NERC TPL reliability planning studies.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the transmission line successfully completed commissioning tests and was accepted for energization. Distinct from in_service_date (commercial operation); commissioning_date marks the technical acceptance milestone. Used in warranty tracking and asset lifecycle management in Ventyx Asset Suite.']
  conductor_size_kcmil decimal(8,2) [note: 'Cross-sectional area of the conductor expressed in thousand circular mils (kcmil), the North American standard unit for conductor sizing. Directly determines the conductor\'s current-carrying capacity and is a key input to IEEE Std 738 dynamic line rating calculations.']
  conductor_type varchar [note: 'Designation of the conductor material and construction used on the transmission line (e.g., ACSR — Aluminum Conductor Steel Reinforced, ACCC — Aluminum Conductor Composite Core, AAAC — All Aluminum Alloy Conductor, HTLS — High-Temperature Low-Sag). Determines ampacity, sag characteristics, and thermal rating methodology per IEEE Std 738.']
  conductors_per_phase varchar [note: 'Number of sub-conductors per phase bundle (e.g., 1 = single, 2 = twin bundle, 3 = triple bundle, 4 = quad bundle). Bundled conductors are used on EHV lines to reduce corona discharge and increase ampacity. Affects thermal rating calculations and power flow model parameters.']
  emergency_rating_mva decimal(10,2) [note: 'Short-term emergency thermal rating of the transmission line in MVA, applicable for a defined duration (typically 15–30 minutes) following an N-1 contingency event. Used in contingency analysis, post-contingency corrective action planning, and NERC TPL reliability studies.']
  ferc_oatt_path_code varchar [note: 'FERC OATT transmission path or flowgate code associated with this line, used in ATC/TTC calculations, transmission service requests, and congestion management. Links the physical line to the commercial transmission path definitions in the OATT tariff schedules.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Unique feature identifier for this transmission line in the Esri ArcGIS Utility Network geospatial system. Enables spatial joins for route mapping, right-of-way analysis, outage impact assessment, and integration with OMS/ADMS systems.']
  in_service_date date [note: 'Date on which the transmission line was energized and placed into commercial service. Used for asset age calculations, depreciation schedules in SAP S/4HANA, FERC Form 1 plant-in-service reporting, and NERC reliability model versioning.']
  insulation_type varchar [note: 'Type of electrical insulation used on the transmission line. For overhead lines, refers to insulator material (porcelain, glass, polymer). For underground/submarine cables, refers to cable insulation type (XLPE, PILC). Affects dielectric performance, maintenance intervals, and failure mode analysis.']
  length_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total route length of the transmission line in kilometers. Metric equivalent of length_miles for international reporting, IEC standard compliance, and cross-border interconnection documentation. Sourced from Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.']
  length_miles decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total route length of the transmission line in miles. Used in impedance calculations, line loss estimation, right-of-way cost allocation, FERC transmission rate base calculations, and O&M cost modeling. Sourced from Esri ArcGIS Utility Network spatial data.']
  line_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the transmission line within the utility\'s network and used in FERC OATT filings, NERC reliability models, and EMS/SCADA systems. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE. Sourced from GE PowerOn ADMS and Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.']
  line_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the transmission line, typically referencing the from/to substations or geographic corridor (e.g., \'Northgate–Riverside 345kV\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE. Used in operational displays, FERC filings, and customer-facing interconnection documents.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Physical construction type of the transmission line. Overhead lines use aerial conductors on towers/poles; underground lines use buried cables; submarine lines cross bodies of water. Determines applicable rating standards, maintenance practices, and outage restoration timelines.']
  load_dump_rating_mva decimal(10,2) [note: 'Extreme short-term (load dump) thermal rating in MVA, applicable for a very brief duration (typically 1–5 minutes) under extreme contingency conditions before protective relay action. Used in NERC TPL extreme event (P5–P7) analysis and system protection scheme design.']
  nerc_bes_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transmission line is classified as part of the NERC Bulk Electric System (BES), which subjects it to mandatory NERC reliability standards (CIP, FAC, TPL, MOD, etc.). Lines operating at ≥100 kV are generally BES-applicable. Critical for NERC compliance program scoping.']
  nerc_facility_rating_code varchar [note: 'NERC-assigned or utility-assigned facility rating identifier used in NERC FAC-008 compliance documentation and transmission planning model submissions (NERC MOD-032). Links this line record to the formal facility rating study documentation.']
  normal_rating_amps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Continuous normal thermal rating expressed in amperes (A) per phase. Complements normal_rating_mva for use in protection relay settings, SCADA alarm thresholds in OSIsoft PI, and conductor temperature monitoring applications where current is the direct measured quantity.']
  normal_rating_mva decimal(10,2) [note: 'Continuous normal (steady-state) thermal rating of the transmission line in megavolt-amperes (MVA) under standard ambient conditions. This is the primary facility rating used in real-time operations, ATC calculations, and NERC FAC-008 compliance. Represents the maximum continuous loading without exceeding conductor temperature limits.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transmission line. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE. Drives inclusion in power flow models, ATC calculations, and NERC reliability assessments. Sourced from GE PowerOn ADMS and Ventyx Asset Suite.']
  owner_entity_code varchar [note: 'NERC-registered entity identifier (e.g., NERC Entity ID or DUNS number) of the primary owner/operator of the transmission line. Used in NERC reliability filings, FERC OATT compliance, and joint-ownership coordination. For lines with multiple owners, represents the operating owner.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the transmission line owned by this utility (0.00–100.00). Joint-ownership lines are common in the bulk power system. Drives rate base allocation in FERC OATT transmission rates, revenue requirement calculations, and co-owner settlement in OpenLink Endur.']
  pnode_code varchar [note: 'RTO/ISO pricing node (PNode) identifier associated with the transmission line\'s terminal buses. Used in LMP (Locational Marginal Price) calculations, congestion cost allocation, FTR settlement, and energy trading position management in OpenLink Endur.']
  protection_scheme varchar [note: 'Primary protection scheme applied to the transmission line (e.g., \'Distance-Zone 1-2-3\', \'Pilot-POTT\', \'Pilot-DCUB\', \'Current Differential\'). Critical for NERC PRC (Protection and Control) standard compliance, relay coordination studies, and system protection scheme documentation.']
  rating_last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the transmission line\'s facility ratings were most recently reviewed and validated by the approving engineer. NERC FAC-008 requires periodic review of facility ratings. Triggers re-review workflow in Ventyx Asset Suite when approaching the required review interval.']
  rating_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to determine the transmission line\'s thermal ratings. Static ratings use fixed conservative assumptions; dynamic line ratings (DLR) use real-time weather and conductor temperature data; ambient-adjusted ratings use seasonal ambient temperature corrections. Required disclosure under NERC FAC-008 and FERC Order 881 (ambient-adjusted ratings mandate).']
  reactance_ohms_per_mile decimal(10,6) [note: 'Positive-sequence inductive reactance of the transmission line in ohms per mile. Critical parameter for power flow models, short-circuit analysis, and distance protection relay settings. Used in NERC MOD-032 power flow base case models submitted to RTOs/ISOs.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transmission line master record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this MASTER_RESOURCE. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and regulatory record-keeping under SOX and NERC CIP.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transmission line master record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer, audit trail compliance under SOX, and detecting stale records in power flow model synchronization workflows.']
  resistance_ohms_per_mile decimal(10,6) [note: 'AC resistance of the transmission line conductor in ohms per mile at 50°C (or specified temperature). A fundamental electrical parameter used in power flow models (PSS/E, PowerWorld), line loss calculations, and impedance-based protection relay settings. Sourced from conductor manufacturer data sheets.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transmission line was or is planned to be permanently retired from service. Nullable for active lines. Used in asset lifecycle management (Ventyx Asset Suite), FERC Form 1 retirements reporting, IRP planning, and NERC model updates.']
  scada_tag_code varchar [note: 'SCADA system tag identifier for the transmission line\'s real-time telemetry point in OSIsoft PI historian. Used to retrieve real-time MW flow, MVAR flow, and current measurements for EMS state estimation, real-time ATC monitoring, and NERC reliability coordinator data exchange.']
  spring_fall_normal_rating_mva decimal(10,2) [note: 'Seasonal normal thermal rating for spring and fall shoulder seasons (typically March–May and September–November) in MVA. Used in seasonal ATC calculations and NERC seasonal assessments where a third seasonal rating is required.']
  summer_normal_rating_mva decimal(10,2) [note: 'Seasonal normal thermal rating for summer conditions (typically June–August) in MVA, reflecting higher ambient temperatures that reduce conductor ampacity. Used in seasonal ATC calculations, summer peak planning, and NERC seasonal assessments submitted to RTOs/ISOs.']
  susceptance_siemens_per_mile decimal(12,8) [note: 'Positive-sequence shunt susceptance (charging susceptance) of the transmission line in siemens per mile. Represents the line\'s capacitive charging current contribution, which is significant for long EHV lines. Used in power flow models, reactive power planning, and Volt-VAR optimization.']
  tower_structure_type varchar [note: 'Type of supporting structure used for the overhead transmission line. Determines structural loading capacity, maintenance access requirements, storm vulnerability, and asset replacement cost estimates in Ventyx Asset Suite capital planning.']
  voltage_class varchar [note: 'Standardized voltage class designation for the transmission line used in asset categorization, rate base segmentation, and FERC OATT rate zone assignment. Complements the precise voltage_kv field with a discrete classification bucket aligned to industry-standard voltage tiers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 69kV|115kV|138kV|161kV|230kV|345kV|500kV|765kV — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  voltage_kv decimal(7,2) [note: 'Nominal operating voltage of the transmission line in kilovolts (kV). Serves as the principal MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this MASTER_RESOURCE. Determines voltage class (e.g., 115 kV, 230 kV, 345 kV, 500 kV, 765 kV), which governs thermal ratings, insulation requirements, and NERC bulk electric system (BES) applicability thresholds (≥100 kV).']
  winter_normal_rating_mva decimal(10,2) [note: 'Seasonal normal thermal rating for winter conditions (typically December–February) in MVA, reflecting lower ambient temperatures that increase conductor ampacity. Used in seasonal ATC calculations, winter peak planning, and NERC seasonal assessments.']

  Note: 'Master record for high-voltage bulk power transmission lines including overhead and underground circuits, with NERC-compliant facility ratings. Captures line identifier, voltage class (kV), circuit configuration, conductor type, thermal rating (MVA), length (miles/km), right-of-way corridor reference, ownership percentage, in-service date, operational status, and facility ratings (normal/emergency/load-dump ratings in MVA/A, seasonal variants for summer/winter/spring-fall, rating methodology, last review date, approving engineer). SSOT for transmission line physical characteristics, electrical parameters, and thermal limits used in power flow models, ATC calculations, real-time operations, and FERC OATT compliance.'
}

Table transmission.transmission_substation {
  transmission_substation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the transmission substation record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the primary key and topological node anchor for the transmission network model. MASTER_RESOURCE role — canonical-attrs-enforced.']
  bes_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.bes_facility. Business justification: Substations with bes_designation attribute have a corresponding BES facility compliance record. This link is essential for NERC compliance teams to track BES compliance status, inspection history, and']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Transmission substations are operationally assigned to a control area for switching authority, state estimation topology, and NERC CIP asset scoping. The rto_iso_region is denormalized; a direct FK is']
  control_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_center. Business justification: Transmission substations are operated under the jurisdiction of a specific control center for switching authority and real-time monitoring. Direct FK enables control center workload management, NERC T']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Substations are the physical nodes within forecast zones. Zonal load forecasts are aggregated from substation-level measurements. IRP and capacity planning require substation-to-zone mapping for trans']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: BES-designated substations (bes_designation attribute) are governed by NERC FAC/TOP/CIP reliability obligations beyond CIP alone. Linking to the primary reliability obligation enables compliance track']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Substations are major capital assets requiring comprehensive asset management for regulatory rate base, depreciation schedules, capital project planning, and FERC reporting. Standard practice in utili']
  atc_contribution_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'This substation\'s contribution to the Available Transfer Capability (ATC) calculation in megawatts (MW) as determined by the most recent power flow study. Used in transmission planning, congestion management, and OATT ATC posting requirements.']
  bes_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this substation is designated as part of the Bulk Electric System (BES) per NERC BES definition. True = BES asset subject to full NERC reliability standards; False = non-BES asset. Critical for determining applicable NERC compliance obligations.']
  bus_configuration varchar [note: 'Electrical bus arrangement of the substation switchyard. Determines switching flexibility, reliability, and maintenance capability. Used in outage planning, NERC TPL reliability studies, and ADMS network modeling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single-bus|double-bus|ring-bus|breaker-and-a-half|double-bus-double-breaker — promote to reference product]']
  cip_asset_classification varchar [note: 'NERC CIP-002 BES Cyber System impact classification for this substation. \'high\' = high-impact BES asset; \'medium\' = medium-impact; \'low\' = low-impact; \'not-applicable\' = not classified as a BES Cyber Asset. Drives cybersecurity control requirements under NERC CIP-003 through CIP-014.']
  climate_zone varchar [note: 'Climate zone classification for the substation site (e.g., ASHRAE climate zone or utility-defined zone). Used in equipment de-rating calculations, extreme weather resilience planning, and NERC EOP-011 cold weather preparedness assessments.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the substation was officially energized and placed into commercial service. Used in asset lifecycle management (Ventyx Asset Suite), depreciation calculations (SAP S/4HANA), and NERC facility registration.']
  control_building_type varchar [note: 'Physical configuration of the substation control house or control building. \'indoor\' = fully enclosed GIS or AIS indoor design; \'outdoor\' = open-air AIS with separate control building; \'hybrid\' = mixed indoor/outdoor; \'mobile\' = temporary or mobile substation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the substation is located (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Used for cross-border interconnection reporting and NERC/FERC jurisdictional classification.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which the substation was permanently retired from service. Null for active substations. Used in asset lifecycle management, regulatory retirement filings, and RAB adjustments.']
  elevation_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation of the substation site above mean sea level in meters. Used in environmental assessments, flood risk analysis, and equipment insulation coordination at high-altitude sites.']
  ferc_facility_code varchar [note: 'FERC-assigned facility identifier for this substation used in FERC Form 1 transmission plant reporting and OATT compliance filings. Required for FERC-jurisdictional transmission assets.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Unique feature identifier for this substation in the Esri ArcGIS Utility Network geospatial system. Used to link the asset record to its spatial representation for network modeling, outage analysis, and field crew dispatch.']
  installed_transformer_mva decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total installed transformer capacity at the substation in megavolt-amperes (MVA). Represents the aggregate nameplate rating of all power transformers at this node. Used in congestion management, ATC calculations, and capacity planning.']
  insulation_medium varchar [note: 'Primary insulation technology used in the substation switchgear. \'AIS\' = Air-Insulated Switchgear; \'GIS\' = Gas-Insulated Switchgear (SF6); \'hybrid\' = combination of AIS and GIS. Affects maintenance requirements, footprint, and environmental compliance (SF6 reporting under EPA regulations).']
  interconnection_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the FERC-filed interconnection agreement (e.g., LGIA or SGIA) associated with this substation\'s point of interconnection (POI). Used in OATT compliance and interconnection queue management.']
  last_major_refurbishment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent major capital refurbishment or upgrade of the substation. Used in asset condition assessment, CAPEX planning, and remaining useful life estimation in Ventyx Asset Suite.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'WGS-84 geographic latitude coordinate of the substation in decimal degrees. Used in GIS (Esri ArcGIS Utility Network) for network modeling, outage analysis, and asset location. Positive values indicate north of the equator.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'WGS-84 geographic longitude coordinate of the substation in decimal degrees. Used in GIS (Esri ArcGIS Utility Network) for network modeling, outage analysis, and asset location. Negative values indicate west of the prime meridian.']
  nerc_node_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known NERC-assigned unique node identifier for this substation used in reliability coordination, power flow models, and NERC compliance reporting. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE. Aligns with NERC FAC-001 and FAC-002 facility registration requirements.']
  nerc_reliability_region varchar [note: 'NERC reliability region designation (e.g., MRO, SERC, WECC, RFC, NPCC, TRE) for this substation. Determines applicable NERC reliability standards, TPL planning criteria, and CIP cybersecurity requirements.']
  number_of_bays varchar [note: 'Total number of switchgear bays (feeder bays, transformer bays, bus-coupler bays) installed at the substation. Used in capacity planning, maintenance scheduling, and asset inventory management in Ventyx Asset Suite.']
  number_of_circuit_breakers varchar [note: 'Total count of high-voltage circuit breakers installed at this substation. Used in asset inventory, maintenance scheduling, and NERC FAC-002 facility rating assessments.']
  number_of_transformers varchar [note: 'Count of power transformers installed at this substation. Used in asset inventory, maintenance planning, and transformer fleet management in Ventyx Asset Suite and SAP S/4HANA PM.']
  operating_entity_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity responsible for operating and maintaining this substation. May differ from the owning entity in cases of contracted O&M or joint-ownership arrangements.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the substation. \'in-service\' = energized and operational; \'out-of-service\' = temporarily de-energized; \'under-construction\' = not yet commissioned; \'decommissioned\' = permanently retired; \'mothballed\' = preserved but inactive. Serves as LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE. Drives asset management workflows in Ventyx Asset Suite and SAP S/4HANA PM.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity that owns this substation. Determines regulatory jurisdiction, rate-setting authority, and OATT applicability. Used in FERC Form 1 reporting and RAB calculations.']
  owning_entity_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the utility or entity that owns this substation. May differ from the operating entity in joint-ownership or leased facility arrangements. Used in FERC Form 1 and NERC registration filings.']
  physical_security_level varchar [note: 'Physical security classification of the substation per NERC CIP-006 Physical Security Program requirements. Determines perimeter security controls, access management, and visitor logging requirements.']
  pnode_code varchar [note: 'The Locational Marginal Price (LMP) pricing node identifier assigned by the RTO/ISO for this substation. Used in energy trading settlement (OpenLink Endur), FTR position management, and DAM/RTM market operations.']
  primary_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal primary (high-side) voltage level of the substation in kilovolts (kV). Represents the principal quantitative rating of this transmission asset. Serves as MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this MASTER_RESOURCE. Used in power flow models, ATC calculations, and NERC reliability assessments.']
  protection_scheme_type varchar [note: 'Primary protection scheme employed at this substation. Determines relay coordination requirements and NERC PRC (Protection and Control) standard applicability. Used in protection engineering and NERC PRC-001 compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: differential|distance|overcurrent|pilot-wire|directional-comparison|hybrid — promote to reference product]']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this substation record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record provenance tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this substation record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for change tracking, data quality monitoring, and incremental ETL processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  sap_functional_location varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance functional location code for this substation. Used to link the substation to work orders, maintenance plans, equipment master records, and cost center assignments in SAP.']
  scada_integration_point varchar [note: 'Identifier or endpoint reference for the SCADA integration point connecting this substation to the Energy Management System (EMS). Used in OSIsoft PI historian configuration and GE PowerOn ADMS real-time data feeds for grid state estimation.']
  seismic_zone varchar [note: 'Seismic hazard zone designation for the substation site per IEEE Std 693 or applicable building code. Used in equipment qualification, civil engineering assessments, and resilience planning.']
  short_circuit_level_ka decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum prospective short-circuit current level at the substation busbars in kiloamperes (kA). Used in equipment rating verification, protection relay coordination, and circuit breaker interrupting capacity assessments.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 3166-2 state or province code where the substation is physically located. Used for jurisdictional regulatory reporting to State Public Utility Commissions (PUCs) and FERC.']
  substation_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the substation in operational systems including EMS, SCADA, and SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance. Typically a 2–10 character mnemonic aligned with the utility\'s internal naming convention.']
  substation_name varchar [note: 'Official human-readable name of the transmission substation as registered in the utility\'s asset management system (Ventyx Asset Suite) and GIS (Esri ArcGIS Utility Network). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE. Used in operational displays, SCADA, and regulatory filings.']
  substation_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the substation. \'transmission\' = bulk power transmission node; \'switching\' = bus-switching station without transformation; \'transformer\' = voltage step-up/step-down station; \'converter\' = AC/DC or DC/AC conversion station; \'collector\' = renewable energy collector substation. Serves as CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  tertiary_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal tertiary winding voltage level in kilovolts (kV) for three-winding transformer substations. Null when no tertiary winding exists. Used in detailed power flow and short-circuit studies.']

  Note: 'Master record for high-voltage transmission substations including switching stations and transformer substations. Captures substation name, NERC node identifier, voltage levels served, bus configuration, geographic coordinates (GIS), ownership, commissioning date, SCADA integration point, and operational status. Serves as the topological node anchor for the transmission network model and interconnects with EMS/SCADA systems.'
}

Table transmission.power_transformer {
  power_transformer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bulk power transformer asset. Primary key for the power transformer master record.']
  bes_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.bes_facility. Business justification: Critical power transformers (is_critical_asset=true) that qualify as BES facilities have a bes_facility compliance record. Linking enables NERC compliance tracking of transformer BES status, inspectio']
  from_bus_ems_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.ems_node. Business justification: Power transformers are modeled as two-winding branches in the EMS with from-bus and to-bus nodes. This FK is fundamental to power flow modeling, state estimation, and contingency analysis. Role-prefix']
  nerc_cip_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.nerc_cip_asset. Business justification: Power transformers at critical substations are BES cyber assets subject to NERC CIP-007 configuration management and CIP-010 change control requirements. Asset-level compliance tracking is mandatory f']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Critical power transformers (is_critical_asset=true) are subject to NERC TPL and FAC obligations for ratings, maintenance intervals, and spare transformer programs. Linking to the governing obligation']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Power transformers are high-value critical assets requiring detailed lifecycle management, condition monitoring, depreciation tracking, and replacement analysis. Utilities maintain comprehensive asset']
  transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transmission substation where this power transformer is installed.']
  book_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current net book value of the transformer after accumulated depreciation. Used for financial reporting, rate case filings, and asset valuation.']
  capital_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original capital cost of the transformer in US dollars including equipment, installation, and commissioning. Used for depreciation calculations, rate base determination, and capital investment reporting.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the transformer was energized and placed into commercial operation. May differ from installation date due to testing and acceptance procedures.']
  condition_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite condition assessment score based on dissolved gas analysis, oil quality tests, winding resistance, insulation power factor, and visual inspections. Typically scaled 0-100 where higher scores indicate better condition. Used for risk-based maintenance prioritization.']
  cooling_class varchar [note: 'Transformer cooling method classification per IEEE standards. ONAN (Oil Natural Air Natural) is self-cooled; ONAF (Oil Natural Air Forced) uses fans; OFAF (Oil Forced Air Forced) uses pumps and fans. Cooling class affects MVA rating and operational efficiency.']
  current_tap_position varchar [note: 'Current tap position setting of the transformer. Used for real-time voltage regulation monitoring and optimization. Typically monitored via SCADA.']
  expected_retirement_date date [note: 'Planned date for transformer retirement or replacement based on asset lifecycle planning, condition assessment, and capital investment programs.']
  ferc_account_code varchar [note: 'FERC Uniform System of Accounts code for the transformer asset. Transmission transformers typically classified under Account 352 (Structures and Improvements) or Account 353 (Station Equipment).']
  gis_location_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking the transformer to its geospatial representation in the utility GIS system. Used for network modeling, outage analysis, and asset visualization.']
  health_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'Normalized health index score combining condition assessment, age, loading history, and failure probability. Used for asset investment planning and replacement prioritization. Typically scaled 0-10 or 0-100.']
  impedance_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage impedance of the transformer on its rated MVA base. Critical parameter for short-circuit calculations, fault current analysis, and power flow modeling in Energy Management Systems (EMS).']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the transformer was installed and commissioned at the substation. Used for calculating asset age, warranty periods, and maintenance schedules.']
  insulation_type varchar [note: 'Type of insulating medium used in the transformer. Mineral oil is most common for transmission transformers; ester fluids offer environmental benefits; dry-type uses solid insulation without liquid.']
  is_critical_asset boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the transformer is designated as a critical asset for grid reliability. Critical assets receive enhanced monitoring, maintenance priority, and security measures per NERC CIP standards.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent comprehensive inspection of the transformer. Used for tracking inspection compliance and scheduling future inspections.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent scheduled or corrective maintenance activity performed on the transformer. Used for maintenance history tracking and interval planning.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the transformer location in decimal degrees. Used for spatial analysis, crew dispatch, and emergency response planning.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the transformer location in decimal degrees. Used for spatial analysis, crew dispatch, and emergency response planning.']
  manufacture_year varchar [note: 'Year the transformer was manufactured. Used for age-based risk assessment, depreciation calculations, and replacement planning.']
  mva_rating decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nameplate power capacity rating of the transformer in megavolt-amperes. Represents the maximum apparent power the transformer can handle under specified conditions. Critical for transmission capacity planning and Available Transfer Capability (ATC) calculations.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity jurisdiction where the transformer is located. Used for reliability compliance reporting, regional coordination, and regulatory oversight. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|ERCOT|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|SPP|TRE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  next_maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next planned maintenance activity based on time-based or condition-based maintenance strategy.']
  oil_volume_gallons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total volume of insulating oil in the transformer in gallons. Used for oil replacement planning, environmental compliance reporting, and spill containment design. Null for dry-type transformers.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the transformer in the transmission network. In-service indicates active operation; standby indicates ready for service but not currently energized; maintenance indicates scheduled or unscheduled maintenance activities.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of ownership arrangement for the transformer. Owned assets are utility property; leased assets are rented; joint ownership involves multiple utilities; customer-owned transformers serve large industrial loads.']
  pcb_contamination_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the transformer contains or is contaminated with PCBs above regulatory thresholds. PCB-contaminated transformers require special handling, disposal procedures, and EPA reporting.']
  phase_configuration varchar [note: 'Electrical phase configuration of the transformer. Three-phase transformers are standard for bulk power transmission; single-phase units may be used in banks; phase-shifting transformers control power flow direction.']
  primary_voltage_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rated voltage of the primary winding in kilovolts. Typically the higher voltage side for step-down transformers or the lower voltage side for step-up transformers.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transformer record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage tracking and audit trail.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transformer record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  rto_iso_name varchar [note: 'Name of the RTO or ISO that operates the transmission grid where this transformer is located. Examples include PJM, CAISO, MISO, NYISO, ISO-NE, SPP. Used for market operations, congestion management, and transmission rights tracking.']
  scada_tag varchar [note: 'Unique SCADA system tag identifier for real-time monitoring and control of the transformer. Links the asset record to telemetry data streams for operational monitoring, alarm management, and Energy Management System (EMS) integration.']
  tap_changer_type varchar [note: 'Type of tap changer installed on the transformer. DETC (De-Energized Tap Changer) requires transformer to be offline for adjustment; LTC (Load Tap Changer) allows voltage adjustment under load. Critical for voltage regulation and grid stability.']
  tap_position_range varchar [note: 'Available tap positions for voltage adjustment, typically expressed as a range (e.g., -10 to +10, or 1 to 33). Each tap position represents a discrete voltage adjustment step.']
  tertiary_voltage_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rated voltage of the tertiary winding in kilovolts, if present. Tertiary windings are used for auxiliary loads, reactive power compensation, or harmonic filtering. Null if transformer has only two windings.']
  transformer_tag varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique asset tag or equipment identifier assigned to the transformer for field identification and maintenance tracking. Typically follows utility naming convention.']
  transformer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transformer based on its functional role in the transmission network. Step-up transformers increase voltage from generation to transmission levels; step-down transformers reduce voltage for distribution or industrial loads.']
  weight_pounds decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total weight of the transformer in pounds including oil, core, windings, and tank. Critical for transportation planning, foundation design, and seismic analysis.']
  winding_connection varchar [note: 'Configuration of primary and secondary winding connections. Delta-wye and wye-delta are most common for transmission transformers. Affects voltage transformation ratio, phase shift, and harmonic performance.']

  Note: 'Master record for bulk power transformers installed at transmission substations. Captures transformer identifier, MVA rating, primary and secondary voltage (kV), impedance, cooling class (ONAN/ONAF), tap changer type, manufacturer, serial number, installation date, condition assessment score, and SCADA tag. Critical asset for transmission capacity and voltage regulation; feeds asset management and reliability reporting.'
}

Table transmission.path {
  path_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for path']
  from_control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Transmission paths connect source and sink control areas for ATC/TTC calculations and interchange scheduling per NERC MOD-001. The from_control_area_code is denormalized. Direct FK enables automated A']
  line_id varchar [note: 'FK to transmission.line.line_id — Transmission paths/flowgates are composed of one or more transmission lines. This is a many-to-many relationship critical for ATC calculations, congestion analysis, and outage impact assessment. The p']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: Transmission paths are rated based on forecasted load conditions for seasonal transfer capability determination. Operations planning uses load forecasts to establish path ratings, calculate seasonal l']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Transmission paths have specific NERC MOD and FERC OATT obligations for ATC posting frequency, TTC calculation methodology, and reliability monitoring. Each path is subject to defined compliance requi']
  available_transfer_capability_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Current available transmission capacity on the path for additional power transfers, calculated as total transfer capability minus existing transmission commitments and capacity benefit margin, measured in megawatts.']
  average_loss_factor_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'Average transmission energy loss rate across the path for typical power flow levels, expressed as a percentage. Used for transmission loss allocation in OATT billing.']
  base_case_transfer_limit_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Normal operating transfer capability limit for the transmission path under base case system conditions, measured in megawatts. Represents the maximum power flow allowed without violating thermal, voltage, or stability criteria.']
  capacity_benefit_margin_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Amount of transmission transfer capability reserved by load serving entities to ensure access to generation from interconnected systems to meet generation reliability requirements, measured in megawatts.']
  congestion_status varchar [note: 'Current congestion state of the transmission path indicating whether power flow is approaching or exceeding transfer limits, requiring congestion management actions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transmission path record was first created in the system.']
  emergency_transfer_limit_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable power transfer on the transmission path under emergency operating conditions, measured in megawatts. Typically higher than base case limit and used for short durations during system contingencies.']
  ems_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transmission path is integrated with the Energy Management System for state estimation, contingency analysis, and optimal power flow calculations. True if EMS-integrated, false otherwise.']
  ferc_approved_loss_rate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transmission loss rates for this path have been approved by FERC as part of the Open Access Transmission Tariff (OATT). True if FERC-approved, false otherwise.']
  flowgate_rating_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Thermal or stability rating of the flowgate (most limiting transmission element) on this path, measured in megawatts. Determines the path transfer capability.']
  identifier varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the transmission path used in operational communications, NERC reporting, and RTO/ISO scheduling. Examples include NERC-registered path names or internal path codes.']
  in_service_date date [note: 'Date when the transmission path was first placed into commercial operation and became available for power transfer.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transmission path record was most recently updated, reflecting changes to path attributes, ratings, or operational parameters.']
  last_rating_study_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent transmission path rating study or transfer capability analysis conducted to determine current operating limits.']
  length_miles decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length of the transmission path in miles, measured along the transmission line route from source to destination.']
  loss_factor_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current transmission loss factors became effective for billing and settlement purposes.']
  loss_factor_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current transmission loss factors expire and are replaced by updated values, typically aligned with tariff revision cycles.']
