  loss_factor_period_type varchar [note: 'Time-of-day period classification for transmission loss factors, distinguishing between peak demand hours and off-peak hours when losses differ due to loading levels.']
  loss_factor_season varchar [note: 'Seasonal period for which the transmission loss factors are applicable, reflecting seasonal variations in system loading and ambient conditions.']
  marginal_loss_factor_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'Incremental transmission loss rate for an additional unit of power flow on this path, expressed as a percentage. Used in economic dispatch and locational marginal pricing calculations.']
  nerc_path_designation varchar [note: 'Official NERC-registered path designation or identifier used for reliability coordination and compliance reporting.']
  nerc_reliability_coordinator varchar [note: 'Name of the NERC Reliability Coordinator responsible for real-time monitoring and coordination of this transmission path to maintain bulk electric system reliability.']
  next_rating_study_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next transmission path rating study or transfer capability reassessment, typically required annually or after significant system changes.']
  oatt_service_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission service offered on this path under the OATT: firm (guaranteed), non-firm (interruptible), network (integrated transmission service), or point-to-point (specific source to sink).']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of ownership structure for the transmission path assets: wholly owned, jointly owned with other utilities, leased from another entity, contracted capacity, or third-party owned.']
  path_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the transmission path including geographic routing, major substations, transmission line segments, and operational characteristics.']
  path_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the transmission path, often reflecting geographic endpoints or operational significance.']
  path_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the transmission path in the transmission network lifecycle.']
  path_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transmission path based on its operational role: flowgate (constrained transmission element), interface (aggregated transfer capability boundary), corridor (multi-element transmission route), tie_line (interconnection between control areas), interconnection (connection to external grid), or internal (within control area).']
  rto_iso_name varchar [note: 'Name of the RTO or ISO that has operational authority or scheduling responsibility for this transmission path. Examples: PJM, CAISO, MISO, ERCOT, SPP, NYISO, ISO-NE.']
  scada_monitoring_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transmission path is monitored in real-time through SCADA systems for power flow, voltage, and equipment status. True if SCADA-monitored, false otherwise.']
  to_control_area_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the destination control area or balancing authority area for power flow on this transmission path.']
  total_transfer_capability_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum amount of electric power that can be transferred over the interconnected transmission network in a reliable manner while meeting all of a specific set of defined pre- and post-contingency system conditions, measured in megawatts.']
  transmission_reliability_margin_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Amount of transmission transfer capability necessary to ensure that the interconnected transmission network is secure under a reasonable range of uncertainties in system conditions, measured in megawatts.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage level of the transmission path in kilovolts. Common high-voltage transmission levels include 115kV, 138kV, 230kV, 345kV, 500kV, and 765kV.']

  Note: 'Defines a logical transmission path or flowgate between two electrical areas or control zones. Captures path identifier, from-area, to-area, associated transmission lines (flowgate elements), base case transfer limit (MW), emergency limit, NERC path designation, transmission loss factors (marginal/average loss percentage, seasonal/peak-off-peak differentiation, effective date range, FERC-approved loss rate), and interchange schedule records (RTO/ISO name, scheduling period, source/sink control area, e-tag identifier, scheduled/actual MW, curtailment status, settlement reference). SSOT for transmission path topology, transfer limits, loss allocation for OATT billing, and scheduled energy flows across control area boundaries supporting NERC BAL compliance and energy trading settlement.'
}

Table transmission.atc_calculation {
  atc_calculation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ATC calculation record. Primary key for the ATC calculation transactional event.']
  contingency_analysis_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.contingency_analysis_run. Business justification: ATC calculations use post-contingency flows from contingency analysis runs to determine TTC/ATC limits per NERC MOD-001. The limiting_contingency is a denormalized text field. Direct FK provides full ']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: ATC calculations require load forecasts to determine available transfer capability under forecasted system conditions. Standard transmission operations planning process uses forecasted load to calcula']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: ATC calculations are mandated by NERC MOD-001 standards and FERC OATT posting requirements. Linking each calculation to the governing NERC MOD obligation enables compliance verification that methodolo']
  peak_demand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.peak_demand. Business justification: NERC and FERC require ATC calculations to be performed under peak demand conditions. Peak demand forecasts define the study case load levels for ATC studies. A transmission planning engineer would exp']
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: NERC and OATT require ATC recalculation when planned outages reduce transmission capacity. Linking atc_calculation to the planned_outage_window that triggered the study enables OASIS posting of update']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.position. Business justification: ATC calculations inform trading position limits on constrained transmission paths. Traders reference specific ATC results when sizing positions for transmission capacity hedging, required for position']
  primary_atc_path_id varchar [note: 'FK to transmission.path.path_id — Every ATC calculation is performed for a specific transmission path. This is the fundamental FK that connects ATC values to the network topology. Required for OASIS posting and OATT scheduling.']
  source_control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: ATC calculations are performed between source and sink balancing authorities per NERC MOD-001. The source_ba_code is denormalized. Direct FK to control_area enables OASIS posting automation, FERC tari']
  state_estimation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.state_estimation_run. Business justification: ATC calculations require state estimator base case solutions for topology, power flow, and contingency analysis. NERC MOD standards mandate ATC calculations use validated EMS state estimation. New FK ']
  triggering_outage_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: Major transmission outages trigger ATC recalculations per FERC OATT requirements. Linking ATC calculations to the triggering outage enables automated recalculation workflows, regulatory audit trails, ']
  atc_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Available Transfer Capability: the measure of the transfer capability remaining in the physical transmission network for further commercial activity over and above already committed uses. Calculated as TTC minus TRM minus CBM minus ETC. Measured in megawatts.']
  base_case_generation_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total system generation assumed in the base case power flow model used for the ATC calculation. Measured in megawatts.']
  base_case_load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total system load assumed in the base case power flow model used for the ATC calculation. Measured in megawatts.']
  calculation_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate ATC: Rated System Path (RSP), Network Response (NR), Available Flowgate Capability (AFC), or other approved method per NERC standards.']
  calculation_run_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the batch or scheduled run that produced this ATC calculation, enabling traceability to the calculation job or process instance.']
  calculation_software varchar [note: 'Name and version of the software application used to perform the ATC calculation (e.g., EMS application, power flow analysis tool, ATC calculation engine).']
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ATC calculation was performed. Represents the principal business event time for this transactional record.']
  calculation_type varchar [note: 'Type of ATC calculation based on the time horizon: day-ahead, hour-ahead, real-time, monthly, or seasonal study.']
  cbm_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Capacity Benefit Margin: the amount of transmission transfer capability reserved for Load-Serving Entities to access generation from interconnected systems to meet generation reliability requirements. Measured in megawatts.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, explanations, or context related to this ATC calculation, such as special operating conditions or unusual circumstances.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ATC calculation record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  ems_snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the EMS real-time or historical snapshot used as input data for the ATC calculation. Represents the system state at a specific point in time.']
  etc_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Existing Transmission Commitments: the sum of all firm transmission service reservations, grandfathered rights, and other committed uses of the transmission system. Measured in megawatts.']
  ferc_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ATC calculation is subject to FERC reporting requirements under OATT compliance (True) or not (False).']
  firm_atc_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'ATC available for firm transmission service reservations, which have the highest priority and are not subject to curtailment except under emergency conditions. Measured in megawatts.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ATC calculation record was last updated or modified. Audit trail for record changes.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission system limit that constrains the ATC: thermal (line/transformer rating), voltage (bus voltage limit), stability (transient or dynamic stability), or other.']
  limiting_facility varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the transmission facility (line, transformer, flowgate) that reaches its thermal, voltage, or stability limit first, constraining the ATC value.']
  nerc_reliability_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity responsible for reliability coordination and compliance for this transmission path: WECC, ERCOT, MRO, NPCC, RF, SERC, SPP, or TRE. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|ERCOT|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|SPP|TRE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  non_firm_atc_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'ATC available for non-firm transmission service reservations, which are lower priority and subject to curtailment when firm service is needed. Measured in megawatts.']
  oasis_posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ATC value was posted to the OASIS system for public access by transmission customers.']
  oatt_service_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission service under OATT for which this ATC is calculated: network integration service, point-to-point firm service, or point-to-point non-firm service.']
  operator_adjustment_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Manual adjustment applied by the transmission operator to the calculated ATC value based on operational judgment, real-time conditions, or reliability concerns. Measured in megawatts.']
  operator_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or justification for any manual operator adjustment applied to the ATC value, documenting the operational or reliability rationale.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of the ATC value posting to OASIS: pending (not yet posted), posted (published to OASIS), revised (updated after initial posting), or withdrawn (removed from OASIS).']
  rto_iso_region varchar [note: 'Name or code of the RTO or ISO region responsible for this transmission path and ATC calculation (e.g., PJM, MISO, CAISO, SPP, ERCOT, NYISO, ISO-NE).']
  scada_data_quality varchar [note: 'Quality indicator for the SCADA telemetry data used in the ATC calculation: good (reliable), suspect (questionable), stale (outdated), or unavailable (missing).']
  sink_ba_code varchar [note: 'Code or identifier of the sink Balancing Authority to which power is being transferred on this transmission path.']
  study_case_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the power flow or contingency analysis study case used as the basis for the ATC calculation. Links to the EMS or planning system study case.']
  study_horizon_end timestamp [note: 'Ending timestamp of the time horizon for which ATC is calculated. Defines the validity period of the ATC value.']
  study_horizon_start timestamp [note: 'Beginning timestamp of the time horizon for which ATC is calculated (e.g., start of the hour or day-ahead period).']
  trm_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Transmission Reliability Margin: the amount of transmission transfer capability reserved to ensure that the interconnected transmission network is secure under a reasonable range of uncertainties in system conditions. Measured in megawatts.']
  ttc_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total Transfer Capability: the maximum amount of electric power that can be transferred over the transmission path in a reliable manner while meeting all reliability criteria. Measured in megawatts.']
  uncertainty_margin_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Additional margin reserved to account for uncertainties in load forecast, generation dispatch, or system conditions not captured in TRM. Measured in megawatts.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the ATC calculation validation process: validated (passed all checks), pending_review (awaiting engineer review), failed (did not pass validation rules), or override (validation failure overridden by operator).']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ATC calculation was validated and approved for posting to OASIS.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of Available Transfer Capability (ATC) calculations performed for a transmission path over a specific time horizon. Captures calculation timestamp, path identifier, Total Transfer Capability (TTC), Transmission Reliability Margin (TRM), Capacity Benefit Margin (CBM), Existing Transmission Commitments (ETC), resulting ATC value (MW), study methodology, and posting status for OASIS. Supports FERC OATT compliance and transmission scheduling.'
}

Table transmission.transmission_service_request {
  transmission_service_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transmission service request under OATT (Open Access Transmission Tariff). Primary key for the transmission service request record.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Transmission service requests are submitted to specific control areas/RTOs per FERC Order 2003 interconnection queue management. The rto_iso_region is denormalized. Direct FK enables queue position tr']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: Transmission service requests require load forecasts for system impact studies to assess whether requested capacity can be accommodated under forecasted system conditions. OASIS study process uses loa']
  market_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.market_participant. Business justification: Transmission service requests are filed by ISO/RTO-registered market participants. OATT queue management, FERC Order 2000 interconnection queue reporting, and ATC allocation all require identifying th']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Transmission service requests under FERC OATT are governed by FERC Order 888/890 non-discrimination obligations. Linking each request to the applicable tariff obligation enables compliance tracking of']
  primary_transmission_ems_node_id varchar [note: 'Pricing node identifier for the point of receipt, used for LMP (Locational Marginal Price) settlement and congestion management in RTO/ISO markets.']
  requestor_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.counterparty. Business justification: Transmission service requestors are trading counterparties. Unified counterparty management enables credit exposure aggregation across transmission reservations and energy trades for the same entity, ']
  transmission_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the executed transmission service agreement (TSA) resulting from this approved request. Links the request to the binding service contract.']
  ancillary_services_required varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ancillary services required to support the transmission service (e.g., scheduling, system control, reactive supply, regulation, frequency response, operating reserve, energy imbalance).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the transmission service request was formally approved by the transmission provider, granting transmission rights to the requestor.']
  approved_capacity_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Amount of transmission capacity approved by the transmission provider in megawatts (MW). May differ from requested capacity based on ATC (Available Transfer Capability) and system impact study results.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transmission service request record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  denial_date date [note: 'Date when the transmission service request was formally denied by the transmission provider due to insufficient ATC, reliability concerns, or other OATT-compliant reasons.']
  denial_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation for denial of the transmission service request, citing specific OATT provisions, ATC constraints, or reliability standards that preclude approval.']
  estimated_upgrade_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in US dollars for transmission system network upgrades required to accommodate the requested service, as determined by the facilities study.']
  facilities_study_status varchar [note: 'Status of the facilities study required to identify transmission system upgrades or network modifications needed to accommodate the requested service.']
  ferc_docket_number varchar [note: 'FERC docket reference number for any related transmission service dispute, complaint, or tariff filing associated with this request.']
  ftr_allocation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approved transmission service includes allocation of Financial Transmission Rights for hedging congestion costs in RTO/ISO markets.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transmission service request record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this transmission service request record. Used for audit and accountability.']
  nerc_reliability_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity responsible for reliability coordination and compliance oversight for this transmission service (e.g., WECC, ERCOT, MRO, NPCC, RF, SERC, SPP, TRE). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|ERCOT|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|SPP|TRE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  network_upgrade_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transmission system network upgrades are required to accommodate the requested service. True if facilities study identified necessary system enhancements.']
  point_of_delivery_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sink point or substation where power will be delivered from the transmission system for point-to-point service. May reference a PNode or substation.']
  point_of_receipt_name varchar [note: 'Name of the source point or substation where power will be received onto the transmission system for point-to-point service. May reference a PNode or substation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'OATT priority ranking for the transmission service request. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Firm service typically has priority 1-6, non-firm service 7.']
  queue_position varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this request in the transmission service queue, determined by submission timestamp. Used for first-come-first-served processing under OATT rules.']
  redirect_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transmission customer has the right to redirect the point of receipt or point of delivery during the service period, subject to ATC availability.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the transmission service request, typically formatted as TSR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN. Used for OATT queue management and FERC reporting.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the transmission service request in the OATT queue. Tracks progression from submission through study, approval, or denial. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SUBMITTED|UNDER-STUDY|CONDITIONALLY-APPROVED|APPROVED|DENIED|WITHDRAWN|SUPERSEDED — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transmission service request was formally submitted to the transmission provider. Determines queue position and priority under OATT first-come-first-served rules.']
  requested_capacity_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Amount of transmission capacity requested in megawatts (MW). Represents the maximum power transfer capability sought under the transmission service agreement.']
  requestor_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact for transmission service request communications and notifications.']
  requestor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the requesting entity for this transmission service request.']
  requestor_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact for urgent transmission service request communications.']
  resale_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transmission customer is permitted to resell or reassign the transmission service capacity to a third party under OATT resale provisions.']
  rollover_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transmission customer has rollover rights to renew the transmission service at the end of the service period under OATT right-of-first-refusal provisions.']
  service_end_date date [note: 'Requested end date for the transmission service. Defines the termination of the service period. Null for open-ended or evergreen NITS agreements.']
  service_start_date date [note: 'Requested start date for the transmission service. Defines the beginning of the service period for capacity reservation and scheduling rights.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission service requested: PTP-FIRM (Point-to-Point Firm), PTP-NON-FIRM (Point-to-Point Non-Firm), NITS (Network Integration Transmission Service), or SECONDARY (Secondary Network Service).']
  study_completion_date date [note: 'Date when all required transmission service studies (system impact and facilities studies) were completed and results delivered to the requestor.']
  study_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a system impact study or facilities study is required to evaluate the transmission service request. True if studies are needed to assess grid reliability impacts.']
  system_impact_study_status varchar [note: 'Status of the system impact study required to evaluate whether the requested transmission service can be provided without adversely affecting system reliability.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date when the transmission service request was voluntarily withdrawn by the requestor before approval or denial.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a request for transmission service under OATT — point-to-point (PTP) or network integration transmission service (NITS). Captures request identifier, service type, requestor entity, source and sink points, requested capacity (MW), service start and end dates, priority, queue position, study requirements, approval status, and FERC docket reference. SSOT for OATT transmission service queue management.'
}

  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.counterparty. Business justification: Transmission service agreements are executed between the utility and a transmission customer (counterparty). Credit exposure management, OATT billing, and FERC compliance reporting all require identif']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Transmission service agreements are executed under specific FERC OATT tariff obligations. While regulatory_filing_id exists, the direct obligation link enables compliance teams to track which FERC obl']
  planning_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.planning_period. Business justification: Long-term transmission service agreements are structured around planning periods for capacity obligation tracking and rate design. Linking TSA to PPA enables integrated FER']

Business justification: Long-term firm transmission service agreements require FERC Section 205 rate filings for approval. Service agreements with non-standard terms or rates trigger mandatory regulatory filings and docket t']

  sink_point_of_delivery varchar [note: 'Designated point of delivery (POD) or sink location where power exits the transmission system under this agreement.']
  source_point_of_receipt varchar [note: 'Designated point of receipt (POR) or source location where power enters the transmission system under this agreement.']
  term_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date when the transmission service agreement expires. Nullable for open-ended or evergreen agreements.']
  term_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date when the transmission service agreement becomes binding and service begins.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date when this transmission service agreement was terminated, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for termination of this transmission service agreement (e.g., customer request, non-payment, breach of terms, expiration).']

  Note: 'Master record for executed transmission service agreements under OATT including associated billing and invoicing. Captures agreement identifier, customer entity, service type (PTP/NITS), contracted capacity (MW), source and sink points, term start and end dates, rate schedule reference, billing determinants, FERC filing status, amendment history, and invoice records (invoice identifier, billing period, billed capacity in MW-months, energy throughput in MWh, ancillary service charges, total amount due, invoice date, due date, payment status). SSOT for transmission customer contractual entitlements and transmission revenue billing within the OATT commercial lifecycle, distinct from retail billing domain.'
}

Table transmission.ftr_position {
  ftr_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the FTR position record. Primary key for the FTR position master data.']
  book_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.book. Business justification: FTR positions are assigned to trading books for P&L attribution, hedge accounting designation, and VAR calculation. internal_book_code is a denormalized book reference. Energy traders expect FTR posit']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: FTR positions are held within a specific RTO/ISO market (control area) for congestion hedging and auction settlement. The rto_iso_market_code and rto_iso_market_name are denormalized. Direct FK enable']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.counterparty. Business justification: FTR positions are purchased at auction by registered counterparties. Credit exposure tracking, FTR auction settlement, and FERC EQR reporting all require identifying the legal entity holding the FTR. ']
  energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.energy_price. Business justification: FTR positions are valued using energy price forecasts for mark-to-market accounting, portfolio risk assessment, and hedging strategy decisions. Treasury and trading functions require price forecasts t']
  hedged_ppa_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.ppa_contract. Business justification: Renewable generators and their offtakers routinely acquire FTR positions to hedge congestion risk between generation source and delivery sink specified in PPAs. This is standard risk management practi']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: FTR positions are sized and hedged against load forecasts for specific paths. FTR portfolio management and auction participation require correlating hedge positions with the load forecast driving cong']
  original_ftr_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original FTR position identifier if this position was created through reconfiguration or modification of an existing position. Null for positions acquired directly from auction.']
  primary_path_id varchar [note: 'FK to transmission.path.path_id — FTR positions hedge congestion on specific source-sink paths. This FK connects financial hedges to physical network elements.']
  arr_conversion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this FTR position was acquired through conversion of an Auction Revenue Right (ARR) allocation. ARRs are allocated to load-serving entities and can be converted to FTRs in annual auctions.']
  arr_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ARR that was converted to create this FTR position, if applicable. Null for FTRs acquired directly through auction.']
  auction_clearing_price_per_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per MW at which this FTR position cleared in the auction, denominated in USD per MW for the contract period. Represents the upfront cost paid to acquire the FTR hedge.']
  auction_date date [note: 'Date on which the FTR auction was conducted and this position was awarded.']
  auction_round varchar [note: 'Identifier for the FTR auction round in which this position was acquired. Typically includes auction type (annual, monthly, reconfiguration) and period designation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this FTR position record was first created in the system.']
  cumulative_settlement_credit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cumulative congestion revenue credits or charges for this FTR position since effective start date, denominated in USD. Used to track overall hedge performance.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the FTR position expires and ceases to accrue congestion revenue. Typically aligned with auction period boundaries (end of month, end of planning year).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the FTR position becomes effective and begins accruing congestion revenue credits or charges.']
  ferc_reporting_category varchar [note: 'FERC reporting classification for this FTR position, used for regulatory compliance reporting under FERC Form 1 and FERC Electric Quarterly Reports (EQR).']
  flow_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of power flow for the FTR position. Prevailing flow aligns with typical congestion patterns; counter-flow opposes typical patterns and may have lower auction prices.']
  ftr_identifier varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for the FTR position as assigned by the RTO/ISO market system. Used for market settlement reconciliation and external reporting.']
  ftr_type varchar [note: 'Type of FTR position held. Obligation FTRs pay or charge the holder based on congestion price differences; Option FTRs only pay when positive, with no charge when negative.']
  hedge_strategy_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the hedging strategy or business purpose for acquiring this FTR position. Examples include load-serving hedge, generation hedge, merchant arbitrage, portfolio optimization.']
  holding_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity holding the FTR position. Utility positions are held for internal hedging; transmission customer positions are held by external market participants.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this FTR position record was last updated in the system.']
  last_settlement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent settlement calculation for this FTR position. Typically monthly for ongoing positions.']
  monthly_settlement_credit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total congestion revenue credit received for this FTR position in the most recent monthly settlement, denominated in USD. Positive values represent credits; negative values represent charges (for obligation FTRs only).']
  mw_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of transmission capacity in megawatts covered by this FTR position. Determines the magnitude of congestion revenue credits or charges.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this FTR position, including hedging rationale, special conditions, or settlement exceptions.']
  on_peak_off_peak_designation varchar [note: 'Time-of-use designation for the FTR position. On-peak FTRs cover weekday business hours; off-peak FTRs cover nights and weekends; 24-hour FTRs cover all hours.']
  portfolio_grouping varchar [note: 'Business grouping or portfolio designation for this FTR position. Used to aggregate positions for risk management, hedging strategy analysis, and financial reporting.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the FTR position. Active positions are in-force and subject to settlement; expired positions have reached their end date; settled positions have completed final financial settlement; cancelled positions were terminated before expiration.']
  reconfiguration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this FTR position was acquired through a reconfiguration auction, which allows holders to modify existing positions mid-period.']
  sink_pnode_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the sink pricing node (withdrawal point) of the FTR path. Congestion revenue is calculated based on the Locational Marginal Price (LMP) difference between source and sink PNodes.']
  sink_pnode_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the sink pricing node for the FTR path.']
  source_pnode_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the source pricing node (injection point) of the FTR path. Congestion revenue is calculated based on the Locational Marginal Price (LMP) difference between source and sink PNodes.']
  source_pnode_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the source pricing node for the FTR path.']
  trading_hub_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether either the source or sink PNode is a major trading hub. Hub-based FTRs typically have higher liquidity and more predictable congestion patterns.']

  Note: 'Master record for Financial Transmission Rights (FTR) positions held by the utility or transmission customers as congestion revenue hedges. Captures FTR identifier, auction round and period, obligation or option type, source PNode, sink PNode, MW quantity, direction (prevailing/counter-flow), auction clearing price ($/MW), monthly settlement credits/charges, RTO/ISO market identifier, portfolio grouping, and expiration date. Supports congestion cost hedging strategy, day-ahead market settlement reconciliation, and ARR-to-FTR conversion tracking.'
}

Table transmission.congestion_event {
  congestion_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transmission congestion event record. Primary key for the congestion event entity.']
  contingency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.contingency. Business justification: Congestion events are frequently caused by contingency conditions (N-1 binding constraints). Linking congestion events to the triggering contingency supports NERC TPL-001 post-event analysis, FTR sett']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Congestion events are scoped to a specific control area/RTO for market settlement, congestion revenue rights allocation, and FERC Order 890 reporting. The rto_iso_region is denormalized; a direct FK e']
  flowgate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transmission flowgate or monitored facility experiencing congestion. A flowgate is a mathematical construct representing one or more transmission elements that may limit power flow.']
  ftr_position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Financial Transmission Right position associated with the congested path, used for FTR settlement reconciliation.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Congestion events triggering TLR procedures (tlr_level attribute) are governed by NERC IRO/TOP standards and FERC market transparency obligations. Linking to the governing obligation enables complianc']
  operating_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.operating_limit. Business justification: Congestion events are triggered when element flows exceed operating limits. The element_rating_mw on congestion_event is a denormalized snapshot of the operating limit. Direct FK enables forensic anal']
  primary_congestion_causal_outage_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: Transmission outages frequently cause congestion events (flowgate binding, shadow prices). Linking congestion to the causal outage enables root cause analysis, market settlement dispute resolution, an']
  primary_congestion_path_id varchar [note: 'FK to transmission.path.path_id — Congestion events occur on specific transmission paths/flowgates. Required for congestion cost analysis, FTR settlement, and FERC reporting.']
  source_pnode_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source pricing node (PNode) at the origin of the congested transmission path, used for LMP calculation.']
  binding_constraint_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific binding constraint in the security-constrained economic dispatch that triggered the congestion event.']
  congestion_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total congestion cost in USD for the event, calculated as the product of curtailed MW, event duration, and shadow price or LMP differential.']
  congestion_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of the congestion on the transmission path: forward (source to sink), reverse (sink to source), or bidirectional (constrained in both directions).']
  congestion_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transmission congestion event ended, marking the point at which the constraint was no longer binding or the event was resolved.']
  congestion_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the congestion event based on shadow price, curtailed MW, and duration: low, moderate, high, or critical.']
  congestion_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transmission congestion event began, marking the point at which the flowgate or path became binding in the dispatch model.']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission constraint that caused the congestion: thermal (line rating), voltage (voltage limit), stability (transient or dynamic stability), interface (multi-element limit), or nomogram (multi-dimensional operating limit).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the congestion event record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  curtailed_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of power in megawatts that was curtailed or redispatched to relieve the congestion on the affected flowgate or path.']
  event_cause varchar [note: 'Primary cause of the congestion event: high demand, generation outage, transmission outage, renewable energy variability, loop flow, unscheduled flow, or market condition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_demand|generation_outage|transmission_outage|renewable_variability|loop_flow|unscheduled_flow|market_condition — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the congestion event in minutes, calculated as the difference between start and end timestamps.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the congestion event, typically assigned by the RTO/ISO or EMS system for external reference and reporting.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the congestion event in the event management lifecycle: active (ongoing), resolved (cleared), under review (post-event analysis), or escalated (requires higher-level intervention).']
  ferc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the congestion event meets FERC reporting thresholds and must be included in quarterly or annual transmission congestion reports.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the congestion event record was last modified, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  lmp_differential_usd_per_mwh decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between sink LMP and source LMP, representing the congestion component of the price spread across the transmission path.']
  market_interval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the RTO/ISO market interval (typically 5-minute or hourly) during which the congestion event occurred or was recorded.']
  market_type varchar [note: 'Type of wholesale electricity market in which the congestion event occurred: day-ahead market (DAM), real-time market (RTM), intra-day market, or ancillary services market.']
  monitored_element_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific transmission element (line, transformer, or interface) that was monitored and became binding during the congestion event.']
  nerc_reliability_region varchar [note: 'NERC reliability region in which the congestion event occurred (e.g., WECC, ERCOT, MRO, NPCC, RF, SERC, TRE, FRCC).']
  operator_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by transmission operators or market operators describing the congestion event, actions taken, and any special circumstances.']
  percent_of_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the element rating at which the monitored element was operating during the congestion event, calculated as (flow / rating) * 100.']
  post_contingency_flow_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected power flow in megawatts on the monitored element following the contingency event, used in security-constrained dispatch.']
  pre_contingency_flow_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Power flow in megawatts on the monitored element under normal (pre-contingency) conditions during the congestion event.']
  remedial_action_taken varchar [note: 'Type of remedial action taken to relieve the congestion: economic redispatch, Transmission Loading Relief (TLR), load curtailment, voltage control, phase shifter adjustment, topology change, or none. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: redispatch|tlr|curtailment|voltage_control|phase_shifter|topology_change|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  scada_alarm_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SCADA alarm that triggered or was associated with the congestion event, linking the event to real-time grid monitoring systems.']
  shadow_price_usd_per_mwh decimal(18,4) [note: 'Shadow price or marginal congestion cost in USD per MWh associated with the binding constraint. Represents the incremental cost of relieving the congestion by one MWh.']
  sink_lmp_usd_per_mwh decimal(18,4) [note: 'Locational Marginal Price at the sink PNode during the congestion event, representing the cost of delivering one additional MWh at that location.']
  sink_pnode_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sink pricing node (PNode) at the destination of the congested transmission path, used for LMP calculation.']
  source_lmp_usd_per_mwh decimal(18,4) [note: 'Locational Marginal Price at the source PNode during the congestion event, representing the cost of delivering one additional MWh at that location.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or code of the source system that generated the congestion event record (e.g., EMS, market management system, SCADA historian).']
  tlr_level varchar [note: 'NERC Transmission Loading Relief level invoked during the congestion event (e.g., TLR 1, TLR 2, TLR 3a, TLR 3b, TLR 4, TLR 5a, TLR 5b, TLR 6), if applicable.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather condition at the time of the congestion event that may have contributed to the constraint (e.g., high temperature reducing line ratings, storm causing outages). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: normal|high_temperature|low_temperature|high_wind|storm|ice|fog — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a transmission congestion event on a specific path or flowgate where physical power flow constraints cause economic dispatch deviations. Captures event identifier, affected path or flowgate, start and end timestamps, congestion direction, shadow price ($/MWh), curtailed MW, LMP differential between source and sink nodes, RTO/ISO market interval, binding constraint identifier, and remedial action taken (redispatch, TLR, curtailment). Used for congestion cost analysis, FTR settlement reconciliation, Transmission Loading Relief (TLR) tracking, and FERC reporting.'
}

Table transmission.transmission_outage {
  transmission_outage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transmission outage event. Primary key for the transmission outage record.']
  cause_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.cause. Business justification: outage.cause is the master cause reference table driving NERC reportability, IEEE 1366 classification, and OE-417 filings. transmission_outage.cause_code and cause_description are denormalized; normal']
  contingency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.contingency. Business justification: Transmission outages correspond to modeled contingencies (N-1 elements) in the EMS. Linking actual outages to their contingency model enables real-time contingency analysis triggering, NERC TPL-001 co']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Transmission outages must be coordinated with and reported to the control area per NERC TOP-001/TOP-002. The rto_iso_region is denormalized. Direct FK enables automated outage notification workflows, ']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: BES facility outages trigger specific NERC EOP/TOP reporting obligations (OE-417, NERC outage reporting). Linking the outage to its governing obligation enables compliance teams to track which standar']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transmission facility (line, transformer, circuit breaker, or other equipment) experiencing the outage.']
  regulatory_correspondence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_correspondence. Business justification: Major transmission outages generate regulatory inquiries from FERC, NERC, or state commissions requiring formal correspondence responses. DOE Form OE-417 submissions and follow-up correspondence are t']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a major storm event if the outage was storm-related. Links to enterprise storm tracking and mutual aid coordination.']
  violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: NERC-reportable BES outages (nerc_reportable_flag=true) generate formal NERC violations. Operations and compliance teams must link the outage event to the resulting violation record for penalty tracki']
  atc_impact_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Reduction in Available Transfer Capability in megawatts caused by the outage. Used for OASIS posting and market participant notification.']
  bes_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is classified as part of the Bulk Electric System under NERC definitions. Determines applicability of reliability standards.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created the outage record. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the outage record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  customer_impact_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of end-use customers indirectly affected by the transmission outage through cascading distribution impacts or load shedding.']
  duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the outage event in hours, calculated from start to end timestamp. Key metric for transmission availability reporting.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transmission facility was restored to service. Null for active outages. Used for duration and availability calculations.']
  equipment_age_years varchar [note: 'Age of the failed or outaged equipment in years at the time of the outage. Used for asset health analysis and replacement planning.']
  estimated_repair_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in USD to repair or replace the failed equipment and restore the facility. Used for financial planning and insurance claims.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transmission facility experiencing the outage. Determines applicable reliability standards and reporting requirements.']
  ferc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outage requires reporting to FERC under transmission service or reliability standards.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity performed on the facility prior to the outage. Used to assess maintenance effectiveness.']
  load_shed_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of customer load in megawatts that was shed or curtailed as a result of the transmission outage. Critical for reliability and regulatory reporting.']
  mw_impact decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated transmission capacity reduction in megawatts due to the outage. Used for congestion analysis and market impact assessment.']
  nerc_outage_reporting_code varchar [note: 'NERC-defined code indicating whether the outage meets criteria for mandatory reporting under NERC standards (e.g., OE-417 reportable event).']
  nerc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outage meets NERC criteria for mandatory reporting to DOE via OE-417 form (e.g., loss of firm load, BES facility outage exceeding thresholds).']
  oe417_report_number varchar [note: 'DOE OE-417 report number if the outage was reported to the Department of Energy. Links to federal emergency and disturbance reporting.']
  outage_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the transmission outage event, typically used in operational communications and reporting to RTOs/ISOs.']
  outage_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transmission outage event. Tracks progression from scheduling through restoration.']
  outage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outage event indicating whether it was scheduled in advance (planned/maintenance) or occurred unexpectedly (forced/emergency). Critical for reliability metrics calculation.']
  restoration_method varchar [note: 'Description of the method used to restore the facility (e.g., repair, replacement, temporary bypass, switching operation).']
  restoration_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the restoration effort based on system impact, customer criticality, and reliability considerations.']
  rto_iso_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of notification to the RTO/ISO. Tracks compliance with coordination timelines and communication protocols.']
  rto_iso_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RTO/ISO was notified of the outage event. Required for compliance with outage coordination procedures.']
  safety_clearance_number varchar [note: 'Safety clearance or hold number issued to protect workers during the outage. Ensures compliance with OSHA and utility safety protocols.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Originally planned restoration date and time for planned outages. Used to track schedule adherence and identify extensions.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Originally planned start date and time for planned outages. Used to track schedule adherence and coordination accuracy.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transmission facility was taken out of service or when the forced outage occurred. Used for duration calculations and reliability reporting.']
  switching_order_number varchar [note: 'Switching order number authorizing the isolation and restoration of the transmission facility. Critical for operational safety and coordination.']
  temperature_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature in Fahrenheit at the time of the outage. Used for equipment performance analysis and thermal rating assessments.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified the outage record. Supports accountability and change tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the outage record was last modified. Tracks changes to outage status, duration, or other attributes throughout the lifecycle.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Operating voltage level in kilovolts of the transmission facility experiencing the outage. Determines BES classification and reporting requirements.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather conditions at the time of the outage (e.g., clear, rain, snow, ice, wind, lightning). Important for cause analysis and predictive modeling.']
  wind_speed_mph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Wind speed in miles per hour at the time of the outage. Critical for weather-related outage analysis and line rating calculations.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Work order or job number associated with the outage event, linking to asset management and maintenance systems.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of planned and forced outages on transmission facilities (lines, transformers, protection devices). Captures outage identifier, facility reference, outage type (planned/forced/maintenance), start and end timestamps, cause code, MW impact, affected paths, RTO/ISO notification status, NERC outage reporting code, restoration actions, and crew reference. Feeds transmission reliability indices and NERC OE-417 reporting. Note: this product captures transmission-specific outage events; the outage domain owns the enterprise outage management platform and customer-facing restoration workflow.'
}
