Business justification: Pre-switching security assessment requires a contingency analysis run to verify post-switching network security per NERC TOP-001. The atc_recalculation_required_flag implies this dependency. Direct FK']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Transmission switching orders require control area approval and RTO/ISO notification per NERC TOP-001/TOP-002. The rto_iso_approval_reference is a denormalized text field. Direct FK enables automated ']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Switching orders on BES facilities must comply with NERC FAC-001/TOP-001 standards. Linking to the governing obligation enables compliance verification that switching procedures meet the applicable re']
  outage_switching_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_switching_order. Business justification: Transmission switching orders executed during outage restoration must link to OMS switching order tracking for unified crew coordination, safety clearance management, and NERC CIP compliance. Real-wor']
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: Transmission switching orders executing planned maintenance are issued against an approved planned_outage_window. Dispatchers require this link to confirm switching authority, enforce the approved out']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event. Business justification: Emergency transmission switching orders issued during storm response must be traceable to the triggering storm_event for NERC OE-417 filings, after-action reviews, and mutual aid cost allocation. tran']
  transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary transmission substation where the switching operation will be executed. Links to the substation master data.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the switching operation was completed and verified. Used to calculate switching duration and operational efficiency metrics.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the switching operation execution began. Captured from SCADA or operator log entry for performance tracking and compliance reporting.']
  affected_equipment_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or tag of the specific equipment device to be switched. References equipment asset registry and SCADA tag database.']
  affected_equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission equipment that will be operated or isolated by this switching order. Determines safety protocols and operational procedures.']
  affected_load_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated or actual load in megawatts that was transferred, interrupted, or affected by the switching operation. Used for impact assessment and reliability reporting.']
  approving_authority_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the approving authority for audit trail and compliance documentation.']
  atc_recalculation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the switching operation changes network topology sufficiently to require recalculation of Available Transfer Capability for transmission service scheduling.']
  cip_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this switching order involves CIP-designated critical cyber assets requiring enhanced security controls and audit logging per NERC CIP standards.']
  completion_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes documenting any deviations from planned procedure, unexpected conditions encountered, or additional actions taken during switching execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this switching order record was first created in the system. Audit trail for order lifecycle tracking.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in minutes from start to completion of the switching operation. Used for performance analysis and future planning.']
  ems_topology_change_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding network topology change event in the Energy Management System. Links switching order to power flow model updates and state estimation.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for switching operation failure if status is failed or suspended. Documents equipment malfunction, safety concerns, or procedural issues.']
  issuing_dispatcher_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the transmission system operator or dispatcher who issued the switching order for audit and accountability purposes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this switching order record was last updated. Tracks changes to order status, execution details, or verification information.']
  nerc_bes_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affected equipment is designated as part of the Bulk Electric System under NERC reliability standards. BES-designated equipment requires enhanced documentation and compliance reporting.']
  oatt_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this switching operation affects transmission capacity or service under OATT agreements, requiring customer notification or ATC recalculation.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the switching order, typically formatted as TSO-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for tracking and reference in operational logs and SCADA systems.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the switching order in the operational workflow. Tracks progression from draft through execution to completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|scheduled|in_progress|completed|cancelled|suspended|failed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the switching order based on operational purpose. Planned orders support scheduled maintenance, emergency orders respond to grid events, restoration orders support outage recovery.']
  outage_event_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the outage event or restoration plan if this switching order is part of emergency restoration or planned outage operations.']
  planned_execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the switching operation is planned to be executed. Used for operational coordination and resource scheduling.']
  post_switching_current_amps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured current flow in amperes through the affected equipment immediately after the switching operation. Validates load redistribution and equipment status.']
  post_switching_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured voltage level in kilovolts at the affected equipment location immediately after the switching operation. Confirms successful execution and grid stability.']
  pre_switching_current_amps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured current flow in amperes through the affected equipment immediately before the switching operation. Critical for load transfer verification.']
  pre_switching_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured voltage level in kilovolts at the affected equipment location immediately before the switching operation. Used for operational verification and safety validation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to the switching order. Critical priority indicates immediate grid reliability impact requiring expedited execution.']
  rto_iso_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of RTO/ISO approval or coordination acknowledgment if required for this switching operation. Links to regional coordination records.']
  rto_iso_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RTO or ISO was notified of this switching operation due to potential impact on regional transmission capacity or market operations.']
  safety_clearance_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the associated safety clearance or work permit authorizing personnel to work on or near the affected equipment. Links to safety clearance records.']
  scada_command_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the SCADA command or control sequence that executed the switching operation. Links to SCADA historian for detailed telemetry and event reconstruction.']
  scada_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the switching operation was executed via SCADA remote control (true) or manual field operation (false). Critical for automation tracking and reliability analysis.']
  switching_action varchar [note: 'Primary switching action to be performed on the affected equipment. Open and close apply to breakers and switches, isolate and energize apply to equipment sections. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|close|isolate|energize|de_energize|ground|test — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  switching_sequence_steps varchar [note: 'Detailed step-by-step procedure for executing the switching operation in correct sequence. Includes device identifiers, actions, and verification checkpoints to ensure safe topology changes.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify successful completion of the switching operation. SCADA telemetry provides remote confirmation, field visual requires on-site operator verification.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the switching operation was verified and confirmed complete. May differ from completion timestamp if verification requires additional checks.']
  work_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance or construction work order that necessitates this switching operation. Links to asset management work order system.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a transmission switching order authorizing changes to the network topology (open/close breakers, isolate equipment). Captures order identifier, issuing dispatcher, affected substation and devices, switching sequence steps, safety clearance reference, planned execution time, actual execution time, and completion status. Supports safe switching operations and SCADA-driven topology changes in EMS.'
}

Table transmission.protection_device {
  protection_device_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for protection_device']
  nerc_cip_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.nerc_cip_asset. Business justification: Protection relays are cyber assets requiring CIP-010 configuration baseline management and CIP-007 patch management. Microprocessor-based relays with network connectivity are explicitly covered under ']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Protection devices on BES facilities are subject to NERC PRC-005 (maintenance and testing) and PRC-019 (coordination) obligations. Linking to the governing PRC obligation enables compliance tracking o']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Protection relays and devices are tracked assets requiring periodic testing, maintenance, and replacement per NERC CIP compliance requirements. Utilities maintain asset records for lifecycle managemen']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) system tag identifier for real-time monitoring and control integration of the protection device.']
  transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the transmission substation where this protection device is physically installed.']
  bay_number varchar [note: 'Substation bay or position number where the protection device is located within the substation layout.']
  cip_asset_classification varchar [note: 'NERC Critical Infrastructure Protection (CIP) impact classification level for cybersecurity requirements (high, medium, low impact BES Cyber System).']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the protection device was placed into active service and energized for protection functions.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Communication protocol used for device integration with SCADA/EMS systems (e.g., DNP3, IEC 61850, Modbus, IEC 60870-5-101/104).']
  device_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or asset tag for the protection device as recorded in asset management systems. Used for field identification and maintenance tracking.']
  device_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the protection device, typically including location and function descriptors.']
  device_subtype varchar [note: 'Detailed subclassification of the device. For relays: distance, differential, overcurrent, directional, pilot, ground fault. For breakers: oil, SF6, vacuum, air blast. For switches: motor-operated, manual, gang-operated.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the protection device by its primary function in the transmission protection scheme.']
  ems_device_code varchar [note: 'Energy Management System (EMS) device identifier for integration with grid operations and state estimation functions.']
  expected_service_life_years varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-rated or utility-defined expected service life of the protection device in years from installation.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware or software version installed on the protection device. Critical for cybersecurity compliance and functional capability tracking.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Geographic Information System (GIS) feature identifier linking the protection device to its geospatial representation in the utility network model.']
  ieee_device_number varchar [note: 'Standard IEEE C37.2 device function number (e.g., 21 for distance relay, 87 for differential relay, 52 for circuit breaker, 89 for disconnect switch).']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the protection device was originally installed and commissioned at the substation.']
  interrupting_rating_ka decimal(10,2) [note: 'For circuit breakers: maximum fault current in kiloamperes (kA) that the breaker is rated to safely interrupt. Critical for fault clearing capability assessment.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Network IP address assigned to the protection device for communication and remote access. Confidential for cybersecurity purposes.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the device last received scheduled preventive maintenance or major overhaul.']
  last_test_date date [note: 'Date when the protection device was last functionally tested or calibrated. Critical for NERC PRC compliance and reliability assurance.']
  nerc_bes_designation boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this protection device is part of a NERC-designated Bulk Electric System (BES) facility and subject to NERC reliability standards.']
  next_maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance activity based on maintenance program intervals.']
  next_test_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required functional test or calibration based on maintenance intervals and regulatory requirements.']
  operating_mechanism_type varchar [note: 'For circuit breakers: type of operating mechanism such as spring-operated, hydraulic, pneumatic, or motor-wound spring.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the protection device indicating availability for protection functions.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership classification of the protection device asset.']
  owning_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code or identifier of the legal entity that owns the protection device asset.']
  protected_element_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific transmission element (line, transformer, bus, etc.) that this device protects. Links to asset registry.']
  protected_element_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission system element that this protection device is assigned to protect.']
  protection_scheme_type varchar [note: 'Overall protection scheme architecture that this device participates in (e.g., primary protection, backup protection, breaker failure, transfer trip, pilot protection).']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this protection device record was first created in the data management system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this protection device record was last modified in the data management system.']
  relay_type varchar [note: 'For protective relays only: specific relay protection function type such as distance (21), differential (87), overcurrent (50/51), directional (67), pilot (85), ground fault (50N/51N), or multi-function.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the protection device was permanently removed from service and decommissioned.']
  sap_equipment_number varchar [note: 'SAP ERP equipment master record number for integration with enterprise asset management, maintenance planning, and financial systems.']
  sap_functional_location varchar [note: 'SAP functional location code representing the hierarchical position of the device within the asset structure (region/substation/bay/device).']
  test_interval_months varchar [note: 'Required testing frequency in months as defined by NERC PRC-005 maintenance program and manufacturer recommendations.']
  trip_counter varchar [note: 'For circuit breakers: cumulative count of trip operations since installation or last counter reset. Used for maintenance scheduling and end-of-life assessment.']
  trip_setting_backup varchar [note: 'For protective relays: backup or secondary trip threshold settings for redundant protection schemes.']
  trip_setting_primary varchar [note: 'For protective relays: primary trip threshold settings including pickup values, time delays, and coordination parameters. Stored as structured text or reference to settings file.']
  voltage_class_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage class in kilovolts (kV) of the transmission system where the protection device is installed. Critical for determining protection coordination requirements.']
  zone_of_protection varchar [note: 'For protective relays: description of the electrical zone or reach that the relay is configured to protect (e.g., Zone 1, Zone 2, Zone 3 for distance relays; primary/backup zones).']

  Note: 'Master record for transmission protection devices including protective relays, high-voltage circuit breakers, and disconnect switches at transmission substations. Captures device identifier, device type (relay/breaker/disconnect), voltage class (kV), for relays: relay type (distance, differential, overcurrent), manufacturer, model, firmware version, zone of protection, trip settings, last test date; for breakers: interrupting rating (kA), operating mechanism type, trip counter, last maintenance date; associated protected element (line/transformer/bus), SCADA integration tag, installation date, and operational status. Critical for NERC PRC compliance, fault clearing performance, switching operations, and fault isolation.'
}

Table transmission.transmission_scada_measurement {
  transmission_scada_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each SCADA measurement record captured from transmission substations and lines.']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.scada_point. Business justification: Transmission SCADA measurements are instances of readings from defined SCADA points. The scada_point_tag is a denormalized reference to grid.scada_point. Direct FK enables unified SCADA data managemen']
  transmission_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the transmission line being measured. Nullable for substation-level measurements not specific to a line.']
  transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the transmission substation where this measurement was captured.']
  alarm_limit_high decimal(18,6) [note: 'The upper threshold value that triggers a high alarm condition when the measurement exceeds this limit.']
  alarm_limit_low decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower threshold value that triggers a low alarm condition when the measurement falls below this limit.']
  alarm_status varchar [note: 'Current alarm status of the measurement based on comparison with configured alarm limits.']
  analog_digital_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement is an analog value (continuous), digital value (discrete state), calculated value (derived), or status indicator.']
  archive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement should be archived to the long-term operational data historian for historical trending and analysis.']
  change_of_state_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement represents a change of state event (e.g., breaker opened/closed) rather than a periodic scan.']
  cip_asset_classification varchar [note: 'NERC CIP classification of the asset from which this measurement originates, determining cybersecurity requirements.']
  contingency_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement is used as input to real-time contingency analysis for N-1 and N-2 reliability assessments.']
  data_retention_days varchar [note: 'The number of days this measurement record should be retained in the operational historian before archival or deletion, per data governance policy.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The source system that generated this measurement record (e.g., GE EMS, OSIsoft PI, ABB MicroSCADA, Siemens Spectrum Power).']
  deadband_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum change in value required before a new measurement is transmitted or logged, used to reduce data volume for slowly changing analog values.']
  ems_state_estimator_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement is used as an input to the EMS state estimation algorithm for real-time grid state calculation.']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the engineering value (e.g., MW, MVAR, kV, A, Hz, degrees, percent, degrees_C).']
  engineering_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The measurement value converted to engineering units (MW, MVAR, kV, amperes, Hz, etc.) after applying scaling factors and calibration.']
  ferc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement is required for FERC regulatory reporting (e.g., Form 1, Form 714, EQR).']
  historian_tag varchar [note: 'Reference tag identifier in the operational data historian system (e.g., OSIsoft PI) where this measurement is archived for long-term storage and trending.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'The date when the measurement instrument or transducer was last calibrated, used to assess measurement accuracy and reliability.']
  measurement_accuracy_class varchar [note: 'The accuracy class or precision rating of the measurement instrument (e.g., 0.2%, 0.5%, 1.0%) per IEEE or IEC standards.']
  measurement_phase varchar [note: 'The electrical phase or phase combination that this measurement represents in a three-phase power system. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A|B|C|AB|BC|CA|ABC|neutral|ground — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  measurement_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned by the SCADA system to track measurement order and detect missing or duplicate transmissions.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Type of measurement being captured: MW (real power), MVAR (reactive power), kV (voltage), amperes (current), frequency (Hz), breaker_status (open/closed), transformer_tap (position), voltage_angle (degrees), power_factor (ratio), temperature (degrees C). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MW|MVAR|kV|amperes|frequency|breaker_status|transformer_tap|voltage_angle|power_factor|temperature — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nerc_reliability_region varchar [note: 'The NERC reliability region where this measurement originates (e.g., WECC, ERCOT, NPCC, SERC, MRO, RFC, SPP, TRE).']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next calibration of the measurement instrument, used for maintenance planning.']
  normal_operating_range_max decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum value of the normal operating range for this measurement type at this location, used for anomaly detection.']
  normal_operating_range_min decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum value of the normal operating range for this measurement type at this location, used for anomaly detection.']
  operator_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a system operator has manually overridden the SCADA measurement value for operational reasons.']
  operator_override_user varchar [note: 'The user ID of the system operator who performed the manual override, if applicable. Used for audit trail and compliance.']
  pnode_code varchar [note: 'The pricing node identifier in the RTO/ISO market system associated with this measurement location, used for LMP (Locational Marginal Price) calculations.']
  quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality indicator for the measurement: good (valid and reliable), suspect (questionable), bad (invalid), manual (operator-entered), estimated (calculated from other values), out_of_range (exceeds limits), stale (not updated within expected interval). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: good|suspect|bad|manual|estimated|out_of_range|stale — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  raw_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The raw numeric value captured from the SCADA system before any engineering unit conversion or validation. For digital/status points, represents the state code.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the measurement was received and processed by the central EMS/SCADA master station.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this measurement record was first inserted into the data historian or lakehouse silver layer.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this measurement record was last updated or modified in the data historian or lakehouse silver layer.']
  rto_iso_region varchar [note: 'The Regional Transmission Organization or Independent System Operator region that has operational authority over this measurement point.']
  rtu_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Remote Terminal Unit that collected and transmitted this measurement to the SCADA master.']
  scada_protocol varchar [note: 'The communication protocol used to transmit this measurement (e.g., DNP3, IEC 61850, Modbus, IEC 60870-5-104).']
  scan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the SCADA system scanned and captured this measurement from the field device or RTU.']
  substituted_value_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement value has been substituted with an estimated or default value due to communication failure or bad quality.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of real-time and historical SCADA/EMS telemetry measurements from transmission substations and lines. Captures measurement identifier, SCADA point tag, measurement type (MW, MVAR, kV, amperes, frequency, breaker status), analog/digital indicator, raw value, engineering unit, quality flag (good/suspect/bad/manual), scan timestamp, substation source, RTU identifier, and historian tag reference. Feeds EMS state estimation, real-time contingency analysis, operator displays, and operational data historian for transmission grid situational awareness. Note: this product captures the transmission-specific SCADA data view; the grid domain owns the EMS/SCADA platform master.'
}

Table transmission.constraint {
  constraint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for constraint']
  contingency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.contingency. Business justification: Transmission market constraints are derived from contingency-defined operating limits. Each binding constraint in the market model corresponds to a specific contingency in the reliability model. Direc']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Transmission constraints are defined within a specific control area/RTO market for congestion management and market clearing. The region field is denormalized. Direct FK enables constraint management ']
  market_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the market participant owning or responsible for the constraint.']
  operating_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.operating_limit. Business justification: Transmission constraints are derived from and bounded by operating limits. The limit_mw on constraint is a denormalized value from operating_limit. Direct FK enables automated constraint limit updates']
  parent_constraint_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on constraint (parent_constraint_id)']
  congestion_price_usd_per_mwh decimal(10,4) [note: 'Congestion price associated with the constraint in US dollars per megawatt‑hour.']
  constraint_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of constraints.']
  constraint_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used in market and operational systems to identify the constraint.']
  constraint_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the constraint purpose and characteristics.']
  constraint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the transmission constraint.']
  constraint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the constraint.']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'Category of constraint based on physical or market definition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the constraint record was created in the system.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Direction of power flow the constraint applies to.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the constraint expires or is scheduled to be retired.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the constraint becomes effective.']
  is_ftr boolean [note: 'Indicates if the constraint is associated with a Flowgate Transmission Right (FTR).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of the constraint.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the limit is a maximum, minimum, or a range.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the geographic location or substation associated with the constraint.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the constraint.']
  reliability_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for reliability coordination (higher number = higher priority).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system where the constraint originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the constraint record.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(6,2) [note: 'Voltage level in kilovolts at which the constraint is defined.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for constraint. Referenced by transmission_constraint_id.'
}

  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Flowgates are defined within or between forecast zones for zonal congestion management. RTO/ISO flowgate ratings and TLR procedures are performed at the zone level. A grid operations engineer would ex']

Table transmission.pnode {
  pnode_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pnode']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Pnodes belong to a specific control area/market zone for LMP calculation and market settlement. The control_area field on pnode is a denormalized text reference. Direct FK enables market settlement re']
  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.ems_node. Business justification: Pricing nodes (pnodes) correspond directly to EMS bus nodes in the power system model. LMP is calculated at pnodes which map to EMS buses. The pnode_reference on ems_node is a denormalized text field.']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Pnodes are aggregated into forecast zones for load and price forecasting in RTO/ISO markets. Zone-to-pnode mapping is fundamental to LMP aggregation, zonal price forecasting, and load disaggregation. ']
  ancillary_service_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node can participate in ancillary service markets.']
  capacity_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum MW that can be transferred through the node under normal operating conditions.']
  congestion_price_usd_per_mwh decimal(12,4) [note: 'Monetary price (USD per MWh) applied when congestion occurs at the node.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the node record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin system or process that provided the node data.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the node entered service.']
  interconnection_point_flag boolean [note: 'True if the node serves as an interconnection point with another transmission system or ISO.']
  is_congested boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the node is experiencing congestion in the current market interval.']
  is_imported boolean [note: 'True if the node record was loaded from an external import rather than generated internally.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the node location (decimal degrees).']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the node location (decimal degrees).']
  market_zone varchar [note: 'Market zone used for pricing and settlement calculations.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the node within the transmission network.']
  ownership_entity varchar [note: 'Organization or utility that holds ownership rights for the node.']
  pnode_category varchar [note: 'Primary business purpose of the node within the utility\'s processes.']
  pnode_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code assigned to the node by the ISO/RTO for market transactions.']
  pnode_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description providing additional context about the node.']
  pnode_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the pricing node used in reports and UI.']
  pnode_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the node.']
  region varchar [note: 'Higher‑level region (e.g., state, province) where the node resides.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node must be reported to FERC/NERC compliance filings.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date on which the node was removed from service, if applicable.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Type of financial settlement applied to transactions at the node.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the node record.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Design voltage of the node in kilovolts.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pnode. Referenced by source_pnode_id.'
}

Table distribution.feeder {
  feeder_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution feeder. Primary key. MASTER_RESOURCE role entity.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Feeders participate in compliance programs like reliability improvement programs, vegetation management programs, and DER integration programs. Tracking program participation enables program effective']
  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.ems_node. Business justification: Feeders are modeled as branch elements in the EMS network model with a head-end bus node. State estimation, contingency analysis, and load flow studies require correlating feeder-level distribution da']
  feeder_distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.substation.substation_id — Every distribution feeder emanates from a substation bus. This is the foundational topological relationship — load flow analysis, fault isolation, and FLISR all require knowing which substation source']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Feeders are the primary distribution asset for which load forecasts are prepared. Load forecasting zones typically align with feeder service areas. Essential for capacity planning, DER hosting capacit']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Feeders are subject to specific reliability obligations under IEEE 1366 standards and state PUC performance requirements (SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI targets). Tracking which obligations apply to which feeders ']
  parent_feeder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to an alternate feeder that can provide backup service to customers on this feeder through normally-open tie switches.']
  pnode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.pnode. Business justification: In RTO/ISO markets, each distribution feeder\'s load is settled at a transmission pricing node (pnode). Distribution planners and settlement teams require this link for LMP-based load forecasting, cong']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Feeders are capital assets requiring asset registry entries for depreciation, FERC accounting, RAB inclusion, and lifecycle management. A utility asset manager would expect every feeder to have a regi']
  to_distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.distribution_substation.distribution_substation_id — Every feeder emanates from a substation bus. This is the fundamental parent-child relationship in distribution topology. The feeder description mentions \'associated substation bus\' — this FK is produc']
  adms_feeder_code varchar [note: 'Feeder identifier as recorded in the GE PowerOn ADMS system, used for real-time monitoring, outage management, and distribution automation.']
  caidi_ytd_minutes decimal(10,3) [note: 'Year-to-date CAIDI reliability metric for this feeder, representing the average outage duration per customer interruption, expressed in minutes.']
  circuit_configuration varchar [note: 'Topology configuration of the feeder circuit describing how the feeder is electrically arranged and connected within the distribution system.']
  conductor_type varchar [note: 'Type and specification of the primary conductor used on the feeder mainline, including material and size designation (e.g., ACSR 336.4 kcmil).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this feeder record was first created in the system.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification of the feeder based on factors such as customer type, load importance, and redundancy availability.']
  customer_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer premises served by this feeder, used for reliability index calculations and outage impact assessment.']
  der_hosting_capacity_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum amount of distributed energy resources (solar, wind, storage) that can be interconnected to the feeder without violating operational constraints, expressed in megawatts.']
  feeder_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the feeder, typically used in operational systems and field communications. Often follows utility-specific naming conventions combining substation code and circuit number.']
  feeder_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the feeder, often including geographic or service area references for operational clarity.']
  feeder_type varchar [note: 'Physical construction type of the feeder circuit indicating whether the conductor infrastructure is overhead, underground, or a combination of both.']
  flisr_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the feeder is equipped with automated FLISR capabilities for rapid fault isolation and service restoration.']
  in_service_date date [note: 'Date when the feeder was first placed into operational service and began serving customer load.']
  installed_der_capacity_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Current total installed capacity of distributed energy resources connected to the feeder, expressed in megawatts.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent comprehensive inspection or assessment of the feeder infrastructure.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this feeder record was most recently updated in the system.']
  length_miles decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total physical length of the feeder circuit measured in miles, including all mainline and lateral segments.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled maintenance activity on the feeder circuit.']
  nominal_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal operating voltage of the feeder expressed in kilovolts. Typical medium-voltage distribution values include 4.16, 12.47, 13.2, 13.8, 23, 34.5 kV.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the feeder indicating its availability and operational state within the distribution network.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Ownership classification indicating whether the feeder is wholly utility-owned, jointly owned with another entity, or owned by a third party.']
  peak_load_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Historical peak load demand observed on the feeder expressed in megawatts, used for capacity planning and load management.']
  phase_configuration varchar [note: 'Electrical phase configuration of the feeder indicating whether it operates as a three-phase, single-phase, or two-phase circuit.']
  protection_scheme varchar [note: 'Description of the protective relay and circuit breaker coordination scheme applied to the feeder for fault detection, isolation, and system protection.']
  rated_capacity_mva decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum rated capacity of the feeder expressed in megavolt-amperes, representing the thermal and electrical limits of the circuit under normal operating conditions.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Public Utility Commission or regulatory body with jurisdiction over this feeder for rate-making and service quality standards.']
  saidi_ytd_minutes decimal(10,3) [note: 'Year-to-date SAIDI reliability metric for this feeder, representing the average outage duration per customer served, expressed in minutes.']
  saifi_ytd_count decimal(10,3) [note: 'Year-to-date SAIFI reliability metric for this feeder, representing the average number of interruptions per customer served.']
  substation_bus_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific bus or breaker position within the substation from which the feeder originates.']
  volt_var_optimization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the feeder participates in automated volt-VAR optimization for voltage regulation and reactive power management.']

  Note: 'Master record for medium-voltage distribution feeders emanating from substations. Captures feeder identifier, nominal voltage (kV), rated capacity (MVA), feeder type (overhead/underground/mixed), circuit configuration, protection scheme, associated substation bus, service territory, GIS network model reference, ADMS feeder ID from GE PowerOn, and operational status. SSOT for feeder identity in the distribution network topology.'
}

Planners and engineers reference this link in annual lo']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Substations are major load aggregation points and primary geographic units for load forecasting. Substation capacity planning and transformer sizing depend on load forecasts prepared at the substation']
  nerc_cip_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.nerc_cip_asset. Business justification: Substations are BES facilities requiring NERC CIP-002 through CIP-014 compliance tracking. CIP impact ratings, security perimeters, and cyber asset inventories are mandatory for substations meeting BE']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Distribution substations are major capital assets requiring asset registry entries for depreciation schedules, FERC account coding, RAB inclusion, and valuation for rate cases. Every utility asset man']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: Distribution substations are the primary infrastructure assets within a utility\'s franchise service territory. Each substation serves customers within a defined geographic franchise area. Adding servi']
  transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_substation. Business justification: Distribution substations receive power from transmission substations via step-down transformers. This is the fundamental grid topology relationship required for outage coordination, voltage management']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the substation facility (organizational contact data, business-confidential).']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as building number, suite, or unit (organizational contact data, business-confidential).']
  asset_criticality_score varchar [note: 'Numerical score (1-100) representing the criticality of this substation to grid reliability and customer service, used for risk-based asset management.']
  average_load_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average demand load in megawatts (MW) served by this substation over a defined period (typically annual), used for utilization analysis.']
  bus_arrangement varchar [note: 'Electrical bus topology: single-bus, double-bus, ring-bus, breaker-and-half, or main-and-transfer. Impacts reliability and switching flexibility.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the substation is located (organizational contact data, business-confidential).']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the substation was first placed into commercial operation and energized for customer service.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this substation record was first created in the system (audit trail).']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that is the authoritative source for this substation data (e.g., GE PowerOn ADMS, Esri ArcGIS, Ventyx Asset Suite).']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the substation was permanently retired from service and de-energized. Null if still in service.']
  der_interconnection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this substation has interconnected DER (solar, wind, battery storage) on its feeders requiring DERMS coordination.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary emergency contact phone number for this substation facility (organizational contact data, business-confidential).']
  flisr_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this substation is equipped with automated FLISR capability for self-healing grid operations via ADMS.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Unique feature identifier in the Esri ArcGIS Utility Network for geospatial asset location and network topology modeling.']
  last_major_upgrade_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent major capital upgrade or refurbishment (transformer replacement, bus reconfiguration, SCADA modernization).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this substation record was last modified in the system (audit trail).']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the substation location for GIS mapping and outage analysis.']
  load_factor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of average load to peak load expressed as a percentage, indicating how efficiently the substation capacity is utilized.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the substation location for GIS mapping and outage analysis.']
  maintenance_zone varchar [note: 'Maintenance planning zone or district responsible for O&M (Operations and Maintenance) activities at this substation.']
  number_of_feeders varchar [note: 'Count of distribution feeders originating from this substation serving downstream customers and load zones.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the substation: in-service (active), out-of-service (offline), under-construction (not yet commissioned), decommissioned (retired), standby (backup), or maintenance (temporary outage).']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership classification: utility-owned (owned by the distribution utility), third-party-owned (IPP or DER owner), joint-venture, leased, or municipal (public entity).']
  peak_load_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Historical peak demand load in megawatts (MW) recorded at this substation, used for capacity planning and load forecasting.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the substation location (organizational contact data, business-confidential).']
  primary_voltage_kv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Incoming high-side voltage level in kilovolts (e.g., 33kV, 11kV) from the transmission or sub-transmission network.']
  rated_capacity_mva decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total nameplate capacity of the substation transformer bank in megavolt-amperes (MVA). Principal quantitative fact for capacity planning and load balancing.']
  reliability_index_caidi_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Customer Average Interruption Duration Index in minutes for this substation service area, reported to PUC for reliability compliance.']
  reliability_index_saidi_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'System Average Interruption Duration Index in minutes for this substation service area, reported to PUC for reliability compliance.']
  reliability_index_saifi_count decimal(10,2) [note: 'System Average Interruption Frequency Index (number of interruptions per customer) for this substation service area, reported to PUC for reliability compliance.']
  scada_rtu_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SCADA Remote Terminal Unit installed at this substation for real-time monitoring and control via OSIsoft PI or equivalent.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the substation is located (organizational contact data, business-confidential).']
  substation_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier or asset tag for the substation used in operational systems (SCADA, ADMS, GIS).']
  substation_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the distribution substation (e.g., \'Main Street Substation\', \'Industrial Park Sub\').']
  substation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the substation by its primary function: step-down (voltage transformation), switching (circuit reconfiguration), distribution (last-mile delivery hub), or collector (renewable energy aggregation).']
  transformer_configuration varchar [note: 'Winding configuration of the transformer bank: delta-wye, wye-wye, delta-delta, wye-delta, open-delta, or scott-t. Determines phase shift and grounding.']
  volt_var_optimization_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this substation participates in volt-VAR optimization (VVO) for voltage regulation and reactive power management.']

  Note: 'Master record for distribution substations (typically 33kV/11kV or 11kV/LV) that step down transmission voltage for last-mile delivery. Captures substation name, location (GIS coordinates), voltage levels, transformer bank configuration, bus arrangement, SCADA RTU identifiers, ADMS node reference, rated capacity (MVA), commissioning date, ownership type, and operational status. Distinct from transmission substations owned by the transmission domain. SSOT for distribution substation identity and electrical configuration in the distribution network topology.'
}

Table distribution.transformer {
  transformer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for transformer']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution substation that serves the feeder supplying this transformer. Used for network topology analysis and outage root cause investigation.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.feeder.feeder_id — Distribution transformers are assigned to feeders. This FK supports transformer loading studies, voltage complaint investigation, and outage impact assessment (how many transformers/customers affected']
  load_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.load_zone. Business justification: Distribution transformers serve customers within specific load zones — the load zone determines the voltage regulation targets, VVO optimization parameters, and load balancing objectives that govern t']
  network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.network_segment. Business justification: Distribution transformers are physically installed on specific network segments (line sections) of the distribution feeder. The network segment determines the transformer\'s electrical location within ']
  primary_transformer_feeder_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.feeder.feeder_id — Distribution transformers are installed on feeders. This relationship is essential for load analysis (aggregating transformer loads to feeder level), outage impact assessment, and capacity planning.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Distribution transformers are individually tracked capital assets in the registry for condition monitoring, depreciation, replacement planning, and warranty tracking. asset_tag and serial_number are d']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.scada_point. Business justification: SCADA-monitored distribution transformers (scada_monitored_flag=true) require a FK to the SCADA point for real-time load monitoring, overload alarm management, and DMS integration. This link enables t']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.service_territory']
  adms_device_code varchar [note: 'Unique device identifier in the GE PowerOn ADMS for real-time monitoring, FLISR automation, and volt-VAR optimization. Links transformer to SCADA telemetry and control functions.']
  condition_rating varchar [note: 'Overall physical and operational condition assessment based on inspection findings, test results, and maintenance history. Used for capital planning and replacement prioritization.']
  cooling_class varchar [note: 'Cooling method classification per IEEE standards. ONAN = Oil Natural Air Natural (self-cooled), ONAF = Oil Natural Air Forced (fan-cooled), dry types use air cooling. Affects loading capability and maintenance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transformer record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification based on number of customers served, load importance, and redundancy availability. Critical assets receive priority for maintenance and replacement.']
  customers_served_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer accounts receiving service from this transformer. Used for outage impact assessment and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculations for PUC reporting.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier linking this transformer to its corresponding feature in the Esri ArcGIS Utility Network. Enables spatial analysis, network tracing, and integration with ADMS.']
  health_index_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated asset health score (0-100) based on age, condition, loading, maintenance history, and failure risk factors. Higher scores indicate better health. Used for investment prioritization and risk management.']
  impedance_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Transformer impedance expressed as a percentage of rated voltage. Used for short-circuit calculations, protective device coordination, and fault current analysis.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the transformer was installed and placed into service at its current location. Used for warranty tracking, maintenance scheduling, and asset lifecycle management.']
  installation_type varchar [note: 'Physical installation configuration of the transformer indicating mounting method and accessibility. Overhead units are pole-mounted, padmount units are ground-level enclosures, vault units are below-grade in concrete vaults.']
  insulation_type varchar [note: 'Dielectric insulation medium used in the transformer. Mineral oil is traditional, ester fluids are environmentally friendly, dry types use air or resin insulation. Impacts fire safety and environmental compliance.']
  kva_rating decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nameplate power capacity rating of the transformer in kilovolt-amperes. Represents the maximum continuous load the transformer can serve under standard operating conditions. Critical for load analysis and transformer loading studies.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent field inspection or condition assessment. Used to track inspection compliance and schedule next inspection per maintenance program.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the transformer location in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping, outage analysis, and field crew dispatch via Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.']
  load_loss_watts decimal(10,2) [note: 'Winding resistance loss in watts at rated load. Used for energy efficiency analysis, loss accounting, and total cost of ownership calculations.']
  load_tap_changer_equipped boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transformer is equipped with a load tap changer for automatic voltage regulation under load. True for LTC-equipped units, false for fixed-tap units.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the transformer location in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping, outage analysis, and field crew dispatch via Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.']
  manufacture_year varchar [note: 'Year the transformer was manufactured. Used for age-based asset management, depreciation calculations, and end-of-life planning.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next routine inspection or condition assessment. Driven by maintenance program intervals and regulatory requirements.']
  no_load_loss_watts decimal(10,2) [note: 'Core loss or excitation loss in watts when the transformer is energized but not loaded. Used for energy efficiency analysis and loss accounting.']
  oil_volume_gallons decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total volume of insulating oil in gallons for liquid-filled transformers. Used for oil sampling scheduling, spill containment planning, and environmental compliance.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the transformer in the distribution network. In-service units are actively serving load, standby units are energized but not loaded, retired units are permanently removed from service.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership classification of the transformer asset. Utility-owned assets are maintained by the utility, customer-owned assets are maintained by the customer but may be utility-monitored.']
  pcb_contaminated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transformer contains or is contaminated with PCBs above regulatory thresholds. True for PCB-contaminated units requiring special handling per EPA regulations.']
  phase_configuration varchar [note: 'Number of electrical phases the transformer is designed to handle. Single-phase units serve residential and small commercial loads, three-phase units serve larger commercial and industrial customers.']
  primary_voltage_kv decimal(8,3) [note: 'Rated primary winding voltage in kilovolts. Typically medium voltage (4.16 kV, 12.47 kV, 13.2 kV, 13.8 kV, 23 kV, 34.5 kV) from the distribution feeder.']
  replacement_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated current replacement cost in US dollars including equipment, installation, and removal. Used for capital planning, insurance valuation, and asset valuation.']
  scada_monitored_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transformer is equipped with SCADA telemetry for real-time monitoring of load, voltage, and status. True for monitored assets, false for non-monitored.']
  tap_position varchar [note: 'Current tap position setting for voltage adjustment. Positive values indicate voltage boost, negative values indicate voltage buck. Used for voltage regulation analysis.']
  transformer_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the transformer based on its electrical purpose and configuration within the distribution network.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transformer record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty coverage expires. Used for warranty claim eligibility and maintenance cost planning.']
  weight_pounds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of the transformer in pounds. Used for transportation planning, pole loading calculations, and installation equipment sizing.']
  winding_configuration varchar [note: 'Electrical connection arrangement of primary and secondary windings. Determines voltage transformation characteristics, grounding requirements, and harmonic performance.']

  Note: 'Master record for pole-mounted and pad-mounted distribution transformers that step down medium voltage to low voltage for customer service. Captures transformer serial number, kVA rating, primary/secondary voltage, phase configuration (single/three-phase), installation type (overhead/padmount/vault), GIS location, feeder assignment, service territory, manufacturer, installation date, condition rating, and ADMS asset reference. SSOT for distribution transformer identity, electrical ratings, and network placement. Supports load analysis, transformer loading studies, and voltage complaint investigation.'
}

A compliance officer tracking device-level obligat']

  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Sectionalizing devices (reclosers, switches, fuses) are capital assets tracked in the registry for depreciation, condition assessment, and maintenance planning. serial_number is denormalized from regi']

  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification based on number of customers served, load importance, and redundancy. Used for prioritizing maintenance and replacement investments.']
