  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or historical information about the device.']
  number_of_operations varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of switching operations performed by the device. Used for maintenance scheduling and end-of-life prediction.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the device in the distribution network. Determines whether device is available for protection and switching operations.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership classification of the device. Determines maintenance responsibility and cost allocation.']
  phase_configuration varchar [note: 'Electrical phase configuration of the device. Determines which phases are controlled or protected by this device. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: three_phase|single_phase_a|single_phase_b|single_phase_c|two_phase_ab|two_phase_bc|two_phase_ac — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pole_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the utility pole where the device is mounted, if applicable. Used for pole loading analysis and joint-use coordination.']
  protection_zone varchar [note: 'Protection coordination zone designation. Used for relay coordination studies and fault isolation logic.']
  rated_continuous_current_amps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum continuous current rating of the device in amperes. Determines load-carrying capacity and thermal limits.']
  rated_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum system voltage rating of the device in kilovolts. Must match or exceed the nominal voltage of the distribution circuit where installed.']
  regulatory_asset_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this device is included in the Regulatory Asset Base (RAB) for rate recovery purposes. Used for regulatory reporting and rate case preparation.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when manufacturer warranty coverage expires. Used for warranty claim eligibility and replacement cost planning.']

  Note: 'Master record for distribution network sectionalizing and protection devices including reclosers, sectionalizers, fuses, load break switches, and automated switches. Captures device type, manufacturer, model, rated voltage and current, interrupting capacity, automation capability (manual/automated/FLISR-enabled), GIS location, feeder segment assignment, SCADA point reference, communication protocol, and operational status. SSOT for all switchable and protective devices on the distribution network. Critical for FLISR automation, fault isolation, and protection coordination in GE PowerOn ADMS.'
}

Table distribution.service_drop {
  service_drop_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service drop connection. Primary key for the service drop master record.']
  customer_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active customer service agreement governing this service drop connection.']
  from_transformer_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.transformer.transformer_id — Service drops connect to distribution transformers. This FK is essential for outage impact assessment (transformer outage → which service drops/customers affected) and voltage complaint investigation.']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the metering point where this service drop terminates at the customer premise.']
  network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the secondary distribution network segment from which this service drop is fed.']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer premise served by this service drop.']
  primary_service_transformer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution transformer from which this service drop originates. Null for services fed directly from secondary mains.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Service drops are capital assets tracked in the registry for depreciation, replacement programs, and FERC accounting. Utilities register service drop infrastructure for lifecycle management and capita']
  service_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.service_point. Business justification: Field crew work orders, asset management, and service connection/disconnection operations require linking the physical service drop conductor to the metering service point it terminates at. Utility fi']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.service_territory']
  to_transformer_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.distribution_transformer.distribution_transformer_id — Service drops connect to distribution transformers (or secondary network). The description mentions \'pole or transformer association\'. This FK is critical for customer-to-network tracing and outage im']
  asset_condition_code varchar [note: 'Current physical condition assessment of the service drop infrastructure based on most recent inspection.']
  attachment_height_ft decimal(6,2) [note: 'Height above ground level in feet where the service drop is attached to the utility pole or structure. Critical for safety clearance compliance.']
  conductor_length_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length of the service drop conductor from the distribution network attachment point to the customer meter point, measured in feet.']
  conductor_size_awg varchar [note: 'American Wire Gauge size of the service drop conductor. Examples: #2, #4, 4/0, 2/0. Determines current-carrying capacity.']
  connection_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the service drop connection. Indicates whether the service is energized and delivering power.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service drop record was first created in the system.']
  der_interconnection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service drop has an approved Distributed Energy Resource (solar, wind, battery) interconnection. True if DER is present, False otherwise.']
  disconnection_date date [note: 'Date when the service drop was disconnected or de-energized. Null if currently active.']
  disconnection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for service drop disconnection. Null if service is active.']
  estimated_remaining_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated remaining useful life of the service drop in years based on condition assessment and typical asset lifecycle.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the service drop feature in the utility Geographic Information System for spatial analysis and mapping.']
  grounding_type varchar [note: 'Type of grounding electrode system used at the service entrance for electrical safety.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the service drop was originally installed and commissioned for service.']
  installation_type varchar [note: 'Physical installation method of the service drop connection.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inspection of the service drop for safety and condition assessment.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity performed on the service drop.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the service drop connection point at the customer premise in decimal degrees.']
  load_management_device_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a demand response or load management device is installed on this service drop. True if device present, False otherwise.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the service drop connection point at the customer premise in decimal degrees.']
  net_metering_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service drop is eligible for net energy metering programs. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  neutral_conductor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service drop includes a dedicated neutral conductor. True if neutral is present, False if neutral is shared or absent.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection of the service drop based on regulatory and utility maintenance standards.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Ownership classification of the service drop infrastructure. Determines maintenance responsibility and asset accounting.']
  phase_configuration varchar [note: 'Electrical phase configuration of the service drop connection.']
  pole_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility pole where the service drop attachment point is located.']
  replacement_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in US dollars to replace the service drop with equivalent new infrastructure. Used for capital planning and asset valuation.']
  service_amperage_rating varchar [note: 'Maximum continuous current rating of the service drop in amperes. Typical residential values: 100A, 200A, 400A.']
  service_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the service drop based on customer type and usage pattern. Determines service standards and restoration priority.']
  service_drop_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the service drop connection, typically assigned by the utility for external reference and field operations.']
  service_entrance_type varchar [note: 'Type of service entrance equipment at the customer premise where the service drop terminates.']
  service_priority_code varchar [note: 'Restoration priority classification for outage management. Critical services (hospitals, emergency facilities) receive highest priority during restoration.']
  service_voltage_nominal_v varchar [note: 'Nominal service voltage in volts delivered at the customer meter point. Typical values: 120, 240, 208, 277, 480.']
  service_voltage_type varchar [note: 'Voltage configuration delivered to the customer premise. Defines the electrical service characteristics.']
  smart_meter_compatible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service drop infrastructure supports Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) smart meter installation. True if compatible, False otherwise.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service drop record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record for the last-mile service connection from the distribution secondary network to a customer premises. Captures service drop identifier, conductor type and size, service voltage (120/240V single-phase or 120/208V three-phase), meter point reference, pole or transformer association, GIS coordinates, installation date, service class (residential/commercial/industrial), and connection status. SSOT for the physical last-mile connection linking the distribution network to the customer metering point. Essential for outage impact assessment and service restoration prioritization.'
}

Table distribution.network_segment {
  network_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution network segment. Primary key representing a topologically distinct section between two sectionalizing points.']
  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.ems_node. Business justification: Distribution network segments are modeled as EMS branches/nodes for state estimation observability and contingency analysis at the transmission-distribution boundary. Distribution planners and EMS ope']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Network segments have specific vegetation management obligations (NERC FAC-003), inspection frequency requirements, and maintenance standards. Tracking which obligations apply to which segments enable']
  primary_network_feeder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent distribution feeder that this segment belongs to. Links segment to the primary circuit originating from a substation.']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: Network segments are physical conductor sections within the distribution network that fall within a specific service territory. The network_segment table already has a service_territory_code STRING co']
  average_load_kw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Typical average load carried by this segment under normal operating conditions. Used for load flow analysis and loss calculations.']
  condition_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical assessment of the physical condition of the segment on a standardized scale (typically 0-100). Based on inspection findings, age, failure history, and predictive analytics. Used for prioritizing capital investments.']
  conductor_size_awg varchar [note: 'Cross-sectional size of the conductor expressed in AWG or kcmil (thousand circular mils). Determines current-carrying capacity and voltage drop characteristics. Common sizes: 4/0, 336.4 kcmil, 477 kcmil.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this network segment record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification based on customer count, load served, presence of critical facilities (hospitals, emergency services), and redundancy availability. Critical segments receive priority during restoration and maintenance planning.']
  customer_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer premises served by this network segment. Used for outage impact assessment and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculations for PUC reporting.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that is the authoritative source for this segment record (e.g., GE PowerOn ADMS, Esri ArcGIS Utility Network). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  emergency_ampacity_amps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Short-term current-carrying capacity allowed during emergency conditions or contingency operations. Typically 120-150% of rated ampacity for limited duration.']
  flisr_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is included in automated FLISR switching logic. FLISR-enabled segments can be automatically isolated and restored during fault conditions to minimize outage duration and customer impact.']
  gis_polyline_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the corresponding polyline feature in the GIS system (Esri ArcGIS Utility Network). Links operational data to geographic representation for mapping and spatial analysis.']
  hosting_capacity_kw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of distributed generation (solar, wind, storage) that can be interconnected to this segment without causing voltage, thermal, or protection violations. Critical for DER interconnection studies and renewable integration planning.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the network segment was originally installed and energized. Used for asset age analysis and replacement planning.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inspection of the segment. Used for compliance with maintenance schedules and condition assessment programs.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this network segment record. Tracks data currency and change history for operational and compliance purposes.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last modified this record. Supports audit trail and data governance requirements.']
  neutral_configuration varchar [note: 'Method of neutral conductor grounding. Multi-grounded neutrals are grounded at multiple points for safety, unigrounded at one point, ungrounded systems have no neutral ground, impedance-grounded use resistors or reactors.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection based on regulatory requirements and utility maintenance policies.']
  nominal_voltage_kv decimal(8,3) [note: 'Design voltage level of the distribution segment in kilovolts. Common distribution voltages: 4.16 kV, 12.47 kV, 13.2 kV, 13.8 kV, 23 kV, 34.5 kV.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the network segment in the distribution grid. Determines whether the segment is actively carrying load, isolated for maintenance, or permanently retired.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership classification of the segment. Most distribution segments are utility-owned, but some may be customer-owned (behind-the-meter), jointly owned with municipalities, or owned by third parties.']
  peak_load_kw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Historical maximum load observed on this segment in kilowatts. Used for capacity planning, load balancing, and identifying overloaded segments requiring reinforcement.']
  phase_configuration varchar [note: 'Electrical phase arrangement of the segment. Three-phase segments serve large loads and industrial customers, two-phase serve medium loads, single-phase serve residential areas. Phase rotation (ABC vs ACB) affects motor operation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: three_phase_abc|three_phase_acb|two_phase_ab|two_phase_bc|two_phase_ac|single_phase_a|single_phase_b|single_phase_c — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  positive_sequence_reactance_ohms_per_km decimal(12,6) [note: 'Positive sequence inductive reactance of the conductor per unit length. Critical for power flow modeling and voltage regulation analysis.']
  positive_sequence_resistance_ohms_per_km decimal(12,6) [note: 'Positive sequence resistance of the conductor per unit length. Used in load flow analysis, voltage drop calculations, and short circuit studies in GE PowerOn ADMS.']
  rated_ampacity_amps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum continuous current-carrying capacity of the conductor in amperes under normal operating conditions. Determined by conductor size, material, installation method, and ambient temperature.']
  record_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the segment record is effective. Supports temporal data management and historical analysis of network topology changes.']
  record_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the segment record expires and is superseded by a new version. Null for current active records. Enables tracking of network topology evolution over time.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State or provincial Public Utility Commission (PUC) that has regulatory authority over this segment. Determines applicable reliability standards, reporting requirements, and rate recovery mechanisms.']
  scada_monitored_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment has real-time SCADA telemetry for current, voltage, and power flow monitoring. SCADA-monitored segments provide operational visibility for grid operators.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the network segment used in operational systems and field communications. Typically derived from GIS naming conventions.']
  segment_length_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Physical length of the network segment measured in kilometers. Used for impedance calculations, voltage drop analysis, and hosting capacity studies.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the network segment, often including geographic or operational context (e.g., \'Main St Feeder 12A Section 3\').']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment based on its role in the distribution network hierarchy. Primary feeders carry bulk power from substations, laterals branch from primaries, sub-laterals branch from laterals, service backbones serve multiple service drops, express feeders bypass load areas, and tie lines connect feeders.']
  volt_var_optimization_zone varchar [note: 'Identifier for the voltage optimization zone this segment belongs to. VVO zones are coordinated groups of segments and voltage control devices managed together to reduce losses and maintain voltage within acceptable limits.']
  zero_sequence_reactance_ohms_per_km decimal(12,6) [note: 'Zero sequence inductive reactance per unit length. Essential for fault current calculations and protection system design.']
  zero_sequence_resistance_ohms_per_km decimal(12,6) [note: 'Zero sequence resistance per unit length. Used in ground fault analysis and protective relay coordination studies.']

  Note: 'Master record representing a topologically distinct section of the distribution network between two sectionalizing points. Captures segment identifier, upstream and downstream boundary devices, conductor type and size, length (km), rated ampacity, nominal voltage, phase configuration, feeder association, GIS polyline reference, and load zone classification. SSOT for distribution network topology between switching points. Used for load flow analysis, fault location, FLISR switching logic, and hosting capacity analysis in GE PowerOn ADMS.'
}

Table distribution.load_zone {
  load_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution load zone. Primary key. This is the SSOT identifier used across GE PowerOn ADMS and Esri ArcGIS Utility Network for zone-level control and planning.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Distribution load zones roll up into a balancing authority control area for AGC load forecasting, demand response dispatch, and NERC BAL compliance reporting. A domain expert expects load zones to be ']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent substation that supplies this load zone. Links the zone to its source substation in the transmission-distribution interface.']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Distribution load zones are operational constructs that directly map to forecast zones for planning. Essential for integrated distribution planning, voltage optimization planning, and capacity studies']
  pnode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.pnode. Business justification: Distribution load zones are mapped to transmission pnodes for LMP-based wholesale settlement, congestion cost allocation, and load forecasting in RTO/ISO markets. FERC settlement and distribution plan']
  primary_load_feeder_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.feeder.feeder_id — Load zones are associated with feeders. The description mentions \'associated feeders\'. This is a many-to-many but the primary association is critical for load balancing operations.']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: Load zones are logical groupings of network segments used for load balancing and DMS/ADMS operations — they exist within the geographic boundaries of a service territory. Adding service_territory_id t']
  capacity_mva decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total installed capacity of the load zone in megavolt-amperes, representing the maximum apparent power the zone infrastructure can support. Used for capacity planning and load transfer studies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this load zone record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  customer_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer premises served by this load zone. Used for reliability index calculations (SAIDI, SAIFI, CAIDI) and outage impact assessment.']
  der_penetration_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total installed capacity of distributed energy resources (solar PV, wind, storage, etc.) within the load zone, measured in megawatts. Used for DER hosting capacity analysis and grid integration planning.']
  dms_zone_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the zone within the DMS/ADMS for automation capabilities. VVO-enabled zones support Volt-VAR Optimization, FLISR-enabled zones support Fault Location Isolation and Service Restoration, manual zones require operator intervention, hybrid zones have partial automation, and advanced zones support full ADMS integration.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the load zone configuration became or will become effective. Used for tracking zone boundary changes, capacity upgrades, and operational transitions.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the load zone configuration expires or is scheduled for decommissioning. Null for active zones with no planned end date.']
  feeder_count varchar [note: 'Number of distribution feeders associated with this load zone. Used for network topology analysis and load balancing.']
  flisr_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FLISR automation is enabled for this load zone. True means automated fault detection, isolation, and restoration; false means manual switching operations.']
  geographic_boundary_wkt varchar [note: 'Geographic boundary of the load zone represented as a WKT (Well-Known Text) polygon. Used for spatial analysis, outage mapping, and GIS integration with Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.']
  hosting_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum amount of additional DER capacity the zone can accommodate without infrastructure upgrades or operational issues, measured in megawatts. Used for interconnection screening and DER integration planning.']
  last_capacity_study_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent distribution capacity study performed for this load zone. Used to track planning cycle compliance and identify zones requiring updated analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this load zone record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization across systems.']
  load_profile_type varchar [note: 'Dominant customer class or load profile characteristic of the zone. Residential zones peak in evening, commercial zones peak mid-day, industrial zones have steady load, mixed zones have diverse profiles, and agricultural zones have seasonal patterns.']
  next_capacity_study_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next distribution capacity study for this load zone. Used for planning resource allocation and ensuring regulatory compliance with capacity planning cycles.']
  nominal_load_mvar decimal(12,3) [note: 'Typical or average reactive power load of the zone under normal operating conditions, measured in megavolt-amperes reactive. Used for Volt-VAR optimization and power factor management.']
  nominal_load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Typical or average real power load of the zone under normal operating conditions, measured in megawatts. Used for load balancing and capacity planning.']
  nominal_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal operating voltage of the load zone in kilovolts. Typical distribution voltages range from 4.16 kV to 34.5 kV.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special considerations, or additional context about the load zone. May include information about unique load characteristics, planned projects, or operational constraints.']
  peak_demand_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Historical or forecasted peak real power demand for the zone, measured in megawatts. Critical for capacity planning and infrastructure investment decisions.']
  peak_demand_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the peak demand was recorded or is forecasted to occur. Used for load profile analysis and demand response planning.']
  planning_area_code varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the broader distribution planning area or service territory that contains this load zone. Used for regional capacity planning and resource allocation.']
  power_factor_target decimal(5,4) [note: 'Target power factor for the load zone, typically between 0.95 and 1.00. Used for Volt-VAR optimization dispatch and capacitor bank control to minimize reactive power losses.']
  reliability_caidi_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Customer Average Interruption Duration Index for the load zone, measured in minutes per interruption. Calculated as SAIDI/SAIFI. Indicates average restoration time. Key reliability metric reported to PUC.']
  reliability_saidi_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'System Average Interruption Duration Index for the load zone, measured in minutes per customer per year. Key reliability metric reported to PUC. Lower values indicate better reliability.']
  reliability_saifi_count decimal(10,2) [note: 'System Average Interruption Frequency Index for the load zone, measured as number of interruptions per customer per year. Key reliability metric reported to PUC. Lower values indicate better reliability.']
  scada_integration_status varchar [note: 'Level of SCADA system integration for real-time monitoring and control of the load zone. Fully integrated zones have complete telemetry and control, partially integrated zones have limited coverage, not integrated zones rely on manual operations, and planned zones are scheduled for integration.']
  utilization_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of peak demand to installed capacity, indicating how heavily the zone infrastructure is utilized. Values approaching 1.0 indicate need for capacity expansion.']
  voltage_regulation_target_max_pu decimal(6,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable voltage level for the zone expressed in per-unit (pu) of nominal voltage. Typically 1.05 pu per ANSI C84.1 Range A. Used by VVO algorithms to maintain voltage within acceptable limits.']
  voltage_regulation_target_min_pu decimal(6,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable voltage level for the zone expressed in per-unit (pu) of nominal voltage. Typically 0.95 pu per ANSI C84.1 Range A. Used by VVO algorithms to maintain voltage within acceptable limits.']
  vvo_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Volt-VAR Optimization is enabled for this load zone. True means VVO algorithms actively control voltage and reactive power devices; false means manual or legacy control.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the load zone, typically a human-readable alphanumeric code used in operational systems and dispatch communications. Externally known identifier for the zone.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the load zone, often reflecting geographic area or substation association (e.g., \'Downtown West Zone\', \'Substation 5 North\').']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the load zone. Active zones are in service, inactive zones are temporarily out of service, planned zones are under construction, decommissioned zones are retired, and maintenance zones are undergoing scheduled work.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the load zone based on its role in the distribution network hierarchy. Primary zones serve major load centers, secondary zones serve neighborhood clusters, tertiary zones serve smaller segments, and special zones serve unique loads (industrial parks, critical infrastructure).']

  Note: 'Master record for distribution load zones — logical groupings of network segments used for load balancing, volt-VAR optimization dispatch, demand forecasting, and capacity planning. Captures zone identifier, zone name, geographic boundary (GIS polygon), nominal load (MW/MVAr), peak demand (MW), associated feeders, voltage regulation targets, power factor targets, and DMS zone classification. Serves as the control zone for VVO dispatch actions and the planning unit for distribution capacity studies. SSOT for load zone definition in GE PowerOn ADMS.'
}

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing planned and emergency switching operations on the distribution network, including the full sequence of individual switching steps. Captures switching order number, order type (planned/emergency/FLISR-automated/load-transfer), initiating event reference, crew assignment, authorization approvals, safety clearances, and completion status. Each order contains an ordered sequence of switching steps, each with: step sequence number, target device, operation type (open/close/tag/untag), required pre/post device state, execution timestamp, operator ID, confirmed device state, and step completion status. Records pre-switch and post-switch network topology for each order. Table distribution.flisr_event {
  flisr_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the FLISR automation event record. Primary key.']
  cause_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.cause. Business justification: FLISR events are triggered by specific fault types requiring standardized cause classification for IEEE 1366 reliability reporting and NERC/PUC regulatory filings. The outage.cause lookup provides the']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution substation serving the affected feeder. Used for substation-level outage aggregation and reporting.']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_event. Business justification: FLISR events trigger DR dispatch for load relief during fault isolation and restoration. Linking FLISR events to coordinated DR events is required for post-event reliability analysis, SAIDI/SAIFI repo']
  energy_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.energy_schedule. Business justification: FLISR events isolating feeders with DERs force schedule deviations that must be reported to the ISO/RTO. This link enables automatic identification of affected energy schedules during fault isolation,']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent outage event record in the Outage Management System (OMS) that this FLISR event is associated with. Links FLISR automation to broader outage lifecycle.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary distribution feeder on which the FLISR event occurred. Used for feeder-level reliability analysis and SAIDI/SAIFI calculation.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: FLISR events carry a puc_reportable_flag indicating they are subject to specific PUC reporting obligations. A compliance officer managing automated restoration reporting expects each FLISR event to re']
  primary_flisr_distribution_network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution network segment (feeder section) identified by FLISR algorithm as containing the fault. References GIS utility network topology.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: FLISR events with puc_reportable_flag=true must be included in PUC regulatory reliability filings. Automated restoration events are specifically tracked in state reliability reports. A regulatory affa']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this FLISR event to a declared storm event, if the fault occurred during a major weather event. Used for storm impact aggregation and mutual aid reporting.']
  triggering_scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.scada_point. Business justification: FLISR events are triggered by SCADA fault indicator alarms. The plain string \'triggering_fault_indicator_code\' is a denormalized SCADA reference. Role-prefix \'triggering_\' used because the event has a']
  weather_input_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.weather_input. Business justification: FLISR event performance is analyzed against weather forecast inputs for storm hardening studies and reliability reporting. weather_condition is a denormalized plain attribute; linking to forecast.weat']
  caidi_contribution_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated contribution of this FLISR event to the CAIDI reliability index, in minutes. Computed as total customer-minutes / customers affected. Measures average restoration time per affected customer.']
  communication_failure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether communication failures with field devices occurred during the FLISR sequence. True if communication issues impacted automation; false otherwise.']
  customers_affected_initial varchar [note: 'Number of customer meters that lost service when the fault occurred and before FLISR restoration actions. Used for SAIFI numerator calculation.']
  customers_affected_post_restoration varchar [note: 'Number of customer meters that remain without service after FLISR restoration sequence completed. Represents customers on the isolated faulted segment awaiting manual repair.']
  customers_restored_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer meters that had service restored by FLISR automation (initial affected minus post-restoration affected). Measures FLISR effectiveness in minimizing outage scope.']
  der_impacted_count varchar [note: 'Number of Distributed Energy Resource (DER) installations (solar, storage, generators) affected by the FLISR event. Used for DER impact analysis and IEEE 1547 compliance reporting.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the FLISR automation event. Tracks progression from initiation through completion or failure.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the FLISR automation event was triggered by the Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS). Represents the real-world moment the fault was detected and FLISR sequence initiated.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why FLISR automation failed or was aborted, if applicable. Captures system errors, communication failures, operator intervention reasons, or topology constraints.']
  fault_current_amps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Magnitude of fault current measured at the triggering fault indicator, in amperes. Used for fault severity analysis and protective device coordination validation.']
  fault_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fault condition that triggered FLISR. Permanent faults require manual repair; temporary faults may clear after isolation; transient faults clear on recloser operation.']
  flisr_algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the FLISR algorithm logic deployed in ADMS at the time of this event. Used for performance trending and algorithm tuning analysis.']
  flisr_event_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the FLISR event, typically generated by GE PowerOn ADMS. Used for external reference and operational tracking.']
  flisr_outcome_status varchar [note: 'Final outcome classification of the FLISR automation attempt. Full success indicates all restoration actions completed; partial success indicates some customers restored; failed indicates no restoration achieved.']
  isolation_devices_operated varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of switching device IDs (reclosers, sectionalizers, automated switches) that were operated to isolate the faulted segment. Captures the isolation sequence executed by FLISR.']
  isolation_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in seconds from fault detection to completion of fault isolation switching. Subset of total restoration time, measures speed of fault containment.']
  load_transferred_kw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of electrical load in kilowatts transferred to alternate feeders during FLISR restoration. Used for load balancing analysis and feeder capacity validation.']
  major_event_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this FLISR event qualifies for exclusion from reliability indices under IEEE 1366 Major Event Day criteria. True if excluded; false if included in normal reliability calculations.']
  operator_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a human operator intervened to override or abort the automated FLISR sequence. True if operator action occurred; false if fully automated.']
  puc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this FLISR event meets the threshold criteria for mandatory reporting to the state Public Utility Commission. True if reportable; false otherwise.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this FLISR event record was first created in the data system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this FLISR event record was last modified in the data system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  restoration_devices_operated varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of switching device IDs operated to restore service to de-energized segments downstream of the fault. Captures the restoration switching sequence executed by FLISR.']
  restoration_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in seconds from fault detection to completion of FLISR restoration switching sequence. Critical metric for CAIDI calculation and FLISR performance benchmarking.']
  saidi_contribution_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated contribution of this FLISR event to the SAIDI reliability index, in customer-minutes. Computed as (customers affected × duration) / total customers served. Critical for PUC reliability reporting.']
  saifi_contribution decimal(10,6) [note: 'Calculated contribution of this FLISR event to the SAIFI reliability index. Computed as customers affected / total customers served. Critical for PUC reliability reporting.']
  scada_alarm_count varchar [note: 'Number of SCADA alarms generated during the FLISR event. High alarm counts may indicate cascading issues or communication problems.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system that generated this FLISR event record, typically GE PowerOn ADMS. Used for data lineage and multi-system integration scenarios.']
  switching_operations_count varchar [note: 'Total number of switching device operations (open/close commands) executed during the FLISR sequence. Used for device wear analysis and maintenance planning.']
  topology_validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of network topology validation performed by ADMS before executing FLISR switching. Invalid topology may prevent or abort FLISR automation.']
  voltage_sag_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage voltage drop observed during the fault condition, relative to nominal voltage. Indicates severity of power quality disturbance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing Fault Location, Isolation, and Service Restoration (FLISR) automation events triggered by GE PowerOn ADMS. Captures event timestamp, triggering fault indicator, faulted segment identified, isolation devices operated, restoration switching sequence executed, customers affected before and after restoration, restoration time (seconds), FLISR algorithm version, and outcome status. SSOT for FLISR automation event history. Critical for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculation, FLISR performance benchmarking, and PUC reporting.'
}

  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: IEEE 1547-2018 requires smart inverter DERs to participate in volt-var optimization. VVO actions that dispatch DER reactive power control must reference the specific DER registry record for DERMS comm']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution substation serving the control zone where the action occurred.']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_event. Business justification: VVO actions are coordinated with DR dispatch for combined CVR+DR demand reduction programs. Linking volt_var_action to dr_event supports combined CVR-DR performance reporting, energy savings verificat']
  energy_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.energy_schedule. Business justification: VVO actions that curtail DER output must reference the active energy schedule to trigger ISO notification of forced curtailment. ADMS/ETRM integration requires this link for real-time DER dispatch coo']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution feeder circuit on which the VVO action was performed.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Volt-VAR optimization programs are often mandated by state PUC energy efficiency obligations or voltage quality standards. A compliance officer tracking VVO program compliance expects volt_var_action ']
  primary_volt_load_zone_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution control zone where the VVO action was executed. Links to the geographic/electrical zone managed by ADMS.']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.scada_point. Business justification: VVO actions are executed via SCADA commands to field devices. The plain string \'scada_command_code\' is a denormalized SCADA reference. A proper FK enables DMS-SCADA command audit trails, VVO performan']
  state_estimation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.state_estimation_run. Business justification: VVO actions are triggered by state estimation results identifying voltage violations. Linking to the triggering state estimation run enables VVO performance analysis, voltage violation audit trails, a']
  tertiary_load_zone_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.load_zone.load_zone_id — VVO control actions are dispatched per control zone. The load_zone FK identifies which zone the optimization action targets, enabling zone-level VVO performance analysis.']
  volt_load_zone_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.load_zone.load_zone_id — VVO actions are executed within a control zone. The description mentions \'control zone\'. Critical for volt-VAR optimization tracking.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Outcome status of the VVO action execution: successful, failed, partial (some devices operated), aborted (cancelled mid-execution), or pending.']
  action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the VVO control action was executed on the distribution network. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the VVO action: voltage regulation, reactive power compensation, power factor correction, Conservation Voltage Reduction (CVR), energy loss reduction, or combined optimization.']
  affected_customer_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer premises affected by the VVO action within the control zone.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether operator approval was required before executing this VVO action (True/False).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VVO action record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  cvr_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'CVR factor achieved by the action: ratio of percent change in demand to percent change in voltage. Typical range 0.5 to 1.5.']
  demand_reduction_kw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Peak demand reduction achieved by the VVO action (typically through CVR), measured in kilowatts (kW).']
  der_active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Distributed Energy Resources (solar, wind, storage) were actively generating/discharging in the control zone during the VVO action (True/False).']
  der_impact_mw decimal(12,4) [note: 'Estimated impact of DER generation/discharge on the VVO optimization, measured in megawatts (MW). Null if no DER active.']
  device_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label of the device operated (e.g., \'CAP-BANK-12A-01\', \'REG-SUBSTATION-5\').']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of distribution device operated: capacitor bank, voltage regulator, Load Tap Changer (LTC) transformer, switched capacitor, static VAR compensator, or other VVO-capable equipment.']
  energy_loss_reduction_kwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated energy loss reduction achieved by the VVO action over the optimization period, measured in kilowatt-hours (kWh).']
  execution_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Time taken to execute the VVO action from command initiation to device confirmation, measured in seconds.']
  execution_mode varchar [note: 'Mode in which the VVO action was executed: automatic (ADMS-initiated), manual (operator-initiated), scheduled (pre-planned), or advisory (recommended but not executed).']
  load_level varchar [note: 'Distribution load level at the time of the VVO action: light, medium, heavy, or peak. Used for optimization performance analysis.']
  optimization_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Time duration over which the VVO optimization was calculated and applied, measured in seconds.']
  optimization_objective varchar [note: 'Primary objective of the VVO action: Conservation Voltage Reduction (CVR), power factor correction, loss minimization, voltage profile improvement, peak demand reduction, or multi-objective optimization.']
  power_factor_after decimal(5,4) [note: 'Power factor in the control zone after the VVO action (ratio of real power to apparent power, range 0.0 to 1.0).']
  power_factor_before decimal(5,4) [note: 'Power factor in the control zone before the VVO action (ratio of real power to apparent power, range 0.0 to 1.0).']
  power_factor_improvement decimal(5,4) [note: 'Improvement in power factor achieved by the VVO action. Positive value indicates power factor increased (closer to unity).']
  reactive_power_after_mvar decimal(12,4) [note: 'Reactive power flow in the control zone after the VVO action, measured in Megavolt-Amperes Reactive (MVAr).']
  reactive_power_before_mvar decimal(12,4) [note: 'Reactive power flow in the control zone before the VVO action, measured in Megavolt-Amperes Reactive (MVAr).']
  reactive_power_reduction_mvar decimal(12,4) [note: 'Reduction in reactive power flow achieved by the VVO action, measured in MVAr. Positive value indicates successful VAR reduction.']
  setpoint_after decimal(10,4) [note: 'Device setpoint value after the VVO action was executed. Units depend on device type.']
  setpoint_before decimal(10,4) [note: 'Device setpoint value before the VVO action (tap position for LTC/regulator, switch state for capacitor, VAR output for SVC). Units depend on device type.']
  setpoint_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the setpoint values: tap position (integer), switch state (on/off), MVAr, kVAr, percent, or per-unit (pu).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated this VVO action record (e.g., \'GE PowerOn ADMS\', \'OSIsoft PI\', \'Manual Entry\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VVO action record was last updated. Audit trail field.']
  violation_description varchar [note: 'Description of any voltage or operational violations detected (e.g., \'Voltage exceeded upper limit at node XYZ\', \'Device operation count exceeded daily limit\'). Null if no violations.']
  violation_detected_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any voltage or operational violations were detected during or after the VVO action (True/False).']
  voltage_after_pu decimal(8,6) [note: 'Average voltage profile in the control zone after the VVO action, measured in per-unit (pu) relative to nominal voltage.']
  voltage_before_pu decimal(8,6) [note: 'Average voltage profile in the control zone before the VVO action, measured in per-unit (pu) relative to nominal voltage.']
  voltage_improvement_pu decimal(8,6) [note: 'Change in voltage profile achieved by the VVO action, measured in per-unit (pu). Positive value indicates voltage increase, negative indicates reduction (CVR).']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather condition at the time of the VVO action (e.g., \'clear\', \'rain\', \'snow\', \'high_wind\'). Used for performance correlation analysis.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for Volt-VAR Optimization (VVO) control actions executed by GE PowerOn ADMS on the distribution network. Captures action timestamp, control zone, device operated (capacitor bank, voltage regulator, LTC transformer), setpoint before and after, voltage profile improvement (pu), reactive power reduction (MVAr), energy loss reduction (kWh), optimization objective (conservation voltage reduction/power factor correction), and execution mode (automatic/manual). SSOT for VVO operational action history. Supports CVR program performance measurement and energy loss reduction reporting.'
}

Table distribution.capacitor_bank {
  capacitor_bank_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capacitor bank asset. Primary key for the capacitor bank master record.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent distribution substation serving the feeder on which this capacitor bank is installed.']
  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.ems_node. Business justification: Capacitor banks are modeled as shunt reactive elements in the EMS network model. Their reactive power contribution is tracked in state estimation for power flow accuracy. This link enables VVO coordin']
  nerc_cip_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.nerc_cip_asset. Business justification: SCADA-controlled capacitor banks in substations can be BES Cyber Assets requiring CIP compliance when they impact BES reliability. The scada_point_id and volt_var_optimization_enabled attributes indic']
  network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.network_segment. Business justification: Capacitor banks are physically installed on specific network segments (conductors/line sections) of the distribution network. The network segment determines the electrical location of the capacitor ba']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Capacitor banks are capital assets requiring registry entries for depreciation, valuation, and maintenance planning. serial_number is denormalized from registry. Utility asset managers track all field']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'SCADA system telemetry point reference for real-time monitoring and remote control of the capacitor bank through the Distribution Management System (DMS) or Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS).']
  acquisition_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to acquire and install the capacitor bank, including equipment, labor, and materials.']
  ambient_temperature_rating_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum ambient temperature rating in degrees Celsius at which the capacitor bank can safely operate per manufacturer specifications.']
  asset_condition_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative health index or condition score (typically 0-100) representing the current physical and operational condition of the capacitor bank based on inspection and diagnostic data.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the capacitor bank, typically including location or circuit reference.']
  bank_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or asset tag assigned to the capacitor bank for operational reference and field identification.']
  bank_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the capacitor bank switching capability: fixed (always on), switched (manual or remote control), or automated (SCADA-controlled for volt-VAR optimization).']
  control_mode varchar [note: 'Primary control strategy governing capacitor bank switching operations: time-based scheduling, voltage-based, VAr-based, temperature-based, power factor correction, or manual operation.']
  control_voltage_v decimal(8,2) [note: 'Control circuit voltage in volts required for operating the capacitor bank switching mechanism and control relays.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacitor bank record was first created in the system.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification of the capacitor bank based on its impact on system reliability, voltage support, and customer service.']
  fuse_rating_amperes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current rating in amperes of the protective fuses installed to protect the capacitor bank from overcurrent and fault conditions.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Unique feature identifier in the utility GIS system linking the capacitor bank to the geospatial network model and asset location database.']
  in_service_date date [note: 'Date when the capacitor bank was placed into active service and began providing reactive power compensation to the distribution network.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the capacitor bank was installed and commissioned in the distribution network.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance activity performed on the capacitor bank.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the capacitor bank installation location in decimal degrees for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the capacitor bank installation location in decimal degrees for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next routine inspection of the capacitor bank per the utility maintenance schedule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional operational notes, special instructions, or historical information about the capacitor bank.']
  number_of_steps varchar [note: 'Count of discrete switching steps or stages available in the capacitor bank for granular reactive power control. Single-step banks have a value of 1.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the capacitor bank in the distribution network lifecycle.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of asset ownership indicating whether the capacitor bank is owned by the utility, customer, third party, or leased.']
  phase_connection varchar [note: 'Electrical phase configuration indicating which phase(s) of the distribution circuit the capacitor bank is connected to for reactive power compensation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: three_phase|phase_a|phase_b|phase_c|phase_ab|phase_bc|phase_ca — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pole_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution pole or structure on which the capacitor bank is mounted, used for field location and maintenance planning.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Procurement purchase order number under which the capacitor bank was acquired.']
  rated_kvar decimal(12,2) [note: 'Nameplate reactive power rating of the capacitor bank in kilovolt-amperes reactive, representing the total reactive power compensation capacity.']
  switching_device_type varchar [note: 'Type of switching mechanism used to energize and de-energize the capacitor bank for reactive power control.']
  switching_operations_count varchar [note: 'Total number of switching operations (on/off cycles) performed by the capacitor bank since installation, used for condition monitoring and maintenance planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacitor bank record was last modified in the system.']
  volt_var_optimization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacitor bank is integrated into the ADMS volt-VAR optimization control scheme for automated voltage and reactive power management.']
  voltage_rating_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage rating of the capacitor bank in kilovolts, indicating the distribution voltage level at which the bank operates.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty coverage for the capacitor bank expires.']

  Note: 'Master record for shunt capacitor banks installed on the distribution network for reactive power compensation and voltage support. Captures bank identifier, rated kVAr, voltage rating, number of steps, switching type (fixed/switched/automated), control mode (time/voltage/VAr/temperature), GIS location, feeder and phase assignment, SCADA point reference, installation date, and operational status. Managed through GE PowerOn ADMS for volt-VAR optimization.'
}

Table distribution.distribution_reliability_event {
  distribution_reliability_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution reliability interruption event. Primary key for IEEE 1366 reliability index calculation and PUC reporting.']

Business justification: NERC and state PUC reliability reporting requires linking distribution outage events to causal upstream transmission outages for root cause analysis and regulatory filings. Distribution reliability en']
  cause_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.cause. Business justification: IEEE 1366 reliability reporting (SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI) and PUC regulatory filings require standardized cause classification. The outage.cause lookup provides IEEE/NERC/PUC cause codes. cause_code and cau']
  control_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_center. Business justification: Reliability events meeting NERC OE-417 thresholds are reported through and coordinated by the grid control center. The control center manages restoration coordination and regulatory submissions. This ']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution substation serving the affected feeder. Used for substation-level reliability aggregation and asset performance correlation.']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_event. Business justification: Reliability events (outages) trigger DR dispatch; linking reliability events to coordinated DR events is required for IEEE 1366 reporting with DR contribution, PUC regulatory filings, and post-event a']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution feeder (primary circuit) affected by the reliability event. Used for worst-performing feeder analysis and targeted reliability improvement programs.']
  grid_reliability_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.grid_reliability_event. Business justification: Major distribution reliability events escalate to grid-level reliability events for transmission-impacting outages. Required for NERC OE-417 reporting, major event day classification, and integrated r']
  network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.network_segment. Business justification: Reliability events (interruptions) occur on specific network segments. Currently reliability_event has segment_id as STRING, but this should be a proper FK to network_segment.network_segment_id for re']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Distribution reliability events must be reported under specific regulatory obligations (IEEE 1366 metrics, state PUC reliability rules). The existing regulatory_filing FK captures the filing, but the ']
  oms_event_id varchar [note: 'Source system identifier from the Outage Management System (OMS) or ADMS. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail back to operational systems.']
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: IEEE 1366 reliability calculations exclude planned interruptions from SAIDI/SAIFI indices. Linking reliability events to planned outage windows enables proper exclusion logic in regulatory filings and']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Reliability events involve failed equipment traceable to the asset registry for condition management, failure rate analysis, and replacement prioritization. Links the outage/reliability domain to asse']
  sectionalizing_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific protective device that operated during the event. Links to asset registry for device maintenance history and performance tracking.']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: Distribution reliability events (SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI contributors) are reported to the PUC by service territory. The puc_reportable_flag and puc_report_date fields confirm regulatory reporting requireme']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the storm event record if this reliability event was part of a larger storm or weather system. Enables storm-level reliability aggregation and mutual aid analysis.']
  transformer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.transformer. Business justification: Distribution reliability interruption events are frequently caused by transformer failures (overloads, insulation failures, oil leaks, lightning strikes). The distribution_reliability_event already li']
  violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: A distribution reliability event exceeding regulatory duration or frequency thresholds directly triggers a compliance violation. Utilities track which outage events resulted in PUC violations for pena']
  weather_input_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.weather_input. Business justification: Reliability events are correlated with weather forecast inputs for IEEE 1366 major event day classification, storm hardening analysis, and PUC regulatory reporting. weather_condition is a denormalized']
  crew_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when field crew arrived at the fault location. Used to calculate crew travel time and on-site restoration duration.']
  crew_dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when field crew was dispatched to investigate and repair the fault. Used to calculate crew response time and restoration performance metrics.']
  critical_customer_affected_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether any critical customers (hospitals, emergency services, water treatment, etc.) were affected by the interruption. Used for priority restoration and regulatory notification.']
  customer_minutes_interrupted decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total customer-minutes of interruption calculated as customers_interrupted × duration_minutes. Used to calculate SAIDI (System Average Interruption Duration Index) per IEEE 1366.']
  customers_interrupted varchar [note: 'Total number of customer accounts experiencing service interruption during this event. Used to calculate SAIFI (System Average Interruption Frequency Index) per IEEE 1366.']
  customers_restored_by_flisr varchar [note: 'Number of customers automatically restored by FLISR automation without manual crew intervention. Used to quantify FLISR benefit and avoided customer-minutes interrupted.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated this reliability event record (e.g., GE PowerOn ADMS, OSIsoft PI, SAP IS-U). Used for data lineage and source system reconciliation.']
  duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the interruption event in minutes, calculated as the difference between restoration_timestamp and event_start_timestamp. Used for IEEE 1366 sustained vs. momentary classification.']
  energy_not_served_mwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total energy not delivered to customers in megawatt-hours (MWh) due to the interruption, calculated as load_interrupted_mw × duration_hours. Used for economic impact analysis and PUC reporting.']
  equipment_age_years varchar [note: 'Age in years of the failed equipment at the time of the event. Used for asset health correlation and age-based replacement program justification.']
  equipment_failed varchar [note: 'Type of distribution equipment that failed and caused the interruption (e.g., transformer, conductor, insulator, pole, underground cable). Used for asset failure analysis and replacement prioritization.']
  estimated_restoration_time timestamp [note: 'Initial estimated time of restoration communicated to customers and regulators when the event began. Used to measure ERT accuracy and customer communication effectiveness.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the reliability event, typically generated by the Outage Management System (OMS) or Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS). Used for external reporting and cross-system correlation.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reliability event began (first customer interruption detected). Captured from SCADA, AMI, or customer call-in data via OMS/ADMS.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interruption event per IEEE 1366 definitions: sustained (>5 minutes), momentary (≤5 minutes), or planned (scheduled maintenance outage).']
  exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the event is excluded from IEEE 1366 reliability index calculations, if ieee_1366_exclusion_flag is true. References specific IEEE 1366 exclusion criteria.']
  fault_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the fault location in decimal degrees. Used for spatial reliability analysis and vegetation management correlation.']
  fault_location_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the physical location where the fault occurred (e.g., street address, pole number, landmark). Used for crew dispatch and asset maintenance correlation.']
  fault_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the fault location in decimal degrees. Used for spatial reliability analysis and vegetation management correlation.']
  flisr_operated_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether FLISR automation successfully operated to isolate the fault and restore service to unaffected customers. Used to measure FLISR effectiveness and MAIFI (Momentary Average Interruption Frequency Index) reduction.']
  flisr_operation_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time in seconds for FLISR automation to isolate the fault and restore service to unaffected segments. Used to measure FLISR performance and reduction in customer-minutes interrupted.']
  ieee_1366_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this event should be excluded from IEEE 1366 reliability index calculations per standard exclusion criteria (e.g., loss of supply, prearranged interruption, customer-owned equipment failure).']
  load_interrupted_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total electrical load in megawatts (MW) interrupted during the event, captured from SCADA or estimated from customer count and load profiles. Used for energy not served (ENS) calculations.']
  major_event_day_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this event occurred on a Major Event Day per IEEE 1366 2.5-beta method. Major Event Days are excluded from annual reliability index calculations to prevent catastrophic events from skewing performance metrics.']
  protective_device_type varchar [note: 'Type of protective device that operated to isolate the fault (e.g., recloser, circuit breaker, fuse, sectionalizer). Used for protective device performance analysis and coordination studies.']
  puc_report_date date [note: 'Date when the reliability event was reported to the Public Utility Commission, if puc_reportable_flag is true. Used to track regulatory reporting compliance.']
  puc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this event must be reported to the state Public Utility Commission per regulatory requirements. Typically true for sustained interruptions affecting a threshold number of customers.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reliability event record was first created in the data warehouse. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reliability event record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  repair_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the repair work performed by field crew to restore service, including equipment replaced, temporary repairs, or permanent fixes.']
  restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when service was fully restored to all affected customers. Used to calculate event duration and customer-minutes interrupted.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts (kV) of the distribution circuit affected by the event (e.g., 12.47, 13.2, 34.5). Used for voltage-class reliability analysis.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for distribution reliability interruption events used to calculate IEEE 1366 reliability indices (SAIDI, SAIFI, CAIDI, MAIFI) for PUC reporting. Captures event identifier, event type (sustained/momentary/planned), cause code per IEEE 1366 Appendix (equipment failure/weather/animal/vegetation/scheduled/unknown), affected feeder and segment, number of customers interrupted, customer-minutes interrupted (CMI), event start and restoration timestamps, duration (minutes), major event day (MED) threshold flag, and IEEE 1366 2.5-beta exclusion flag. SSOT for PUC reliability reporting and IEEE 1366 index calculation. Supports annual reliability filings, worst-performing feeder analysis, and reliability improvement program tracking.'
}

  event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this reading to an outage event if the reading was captured during or immediately after an outage. Null if no outage was active at reading time.']
  flisr_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this reading to a FLISR automation event if the reading was captured during an automated switching sequence. Null if no FLISR event was active.']
  forecast_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.model. Business justification: Feeder load readings are used to validate and calibrate forecast models — the accuracy assessment process (MAPE, RMSE tracking) requires linking actual feeder readings to the specific forecast model t']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: Actual feeder load readings must be compared against load forecasts for accuracy measurement and model calibration. Essential for forecast accuracy tracking, MAPE calculation, model validation, and co']
  load_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.load_zone. Business justification: Feeder load readings are the primary data source for load zone demand analysis, load balancing, and DMS/ADMS state estimation. Load zones are logical groupings of network segments used for load balanc']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the specific monitoring point on the feeder where the reading was captured (e.g., feeder head, midpoint sensor, tie point). Corresponds to SCADA tag or PI point identifier.']
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: Pre- and post-planned-outage load readings are required to verify restoration and confirm load levels before re-energization.   primary_feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution feeder or circuit segment where the load measurement was taken. Links to the feeder asset in the distribution network topology.']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.scada_point. Business justification: Feeder load readings are sourced from SCADA telemetry points. Linking to the source scada_point enables data lineage traceability, SCADA data quality validation, and PI historian tag reconciliation re']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event.
