Captures certificate serial number, generation source facility or DER, generation period, technology type, vintage year, issuing registry (WREGIS, M-RETS, PJM-GATS, NAR), certificate status (issued, transferred, retired, expired), retirement reason (RPS compliance, voluntary, export), and associated RPS obligation period. SSOT for REC inventory, lifecycle tracking, and compliance retirement within the renewable domain.'
}

Table renewable.rps_obligation {
  rps_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the RPS compliance obligation record. Primary key for the RPS obligation entity.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: RPS obligations are regulatory compliance obligations under state renewable portfolio standards. Links RPS-specific tracking to enterprise compliance framework. Essential for integrated compliance man']
  planning_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.planning_period. Business justification: RPS compliance obligations defined per planning period (annual or multi-year cycles). Planning period context essential for tracking obligation vintages, banking/borrowing rules, multi-year compliance']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: RPS obligations are calculated based on retail electricity sales within a specific service territory. Regulatory compliance teams must link each RPS obligation to its service territory for accurate ob']
  acp_exposure_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total financial exposure in dollars for Alternative Compliance Payments based on projected shortfall multiplied by ACP rate. Represents potential penalty cost if shortfall is not remedied.']
  acp_paid_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual Alternative Compliance Payment amount in dollars paid to the state for this compliance period. Null if no ACP has been paid.']
  acp_rate_per_mwh decimal(10,2) [note: 'Regulatory penalty rate per MWh for shortfall, expressed in dollars per MWh. This is the Alternative Compliance Payment rate that the utility must pay for each MWh of unmet RPS obligation.']
  compliance_deadline_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the utility must demonstrate full compliance with the RPS obligation for this period. Typically 3-6 months after the compliance period end date.']
  compliance_percentage_achieved decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current compliance percentage achieved based on RECs retired to date divided by required renewable MWh, expressed as a percentage. Indicates progress toward meeting the RPS obligation.']
  compliance_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the compliance period for this RPS obligation. Typically December 31 of the compliance year.']
  compliance_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the compliance period for this RPS obligation. Typically January 1 of the compliance year.']
  compliance_period_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which this RPS obligation applies. RPS compliance is typically measured annually or over multi-year periods.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the RPS obligation compliance. In_progress indicates the compliance period is active; compliant indicates full compliance achieved; shortfall indicates projected or actual deficit; surplus indicates excess compliance; filed indicates compliance report submitted; under_review indicates regulatory review in progress.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RPS obligation record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Status of the regulatory compliance filing to the state PUC. Not_filed indicates no filing yet; draft indicates filing in preparation; submitted indicates filing delivered to regulator; accepted indicates regulator approval; rejected indicates regulator rejection; amended indicates revised filing submitted.']
  filing_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the RPS compliance filing was submitted to the state PUC. Null if not yet filed.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code identifying the regulatory jurisdiction imposing the RPS obligation (e.g., CA for California, NY for New York, MA for Massachusetts).']
  last_reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent reconciliation of the RPS compliance position. Indicates when RECs retired, inventory, and shortfall/surplus calculations were last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, assumptions, or special circumstances related to this RPS obligation. May include details on carve-out strategies, banking decisions, or regulatory correspondence.']
  obligated_retail_sales_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total retail electricity sales in MWh subject to the RPS obligation for this compliance period. This is the base quantity against which the renewable percentage requirement is applied.']
  offshore_wind_carveout_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Absolute quantity of offshore wind energy in MWh required to meet the offshore wind carve-out. Null if no offshore wind carve-out applies.']
  offshore_wind_carveout_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Technology-specific carve-out percentage requiring a minimum portion of the RPS obligation to be met with offshore wind generation. Null if no offshore wind carve-out applies.']
  projected_shortfall_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Projected shortfall in MWh between the required renewable MWh and the sum of RECs retired plus RECs in inventory. Positive value indicates a deficit; null or zero indicates compliance is on track or surplus exists.']
  projected_surplus_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Projected surplus in MWh beyond the required renewable MWh. Positive value indicates excess RECs available for banking or future compliance periods; null or zero indicates no surplus.']
  reconciliation_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the RPS compliance position is reconciled and updated. Indicates how often the running position is refreshed.']
  recs_inventory_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of RECs in MWh currently held in inventory and available for retirement toward this compliance period. Represents banked RECs not yet applied.']
  recs_retired_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of RECs in MWh that have been retired to date for this compliance period. This is the running compliance position representing actual compliance achieved.']
  required_renewable_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Absolute quantity of renewable energy in MWh required to meet the RPS obligation. Calculated as obligated_retail_sales_mwh multiplied by required_renewable_percentage.']
  required_renewable_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mandated percentage of retail sales that must be served by renewable energy sources for this compliance period. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 33.00 for 33%).']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal business unit or individual responsible for managing this RPS obligation and ensuring compliance.']
  rps_rec_retirement varchar [note: 'FK to renewable.rec_certificate.rec_certificate_id — RPS obligations are satisfied by retiring RECs. The compliance position tracks RECs retired against the obligation. This FK is essential for compliance reconciliation and regulatory reporting.']
  solar_carveout_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Absolute quantity of solar energy in MWh required to meet the solar carve-out. Null if no solar carve-out applies.']
  solar_carveout_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Technology-specific carve-out percentage requiring a minimum portion of the RPS obligation to be met with solar generation. Null if no solar carve-out applies.']
  storage_carveout_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Absolute quantity of energy storage in MWh required to meet the storage carve-out. Null if no storage carve-out applies.']
  storage_carveout_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Technology-specific carve-out percentage requiring a minimum portion of the RPS obligation to be met with energy storage systems. Null if no storage carve-out applies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RPS obligation record was last modified. Audit field for data lineage and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance obligation and running position record per compliance period and state jurisdiction. Captures the regulatory target (obligated retail sales MWh, required renewable percentage, technology-specific carve-outs for solar/offshore wind/storage, ACP rate) and the running compliance position against that target (RECs retired to date, RECs in inventory, projected shortfall or surplus MWh, compliance percentage achieved, last reconciliation timestamp). Includes compliance deadline, filing status, and alternative compliance payment exposure. SSOT for RPS obligation targets AND compliance position tracking within the renewable domain — no separate position product exists. Feeds regulatory filings to state PUCs and internal compliance dashboards.'
}

Table renewable.ppa_contract {
  ppa_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ppa_contract']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the independent power producer (IPP), project developer, or renewable energy supplier that is the counterparty to this PPA contract.']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: A PPA contract is executed for a specific renewable generation facility or DER. The ppa_contract currently captures generation_source as a free-text STRING, but lacks a structured FK to the der_regist']
  path_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.path. Business justification: PPA contracts for renewable energy delivery require a FERC OATT transmission path to move energy from source to sink. The path determines ATC availability, congestion exposure, and FTR hedging require']
  planning_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.planning_period. Business justification: PPA contracts span multi-year terms aligned with utility planning periods for capacity procurement, IRP filings, and regulatory cost recovery. Planning period context essential for contract portfolio ']
  pnode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.pnode. Business justification: PPA contracts are settled at a specific pricing node (pnode) for LMP-based energy settlement and congestion cost attribution. FERC OATT and ISO/RTO market rules require identifying the delivery pnode.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: PPAs often require FERC or state PUC approval filings. Utilities must track which regulatory filing authorized each contract for compliance and audit purposes. Standard practice in regulated markets f']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: PPA contracts are subject to the regulatory jurisdiction of the service territory where energy is delivered. Territory assignment drives RPS compliance applicability, FERC/state regulatory filing requ']
  base_price_per_mwh decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base energy price in USD per MWh at contract inception, before escalation or indexing adjustments. Business-confidential pricing term.']
  capacity_factor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected or contracted capacity factor (ratio of actual output to maximum possible output) expressed as a percentage, used for forecasting and performance evaluation.']
  contract_amendment_count varchar [note: 'Number of amendments or modifications made to the original PPA contract since execution.']
  contract_execution_date date [note: 'Date when the PPA contract was signed and executed by both parties.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the PPA contract, typically referencing the project or facility name.']
  contract_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to the PPA contract.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the PPA contract, used in legal documents, regulatory filings, and counterparty communications.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PPA contract: draft (under negotiation), pending approval (awaiting internal or regulatory approval), active (in force), suspended (temporarily halted), terminated (ended early), or expired (reached end of term).']
  contract_term_years varchar [note: 'Total duration of the PPA contract in years from effective start date to effective end date.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PPA pricing structure: fixed price (flat rate), indexed (tied to market index), escalating (annual increases), hybrid (combination), or tolling (capacity payment plus energy).']
  contracted_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum generation capacity in megawatts (MW) that the counterparty is obligated to make available under this PPA contract.']
  contracted_energy_volume_mwh_annual decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total annual energy volume in megawatt-hours (MWh) that the utility commits to purchase under this PPA contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PPA contract record was first created in the system.']
  curtailment_compensation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty is entitled to compensation for curtailed energy (True) or not (False).']
  curtailment_provision varchar [note: 'Contractual terms allowing the utility to curtail (reduce or halt) energy delivery from the renewable facility due to grid constraints, oversupply, or operational needs, including compensation or penalty terms.']
  delivery_point_poi varchar [note: 'Physical location or substation where energy is delivered from the renewable facility to the utility grid, also known as the Point of Interconnection (POI).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the PPA contract term expires and energy delivery obligations cease. Nullable for open-ended or evergreen contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the PPA contract becomes legally binding and energy delivery obligations commence.']
  ferc_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or docket ID for the FERC filing associated with this PPA contract, if applicable for wholesale market transactions.']
  force_majeure_clause varchar [note: 'Legal clause defining events beyond the control of either party (natural disasters, regulatory changes, grid failures) that excuse non-performance without penalty.']
  generation_source varchar [note: 'Type of renewable energy resource covered by this PPA: solar (photovoltaic or thermal), wind (onshore or offshore), hydro (run-of-river or reservoir), battery storage (energy storage system), biomass, or geothermal.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or modification to the PPA contract. Nullable if no amendments have been made.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PPA contract record was last updated in the system.']
  minimum_delivery_obligation_mwh decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum annual energy volume in MWh that the counterparty must deliver to avoid penalties or contract breach.']
  performance_guarantee_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual terms specifying performance guarantees, including availability targets, capacity factor commitments, and associated liquidated damages or penalties for underperformance.']
  price_escalation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual percentage rate at which the energy price escalates over the contract term. Null for fixed-price contracts. Business-confidential pricing term.']
  price_index_reference varchar [note: 'Name of the market index or benchmark to which the PPA price is tied (e.g., Locational Marginal Price (LMP) at specific PNode, CPI, natural gas index). Null for fixed-price contracts.']
  rec_ownership varchar [note: 'Specifies which party owns the Renewable Energy Certificates (RECs) generated by the facility: utility (utility retains RECs for RPS compliance), counterparty (IPP retains RECs), shared (split between parties), or unbundled (RECs sold separately).']
  rec_transfer_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed terms governing the transfer, retirement, and tracking of RECs, including timing, registry requirements, and compliance obligations.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the PPA contract received required regulatory approval from the Public Utility Commission (PUC) or other governing body. Nullable if approval is not required.']
  renewable_attribute_transfer varchar [note: 'Detailed terms governing the transfer of renewable energy attributes (environmental benefits, carbon offsets, green tags) beyond RECs, including timing and compliance obligations.']
  rps_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PPA contract contributes to the utility\'s Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance obligations (True) or not (False).']
  rps_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State or regulatory jurisdiction whose RPS requirements this PPA contract helps satisfy (e.g., California, New York, Texas).']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which energy delivery and payments are settled between the utility and counterparty: monthly, quarterly, or annual.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Contractual terms under which either party may terminate the PPA early, including notice periods, breach conditions, and financial penalties.']

  Note: 'Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) master record for renewable energy procurement contracts between the utility and independent power producers (IPPs) or project developers. Captures counterparty, contracted capacity (MW), energy volume (MWh/year), contract term, pricing structure (fixed, indexed, escalating), delivery point (POI/PNode), REC ownership and transfer terms, curtailment provisions, force majeure clauses, FERC filing reference, and contract status. SSOT boundary: scoped ONLY to renewable-source PPAs where REC ownership, renewable attributes, and RPS compliance are integral contract terms. Conventional/thermal PPAs, tolling agreements, and wholesale trading contracts are managed in the trading domain. Cross-domain SSOT adjudication pending with global architect.'
}

Table renewable.ppa_settlement {
  ppa_settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ppa_settlement']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the counterparty entity (renewable generator, independent power producer, or purchasing utility) involved in this PPA settlement.']
  curtailment_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.curtailment_event. Business justification: ppa_settlement captures curtailment_credit_amount and curtailment_mwh as financial settlement line items, but has no FK to the specific curtailment_event that triggered the credit. Adding curtailment_']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the renewable generation facility (wind farm, solar array, hydro plant, battery storage) that produced the generation for this settlement.']
  energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.energy_price. Business justification: PPA settlements reference market price forecasts for imbalance charge calculation, curtailment credit valuation, and contract performance assessment. Required for accurate financial settlement and rep']
  force_majeure_outage_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: PPA settlements account for curtailment and unavailability caused by grid outages under force majeure provisions. Counterparties dispute settlement amounts when outages prevent contracted delivery. Re']
  forecast_renewable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.forecast_renewable. Business justification: Settlement reconciliation compares actual generation against renewable forecast that informed dispatch and pricing. Variance analysis between settlement actuals and forecast drives curtailment credits']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter. Business justification: PPA settlements require metered generation from specific revenue meters at generation facilities. Monthly PPA invoice reconciliation and dispute resolution link settlement calculations to source meter']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: PPA financial settlement calculations require the settlement meter point for LMP pricing node identification and grid topology reference. ppa_settlement has meter_id but meter_point carries lmp_price_']
  original_settlement_ppa_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original settlement record if this is an adjustment settlement. Null for original settlements.']
  power_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to generation.power_plant. Business justification: PPA settlements require metered generation data from the renewable power plant to calculate invoice amounts, reconcile contracted vs. actual delivery, and resolve disputes over curtailment credits or ']
  primary_ppa_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent renewable PPA contract under which this settlement is executed.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: PPA settlements for utility-owned generation reference the physical asset for cost allocation, depreciation matching, and FERC compliance reporting. Asset-specific PPAs require direct linkage for regu']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: PPA settlements often reference regulatory filings for cost recovery, rate base treatment, and prudency review. Utilities file settlement data with regulators to justify purchased power costs in rate ']
  rps_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.rps_obligation. Business justification: ppa_settlement has rps_compliance_year (INT) as a text/numeric field but no structured FK to the rps_obligation record for that compliance year. Adding rps_obligation_id enables direct linkage between']
  adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this settlement record is an adjustment to a previously finalized settlement.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for the settlement adjustment, such as meter data correction, contract amendment, or billing error correction.']
  ancillary_services_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Charges in dollars for ancillary services (regulation, spinning reserves, voltage support) allocated to this settlement period.']
  availability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Facility availability percentage during the settlement period, representing the proportion of time the facility was available for generation.']
  capacity_factor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Capacity factor percentage for the renewable facility during the settlement period, calculated as actual generation divided by maximum potential generation.']
  contracted_price_per_mwh decimal(12,4) [note: 'The contracted unit price in dollars per MWh as specified in the PPA contract for this settlement period. May vary by time-of-use or seasonal schedules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this settlement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this settlement record.']
  curtailment_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial credit or compensation in dollars for curtailed generation as specified in the PPA contract curtailment provisions.']
  curtailment_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total energy curtailed during the settlement period due to grid constraints, economic dispatch, or operational requirements, measured in megawatt-hours.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this settlement is under dispute by either party.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for dispute if the dispute flag is true. Null if no dispute exists.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'Date the dispute was resolved, if applicable. Null if dispute is unresolved or no dispute exists.']
  gross_settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross financial settlement amount in dollars calculated as metered generation multiplied by contracted price, before adjustments.']
  imbalance_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial charge or credit in dollars for deviations between scheduled and actual generation, calculated per RTO/ISO imbalance settlement rules.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was issued for this settlement.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number issued by the renewable generator or counterparty for this settlement period.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this settlement record was last modified.']
  metered_generation_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total metered energy generation delivered during the settlement period, measured in megawatt-hours. Source of truth for settlement calculations.']
  net_settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net financial settlement amount in dollars after all adjustments, credits, and charges. This is the final payable or receivable amount.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Actual date payment was made or received for this settlement.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is due per the PPA contract payment terms.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Payment transaction reference number from the financial system for traceability and reconciliation.']
  rec_quantity_transferred varchar [note: 'Number of RECs transferred to the purchasing utility as part of this settlement, typically one REC per MWh of renewable generation.']
  rec_tracking_system_code varchar [note: 'External identifier from the REC tracking system registry confirming the REC transfer transaction.']
  rec_transfer_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether REC transfer has been confirmed in the applicable REC tracking system (e.g., WREGIS, M-RETS, NEPOOL GIS).']
  rps_compliance_year varchar [note: 'The compliance year for which this settlement and associated RECs count toward the utility\'s RPS obligations.']
  settlement_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement was approved for invoicing and payment.']
  settlement_calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement calculation was executed by the energy trading and risk management system.']
  settlement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this settlement, including special circumstances, operational issues, or contractual clarifications.']
  settlement_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the settlement transaction, typically used in invoicing and payment reconciliation.']
  settlement_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the settlement period for which generation and financial calculations apply.']
  settlement_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the settlement period for which generation and financial calculations apply.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the settlement record in the settlement-to-payment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|calculated|invoiced|paid|disputed|adjusted|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transmission_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transmission service charges in dollars allocated to this settlement period for delivery of renewable generation to the point of interconnection or delivery point.']

  Note: 'Monthly or periodic financial settlement record for each active renewable PPA contract. Captures settlement period, metered generation (MWh), contracted price ($/MWh), settlement amount ($), curtailment adjustments (MWh and $), imbalance charges, REC transfer confirmation, invoice reference, payment status, and dispute flags. SSOT boundary: scoped ONLY to renewable PPA settlements where REC transfer and curtailment credit calculations are integral. Wholesale market settlements and conventional contract settlements are managed in the trading domain. Cross-domain SSOT adjudication pending with global architect.'
}

Table renewable.curtailment_event {
  curtailment_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the curtailment event record. Primary key for this product.']
  causing_transmission_outage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_outage. Business justification: Transmission line or substation outages directly cause renewable generation curtailment when the outage isolates generation or creates post-contingency overloads requiring dispatch reduction. Linking ']
  contingency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.contingency. Business justification: Renewable curtailments triggered by specific contingency analysis results (thermal overloads, voltage violations). Operators need traceability from curtailment instruction to triggering contingency fo']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Curtailment instructions originate from control area operators managing transmission congestion, voltage violations, and reliability constraints. Required for NERC compliance reporting and operational']
  curtailment_meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: Curtailment events are associated with specific meter points for grid topology analysis, LMP-based curtailment compensation calculation, and settlement. curtailment_event has meter_id but meter_point ']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_substation. Business justification: Curtailment events driven by substation-level capacity constraints require direct substation link for capacity planning, thermal limit tracking, and investment prioritization. Standard practice in DER']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: Curtailment events are often triggered by outages (transmission constraints, distribution faults requiring generation reduction). Operators distinguish economic curtailment from reliability-driven cur']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.feeder. Business justification: Curtailment events must track the specific feeder experiencing grid constraints for reliability reporting (SAIDI/SAIFI impact), hosting capacity analysis, and operational constraint management. Essent']
  forecast_renewable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.forecast_renewable. Business justification: Curtailment events are compared against renewable generation forecasts to assess forecast accuracy, grid constraint prediction, and curtailment risk modeling. Essential for operational and financial p']
  load_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.load_zone. Business justification: Curtailment events triggered by distribution load zone thermal or voltage constraints require load zone attribution for VVO coordination and curtailment reporting. Load zone-level curtailment analysis']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter. Business justification: Curtailment compliance verification requires metered output data before/during/after curtailment instruction. Curtailment compensation calculation and performance verification link event to meter read']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Curtailment events may be mandated by compliance obligations (environmental limits, reliability standards, NERC directives). Tracking the driving obligation justifies curtailment decisions and support']
  power_plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the renewable generation facility (wind farm, solar array, battery storage, hydroelectric plant) affected by this curtailment event.']
  ppa_contract_id varchar [note: 'FK to renewable.ppa_contract.ppa_contract_id — Curtailment events affecting PPA-contracted facilities need to reference the PPA contract for curtailment credit calculations and settlement adjustments.']
  primary_der_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to renewable.der_registry.der_registry_id — Curtailment events must reference the affected facility/DER. Essential for PPA curtailment credit calculations and lost revenue tracking.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Grid-directed curtailments of utility-scale renewable assets (wind farms, solar plants) tracked against physical assets for compensation calculations, reliability reporting, and transmission constrain']
  rps_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.rps_obligation. Business justification: curtailment_event has rps_compliance_impact_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating the event affects RPS compliance, but lacks a structured FK to the specific rps_obligation record impacted. Adding rps_obligation_']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.scada_point. Business justification: Curtailment execution monitored via SCADA points for real-time compliance verification and response time measurement. Operators track actual MW reduction against instruction via SCADA telemetry for se']
  tertiary_der_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to renewable.der_registry.der_registry_id — Curtailment events reference the affected DER or facility. FK to der_registry enables curtailment impact analysis per asset.']
  transmission_constraint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific transmission constraint or flowgate that caused the curtailment, as defined by the ISO/RTO.']
  actual_output_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The actual generation output in megawatts (MW) delivered during the curtailment period after reduction.']
  available_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The generation capacity in megawatts (MW) that was available from the resource at the time of curtailment, based on weather conditions and equipment status.']
  compliance_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this curtailment event must be included in regulatory compliance reports to FERC, state PUC, or other governing bodies.']
  congestion_zone varchar [note: 'The transmission congestion zone or pricing node (PNode) associated with the curtailment event, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this curtailment event record was first created in the system.']
  curtailed_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The amount of generation capacity in megawatts (MW) that was curtailed or reduced during the event.']
  curtailed_energy_mwh decimal(15,3) [note: 'The total energy volume in megawatt-hours (MWh) that was not generated due to the curtailment event, calculated as the difference between available and actual generation over the curtailment duration.']
  curtailment_credit_amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'The financial credit or compensation amount in US dollars owed to the facility owner for this curtailment event, as calculated per PPA terms.']
  curtailment_credit_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this curtailment event qualifies for financial compensation or credit under the applicable PPA or regulatory framework.']
  curtailment_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the curtailment event in minutes, calculated from start to end timestamp.']
  curtailment_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the curtailment event ended and generation was restored to available capacity. Null if curtailment is still active.']
  curtailment_event_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number for the curtailment event, used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  curtailment_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the specific reason for the curtailment event, including operational context and decision rationale.']
  curtailment_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the curtailment event began and generation output was reduced below available capacity.']
  curtailment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the curtailment event: initiated (instruction issued), active (curtailment in effect), restored (generation resumed), cancelled (instruction withdrawn), or expired (time limit reached).']
  curtailment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the curtailment reason: economic (negative pricing, market conditions), reliability (grid stability), transmission constraint (congestion), regulatory (compliance mandate), environmental (permit limits), or operational (equipment limitation).']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this curtailment event data (e.g., DERMS, ADMS, SCADA, EMS).']
  estimated_lost_revenue_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'The estimated financial impact in US dollars of the curtailed energy, calculated using applicable market prices or PPA rates at the time of curtailment.']
  forecast_accuracy_variance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage variance between forecasted and actual available generation capacity at the time of curtailment, used for forecast model improvement.']
  grid_constraint_type varchar [note: 'The specific grid constraint or limitation that triggered the curtailment: transmission congestion, voltage limit, frequency deviation, thermal overload, stability limit, or none if not constraint-driven.']
  instruction_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the curtailment instruction: ISO/RTO dispatch, ADMS (Advanced Distribution Management System), DERMS (Distributed Energy Resource Management System), manual operator, market signal, or automatic control system.']
  instruction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the curtailment instruction was issued to the facility or DER operator.']
  iso_rto_code varchar [note: 'The ISO or RTO jurisdiction under which this curtailment event occurred: CAISO, ERCOT, MISO, PJM, SPP, NYISO, ISO-NE, or OTHER. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CAISO|ERCOT|MISO|PJM|SPP|NYISO|ISONE|OTHER — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this curtailment event record was last updated or modified.']
  market_price_per_mwh decimal(10,2) [note: 'The locational marginal price (LMP) or applicable market price per megawatt-hour at the time and location of the curtailment event.']
  notification_lead_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The number of minutes of advance notice provided to the facility operator before curtailment execution.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether advance notification was sent to the facility operator or DER owner prior to curtailment execution.']
  post_event_review_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a post-event operational review has been completed to analyze the curtailment decision and outcomes.']
  ppa_rate_per_mwh decimal(10,2) [note: 'The contracted power purchase agreement rate per megawatt-hour applicable to this facility, used for curtailment credit calculations.']
  rec_adjustment_mwh decimal(15,3) [note: 'The adjustment in megawatt-hours to REC generation accounting due to this curtailment event, if applicable.']
  rec_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this curtailment event impacts the generation and issuance of renewable energy certificates (RECs) for the affected facility.']
  restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when full generation capacity was confirmed restored following the curtailment event.']
  review_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of findings from the post-event review, including lessons learned and recommended process improvements.']
  rps_compliance_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this curtailment event affects the utility\'s renewable portfolio standard compliance calculations or reporting obligations.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Description of the weather conditions at the time of curtailment (e.g., high wind, peak solar irradiance, low hydro flow) that influenced available capacity.']

  Note: 'Record of each renewable generation curtailment event where a facility or DER output is intentionally reduced below available capacity. Captures curtailment start/end timestamp, affected DER or facility reference, curtailment type (economic, reliability, transmission constraint, regulatory), instruction source (ISO/RTO, ADMS, operator), curtailed energy volume (MWh), estimated lost revenue ($), associated grid constraint or congestion zone, and restoration timestamp. SSOT for renewable curtailment tracking within this domain. Supports FERC compliance reporting, PPA curtailment credit calculations, REC generation impact analysis, and grid operations post-event review.'
}

Table renewable.nem_account {
  nem_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the NEM account record. Primary key for the NEM account entity.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account enrolled in the NEM program. Links to the billing customer account master.']
  customer_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_service_agreement. Business justification: The customer_service_agreement governs the rate schedule, service class, and NEM agreement type for a NEM customer. A direct FK from nem_account to customer_service_agreement is essential for billing ']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: NEM accounts originate from interconnection applications; utilities track which application enabled NEM enrollment for regulatory compliance and billing. Replaces denormalized application_number text ']
  interconnection_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: NEM accounts reference utility-owned interconnection equipment (service transformers, meter sockets, service panel upgrades) capitalized by the utility. Links NEM program costs to rate base assets for']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure) meter measuring bidirectional energy flow for the NEM account. Links to the meter registry for interval data and device metadata.']
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: NEM customers are notified of planned outages affecting BTM system export capability and billing credits. Utilities track planned outages for NEM customer communication and credit adjustment per tarif']
  primary_der_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to renewable.der_registry.der_registry_id — NEM accounts are tied to specific BTM DER installations. The NEM account must reference the DER registry entry for the BTM system generating the credits.']
  annual_true_up_date date [note: 'Anniversary date when cumulative NEM credits are reconciled. Any remaining credit balance may be paid out, rolled over, or forfeited based on program rules.']
  battery_storage_capacity_kwh decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total energy storage capacity of paired battery system in kilowatt-hours. Null if no battery is installed. Used for SGIP (Self-Generation Incentive Program) eligibility and VPP dispatch.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the current NEM billing period. Marks the cutoff for credit calculation and ledger posting.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the current NEM billing period for which credit transactions are recorded. Aligns with the customer billing cycle.']
  btm_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of distributed energy resource installed behind the customer meter. Identifies the generation technology for capacity planning and forecasting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the NEM account record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which unused NEM credits expire if not applied or paid out. Typically aligns with the annual true-up date for programs with credit expiration policies.']
  cumulative_credit_balance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Running total of NEM credits available to offset future charges. Accumulates across billing periods until the annual true-up date.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the customer was officially enrolled in the NEM program and began receiving export credits. Marks the start of the NEM billing cycle.']
  export_credit_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar value of NEM credits earned during the billing period, calculated as net_export_kwh multiplied by export_rate_per_kwh. Posted to the cumulative credit balance.']
  export_limit_kw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum instantaneous power export allowed to the grid in kilowatts. May be lower than installed capacity due to interconnection constraints or grid hosting capacity limits.']
  export_rate_per_kwh decimal(10,6) [note: 'Compensation rate in dollars per kWh for energy exported to the grid. Rate varies by NEM program type and may differ from retail rate. Used to calculate export credit value.']
  gross_consumption_kwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total energy consumed by the customer from the grid during the billing period, measured in kilowatt-hours. Does not net out BTM generation.']
  gross_generation_kwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total energy generated by the BTM system during the billing period, measured in kilowatt-hours. Includes both self-consumed and exported energy.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the BTM generation system was physically installed and commissioned. Used for warranty tracking and depreciation calculations.']
  installed_capacity_kw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nameplate capacity of the BTM generation system in kilowatts. Used for interconnection limits, export calculations, and grid impact analysis.']
  installer_company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the contractor or company that installed the BTM generation system. Used for quality assurance and warranty claims.']
  installer_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued contractor license number for the installer. Verifies that installation was performed by a certified professional.']
  inverter_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the inverter. Used for equipment certification verification and technical support.']
  inverter_model varchar [note: 'Model number of the inverter installed. Used to verify compliance with interconnection standards and grid support functions.']
  inverter_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number of the primary inverter converting DC generation to AC for grid export. Used for equipment tracking, warranty claims, and performance monitoring.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent utility or third-party inspection of the BTM system. Used to verify ongoing compliance with safety and interconnection standards.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the NEM account record. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified the record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  nem_program_type varchar [note: 'Specific NEM tariff program under which the customer is enrolled. Determines export credit rates, true-up rules, and credit expiration policies. NEM 1.0 offers full retail rate credits; NEM 2.0 includes non-bypassable charges; NEM 3.0/NEM-A uses export compensation rates below retail.']
  net_export_kwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net energy exported to the grid during the billing period after self-consumption. Positive value indicates surplus generation sent to the grid. Basis for export credit calculation.']
  net_import_kwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net energy imported from the grid during the billing period after accounting for BTM generation. Positive value indicates net consumption from the grid.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection of the BTM system. Used for compliance tracking and maintenance planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special conditions, or exceptions related to the NEM account. Used by customer service and billing operations.']
  permission_to_operate_date date [note: 'Date the utility granted permission for the BTM system to operate and export to the grid. Marks the official start of interconnection and may differ from enrollment date.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the NEM account. Active accounts receive export credits; suspended accounts are temporarily paused; terminated accounts are closed.']
  rec_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether generation from this NEM account is eligible for REC issuance. True if the system meets RPS (Renewable Portfolio Standard) eligibility criteria.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the NEM account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts. Marks the end of credit accrual and triggers final true-up processing.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for NEM account termination. Used for program analytics and customer retention analysis.']
  true_up_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of credit adjustment applied during the annual true-up reconciliation. May represent payout, rollover, or forfeiture of remaining credits based on program rules.']
  vpp_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NEM account is enrolled in a VPP aggregation program for demand response or grid services. True if the customer has opted into VPP dispatch.']

  Note: 'Net Energy Metering (NEM) account master record and full credit transaction ledger for customers with Behind-the-Meter (BTM) generation. Captures customer account reference, interconnection application number, BTM system type (solar PV, battery+solar, wind), installed capacity (kW), NEM program type (NEM 1.0, NEM 2.0, NEM 3.0/NEM-A), enrollment date, export rate ($/kWh), annual true-up date, and program status. Includes all billing-period credit transactions: gross consumption/generation (kWh), net import/export (kWh), export credit value ($), cumulative credit balance ($), true-up adjustments, and credit expiry dates. SSOT for NEM program enrollment, credit ledger, and annual true-up processing — no separate credit transaction product exists. Credit summaries feed into the billing domain for invoice generation.'
}

Table renewable.interconnection_queue {
  interconnection_queue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for interconnection_queue']
  capacity_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.capacity_requirement. Business justification: Queue projects in advanced stages (facilities study complete, agreement executed) contribute to meeting future capacity requirements. Capacity planning models track queue progression to assess resourc']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.feeder. Business justification: Interconnection queue applications target a specific distribution feeder POI. Feeder hosting capacity studies, impact assessments, and queue cluster studies require direct feeder assignment to evaluat']
  pnode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.pnode. Business justification: ISO/RTO interconnection studies assign a pnode to each new generation project upon study completion. This pnode assignment is a standard interconnection process outcome required for market registratio']
  poi_transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_substation. Business justification: Every interconnection application identifies a specific transmission substation as the point of interconnection (POI). System impact and facilities studies are conducted at this substation. A domain e']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Queue projects assigned to forecast zones for aggregate capacity modeling, transmission constraint analysis, and zone-level generation buildout in IRP studies. Zone assignment drives network upgrade c']
  affected_systems_coordination_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interconnection requires coordination with adjacent transmission systems or neighboring utilities due to potential impacts on their facilities.']
  applicant_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the applicant or project developer for interconnection study communications and milestone notifications.']
  applicant_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the applicant or project developer for interconnection coordination and study discussions.']
  applicant_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity or developer submitting the interconnection request for the renewable generation or storage project.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the interconnection request assigned by the utility or ISO/RTO. Used in regulatory filings and customer communications.']
  application_received_date date [note: 'Date the interconnection application was received and accepted by the utility or ISO/RTO, establishing the queue position and study timeline.']
  cluster_study_group varchar [note: 'Identifier for the cluster study group if this application is being studied as part of a batch of interconnection requests in the same geographic area or queue window.']
  commercial_readiness_deposit_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the applicant has posted the commercial readiness deposit or financial security required under FERC Order 2023 to demonstrate project viability and reduce speculative queue entries.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the country where the proposed facility will be located. Primarily USA, CAN (Canada), or MEX (Mexico) for North American interconnection queues.']
  county varchar [note: 'County where the proposed renewable generation or storage facility will be located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interconnection queue record was first created in the system.']
  environmental_permit_status varchar [note: 'Status of environmental permitting for the renewable project, including federal and state environmental reviews, endangered species assessments, and wetlands permits.']
  expected_commercial_operation_date date [note: 'Target date for the renewable facility to achieve commercial operation and begin energy delivery, as declared by the applicant.']
  facilities_study_completion_date date [note: 'Date the facilities study was completed and results delivered to the applicant. Null if study is in progress or not started.']
  facilities_study_start_date date [note: 'Date the facilities study phase commenced. Null if study has not yet started.']
  feasibility_study_completion_date date [note: 'Date the feasibility study was completed and results delivered to the applicant. Null if study is in progress or not started.']
  feasibility_study_start_date date [note: 'Date the feasibility study phase commenced. Null if study has not yet started.']
  ferc_oatt_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interconnection request is being processed under FERC OATT pro forma procedures for wholesale generation interconnection. True for bulk/wholesale projects; False for retail/distribution interconnections.']
  generation_capacity_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Requested nameplate generation capacity of the renewable facility in megawatts. For hybrid projects, represents the generation component only.']
  interconnection_agreement_executed_date date [note: 'Date the final interconnection agreement was executed between the applicant and the utility or ISO/RTO. Null if agreement not yet signed.']
  interconnection_facilities_cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in dollars for direct interconnection facilities such as gen-tie lines, transformers, and switchgear required to connect the facility to the POI.']
  interconnection_service_type varchar [note: 'Type of interconnection service requested under FERC OATT. Energy Resource allows injection but no delivery guarantee; Network Resource includes firm transmission rights.']
  iso_rto_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Name of the ISO or RTO managing the interconnection queue if the project is in an organized wholesale market region (e.g., CAISO, PJM, MISO, ERCOT, NYISO, ISO-NE, SPP). Null for non-ISO utility territories.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interconnection queue record was last updated, reflecting changes to study phase, status, or milestone dates.']
  network_upgrade_cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in dollars for required transmission network upgrades to accommodate the interconnection, as determined by system impact and facilities studies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special conditions, or coordination notes related to the interconnection request and study process.']
  poi_voltage_kv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Transmission or distribution voltage level in kilovolts at the proposed point of interconnection. Determines study complexity and upgrade requirements.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Business name or designation of the proposed renewable generation or storage facility as provided by the applicant.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the renewable generation or storage technology proposed for interconnection. Hybrid projects combine generation and storage at the same POI. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: solar|wind|battery_storage|hybrid_solar_storage|hybrid_wind_storage|hydroelectric|other_renewable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  queue_position varchar [note: 'Sequential position in the interconnection queue determining study priority and processing order. Lower numbers indicate earlier queue entry and higher priority.']
  queue_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interconnection queue entry. Active indicates ongoing study; Withdrawn indicates applicant termination; Approved indicates studies complete and agreement executed; In Service indicates facility is operational.']
  site_control_documentation_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the applicant has provided evidence of site control (land lease, purchase option, or ownership) as required for interconnection study progression under FERC Order 2023 reforms.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province where the proposed renewable generation or storage facility will be located.']
  storage_capacity_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Requested nameplate storage discharge capacity in megawatts for battery storage or hybrid projects. Null for generation-only projects.']
  storage_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Energy storage duration in hours at rated discharge capacity. Represents the time the battery can discharge at full power. Null for generation-only projects.']
  study_deposit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total deposit amount paid by the applicant to fund interconnection studies. Typically covers feasibility, system impact, and facilities study costs.']
  study_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the interconnection study process. Feasibility assesses viability, System Impact evaluates grid impacts, Facilities designs required upgrades.']
  system_impact_study_completion_date date [note: 'Date the system impact study was completed and results delivered to the applicant. Null if study is in progress or not started.']
  system_impact_study_start_date date [note: 'Date the system impact study phase commenced. Null if study has not yet started.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date the applicant withdrew the interconnection request from the queue. Null if application remains active or was approved.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason provided by the applicant for withdrawing the interconnection request, such as project economics, permitting issues, or site constraints.']

  Note: 'Queue management record for utility-scale and large renewable generation/storage interconnection requests submitted to the utility or ISO/RTO under FERC OATT. Captures queue position, applicant, project type (solar, wind, storage, hybrid), requested capacity (MW), proposed POI substation, study phase (feasibility, system impact, facilities), study fees, milestone dates, cluster study group, FERC OATT compliance status, and queue withdrawal/approval status. SSOT boundary: scoped ONLY to bulk/wholesale renewable generation interconnection queue entries managed as part of the renewable procurement pipeline. Customer-level BTM interconnection applications (residential solar, EV chargers, small DER) are managed in the interconnection domain. Cross-domain SSOT adjudication pending with global architect.'
}

Table renewable.vpp_configuration {
  vpp_configuration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Virtual Power Plant configuration record. Primary key.']
  aggregation_meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: VPP configurations require an aggregation meter point for ISO/RTO market settlement and dispatch verification. vpp_configuration has settlement_meter_id(FK→meter) but meter_point carries contracted_ca']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.aggregator']
  capacity_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.capacity_requirement. Business justification: VPPs provide dispatchable capacity that counts toward capacity requirements. Aggregated DER capacity (with availability/performance adjustments) is credited in resource adequacy calculations and ISO c']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: VPPs participate in control area AGC, regulation reserves, and ancillary services markets. Control area operators dispatch VPPs for frequency regulation and must track aggregated capacity for reserve ']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to renewable.der_registry.der_registry_id — VPP configurations aggregate multiple DERs. The VPP must reference participating DERs from the registry. This is a many-to-many relationship critical for VPP dispatch and settlement.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary distribution feeder circuit serving the majority of DERs in this VPP aggregation. Used for distribution-level constraint management.']
  forecast_renewable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.forecast_renewable. Business justification: VPPs aggregate DERs whose output is modeled in renewable forecasts for dispatch optimization, market bidding, and grid services. Forecast accuracy at VPP level drives ISO settlement, performance payme']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: VPPs provide demand response and load modulation. Load forecasts must account for VPP dispatch capability to avoid double-counting capacity and ensure accurate net load projections for resource adequa']
  load_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.load_zone. Business justification: VPP dispatch is coordinated within distribution load zones for volt-var optimization and demand management. VVO operators need to know which load zone a VPP configuration serves to coordinate DER disp']
  maintenance_planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: VPP system reconfigurations and software upgrades are scheduled during planned outage windows to avoid market dispatch conflicts. Utilities coordinate VPP maintenance with grid planned outages for ope']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: VPP programs must comply with ISO/RTO market rules, FERC Orders 841/2222, state DER regulations. Tracks which compliance obligation governs each VPP configuration. Essential for market participation a']
  pnode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.pnode. Business justification: Virtual power plants registered in ISO/RTO markets are assigned a pnode for aggregated energy settlement and dispatch. ISO/RTO market rules require a pnode assignment for VPP market participation, cap']
  settlement_meter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the revenue-quality meter or virtual meter used for settlement and billing of VPP dispatch performance in ISO/RTO markets.']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event. Business justification: VPPs are dispatched during storm events for emergency load relief and black start support. Utilities track VPP storm response for resilience planning and FEMA/NERC event reporting.']
  activation_window_end_time timestamp [note: 'Daily time-of-day (HH:MM format) when the VPP is no longer eligible for dispatch. Supports time-of-use constraints and operational availability windows.']
  activation_window_start_time timestamp [note: 'Daily time-of-day (HH:MM format) when the VPP becomes eligible for dispatch. Supports time-of-use constraints and operational availability windows.']
  aggregation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the VPP aggregation strategy: demand response (load reduction), storage dispatch (battery discharge/charge), solar curtailment (PV output reduction), wind curtailment, load shifting, or mixed DER (combination of multiple resource types).']
  average_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rolling average performance score (0-100 scale) across all dispatch events, calculated as the ratio of actual delivered capacity to requested capacity. Used for aggregator performance evaluation.']
  baseline_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to establish the counterfactual baseline for measuring VPP dispatch performance: customer baseline load (CBL), day-ahead forecast, historical average, or control group comparison.']
  capacity_payment_rate_per_mw_month decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monthly capacity payment rate in dollars per MW for maintaining the VPP in a dispatchable state, independent of actual dispatch events.']
  communication_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum acceptable communication latency in milliseconds between the DERMS platform and participating DER assets for dispatch signal transmission and acknowledgment. Critical for fast-response ancillary services.']
  compliance_certification_date date [note: 'Date on which the VPP configuration received compliance certification from the applicable ISO/RTO or Public Utility Commission (PUC) for market participation.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration or termination date of the VPP aggregation contract or program enrollment period. Null for open-ended contracts.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the VPP aggregation contract or program enrollment period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VPP configuration record was first created in the system.']
  dispatch_protocol varchar [note: 'Method by which dispatch commands are issued to the VPP: automated (DERMS-driven without human intervention), manual (operator-initiated), semi-automated (operator approval required), or api_driven (external system integration).']
  geographic_zone varchar [note: 'ISO/RTO pricing zone, load zone, or distribution service territory in which the VPP operates. Used for locational marginal pricing (LMP) and congestion management.']
  incentive_rate_per_mwh decimal(10,2) [note: 'Financial incentive rate in dollars per MWh paid to the VPP for successful dispatch performance and energy delivery.']
  iso_rto_market_code varchar [note: 'Unique market participant identifier assigned by the ISO/RTO for this VPP in wholesale energy, capacity, or ancillary services markets.']
  iso_rto_registration_status varchar [note: 'Registration status of the VPP with the applicable ISO or RTO for wholesale market participation: registered (approved and active), pending (application submitted), not registered (no application), expired (registration lapsed), or rejected (application denied).']
  last_dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent dispatch event issued to this VPP. Used for operational monitoring and performance tracking.']
  maximum_dispatch_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum duration in minutes that a single dispatch event can be sustained before requiring a rest period or recharge cycle (relevant for battery storage VPPs).']
  minimum_dispatch_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum duration in minutes that a dispatch event must be sustained once activated. Ensures operational stability and prevents rapid cycling of DER assets.']
  minimum_performance_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of requested dispatch capacity that must be delivered for the event to qualify for settlement payment. Typical values range from 70% to 90%.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VPP configuration record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or configuration comments related to this VPP aggregation.']
  participating_der_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual DER assets (solar arrays, battery storage units, demand response participants, etc.) currently enrolled and participating in this VPP aggregation.']
  penalty_rate_per_mwh decimal(10,2) [note: 'Financial penalty rate in dollars per MWh applied when the VPP fails to meet minimum performance thresholds during a dispatch event.']
  performance_measurement_interval_minutes varchar [note: 'Time interval in minutes over which VPP dispatch performance is measured and aggregated for settlement purposes (e.g., 5-minute, 15-minute, or hourly intervals).']
  ramp_rate_mw_per_minute decimal(10,3) [note: 'Rate at which the VPP can increase or decrease its aggregated output in megawatts per minute. Critical for frequency regulation and ramping services.']
  regulatory_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the state or federal regulatory program under which the VPP operates (e.g., California DRAM, NYISO DER Participation Model, PJM DER Aggregation).']
  telemetry_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'Frequency in seconds at which telemetry data is transmitted from participating DERs to the DERMS platform during active dispatch events.']
  telemetry_reporting_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time telemetry data (voltage, frequency, power output) from participating DERs is transmitted to the ISO/RTO or utility control center during dispatch events.']
  total_aggregated_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total nameplate capacity in megawatts (MW) of all Distributed Energy Resources (DERs) aggregated within this VPP portfolio. Represents the maximum dispatchable capacity under ideal conditions.']
  total_dispatch_events_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all dispatch events issued to this VPP since configuration inception. Used for performance analytics and settlement auditing.']
  voltage_class_kv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage class in kilovolts (kV) of the distribution system at which the VPP DERs are interconnected (e.g., 12.47 kV, 34.5 kV).']
  vpp_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or registration code for the VPP used in ISO/RTO market systems and DERMS platforms.']
  vpp_name varchar [note: 'Business name or designation of the Virtual Power Plant aggregation portfolio.']
  vpp_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the VPP configuration: active (operational and dispatchable), inactive (configured but not operational), suspended (temporarily disabled), pending approval (awaiting ISO/RTO or regulatory approval), decommissioned (retired), or testing (pilot phase).']

  Note: 'Virtual Power Plant (VPP) master record defining DER aggregation into a dispatchable virtual fleet, including complete dispatch event history. Captures VPP name, aggregation type (demand response, storage dispatch, solar curtailment), total aggregated capacity (MW), participating DER count, ISO/RTO registration status, DERMS program reference, dispatch protocol (automated/manual), communication latency requirements, and activation eligibility windows. Includes all dispatch event records: timestamp, dispatch trigger (ISO/RTO signal, operator command, automated rule), requested MW, actual MW response, response time, participating DER count, dispatch success rate, settlement-eligible energy (MWh), and performance score. SSOT for VPP portfolio management AND dispatch performance — no separate dispatch event product exists. Supports FERC Order 2222 market participation and settlement.'
}

  battery_storage_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the battery energy storage system (BESS) asset. Primary key for the battery storage asset master record.']

Separate depreciation tracking required for rate base treatment, tax op']

  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.ems_node. Business justification: Storage assets represented as EMS nodes for real-time dispatch, state estimation, and power flow modeling. Grid operators require EMS node representation for accurate network analysis, voltage control']

  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Battery storage assets are located within forecast zones for capacity planning, resource adequacy modeling, and dispatch optimization. Required for integrating storage into load and generation forecas']
