Required by manufacturers for warranty validity and by ISOs']
  maintenance_planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: Battery storage maintenance is scheduled as planned outages coordinated with grid operations. Utilities track battery planned outages for market availability reporting and capacity payment adjustments']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter. Business justification: Battery storage assets require revenue-grade metering for ISO/RTO settlement, RPS compliance, and performance monitoring. Direct FK from battery_storage_asset to its associated meter enables storage d']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: Battery storage assets are connected at specific meter points for grid settlement, dispatch management, and LMP-based arbitrage optimization. meter_point carries der_capacity_kw, contracted_capacity_k']
  planning_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.planning_period. Business justification: Storage assets planned and deployed within specific planning periods for capacity credit, renewable integration, and transmission deferral. Planning period context drives depreciation schedules, rate ']
  pnode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.pnode. Business justification: Battery storage assets participating in ISO/RTO markets are registered at a specific pnode for energy, capacity, and ancillary service settlement. Market registration and dispatch instructions referen']
  poi_transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_substation. Business justification: Battery storage assets connect to the grid at a specific transmission substation (POI). This link is required for interconnection studies, protection coordination, switching order management, and oper']
  primary_battery_der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Battery energy storage systems (BESS) are Distributed Energy Resources and must be registered in the DER registry. This FK establishes the authoritative link between the asset master record and its DE']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the renewable generation asset (solar, wind) with which this battery storage system is co-located, if applicable. Null if standalone storage.']
  vpp_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.vpp_configuration. Business justification: Battery storage assets are aggregated into VPP portfolios for coordinated dispatch to wholesale markets and grid services. VPP configuration defines dispatch protocols, telemetry requirements, and set']
  warranty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_warranty. Business justification: Battery warranties are complex (capacity guarantees, cycle limits, throughput warranties, degradation curves). Separate tracking required for performance-based warranty claims and lifecycle cost model']
  annual_om_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated annual operations and maintenance (O&M) cost for the battery storage system, measured in US dollars. Includes routine maintenance, monitoring, and performance optimization.']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the battery storage facility (e.g., \'Riverside Solar + Storage\', \'Downtown Grid Battery\').']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique asset tag or serial number assigned to the battery storage system for field identification and maintenance tracking.']
  capital_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total capital expenditure (CAPEX) for the battery storage system acquisition and installation, measured in US dollars. Includes equipment, installation, and interconnection costs.']
  charge_ramp_rate_mw_per_min decimal(8,3) [note: 'Maximum rate at which the battery storage system can increase its charging power, measured in megawatts per minute. Critical for grid frequency regulation and ancillary services.']
  co_location_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the battery storage system is co-located with a renewable generation asset (e.g., solar array, wind farm) for integrated dispatch and energy arbitrage.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the battery storage system was officially commissioned and placed into commercial operation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this battery storage asset record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  current_soc_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current real-time state of charge (SoC) percentage of the battery storage system. Updated continuously from SCADA or BMS integration.']
  cycle_count varchar [note: 'Total number of charge-discharge cycles the battery storage system has completed since commissioning. Key metric for tracking battery degradation and remaining useful life.']
  degradation_rate_percent_per_year decimal(5,3) [note: 'Annual capacity degradation rate of the battery storage system expressed as a percentage per year. Used for forecasting remaining useful life and replacement planning.']
  derms_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the battery storage system is integrated with the utility Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS) for centralized dispatch and control.']
  discharge_ramp_rate_mw_per_min decimal(8,3) [note: 'Maximum rate at which the battery storage system can increase its discharge power, measured in megawatts per minute. Critical for grid frequency regulation and ancillary services.']
  expected_end_of_life_date date [note: 'Projected date when the battery storage system will reach end of useful life based on degradation rate, cycle count, and manufacturer specifications. Used for asset retirement planning.']
  geographic_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the battery storage system installation site in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping and outage analysis.']
  geographic_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the battery storage system installation site in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping and outage analysis.']
  interconnection_voltage_kv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Voltage level at which the battery storage system interconnects to the electric grid, measured in kilovolts (kV). Determines transmission vs distribution classification.']
  iso_rto_market_participant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the battery storage asset is registered and actively participating in ISO/RTO wholesale energy and ancillary services markets.']
  iso_rto_registration_code varchar [note: 'Unique registration identifier assigned by the Independent System Operator (ISO) or Regional Transmission Organization (RTO) for market participation and dispatch scheduling.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent scheduled or unscheduled maintenance activity performed on the battery storage system.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this battery storage asset record was last modified. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  max_soc_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable state of charge (SoC) percentage for the battery system to prevent overcharging and preserve battery health. Operational upper limit.']
  min_soc_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable state of charge (SoC) percentage for the battery system to prevent deep discharge and preserve battery health. Operational lower limit.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC) regional entity jurisdiction where the battery storage asset operates (WECC, ERCOT, MRO, NPCC, RF, SERC, SPP, TRE). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|ERCOT|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|SPP|TRE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the next planned preventive maintenance activity for the battery storage system. Used for work order scheduling and resource planning.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the battery storage asset (commissioned, in-service, standby, under maintenance, degraded performance, or decommissioned).']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Ownership structure of the battery storage asset (utility-owned, third-party owned under PPA, customer-owned BTM, or joint venture).']
  primary_use_case varchar [note: 'Primary operational use case or market participation mode for the battery storage system (energy arbitrage, frequency regulation, spinning reserve, capacity market, peak shaving, or renewable firming).']
  rated_energy_capacity_mwh decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total energy storage capacity of the battery system measured in megawatt-hours (MWh). Represents the total amount of energy that can be stored and discharged.']
  rated_power_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum continuous power output or input capacity of the battery storage system measured in megawatts (MW). Represents the instantaneous charge or discharge rate.']
  round_trip_efficiency_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Round-trip energy efficiency of the battery storage system expressed as a percentage. Represents the ratio of energy discharged to energy charged, accounting for conversion and storage losses.']
  scada_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the battery storage system is integrated with the utility SCADA system for real-time monitoring and control.']
  site_address varchar [note: 'Physical street address of the battery storage system installation site. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  site_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the battery storage system is located.']
  site_postal_code varchar [note: 'US postal ZIP code of the battery storage system installation site. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  site_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the battery storage system is located. Used for regulatory jurisdiction and RPS compliance tracking.']
  storage_technology_type varchar [note: 'Type of battery energy storage technology deployed (lithium-ion, flow battery, compressed air energy storage, sodium-sulfur, lead-acid, or other emerging technologies).']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty for the battery storage system expires. Critical for maintenance planning and capital replacement forecasting.']

  Note: 'Master record for utility-scale and grid-connected battery energy storage systems (BESS), including day-ahead and real-time dispatch schedules. Captures storage technology type (lithium-ion, flow battery, compressed air), rated power (MW), rated energy capacity (MWh), SoC range limits, round-trip efficiency, cycle count, degradation rate, charge/discharge ramp rates, interconnection voltage level, co-location flag, and ISO/RTO storage registration status. Includes dispatch schedules: interval timestamps, scheduled charge/discharge MW, target SoC, market participation mode (energy arbitrage, frequency regulation, spinning reserve, capacity), ISO/RTO schedule submission reference, and revision history. SSOT for battery storage asset master data AND dispatch scheduling — no separate dispatch schedule product exists. Excludes generation-domain thermal storage or pumped hydro assets.'
}

Table renewable.generation_meter_read {
  generation_meter_read_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for generation_meter_read']
  curtailment_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.curtailment_event. Business justification: generation_meter_read has curtailment_flag (BOOLEAN) and curtailed_energy_kwh capturing curtailment data at the interval level, but lacks a FK to the specific curtailment_event that caused the curtail']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: Generation meter reads provide forensic data for outage investigations (voltage sags, frequency deviations, timestamp correlation). AMI data validates outage start/end times and customer impact. Essen']
  forecast_renewable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.forecast_renewable. Business justification: Meter reads from renewable generators validate forecast accuracy. Actual vs. forecast variance analysis drives model recalibration, curtailment event reconciliation, REC issuance verification, and PPA']
  generating_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to generation.generating_unit. Business justification: Renewable generation meter reads (from solar/wind farms) must reference the specific generating unit for NERC GADS reporting, ISO/RTO settlement, REC issuance, and operational performance tracking. Me']
  generation_der_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to renewable.der_registry.der_registry_id — Generation meter reads reference the DER or facility being metered. For distributed resources, this must resolve to der_registry. Critical for REC issuance, PPA settlement, and NEM credit calculation.']
  interconnection_der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Generation meter reads for DER should reference the interconnection domain\'s der_system record to access POI specifications, protection settings, and export limits for settlement validation. Replaces ']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter. Business justification: Generation meter reads should reference physical meter asset for data lineage and validation. Revenue meter data validation and VEE processing require generation reads linked to meter asset. Replaces ']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: Generation meter reads require meter point data for settlement node identification, LMP pricing, and grid topology reporting. generation_meter_read has meter_id but meter_point carries lmp_price_id, r']
  nem_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.nem_account. Business justification: generation_meter_read has nem_credit_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating the read contributes to NEM credits, but lacks a FK to the specific nem_account receiving the credit. Adding nem_account_id enables direc']
  pnode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.pnode. Business justification: Renewable generation meter reads are recorded at a market settlement point (pnode) for ISO/RTO energy settlement and REC issuance. \'market_settlement_point\' is a denormalized pnode reference replaced ']
  power_plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the renewable generation facility (wind farm, solar array, hydro plant, battery storage) where this meter read was captured.']
  ppa_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.ppa_contract. Business justification: generation_meter_read has ppa_settlement_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating the read is relevant to PPA settlement, but lacks a direct FK to the ppa_contract governing the settlement. Adding ppa_contract_id en']
  primary_generation_der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Behind-the-Meter (BTM) DER asset for customer-owned generation. Null for utility-scale facilities.']
  validation_forecast_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.run. Business justification: Actual generation meter reads validate forecast accuracy and feed model training. Linking reads to forecast runs enables systematic accuracy measurement and model improvement workflows.']
  vpp_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the VPP aggregation configuration if this DER participates in a virtual power plant program.']
  ambient_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature at the generation site during the interval. Used for performance analysis and generation forecasting model validation.']
  availability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the generation asset was available for operation during the interval. False during planned or forced outages.']
  capacity_factor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of actual generation to maximum possible generation during the interval, expressed as percentage. Key performance indicator for renewable assets.']
  capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Nameplate capacity of the generation facility or DER asset in megawatts. Used for capacity factor and performance ratio calculations.']
  charge_discharge_mode varchar [note: 'Operating mode of battery storage system during the interval. Applicable to battery storage assets for dispatch verification.']
  curtailed_energy_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Amount of potential generation curtailed during the interval in kilowatt-hours. Used for curtailment compensation calculations and renewable integration analysis.']
  curtailment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether generation was curtailed during this interval due to grid constraints, negative pricing, or dispatch instructions from ISO/RTO.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Overall data quality indicator for the meter read. Used to filter reads for financial settlement and regulatory reporting.']
  data_source_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the read was captured by the source system (AMI, SCADA, MDM). May differ from read_timestamp due to communication latency.']
  daylight_saving_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the read timestamp falls within daylight saving time. Used for interval alignment during DST transitions.']
  frequency_hz decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average grid frequency during the interval in hertz. Must remain within NERC reliability standards (59.95-60.05 Hz for North America).']
  gross_generation_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total energy generated during the interval in kilowatt-hours before auxiliary load deduction. Used for REC issuance calculations and renewable portfolio standard compliance reporting.']
  interval_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration of the meter read interval in minutes. Typically 15 or 60 minutes for generation metering per FERC Order 2222 and state RPS requirements.']
  irradiance_w_per_m2 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Solar irradiance measured at the site in watts per square meter. Applicable to solar generation facilities for performance ratio calculations.']
  lmp_energy_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Energy component of the locational marginal price at the settlement point during the interval in dollars per MWh. Used for revenue calculation.']
  meter_multiplier decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to raw meter register values to calculate actual generation. Accounts for current transformer and potential transformer ratios.']
  meter_read_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the meter read record indicating whether it is preliminary, final, or has been revised. Critical for settlement finalization.']
  nem_credit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BTM generation read qualifies for NEM bill credit under state net metering programs. Applicable only to DER assets.']
  net_generation_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Net energy delivered to the grid after deducting auxiliary load and station service consumption. Used for PPA settlement verification and NEM credit determination.']
  outage_type varchar [note: 'Type of outage or unavailability during the interval if availability_flag is false. Used for reliability reporting and PPA force majeure claims.']
  power_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of real power to apparent power during the interval. Must meet interconnection agreement requirements typically between 0.95 leading and 0.95 lagging.']
  ppa_settlement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this read is included in PPA settlement calculations. False for reads with poor data quality or outside contract delivery periods.']
  reactive_power_kvar decimal(18,3) [note: 'Reactive power measured during the interval in kilovolt-amperes reactive. Required for power factor compliance and grid stability analysis.']
  read_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the meter read was obtained. Distinguishes between automated AMI reads, manual field reads, and estimated values.']
  read_source varchar [note: 'Source system that captured or provided the meter read data. Indicates data provenance for audit and quality validation.']
  read_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the meter read interval was captured. Represents the end of the interval period. Critical for REC issuance and PPA settlement calculations.']
  rec_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this generation interval qualifies for REC issuance based on facility certification, data quality, and state RPS program rules.']
  rec_serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number of the REC certificate issued for this generation interval. Assigned by REC tracking system (e.g., WREGIS, M-RETS, PJM-GATS).']
  rec_vintage_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year assigned to RECs issued from this generation interval. Determines eligibility for state RPS compliance periods.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this meter read record was first inserted into the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit trails.']
  settlement_period varchar [note: 'ISO/RTO settlement period identifier (e.g., YYYYMM or trading day) to which this read is assigned for financial settlement.']
  state_of_charge_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Battery energy storage system state of charge at interval end as percentage of total capacity. Applicable to battery storage assets.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Time zone of the meter read timestamp. Critical for accurate interval alignment across facilities in different time zones.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the VEE validation process was completed for this read. Used for data quality audit trails.']
  vee_status varchar [note: 'Data quality status from the VEE process indicating whether the read is actual, estimated, or edited. Critical for REC issuance eligibility and settlement accuracy.']
  voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Average voltage at the point of interconnection during the interval in kilovolts. Used for power quality monitoring and interconnection compliance.']
  wind_speed_mps decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average wind speed at hub height during the interval. Applicable to wind generation facilities for capacity factor analysis.']

  Note: 'Interval meter read records specific to renewable generation facilities and BTM generation assets within the renewable domain. Captures meter serial number, facility or DER reference, read timestamp, interval duration (15-min, hourly), gross generation (kWh), net generation after auxiliary load (kWh), reactive power (kVAR), power factor, meter read source (AMI, SCADA, manual), VEE status, and data quality flag. SSOT boundary: scoped ONLY to generation-side reads used for REC issuance calculations, PPA settlement verification, and NEM credit determination. Customer consumption meter reads, AMI infrastructure, and meter asset management are managed in the metering domain. Cross-domain SSOT adjudication pending with global architect.'
}

Table renewable.der_enrollment {
  der_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for der_enrollment']
  commissioning_planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: DER enrollment commissioning and testing requires planned grid outages for safe interconnection verification. Utilities coordinate DER enrollment milestones with planned outage schedules to meet progr']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: DER program enrollments must track the customer account for incentive payments, bill credits, capacity payments, and compliance reporting. Essential for financial settlement and customer billing integ']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility program, VPP, demand response initiative, or DERMS portfolio that the DER is enrolled in. Links to program master data containing terms, incentives, and operational parameters.']
  forecast_renewable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.forecast_renewable. Business justification: Enrolled DERs contribute to aggregate renewable forecasts. Enrollment status (active/inactive) and enrolled capacity determine which assets are included in zone-level and portfolio-level renewable gen']
  incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to renewable.incentive_program.incentive_program_id — DER enrollments in incentive programs need to reference the specific incentive_program. Supports tracking which DERs are enrolled in which incentive programs.']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: DER program enrollments require completed interconnection; tracking the originating application enables verification of PTO status and capacity eligibility for demand response and VPP programs. Replac']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: DER program enrollments are tied to specific service points for VPP dispatch and settlement. VPP dispatch requires enrollment linked to service point for location-based control, feeder-level aggregati']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: DER program enrollments must comply with tariff requirements, interconnection standards, ISO/RTO market rules. Links enrollment to governing compliance obligation. Essential for program eligibility ve']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.premise. Business justification: DER enrollments are physically located at a customer premise. A premise_id on der_enrollment supports geographic analysis of enrolled DER capacity, grid hosting capacity planning by feeder/substation,']
  primary_der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific DER asset being enrolled. Links to the DER registry containing technical specifications, interconnection details, and asset metadata.']
  settlement_meter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the revenue-grade meter used for program settlement calculations, incentive payments, and NEM credit determination. Links to MDM system for interval data retrieval.']
  tou_rate_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.tou_rate_program. Business justification: DER enrollments in demand response and VPP programs are coordinated with TOU rate programs — customers enrolled in DER programs typically have TOU rates that align dispatch incentives. Linking enables']
  vpp_configuration_id varchar [note: 'FK to renewable.vpp_configuration.vpp_configuration_id — DER enrollments into VPP programs must reference the specific VPP configuration. Critical for dispatch eligibility and aggregation.']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enrollment automatically renews at the end of the term. True for evergreen enrollments, false for fixed-term enrollments requiring explicit renewal.']
  baseline_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the customer baseline load (CBL) for demand response performance measurement. Customer baseline load uses historical consumption patterns, meter-before-meter-after compares pre/post installation, control group uses similar non-participating customers, day-matching uses same-day-type averages, regression uses statistical modeling.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Communication protocol used for telemetry, dispatch commands, and control signals between the DER and DERMS or VPP platform. IEEE 2030.5 (Smart Energy Profile 2.0) for residential, OpenADR 2.0b for commercial DR, DNP3 for utility-scale SCADA integration.']
  control_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Network endpoint URL for sending dispatch commands, curtailment signals, and control instructions to the DER. Separate from telemetry for security and operational isolation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  dispatch_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for dispatch order within the VPP or DERMS portfolio. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for economic dispatch optimization and curtailment sequencing.']
  enrolled_capacity_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Nameplate capacity in kilowatts (kW) enrolled in the program. May be less than the DER\'s total capacity if partial enrollment is allowed. Used for dispatch calculations, incentive payments, and portfolio aggregation.']
  enrolled_energy_capacity_kwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Energy storage capacity in kilowatt-hours (kWh) enrolled for battery storage systems. Represents the usable energy that can be dispatched or charged under program control. Null for non-storage DERs.']
  enrollment_application_date date [note: 'Date when the customer submitted the enrollment application. Used for tracking application processing time and program participation trends.']
  enrollment_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the utility approved the enrollment application and authorized program participation. Used for compliance reporting and customer communications.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the DER was officially enrolled in the program and participation became effective. Used for program term calculations, incentive eligibility, and compliance reporting.']
  enrollment_effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when enrollment became active and the DER began participating in program dispatch, telemetry, or credit calculations. Critical for real-time market operations and settlement.']
  enrollment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional enrollment details, special conditions, customer preferences, or operational notes not covered by structured fields.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the enrollment record. Human-readable unique number used in customer communications, program reporting, and regulatory filings.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment. Pending indicates application submitted but not yet approved, active indicates full participation, suspended indicates temporary hold, terminated indicates program exit, opted_out indicates customer-initiated withdrawal, expired indicates term completion.']
  enrollment_term_end_date date [note: 'End date of the contractual enrollment term. Null for open-ended enrollments. Used to trigger renewal processes and calculate program participation duration.']
  enrollment_term_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the contractual enrollment term. May differ from enrollment_date if there is a delay between enrollment and term commencement.']
  export_limit_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum power in kilowatts (kW) that the DER is permitted to export to the grid under the interconnection agreement. Used to enforce dispatch constraints and prevent grid violations.']
  import_limit_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum power in kilowatts (kW) that the DER is permitted to import from the grid for charging (applicable to battery storage systems). Null for generation-only DERs.']
  incentive_rate_per_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Capacity incentive payment rate in dollars per kilowatt of enrolled capacity. Used for calculating monthly or annual capacity payments to program participants.']
  incentive_rate_per_kwh decimal(10,4) [note: 'Energy incentive payment rate in dollars per kilowatt-hour of dispatched or curtailed energy. Used for calculating performance-based incentive payments.']
  last_dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent dispatch event or control signal sent to this enrolled DER. Used for monitoring program activity and asset utilization.']
  last_opt_out_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent opt-out event. Used for tracking opt-out patterns and customer engagement analysis.']
  opt_out_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is permitted to opt out of individual dispatch events or curtailment requests. True for voluntary DR programs, false for firm capacity commitments.']
  opt_out_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the customer has opted out of dispatch events during the current enrollment term. Used to enforce opt-out limits and assess program reliability.']
  opt_out_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of opt-outs allowed per enrollment term. Null if unlimited opt-outs are permitted. Exceeding this limit may result in enrollment suspension or termination.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the program type. Net Energy Metering (NEM) for BTM generation credits, Virtual Power Plant (VPP) for aggregated dispatch, Demand Response (DR) for load curtailment, Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) for compliance tracking, storage incentive for battery programs, or feed-in tariff for fixed-rate purchase agreements.']
  rec_issuance_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether generation from this enrolled DER is eligible for REC issuance under state RPS programs. True for qualifying renewable generation, false for storage-only or non-renewable DERs.']
  rec_tracking_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier in the regional REC tracking system (e.g., NEPOOL GIS, WREGIS, M-RETS, PJM-EIS) for this enrolled DER. Used for automated REC issuance and retirement tracking.']
  telemetry_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Network endpoint URL for real-time telemetry data transmission from the DER to the DERMS or VPP aggregator. Used for monitoring generation, state of charge, and operational status.']
  telemetry_frequency_seconds varchar [note: 'Frequency in seconds at which the DER transmits telemetry data to the DERMS or VPP platform. Typical values range from 5 seconds for utility-scale assets to 300 seconds (5 minutes) for residential BTM systems.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment was terminated or the customer exited the program. Null for active enrollments. Used for calculating program participation duration and churn analysis.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for enrollment termination. Customer request indicates voluntary withdrawal, non-compliance indicates failure to meet program requirements, equipment failure indicates technical issues, program closure indicates utility-initiated termination, term expiration indicates natural end of contract, relocation indicates customer moved.']
  total_dispatch_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of dispatch events or curtailment requests issued to this enrolled DER during the current enrollment term. Used for performance tracking and program evaluation.']
  total_dispatched_energy_kwh decimal(15,3) [note: 'Cumulative energy in kilowatt-hours (kWh) dispatched or curtailed from this enrolled DER during the current enrollment term. Used for settlement calculations and program impact assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  vpp_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current enrollment status in Virtual Power Plant aggregation programs. Enrolled indicates active participation in VPP dispatch. Not enrolled indicates the resource is not participating. Pending indicates application under review. Suspended indicates temporary removal from dispatch. [Moved from der_registry: This attribute in der_registry represents the enrollment status of a DER in VPP programs, but a single DER can be enrolled in MULTIPLE VPP configurations simultaneously with different statuses. The status belongs to each specific enrollment relationship (DER + VPP combination), not to the DER itself. Moving this to der_enrollment allows tracking distinct enrollment statuses per VPP.]']

  Note: 'Enrollment record linking a specific DER to a utility program, VPP, demand response event, or DERMS-managed portfolio. Captures enrollment date, DER reference, program type (NEM, VPP, DR, RPS, storage incentive), program term, enrolled capacity (kW), communication protocol (IEEE 2030.5, OpenADR, DNP3), telemetry endpoint, enrollment status, and opt-out history. SSOT for DER program participation lifecycle within the renewable domain. Manages the many-to-many relationship between DERs and utility programs.'
}

Table renewable.incentive_program {
  incentive_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for incentive_program']
  implementing_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Incentive programs implement specific regulatory obligations mandated by statute or commission order (e.g., solar rebate programs mandated by renewable energy legislation). Each program must be tracea']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: Incentive programs (solar rebates, storage incentives) are administered within specific utility service territories. Territory determines program availability, budget caps, and regulatory jurisdiction']
  administering_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the government agency, utility, or third-party administrator responsible for managing the incentive program.']
  application_window_end_date date [note: 'Date when the program stops accepting new applications. Null if continuously open.']
  application_window_start_date date [note: 'Date when the program begins accepting new applications from eligible participants.']
  budget_expended_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of program budget that has been committed or paid out to date in USD.']
  budget_remaining_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining available budget for the incentive program in USD, calculated as program_budget_amount minus budget_expended_amount.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for program inquiries and application support.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for program inquiries and application support.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incentive program record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the incentive program expires or is scheduled to close. Null for open-ended programs.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the incentive program becomes active and begins accepting applications or issuing incentives.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the eligibility requirements for participation in the incentive program, including customer class, technology specifications, interconnection requirements, and geographic restrictions.']
  eligible_technology_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of renewable energy technology types eligible for this incentive program (e.g., solar_pv, wind, battery_storage, hydroelectric, biomass, geothermal).']
  enrolled_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total capacity in megawatts (MW) currently enrolled or committed under the incentive program.']
  enrolled_customer_count varchar [note: 'Current number of customers or participants enrolled in the incentive program.']
  enrollment_cap_customer_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of customers or participants that can enroll in the incentive program. Null if no customer cap applies.']
  enrollment_cap_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum total capacity in megawatts (MW) that can be enrolled in the incentive program. Null if no capacity cap applies.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of funding for the incentive program: ratepayer = utility customer rates, federal_grant = federal government funding, state_grant = state government funding, utility_shareholder = utility equity, carbon_credit = carbon offset revenue, rps_acp = RPS Alternative Compliance Payment funds.']
  incentive_rate_structure varchar [note: 'Structure or basis for calculating the incentive payment: per_kw = dollars per kilowatt of capacity, per_mwh = dollars per megawatt-hour of generation, per_rec = dollars per Renewable Energy Certificate, percentage_of_cost = percentage of project cost, fixed_amount = lump sum, tiered = multiple rate tiers based on size or technology.']
  incentive_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the incentive rate value (e.g., USD_per_kW, USD_per_MWh, USD_per_REC, percent, USD for fixed amounts).']
  incentive_rate_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the incentive rate corresponding to the rate structure (e.g., 0.25 for $0.25/kW, 30.0000 for 30% of cost).']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Two or three-letter code identifying the regulatory jurisdiction or state where the incentive program applies (e.g., CA, NY, TX).']
  maximum_system_size_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum renewable energy system capacity in kilowatts (kW) eligible for the incentive. Null if no maximum applies.']
  minimum_system_size_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum renewable energy system capacity in kilowatts (kW) required for eligibility. Null if no minimum applies.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which incentive payments are issued to participants: one_time = single upfront payment, monthly/quarterly/annual = recurring payments, per_mwh = payment per unit of generation.']
  payment_term_years varchar [note: 'Number of years over which incentive payments are made for recurring payment structures. Null for one-time payments.']
  program_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated for the incentive program in USD. Used for tracking program expenditures and remaining capacity.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or identifier for the incentive program used in regulatory filings and customer communications.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the renewable energy incentive program as published by the administering agency or utility.']
  program_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to the incentive program administration or policy changes.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the incentive program indicating whether it is accepting applications and issuing incentives.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the incentive program. ITC = Investment Tax Credit, PTC = Production Tax Credit, SREC = Solar Renewable Energy Certificate, storage_incentive = battery/storage incentive, interconnection_rebate = grid connection rebate, green_tariff = voluntary renewable energy tariff.']
  program_website_url varchar [note: 'Web address where customers and stakeholders can access detailed program information, application forms, and guidelines.']
  rec_tracking_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the REC tracking system used to verify and retire RECs associated with this program (e.g., NEPOOL GIS, WREGIS, M-RETS, PJM GATS).']
  requires_interconnection_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the participant must receive utility interconnection approval before receiving incentive payments.']
  requires_rec_transfer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether participants must transfer ownership of Renewable Energy Certificates (RECs) to the utility or program administrator as a condition of receiving incentives.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incentive program record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Master record for renewable energy incentive programs administered by the utility or mandated by state/federal regulators. Captures program name, program type (ITC, PTC, SREC, storage incentive, interconnection rebate, green tariff), eligible technology types, incentive rate or structure ($/kW, $/MWh, $/REC), program budget ($), enrollment cap (MW or customers), application window, eligibility criteria, administering agency, and program status. SSOT for renewable incentive program definitions within this domain. Supports program management, budget tracking, and regulatory reporting.'
}

Linking incentive_application to customer_service_agree']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incentive application record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Customer or project application record for a renewable energy incentive program. Captures applicant reference, program reference, application date, project type and capacity (kW), estimated annual generation (MWh), requested incentive amount ($), supporting documentation status, review stage, approval/denial date, approved incentive amount ($), payment schedule, and compliance conditions. SSOT for incentive application lifecycle from submission through disbursement within the renewable domain.'
}

Table renewable.rps_ppa_contribution {
  rps_ppa_contribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the rps_ppa_contribution association']
  ppa_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the PPA contract that contributes renewable energy and RECs toward the RPS obligation.']
  rps_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the RPS compliance obligation that this PPA contribution satisfies.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of this PPA contract\'s total annual output allocated to this specific RPS obligation. Used when a single PPA contract is split across multiple state jurisdictions or compliance obligations.']
  carveout_tier varchar [note: 'Technology-specific carve-out tier that this contribution satisfies (solar, offshore_wind, storage, general_renewable). RPS obligations have technology-specific requirements, and this attribute tracks which tier each PPA contribution fulfills.']
  compliance_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which this PPA contribution applies to the RPS obligation. A single PPA contract contributes to multiple RPS obligations across different compliance years.']
  contribution_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of renewable energy in MWh from this PPA contract that is allocated toward satisfying this specific RPS obligation. This is the core compliance metric tracked for regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contribution record was first created in the system. Audit field.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this PPA contract stopped contributing energy toward this RPS obligation. Nullable if contribution is ongoing. Tracks historical allocation changes.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this PPA contract began contributing energy toward this RPS obligation. May differ from PPA contract execution date if the contract was allocated to this obligation mid-period.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contribution record was last updated. Tracks changes to allocation, verification status, or contribution volumes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special circumstances, regulatory exceptions, or allocation methodology for this contribution.']
  rec_retirement_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of RECs in MWh retired from this PPA contract to satisfy this RPS obligation. May differ from contribution_mwh if RECs are banked or purchased separately.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or docket ID for the state PUC filing that documents this PPA contribution. Required for audit trail and regulatory verification.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory verification status of this contribution. Indicates whether the state PUC has accepted this PPA contribution as valid for RPS compliance (pending, verified, rejected, under_review).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance contribution relationship between RPS obligations and PPA contracts. It captures the specific renewable energy volume (MWh) and RECs that each PPA contract contributes toward satisfying each RPS obligation, including technology-specific carve-out allocations (solar, offshore wind), verification status, and the compliance period during which the contribution applies. Each record links one RPS obligation to one PPA contract with attributes that exist only in the context of this compliance contribution relationship. This is the SSOT for demonstrating to state regulators which contracts fulfilled which obligations.. Existence Justification: In renewable energy compliance operations, utilities actively manage a portfolio of PPA contracts to satisfy multiple RPS obligations across different state jurisdictions and compliance years. A single PPA contract contributes renewable energy and RECs to multiple RPS obligations (different states, different years, different carve-out tiers), and each RPS obligation is satisfied by aggregating contributions from multiple PPA contracts. This is a genuine operational M:N relationship that utilities must demonstrate to state regulators through detailed compliance filings.'
}

Table demand.dr_program {
  dr_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand response program. Primary key.']

Business justification: DR programs are designed within control area operational boundaries to meet reserve requirements and reliability standards. Needed for capacity planning, NERC reserve margin calculations, and coordina']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: DR programs are designed for specific geographic zones with zone-specific load characteristics, peak demand patterns, and capacity requirements. Essential for program design, enrollment targeting, and']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: DR programs require DER interconnection approval before enrollment eligibility. ISO/RTO capacity market registration tracking needs interconnection status verification. Program managers validate inter']
  load_forecast_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.model. Business justification: DR programs specify the load forecast model used for baseline calculations and event planning. Required for regulatory compliance (FERC Order 2222) and ISO/RTO M&V protocols to ensure consistent basel']
  load_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.load_zone. Business justification: DR programs are designed with load zone geographic scope for VVO/CVR-DR coordination and locational capacity market products. Program-level load zone scoping is used in distribution planning, regulato']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: DR programs must comply with specific regulatory obligations (NERC reliability standards, FERC Order 2222, state PUC DR requirements). Essential for compliance tracking, audit evidence, and regulatory']
  planning_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.planning_period. Business justification: DR programs are designed and budgeted within utility IRP planning periods for capacity resource adequacy. Links program design to long-term capacity planning, enabling cost-effectiveness analysis and ']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: DR programs are compliance programs requiring training, certification, audit oversight, and evidence retention per FERC Order 2222 and state PUC requirements. Links DR program to its compliance progra']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: DR programs require PUC/FERC regulatory approval through tariff filings for program authorization, rate design, cost recovery, and compliance reporting. Links program to authorizing regulatory filing ']
  relief_path_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.path. Business justification: DR programs are registered with ISO/RTO to provide congestion relief on specific transmission paths. FERC OATT and ISO/RTO tariffs require DR programs to identify the transmission path they are design']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: DR programs are designed, approved, and operated within specific service territories due to regulatory jurisdiction, franchise boundaries, tariff structures, and eligibility rules. Essential for progr']
  target_constraint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.constraint. Business justification: DR programs are designed and approved to address specific recurring transmission constraints. FERC and ISO/RTO program approval processes require identifying the target constraint, enabling constraint']
  tou_rate_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.tou_rate_program. Business justification: DR programs are structured around TOU rate programs — peak period definitions, eligible_customer_segment, and event_notification_lead_time align with TOU peak/off-peak windows. Program design, custome']
  ami_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer must have Advanced Metering Infrastructure (smart meter) installed to participate in this demand response program. True for programs requiring interval data; False for programs using deemed savings or aggregated measurements.']
  baseline_adjustment_cap_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage adjustment allowed to baseline calculation to account for day-of variations. Prevents gaming and ensures baseline accuracy.']
  baseline_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Specific algorithm used to calculate customer baseline load for measurement and verification. High 10 of Last 10 averages the highest 10 days from the last 10 similar days; Average 5 of 10 averages middle 5 of last 10 days; Day Matching uses same day-of-week; Regression uses statistical model; Weather Normalized adjusts for temperature.']
  behavioral_dr_delivery_channel varchar [note: 'Primary communication channel used to deliver behavioral demand response messages and engagement content to customers. Applicable only to behavioral DR program types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|mobile_app|web_portal|home_energy_report|in_home_display|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  behavioral_dr_engagement_scoring_parameters varchar [note: 'Parameters and weights used to calculate customer engagement scores in behavioral demand response programs, including message open rates, web portal logins, mobile app usage, survey responses, and behavioral action completion.']
  behavioral_dr_gamification_rules varchar [note: 'Description of gamification elements used in behavioral demand response campaigns, including points systems, leaderboards, badges, challenges, and rewards structures designed to drive sustained engagement and load reduction.']
  behavioral_dr_peer_comparison_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to compare customer energy usage to peers in behavioral demand response campaigns. Neighbor Comparison uses geographic proximity; Similar Home Comparison uses home characteristics; Percentile Ranking shows position in distribution. None if peer comparison not used.']
  cost_effectiveness_ratio decimal(8,4) [note: 'Calculated benefit-cost ratio for this demand response program using the specified cost-effectiveness test method. Values greater than 1.0 indicate program is cost-effective. Updated annually based on actual performance.']
  cost_effectiveness_test_method varchar [note: 'Regulatory cost-effectiveness test used to evaluate this demand response program. Total Resource Cost (TRC) test includes all costs and benefits; Program Administrator Cost (PAC) test uses utility perspective; Ratepayer Impact Measure (RIM) test evaluates rate impacts; Participant Cost Test (PCT) evaluates customer economics; Societal Cost Test (SCT) includes externalities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this demand response program record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the demand response program is scheduled to terminate. Null for open-ended programs.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the demand response program becomes active and eligible for customer enrollment and event dispatch.']
  eligible_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment(s) eligible to enroll in this demand response program. Segmentation based on rate class and usage characteristics.']
  enrolled_capacity_actual_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Current actual aggregate enrolled load reduction capacity in megawatts across all active customer enrollments in this program.']
  enrolled_capacity_target_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Target aggregate load reduction capacity in megawatts that the utility aims to enroll in this demand response program. Used for resource adequacy planning and regulatory performance reporting.']
  event_notification_lead_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum number of hours advance notice provided to customers before a demand response event is dispatched. Regulatory requirement varies by program type and jurisdiction.']
  incentive_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base incentive amount paid to customers. Units depend on incentive_structure_type: dollars per year for fixed payment, dollars per kWh for per-kWh curtailed, dollars per kW-month for capacity payment.']
  incentive_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for incentive payments. USD for United States utilities.']
  incentive_structure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how customer incentives are calculated and paid. Fixed Payment provides flat annual amount; Per kWh Curtailed pays based on measured load reduction; Capacity Payment compensates for availability; Energy Payment compensates for actual curtailment; Bill Credit reduces customer bill; Tiered Incentive varies payment by performance level.']
  iso_rto_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ISO or RTO where this demand response program is registered for wholesale market participation. None indicates retail-only program. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CAISO|ERCOT|MISO|PJM|NYISO|ISO_NE|SPP|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  iso_rto_registration_number varchar [note: 'Registration identifier assigned by the ISO or RTO for wholesale market participation. Required for programs that bid demand response capacity into day-ahead or real-time markets.']
  max_event_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable duration in hours for a single demand response event under this program. Protects customers from excessive curtailment.']
  max_event_hours_per_year decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative hours of demand response events that can be dispatched in a calendar year. Protects customers from excessive annual curtailment burden.']
  max_events_per_year varchar [note: 'Maximum number of demand response events that can be called in a calendar year under this program. Regulatory limit to ensure customer participation remains voluntary and reasonable.']
  measurement_verification_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate baseline load and measure actual load reduction during demand response events. Customer Baseline uses historical usage patterns; Meter Before-After compares pre-event and event-period readings; Control Group compares participants to non-participants; Regression Analysis uses statistical models; Deemed Savings applies fixed reduction values.']
  minimum_load_requirement_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum peak demand in kilowatts that a customer must have to be eligible for enrollment in this demand response program. Null if no minimum applies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this demand response program record was last modified in the system.']
  opt_out_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers are allowed to opt out of individual demand response events without penalty. True for voluntary programs; False for firm interruptible tariffs.']
  opt_out_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial penalty assessed to customers who opt out of a demand response event when opt-out is allowed but discouraged. Null if no penalty applies.']
  performance_reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which program performance metrics must be reported to ISO/RTO and PUC. Event-based reporting required for wholesale market programs; periodic reporting for retail programs.']
  performance_reporting_obligation varchar [note: 'Description of specific performance metrics and reporting obligations required by regulators and market operators, including enrolled capacity, event performance, customer participation rates, and measurement and verification results.']
  program_budget_annual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total annual budget allocated for this demand response program, including incentive payments, marketing, administration, measurement and verification, and technology costs.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demand response program. Draft indicates program design phase; Pending Approval awaits PUC/FERC authorization; Active means program is enrolling customers and dispatching events; Suspended indicates temporary halt; Closed means no new enrollments but existing customers remain; Retired indicates program termination.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand response program type. Direct Load Control (DLC) allows utility to remotely control customer devices; Interruptible Tariff provides bill credits for load curtailment; Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) charges higher rates during peak events; Time of Use (TOU) varies rates by time period; Behavioral DR uses engagement and gamification; Virtual Power Plant (VPP) aggregates distributed resources; Wholesale Market DR participates in ISO/RTO markets; Emergency DR responds to grid emergencies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_load_control|interruptible_tariff|cpp|tou|behavioral_dr|vpp_aggregation|wholesale_market_dr|emergency_dr — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the PUC or FERC issued final approval for this demand response program to commence operations.']
  regulatory_authorization_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory authorization under which this demand response program operates. PUC Tariff for state-approved retail programs; FERC Tariff for wholesale programs; Pilot Program for experimental initiatives; Emergency Authorization for grid reliability programs; Market Rule for ISO/RTO market products.']
  regulatory_docket_number varchar [note: 'PUC or FERC docket number under which this demand response program was approved. Used for regulatory compliance tracking and audit trail.']
  vpp_aggregation_model varchar [note: 'Business model for aggregating distributed energy resources into a virtual power plant. Utility-Owned means utility directly controls customer devices; Third-Party Aggregator uses external aggregator; Hybrid combines both models. None if not a VPP program.']
  vpp_dispatch_protocol varchar [note: 'Communication protocol used to dispatch demand response signals to distributed energy resources in virtual power plant programs. OpenADR is open standard; IEEE 2030.5 is smart grid standard; Proprietary uses vendor-specific protocol. None if not applicable.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Demand Response program offered by the utility, including direct load control, interruptible tariff, CPP, TOU, behavioral DR (with delivery channel, peer comparison methodology, gamification rules, and engagement scoring parameters), VPP aggregation, and wholesale market DR products. Captures program type, ISO/RTO registration, enrolled capacity targets, incentive structure, measurement and verification methodology, regulatory authorization (PUC/FERC), program lifecycle status, performance reporting obligations, and behavioral campaign parameters. SSOT for all DR program definitions including behavioral DR campaign configurations.'
}

Table demand.dr_enrollment {
  dr_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand response enrollment record. Primary key for the DR enrollment entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account enrolled in the demand response program. Links to the customer account master record in the Customer Information System (CIS).']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party demand response aggregator managing this customer enrollment on behalf of the utility. Null for direct utility-managed enrollments. Links to the aggregator master record defining aggregator contracts, settlement terms, and performance obligations.']
  customer_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_service_agreement. Business justification: DR enrollment must reference the specific service agreement governing rate schedule, service class, and contractual terms. Critical for tariff compliance, billing integration, and ensuring enrolled lo']
  der_interconnection_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.der_interconnection_point. Business justification: Premises with DER assets (solar, battery storage) enrolled in DR/VPP programs require linkage to their interconnection point for telemetry, dispatch control, baseline calculation, and settlement. Esse']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: DR enrollment tracks specific DER system providing curtailment capability. Measurement & verification requires system nameplate capacity, inverter specifications, and export limits. Baseline calculati']
  distribution_transformer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.transformer. Business justification: DR enrollments must track the distribution transformer serving the enrolled premise for capacity planning, hosting capacity analysis, and assessing grid impact during curtailment events. Essential for']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific demand response program in which the customer is enrolled. Links to the DR program master record defining program rules, incentives, and dispatch protocols.']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Enrollments are aggregated by zone for ISO/RTO reporting, baseline calculation, and capacity market participation. Zone-level aggregation is required for settlement, performance reporting, and resourc']
  interruptible_tariff_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the interruptible tariff agreement contract that governs the terms, conditions, and compensation for load curtailment. Links to the tariff agreement master record defining rate discounts, curtailment obligations, and penalty provisions.']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) meter device used to measure interval load data for baseline calculation and measurement and verification (M&V). Links to the meter device master record in Meter Data Management (MDM).']
  pnode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.pnode. Business justification: Each DR enrollment is registered at a specific pnode for ISO/RTO market participation and LMP-based settlement. ISO/RTO tariffs require pnode assignment at enrollment for capacity market registration,']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: Enrollment eligibility validation, tariff schedule assignment, and PUC regulatory reporting all require service territory association on DR enrollments. Utility enrollment systems enforce service terr']
  average_curtailment_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The average actual load curtailment in kilowatts (kW) achieved by the customer across all participated DR events during the current enrollment period. Used for program performance analysis and capacity planning.']
  baseline_adjustment_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether day-of or morning-of baseline adjustments are enabled for this enrollment to account for real-time load variations. True: adjustments are applied. False: unadjusted baseline is used for M&V.']
  baseline_calculation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to calculate the customer baseline load (CBL) for measurement and verification (M&V) of load curtailment performance. High X of Y: average of highest X days in prior Y days. Mid X of Y: average of middle X days in prior Y days. Regression: statistical regression model. Control Group: comparison to similar non-participating customers. Meter Before Meter After: direct comparison of meter readings.']
  capacity_market_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment is registered to participate in the ISO/RTO capacity market and is eligible to receive capacity payments for committed load reduction. True: participating in capacity market. False: energy-only or retail program participation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this enrollment record was first created in the data platform. System-generated audit timestamp for data lineage and compliance.']
  direct_load_control_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the utility has direct load control capability to remotely curtail customer load via smart thermostats, water heaters, or other controllable devices. True: DLC enabled. False: behavioral or manual curtailment only.']
  enrolled_load_capacity_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The committed load reduction capacity in kilowatts (kW) that the customer has enrolled to curtail during demand response events. This is the System of Record (SSOT) for enrolled capacity reported to ISO/RTO for capacity market settlements and to PUC for program performance audits.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which the customer enrolled in the demand response program. Web Portal: customer self-service online. Mobile App: enrollment via utility mobile application. Call Center: enrollment via customer service representative. Field Enrollment: enrollment by field agent or installer. Aggregator: enrollment through third-party demand response aggregator. Auto Enrollment: automatic enrollment based on rate class or program rules. Utility Initiated: proactive enrollment by utility for targeted customers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_portal|mobile_app|call_center|field_enrollment|aggregator|auto_enrollment|utility_initiated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  enrollment_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the demand response enrollment becomes active and the customer becomes eligible to participate in DR events and receive incentive payments. Must align with program enrollment windows and ISO/RTO capacity commitment deadlines.']
  enrollment_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the demand response enrollment expires and the customer is no longer eligible for DR event dispatch or incentive payments. Null for open-ended enrollments subject to annual renewal or customer opt-out.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique enrollment number assigned to the customer for this DR program participation. Used in customer communications, billing statements, and regulatory reporting. Format: DR-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  enrollment_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system of record from which this enrollment record originated. Examples: SAP IS-U, Oracle CC&B, proprietary DR management system, aggregator platform.']
  enrollment_source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this enrollment record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage traceability and reconciliation with upstream systems.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demand response enrollment. Pending: enrollment submitted but not yet active. Active: enrollment is in effect and customer is eligible for DR events. Suspended: temporarily inactive due to non-compliance or customer request. Terminated: enrollment ended by utility or customer. Expired: enrollment period ended. Opted Out: customer exercised opt-out right.']
  enrollment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the customer enrollment was submitted and recorded in the system. Represents the business event time of enrollment initiation, distinct from audit timestamps.']
  iso_rto_registration_number varchar [note: 'The unique registration identifier assigned by the ISO or RTO for this demand response resource enrollment. Used for wholesale market participation, capacity market bidding, and settlement reconciliation with the ISO/RTO.']
  last_opt_out_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent opt-out event when the customer declined to participate in a dispatched DR event. Null if the customer has never opted out during the current enrollment period.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this enrollment record was most recently modified in the data platform. System-generated audit timestamp for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  notification_lead_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The minimum advance notice time in minutes required to notify the customer before a demand response event dispatch. Defined by program rules and customer agreement. Typical values range from 15 minutes to 24 hours.']
  notification_preference varchar [note: 'The customer preferred method for receiving demand response event notifications and dispatch alerts. Email: email notification. SMS: text message. Phone: automated phone call. Mobile App: push notification via utility mobile app. None: no notification requested.']
  opt_out_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of times the customer has exercised their opt-out right to decline participation in a dispatched DR event during the current enrollment period. Used to track customer engagement and enforce program opt-out limits.']
  participation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of dispatched DR events in which the customer successfully participated and achieved the required load curtailment during the current enrollment period. Calculated as (successful events / total dispatched events) * 100. Used for program performance reporting and customer incentive tier determination.']
  puc_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment must be included in Public Utility Commission (PUC) demand response program participation and performance reports. True: include in PUC reporting. False: exclude from regulatory reporting.']
  telemetry_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time telemetry data from the customer meter or behind-the-meter (BTM) devices is enabled for this enrollment to support real-time dispatch verification and settlement. True: telemetry enabled. False: interval data only.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which the enrollment was terminated. Null for active, pending, or suspended enrollments. Populated when enrollment status transitions to terminated.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for enrollment termination when enrollment status is terminated. Customer Request: customer voluntarily withdrew. Non-Compliance: customer failed to meet program requirements. Program Ended: DR program was discontinued. Account Closed: customer account was closed. Moved: customer moved out of service territory. Aggregator Change: customer switched to different aggregator. Other: other termination reason. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|non_compliance|program_ended|account_closed|moved|aggregator_change|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_incentive_payments_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'The cumulative total of all incentive payments in United States Dollars (USD) paid to the customer for demand response participation during the current enrollment period. Includes capacity payments, energy payments, and performance bonuses.']
  vpp_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment is part of a Virtual Power Plant (VPP) aggregation that dispatches distributed energy resources (DER) as a single coordinated resource. True: enrolled in VPP. False: standalone DR enrollment.']

  Note: 'Master record capturing a customer account\'s enrollment in a specific DR program, including enrolled load capacity (kW), service point, meter, enrollment effective and expiration dates, enrollment channel, opt-out history, interruptible tariff agreement reference, and current enrollment status. SSOT for enrolled load capacity by customer and program. Supports ISO/RTO capacity reporting and PUC program participation audits.'
}

Table demand.dr_event {
  dr_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand response event. Primary key for the DR event record.']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party demand response aggregator managing this event on behalf of the utility or ISO/RTO, if applicable.']
  congestion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.congestion_event. Business justification: DR events are dispatched in direct response to transmission congestion events. Linking DR dispatch to the triggering congestion event enables cost-benefit analysis, settlement reconciliation, and regu']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: DR events are dispatched to manage control area ACE and frequency deviations. Operators must track which control area initiated each event for NERC CPS1/CPS2 compliance reporting and real-time balanci']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: DR event dispatch targets specific DER systems for curtailment. VPP aggregation and DERMS integration require system-level identification for telemetry and control commands. Event performance measurem']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific distribution substation targeted by this DR event, if geographic_scope is substation-level.']
  dr_triggering_outage_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: DR events are frequently dispatched in response to grid emergencies that manifest as outage risks or actual supply shortfalls. Emergency operations centers coordinate DR dispatch with OMS to manage sy']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific distribution feeder targeted by this DR event, if geographic_scope is feeder-level.']
  forecast_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.forecast_run. Business justification: Events reference the specific forecast run that triggered dispatch for audit trail and settlement disputes. Critical for ISO/RTO day-ahead and real-time market operations where forecast accuracy impac']
  grid_reliability_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.grid_reliability_event. Business justification: DR events are dispatched in response to NERC-reportable grid reliability events. NERC OE-417 reporting and post-event reliability analysis require direct correlation between the grid reliability incid']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: Event dispatch decisions require load forecasts to determine timing, magnitude, and necessity of curtailment. Operators compare forecasted peak load against available capacity to trigger DR events for']
  load_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.load_zone. Business justification: Load zone is the primary geographic dispatch unit for DR events in distribution operations. VVO/CVR-DR coordination, feeder capacity relief dispatch, and ISO/RTO locational DR products all require loa']
