  network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.network_segment. Business justification: DR events are dispatched against specific network segments for locational load relief and FLISR coordination. Distribution operators require segment-level DR dispatch records for post-event reliabilit']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: DR events must comply with dispatch protocols mandated by regulatory obligations (e.g., NERC EOP-011 emergency operations, ISO/RTO market rules). Essential for event compliance validation, audit trail']
  primary_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent demand response program under which this event was dispatched (e.g., CPP, TOU, direct load control, interruptible tariff).']
  relief_flowgate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.flowgate. Business justification: DR events are dispatched by grid operators to relieve specific flowgate overloads. Transmission operators and ISO/RTOs track which flowgate triggered each DR dispatch for post-event reliability analys']
  to_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Every DR event is dispatched under a specific program. This FK is required for program-level performance reporting and ISO/RTO settlement.']
  transmission_outage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_outage. Business justification: DR events are called during transmission outages to manage system loading and prevent cascading failures. Emergency DR dispatch during N-1 or N-2 contingencies is standard grid operations. Links DR re']
  transmission_path_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.path. Business justification: DR events are dispatched to relieve congestion on specific transmission paths. Operators call DR to manage path loading, avoid TLR procedures, and maintain ATC. Critical for congestion management and ']
  triggering_agc_signal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.agc_signal. Business justification: AGC signals are the direct operational trigger for emergency DR dispatch when frequency deviations require demand-side response. Grid operators and NERC BAL compliance teams must correlate AGC dispatc']
  triggering_contingency_analysis_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.contingency_analysis_run. Business justification: DR events are dispatched as corrective actions when contingency analysis identifies thermal or voltage violations requiring load relief. NERC TPL compliance documentation requires tracing DR correctiv']
  triggering_energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.energy_price. Business justification: Events track forecasted energy prices (LMP) at dispatch time for economic dispatch decisions. Used in price-responsive DR programs and ISO/RTO energy market settlements where dispatch is triggered by ']
  triggering_load_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.load_forecast. Business justification: DR events are dispatched when load forecasts project peak conditions exceeding reserve margins. Linking dr_event to the specific load_forecast run that triggered dispatch supports ISO/RTO settlement v']
  triggering_operating_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.operating_limit. Business justification: DR events are dispatched when specific transmission operating limits are approached or violated. NERC FAC-002 and TOP-001 compliance require documenting which operating limit triggered demand-side cor']
  triggering_peak_demand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.peak_demand. Business justification: DR events are triggered by forecasted peak demand conditions for capacity relief. Links event dispatch to the specific peak demand forecast that triggered the call, essential for ISO/RTO settlement an']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific load zone or pricing zone targeted by this DR event, if geographic_scope is zone-level.']
  actual_curtailment_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The actual load reduction in megawatts (MW) achieved during this DR event, calculated through baseline comparison and M&V processes.']
  actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual duration of the DR event in minutes, calculated from actual_start_timestamp to actual_end_timestamp, used for settlement.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when load curtailment ended, used for M&V and settlement calculations.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when load curtailment began, used for measurement and verification (M&V) and settlement calculations.']
  baseline_load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The calculated baseline load in megawatts (MW) representing what the load would have been without the DR event, used as the reference for curtailment measurement.']
  baseline_methodology varchar [note: 'The calculation method used to determine the baseline load for this event (e.g., 10-of-10, 5-of-10, regression-based, customer-specific), critical for M&V accuracy and settlement.']
  cancellation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this DR event was cancelled before execution. True indicates the event was cancelled.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the DR event was cancelled, used for operational review and reporting.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the DR event was cancelled, if cancellation_flag is True.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this DR event met all regulatory and contractual compliance requirements (notification timing, curtailment delivery, reporting).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this DR event record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  dispatch_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated the DR event dispatch (e.g., ADMS, DERMS, EMS, manual dispatch console), used for system integration tracking.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the DR event was dispatched or issued to participants. This is the official event start trigger time.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The planned duration of the DR event in minutes, calculated as the difference between scheduled_start_timestamp and scheduled_end_timestamp.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number for the DR event, used for external communication and reporting to ISO/RTO and customers.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DR event: scheduled (future event), active (currently dispatched), completed (finished successfully), cancelled (terminated before start), aborted (terminated during execution), or pending (awaiting approval).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DR event based on its purpose and trigger: emergency (grid reliability), economic (price-driven), test (program validation), scheduled (planned curtailment), voluntary (opt-in), or mandatory (contractual obligation).']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic extent of the DR event: system_wide (entire service territory), zone (load zone or pricing zone), substation (specific substation), feeder (distribution feeder), or premise (individual customer site).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this DR event record was last updated, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  lmp_at_dispatch decimal(10,2) [note: 'The locational marginal price in dollars per MWh at the relevant pricing node (PNode) at the time of event dispatch, used for economic DR event valuation.']
  market_product_type varchar [note: 'The wholesale market product associated with this DR event (e.g., day-ahead energy, real-time energy, regulation, spinning reserve, non-spinning reserve), if dispatched through ISO/RTO markets.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special circumstances, or additional context about the DR event execution and outcomes.']
  notification_lead_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The advance notice period in minutes provided to participants between notification and event start, measured as the difference between notification_timestamp and dispatch_timestamp.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when participants were notified of the upcoming DR event, used to calculate notification lead time compliance.']
  opted_out_count varchar [note: 'The number of enrolled customers who opted out of this specific DR event, tracked for program compliance and customer behavior analysis.']
  participant_count varchar [note: 'The total number of customers or premises that participated in this DR event, used for program performance reporting.']
  performance_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'The ratio of actual curtailment to target curtailment expressed as a percentage (0-100), used to measure event effectiveness and participant compliance.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when load curtailment is scheduled to end for this DR event.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when load curtailment is scheduled to begin for this DR event.']
  settlement_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'The final price in dollars per MWh used for customer incentive payment settlement for this DR event, may differ from LMP based on program rules.']
  target_curtailment_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The target load reduction in megawatts (MW) requested for this DR event across all participating customers and assets.']
  temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'The average ambient temperature in degrees Fahrenheit during the DR event, used for weather normalization in M&V calculations.']
  total_incentive_payment decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total incentive payment amount in dollars paid to all participants for this DR event, calculated based on actual curtailment and settlement price.']
  triggering_entity varchar [note: 'The organization or system that initiated the DR event: utility (internal dispatch), ISO/RTO (market operator), aggregator (third-party DR provider), customer (self-curtailment), or system (automated trigger).']
  vpp_dispatch_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this DR event was dispatched as part of a virtual power plant (VPP) aggregation strategy.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Description of the prevailing weather conditions during the DR event (e.g., extreme heat, cold snap, normal), used for baseline adjustment and event analysis.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each DR dispatch event issued by the utility, ISO/RTO, or aggregator, including event type (emergency, economic, test), triggering entity, dispatch timestamp, notification lead time, target curtailment MW, geographic scope (zone, feeder, substation), event duration, cancellation flag, and post-event status. Core operational record for DR event lifecycle management and OMS/ADMS integration.'
}

Table demand.dr_event_participant {
  dr_event_participant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand response event participant record. Primary key for this transactional entity linking a customer, aggregated resource, or Direct Load Control (DLC) device to a specific DR event.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account participating in the DR event. Used for billing, incentive payment processing, and customer-level performance aggregation.']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party aggregator managing this participant resource. Used for wholesale market DR programs and aggregated DER portfolios. Null for utility-direct programs.']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: DR event participants are often DER assets (solar, storage, EVs) enrolled in demand response programs. Real-world DR operations track which specific DER participated in each event for settlement calcu']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Participant-level performance measurement requires DER system technical specifications for baseline calculation and curtailment verification. Device command type and target setpoint validation depend ']
  direct_load_control_device_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Direct Load Control (DLC) device or Distributed Energy Resource (DER) that was dispatched during the event. Applicable for DLC programs, smart thermostats, battery storage, and VPP resources. Null for behavioral DR programs.']
  dr_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer enrollment record that authorized participation in this DR event. Links to the active enrollment in the DR program at the time of event dispatch.']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Compliance reporting and settlement require linking participant response to approved interconnection application for capacity verification. Incentive payment eligibility validation checks interconnect']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: Participant-level performance measurement requires comparing actual response against forecasted baseline load for the event window. Essential for M&V, settlement calculation, and performance ratio det']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: DR dispatch targets specific meter_points for load control. Participant performance tracking, dispatch confirmation, and settlement reporting require identifying the meter_point (carrying interval dat']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical premise location where the DR event participation occurred. Used for geographic analysis, load zone aggregation, and distribution system impact assessment.']
  primary_dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent DR event that this participant record is associated with. Links to the specific dispatch event across all DR program types (DLC, behavioral, interruptible, VPP, wholesale market).']
  to_dr_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event.dr_event_id — Every participant response record must reference the event it responds to. This is the core header-to-line relationship for DR event performance tracking.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the participant device or system acknowledged receipt of the dispatch command. Used to measure communication latency and confirm successful command delivery for DLC and VPP resources.']
  actual_load_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Measured actual load in kilowatts consumed by the participant during the DR event window. Sourced from Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) interval data or SCADA telemetry for real-time measurement.']
  baseline_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate the baseline load for this participant. Common methods include high X of Y days, regression-based, or control group comparison. Must align with program rules and regulatory requirements for Measurement and Verification (M&V).']
  baseline_load_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Calculated baseline load in kilowatts representing what the participant would have consumed absent the DR event. Used as the reference point for measuring actual curtailment performance. Calculated using the baseline methodology specified in the program rules.']
  committed_curtailment_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Load reduction in kilowatts that the participant committed to provide during the DR event. Used for interruptible tariff, VPP, and wholesale market programs where participants bid or contract specific curtailment amounts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this participant event record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  curtailment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration in minutes that the participant actively curtailed load during the DR event. Used for calculating energy reduction (kWh) and validating minimum participation requirements.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the quality and reliability of the meter data used for performance measurement. Flags estimated or missing interval data that may affect settlement accuracy and require manual review.']
  device_command_type varchar [note: 'Type of control command issued to the DLC device or DER during the event. Applicable for direct load control programs. Null for behavioral and interruptible tariff programs.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the DR event dispatch command was issued to this participant. Represents the moment the utility or aggregator initiated the curtailment request or device control signal.']
  energy_reduction_kwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total energy reduction in kilowatt-hours achieved by the participant during the DR event, calculated as measured curtailment (kW) multiplied by duration (hours). Used for program impact reporting and wholesale market settlement.']
  engagement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated engagement score for behavioral DR participants based on notification interaction (open, click, response). Used for segmentation, targeted messaging, and gamification strategies to improve participation rates.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for participant non-response or under-performance during the DR event. Used for root cause analysis, device maintenance prioritization, and program improvement initiatives.']
  incentive_payment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated incentive payment amount in local currency earned by the participant for this DR event based on measured curtailment, program rate structure, and performance ratio. Subject to final settlement and adjustment.']
  incentive_payment_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the incentive payment amount. Supports multi-currency operations for utilities operating across international jurisdictions.']
  incentive_payment_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this participant performance qualifies for incentive payment based on program rules, minimum performance thresholds, and compliance requirements. False indicates disqualification due to non-performance or rule violation.']
  measured_curtailment_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Actual measured load reduction in kilowatts achieved by the participant during the DR event, calculated as baseline load minus actual load. This is the verified performance used for incentive payment calculation and program reporting.']
  mv_method varchar [note: 'Measurement and Verification method applied to validate the participant curtailment performance. Determines the data sources and calculation approach used for performance settlement and regulatory reporting.']
  non_performance_penalty_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a penalty applies to this participant for failing to meet committed curtailment obligations. Applicable for interruptible tariff and wholesale market programs with contractual performance requirements.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel used to notify or dispatch this participant. For behavioral DR: notification method. For DLC: control protocol. Used for engagement analysis and communication effectiveness tracking.']
  notification_delivery_status varchar [note: 'Status of the event notification delivery to the participant for behavioral DR programs. Tracks email, SMS, or mobile app notification delivery confirmation and engagement.']
  opt_out_reason varchar [note: 'Participant-provided reason for opting out of the DR event. Used for customer satisfaction analysis and program design refinement to reduce opt-out frequency.']
  opt_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the participant exercised their opt-out right to decline participation in this specific DR event. Applicable for programs allowing per-event opt-out within contractual limits.']
  participant_to_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Links event participation back to the enrolled resource. Required to validate that participants are enrolled and to track per-enrollment performance history.']
  participant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DR participant type indicating the program mechanism and dispatch method. Determines which performance metrics and measurement methods apply to this participation record.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this participant record within the DR event. Tracks progression from dispatch through acknowledgment, response, and completion or failure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dispatched|acknowledged|responding|completed|failed|opted_out|non_responsive — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  peer_comparison_percentile varchar [note: 'Percentile ranking of this participant performance relative to similar participants in the same program cohort. Used for behavioral DR feedback and social comparison messaging (e.g., You performed better than 75% of your neighbors).']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated penalty amount in local currency assessed against the participant for non-performance or under-performance during the DR event. Applied per contractual terms for interruptible and wholesale market programs.']
  performance_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of measured curtailment to committed curtailment, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.95 for 95% performance). Used to assess participant compliance with contractual obligations and determine penalty or bonus payment eligibility.']
  response_classification varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the participant response quality based on performance ratio thresholds. Used for behavioral segmentation, program effectiveness analysis, and targeted engagement strategies.']
  response_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the participant response concluded, either by event completion, restore command, or participant opt-out. Marks the end of the performance measurement window.']
  response_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the participant began actively responding to the DR event, as measured by load reduction or device state change. Marks the beginning of the performance measurement window.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the financial settlement process for this participant event record. Tracks progression from initial calculation through approval, payment, and any dispute resolution.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the financial settlement for this participant event was finalized and approved for payment processing. Used for payment reconciliation and audit trail.']
  target_setpoint_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target setpoint value. Ensures correct interpretation of device control parameters across different resource types.']
  target_setpoint_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target setpoint value commanded to the device during the DR event (e.g., thermostat temperature in degrees, battery discharge rate in kW). Applicable for DLC and VPP resources with adjustable setpoints.']
  to_dr_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Every participant response must reference the enrollment that authorized participation. Required for incentive eligibility validation and capacity reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this participant event record was last modified. Audit field for tracking changes to performance metrics, settlement status, or data quality adjustments.']

  Note: 'Transactional record linking an enrolled customer, aggregated resource, or DLC device to a specific DR event, serving as the single authoritative response record across ALL DR program types. For direct load control: captures device commands (shed/restore/cycle with target setpoints), acknowledgment status, actual device response, load shed achieved, dispatch duration, and failure reasons. For behavioral DR: captures notification delivery confirmation, peer comparison data, engagement scoring, pre-event baseline consumption, actual consumption during event window, calculated reduction, and response classification. For interruptible tariff, VPP, and wholesale market dispatch: captures committed curtailment (kW), actual measured curtailment (kW), performance ratio, incentive payment eligibility, non-performance penalty flag, and M&V method applied. SSOT for per-resource event performance tracking across all DR program types including DLC device-level response and behavioral DR customer engagement.'
}

Table demand.load_baseline {
  load_baseline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the load baseline calculation record. Primary key for the load baseline entity.']
  baseline_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.model. Business justification: Baselines are calculated using specific forecast models for regulatory compliance. ISO/RTO tariffs and PUC orders require documented baseline methodologies; linking to the model ensures traceability a']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Load baselines must be calculated within control area boundaries for accurate curtailment measurement in ISO/RTO settlements. Control area load forecasts and actual interchange schedules are used to v']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Baseline calculations for DER-backed DR resources must account for the DER asset\'s generation/storage profile to calculate net load impact. ISO/RTO settlement rules (PJM, CAISO) require baselining the']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Baseline calculation for behind-the-meter DER requires system capacity and export capability from interconnection approval. ISO/RTO baseline methodology depends on interconnection configuration (expor']
  dr_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Baselines are calculated per enrolled resource. Required for M&V — you cannot measure curtailment without linking baseline to the enrolled customer/resource.']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific DR event for which this baseline is applicable. Null if this is a standing baseline not tied to a specific event.']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Demand Response program under which this baseline is calculated. Links to the DR program master record.']
  forecast_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.forecast_run. Business justification: Baselines reference the forecast run used for calculation to ensure auditability. Required for ISO/RTO baseline submissions and PUC compliance reviews where baseline calculation inputs must be traceab']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: Baselines are calculated using historical load patterns and must align with load forecasts for accuracy validation, adjustment factor calculation, and weather normalization. Critical for ISO/RTO basel']
  load_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.load_zone. Business justification: Load baselines are calculated at load zone level for zone-level DR dispatch and VVO coordination. Zone-level baseline methodology is required for locational DR settlement and ISO/RTO baseline submissi']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) meter device used to capture interval data for baseline calculation.']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: Baseline calculation engines use meter_point attributes (interval_length_minutes, tou_enabled_flag, load_profile_class, vee_processing_flag) to configure the baseline methodology. PUC reporting and IS']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise where the baseline load is measured. Links to the premise master record.']
  reference_load_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.load_forecast. Business justification: Load baselines are calibrated against load forecasts for the same period to validate baseline accuracy. ISO/RTO M&V methodologies require forecast-to-baseline comparison for weather-adjusted baseline ']
  source_interval_reading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.interval_reading. Business justification: Load baseline is calculated from historical interval readings (baseline_source_days). Linking to the anchor interval_reading record supports audit trails for baseline_methodology validation and regula']
  superseded_by_baseline_load_baseline_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the baseline record that supersedes this one. Null if this is the current active baseline. Used to maintain baseline version history.']
  adjustment_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Multiplicative adjustment factor applied to the baseline to account for day-of conditions (e.g., weather, operational changes). Typically ranges from 0.8 to 1.2. A value of 1.0 indicates no adjustment.']
  adjustment_method varchar [note: 'The method used to adjust the baseline for day-of conditions. Options include none (no adjustment), additive (add/subtract a fixed amount), multiplicative (apply a scaling factor), weather-normalized (adjust based on weather data), or pre-event-hours (adjust based on load in hours immediately before the event).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this baseline was approved for use. Required for regulatory audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who approved this baseline for use in M&V calculations. Required for regulatory audit trail.']
  baseline_calculation_date date [note: 'The date on which this baseline load profile was calculated. Used for version control and audit trail purposes.']
  baseline_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this baseline load profile becomes effective for Measurement and Verification (M&V) purposes.']
  baseline_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this baseline load profile expires and is no longer valid for M&V calculations. Null if the baseline remains valid until superseded.']
  baseline_load_profile_json varchar [note: 'JSON-encoded array of baseline load values (kWh) for each interval within the event window. Each element represents the expected load for a specific time interval. Format: [{"interval_start":"2024-07-15T14:00:00","load_kwh":125.4},{"interval_start":"2024-07-15T14:15:00","load_kwh":128.2}].']
  baseline_methodology varchar [note: 'The methodology used to calculate the customer baseline load. Common methods include 10-of-10 (average of 10 most recent similar days), high-10-of-10 (highest 10 days), mid-8-of-10 (middle 8 of 10), regression-based, weather-adjusted, or customer-specific algorithms approved by the ISO/RTO or PUC.']
  baseline_source_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of dates (YYYY-MM-DD) used as source data for calculating this baseline. Provides traceability to the historical load data used in the calculation.']
  baseline_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the baseline record. Indicates whether the baseline is in draft, pending approval, approved for use, actively in use, superseded by a newer version, or rejected.']
  baseline_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this baseline calculation. Increments with each recalculation or adjustment. Used for audit trail and historical comparison.']
  calculation_engine varchar [note: 'Name and version of the software system or calculation engine used to compute this baseline (e.g., Oracle MDM Baseline Calculator v3.2, Custom Python Engine v1.5). Used for audit and reproducibility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this baseline record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quality score (0-100) indicating the reliability of the input meter data used for baseline calculation. Based on completeness, accuracy, and validation status of interval data.']
  estimation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to estimate missing interval data in the baseline calculation. Options include none (no estimation), linear interpolation, similar-day substitution, regression-based estimation, or carry-forward from previous interval.']
  event_window_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The end timestamp of the DR event window for which this baseline applies. Defines the conclusion of the curtailment measurement period.']
  event_window_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The start timestamp of the DR event window for which this baseline applies. Defines the beginning of the curtailment measurement period.']
  exclusion_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of dates (YYYY-MM-DD) that were excluded from the baseline calculation due to holidays, prior DR events, or anomalous load conditions.']
  interval_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The duration of each interval in the baseline load profile, typically 15 or 60 minutes. Aligns with AMI meter reading intervals.']
  iso_rto_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this baseline was submitted to the ISO/RTO for settlement. Null if not yet submitted.']
  iso_rto_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this baseline has been submitted to the ISO/RTO for settlement purposes. True if submitted, False otherwise.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user or system process that last modified this baseline record. Required for audit trail.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this baseline record was last modified. Updated whenever any field in the record changes.']
  missing_interval_count varchar [note: 'Number of intervals with missing or invalid meter data that were estimated or excluded from the baseline calculation. Used to assess baseline reliability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special circumstances, manual adjustments, or exceptions applied during baseline calculation. Used for audit trail and operational transparency.']
  peak_baseline_demand_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'The maximum demand (kW) observed in the baseline load profile during the event window. Used for capacity-based DR program settlement.']
  puc_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this baseline is included in PUC program performance reporting. True if included, False otherwise.']
  temperature_sensitivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer\'s load is temperature-sensitive and requires weather-adjusted baseline methodology. True if temperature-sensitive, False otherwise.']
  to_dr_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Baselines are calculated per enrolled resource. Without this FK, you cannot match baselines to the correct customer enrollment for M&V calculations.']
  total_baseline_energy_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'The sum of all interval baseline load values across the entire event window, expressed in kilowatt-hours (kWh). Used for aggregate curtailment measurement.']
  weather_station_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the weather station used for weather-adjusted baseline calculations. Links to external weather data sources (e.g., NOAA, local meteorological services).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user or system process that created this baseline record. Required for audit trail.']

  Note: 'Calculated customer baseline load profile used as the reference point for measuring curtailment during DR events. Stores baseline methodology (e.g., 10-of-10, regression, weather-adjusted), baseline interval values (kWh per 15-min or hourly), calculation date, applicable event window, adjustment factors, and version history. SSOT for M&V baseline data required by ISO/RTO settlement and PUC program reporting.'
}

Table demand.curtailment_measurement {
  curtailment_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the curtailment measurement record. Primary key for post-event measurement records capturing actual load reduction achieved during a Demand Response (DR) event interval.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Measurement & verification data serves as primary audit evidence for DR performance audits. Essential for M&V audit support, compliance documentation, and providing granular interval data to auditors ']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Curtailment M&V for DER resources requires linking measurements to the specific DER asset for REC adjustment calculations (curtailed generation doesn\'t earn RECs), PPA settlement impact analysis, and ']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Measurement & verification of curtailment performance requires DER system nameplate capacity, inverter specifications, and export limits from interconnection approval. Settlement quantity calculation ']
  dr_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enrolled DR resource (customer premise, aggregated load zone, or DER asset) that participated in the curtailment event.']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent DR event during which this curtailment was measured. Links to the DR event dispatch record that triggered the load reduction.']
  interval_reading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.interval_reading. Business justification: DR M&V process compares actual metered interval readings against the load baseline to compute curtailment_kw. Linking curtailment_measurement to the specific interval_reading record enables settlement']
  lmp_price_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the LMP pricing node or zone where this curtailment resource is located. Used for wholesale market settlement and valuation of the load reduction at the nodal or zonal LMP.']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: M&V processes compare actual curtailment against forecasted load to calculate performance and settlement quantities. Required for ISO/RTO settlement, incentive payment calculation, and regulatory comp']
  load_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.load_zone. Business justification: Curtailment measurements are validated against load zone SCADA data for locational M&V and settlement. Zone-level curtailment measurement is required for ISO/RTO locational settlement and PUC-mandated']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the meter or metering point from which interval data was sourced for this measurement. May be an AMI meter, SCADA point, or aggregated virtual meter.']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: ISO/RTO M&V settlement requires linking curtailment measurements to the specific meter_point (which carries interval data collection flags, load profile class, TOU settings) to validate measured curta']
  primary_curtailment_dr_event_participant_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event_participant.dr_event_participant_id — Curtailment measurements are interval-level detail records for a participant\'s event performance. Required for M&V settlement calculations.']
  primary_curtailment_load_baseline_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.load_baseline.load_baseline_id — Each curtailment measurement references the baseline used for comparison. Required for auditable M&V calculations.']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.scada_point. Business justification: Curtailment measurements for DR M&V are validated against SCADA telemetry at the customer metering point. ISO/RTO M&V protocols and FERC Order 745 require SCADA-validated curtailment data; linking to ']
  settlement_pnode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.pnode. Business justification: Curtailment measurements are settled against the LMP at the customer\'s registered pnode. ISO/RTO M&V protocols and FERC settlement rules require each curtailment interval to reference the settlement p']
  to_dr_event_participant_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event_participant.dr_event_participant_id — Curtailment measurements are taken per participant per event. This FK links measured load reduction to the specific participant response record.']
  to_load_baseline_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.load_baseline.load_baseline_id — Each curtailment measurement references the baseline used to calculate the reduction. Required for M&V audit trail.']
  vee_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.vee_rule. Business justification: curtailment_measurement.vee_flag and vee_method_code indicate VEE validation is applied to curtailment data. Linking to the specific vee_rule applied supports regulatory M&V audit trails, ISO/RTO sett']
  baseline_adjustment_factor decimal(8,4) [note: 'Multiplicative adjustment factor applied to the baseline to account for day-of conditions (weather, operational changes). Typically ranges from 0.8 to 1.2. A value of 1.0 indicates no adjustment.']
  baseline_demand_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated baseline power demand in kilowatts (kW) representing what the resource would have demanded absent the DR event. Used as the reference for curtailment calculation.']
  baseline_load_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated baseline energy consumption in kilowatt-hours (kWh) representing what the resource would have consumed absent the DR event. Calculated using the baseline methodology specified in the DR program rules.']
  baseline_method_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the baseline calculation methodology applied for this measurement. Common methods include High X of Y (e.g., average of highest 3 of last 10 similar days), Mid X of Y, regression-based, control group, meter-before, or customer-provided baseline.']
  bid_quantity_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'The bid quantity in kilowatts (kW) submitted by the resource or aggregator for this DR event interval. May differ from enrolled_capacity_kw if the resource bid a different amount for this specific event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this curtailment measurement record was first created in the system. Represents the initial calculation or data ingestion time.']
  curtailment_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated load curtailment achieved during the interval in kilowatts (kW). Computed as baseline_demand_kw minus metered_demand_kw. Positive values indicate successful load reduction; negative values indicate load increase.']
  curtailment_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated energy curtailment achieved during the interval in kilowatt-hours (kWh). Computed as baseline_load_kwh minus metered_load_kwh. Used for settlement and incentive payment calculation.']
  data_quality_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the quality status of the metered data. ACTUAL indicates raw meter data; ESTIMATED indicates VEE-filled gaps; VALIDATED indicates passed quality checks; SUSPECT indicates potential data issues; MISSING indicates no data received; REJECTED indicates failed validation.']
  data_source_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source of the metered load data. Values include AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure), SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition), MANUAL (manual meter read), ESTIMATED (VEE-estimated), AGGREGATED (sum of multiple meters), or TELEMETRY (real-time sensor data).']
  enrolled_capacity_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'The enrolled or nominated load reduction capacity in kilowatts (kW) for this resource during the DR event. Represents the committed curtailment target used for performance evaluation and settlement.']
  incentive_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated incentive payment amount in currency for this measurement interval. Computed as settlement_quantity_kw multiplied by incentive_rate_per_kwh and interval duration. Subject to program caps and adjustments.']
  incentive_rate_per_kwh decimal(18,6) [note: 'The incentive payment rate in currency per kilowatt-hour ($/kWh) applicable to this measurement interval. Rate may vary by time-of-use period, event type, or performance tier.']
  interval_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration of the measurement interval in minutes. Common values are 5, 15, or 60 minutes depending on ISO/RTO market settlement rules and meter data granularity.']
  interval_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the measurement interval during the DR event. Defines the boundary of the measurement period for load curtailment calculation.']
  interval_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the measurement interval during the DR event. Typically aligned to 5-minute, 15-minute, or hourly intervals per ISO/RTO settlement rules.']
  iso_rto_market_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the ISO/RTO wholesale market to which this curtailment measurement is reported for settlement. Values include CAISO, ERCOT, ISONE, MISO, NYISO, PJM, SPP, or NONE for retail-only programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CAISO|ERCOT|ISONE|MISO|NYISO|PJM|SPP|NONE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lmp_price_per_mwh decimal(18,6) [note: 'The Locational Marginal Price in currency per megawatt-hour ($/MWh) at the resource location during this interval. Used to value the curtailment for wholesale market settlement or avoided cost calculation.']
  measurement_status_code varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the curtailment measurement record. PRELIMINARY indicates initial calculation; VALIDATED indicates passed M&V review; SETTLED indicates used for payment calculation; DISPUTED indicates under review; ADJUSTED indicates corrected after dispute; FINAL indicates locked for regulatory reporting.']
  metered_demand_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual power demand measured during the interval in kilowatts (kW). Represents the average or peak demand during the measurement period depending on metering configuration.']
  metered_load_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual energy consumption measured during the interval in kilowatt-hours (kWh). Sourced from AMI meter data, SCADA telemetry, or estimated via Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) process.']
  mv_method_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Measurement and Verification (M&V) protocol applied to validate the curtailment calculation. Common protocols include IPMVP Options A-D (International Performance Measurement and Verification Protocol) or utility-specific custom methods.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Penalty amount in currency assessed for under-performance or non-compliance during this interval. Applied when actual curtailment falls below minimum performance thresholds defined in the DR program agreement.']
  performance_ratio decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of actual curtailment achieved to the enrolled capacity or bid quantity for this resource during the interval. Calculated as curtailment_kw divided by enrolled_capacity_kw. Values above 1.0 indicate over-performance; below 1.0 indicate under-performance.']
  reported_to_iso_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this measurement has been reported to the ISO/RTO for wholesale market settlement. True indicates the record was included in the ISO/RTO performance report; False indicates retail-only or not yet reported.']
  reported_to_iso_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this measurement was submitted to the ISO/RTO for wholesale market settlement. Used for tracking reporting deadlines and settlement cycles.']
  reported_to_puc_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this measurement has been included in regulatory reporting to the state Public Utility Commission (PUC). True indicates the record was included in DR program performance reports; False indicates not yet reported.']
  settlement_quantity_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'The final settled curtailment quantity in kilowatts (kW) used for incentive payment calculation. May differ from curtailment_kw after applying performance thresholds, caps, or adjustments per program rules.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this curtailment measurement record was last modified. Tracks revisions due to data corrections, dispute resolution, or re-calculation with updated baseline methods.']
  validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this measurement record was validated and approved for settlement. Marks the transition from preliminary to validated status in the measurement lifecycle.']
  validation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting validation findings, data quality issues, adjustments applied, or dispute resolution details for this measurement record. Used for audit trail and M&V documentation.']
  vee_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the metered data was processed through Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) logic. True indicates VEE was applied to fill gaps or correct suspect data; False indicates raw meter data was used.']
  weather_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage at the resource location during the measurement interval. Used for baseline adjustment in weather-sensitive load profiles.']
  weather_temperature_f decimal(6,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature in degrees Fahrenheit at the resource location during the measurement interval. Used for baseline adjustment and performance analysis. May be sourced from weather station data or utility weather service.']

  Note: 'Post-event measurement record capturing actual load curtailment achieved by an enrolled resource during a DR event interval. Stores interval-level metered load (kWh/kW), baseline load, calculated curtailment (kW), data source (AMI, SCADA, estimated), validation status, VEE flags, and M&V method applied. Feeds DR settlement, incentive payment calculation, and ISO/RTO performance reporting.'
}

Table demand.dr_incentive_payment {
  dr_incentive_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand response incentive payment transaction. Primary key for the DR incentive payment record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with this incentive payment for billing integration.']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand response aggregator managing the customer enrollment and receiving payment on behalf of enrolled customers.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Incentive payments are audited for accuracy and regulatory compliance, especially for cost recovery in rate cases. Required for financial audit trail, settlement verification, and demonstrating pruden']
  capacity_peak_demand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.peak_demand. Business justification: Capacity payments are calculated based on peak demand forecasts for the planning period. Links incentive calculations to the forecasted peak used in capacity market clearing, essential for ISO/RTO cap']
  dr_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Payments are issued to enrolled customers/aggregators. This FK links the payment to the enrollment record for customer identification and payment routing.']
  energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.energy_price. Business justification: Payment calculations for energy-based DR programs use LMP forecasts for valuation and settlement pricing. Essential for economic dispatch decisions, participant compensation, and cost-effectiveness an']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Payment eligibility verification requires confirmed interconnection approval status. Capacity payments are tied to interconnection-approved export capacity limits. ISO/RTO settlement reconciliation va']
  meter_read_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_read. Business justification: Incentive payment calculation is substantiated by verified meter reads confirming energy_curtailed_kwh. Payment disputes, reconciliation_status resolution, and regulatory audits require tracing the gr']
  pnode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.pnode. Business justification: DR incentive payments are calculated using LMP at the customer\'s registered pnode. The existing `pnode_identifier` is a denormalized plain-text reference; replacing it with a proper FK to transmission']
  primary_dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific demand response event that triggered this incentive payment.']
  primary_dr_event_participant_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event_participant.dr_event_participant_id — Incentive payments are calculated based on participant performance in events. Required for financial reconciliation and customer payment accuracy.']
  primary_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand response program under which this payment is made (e.g., CPP, TOU, direct load control, interruptible tariff).']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer receiving the incentive payment. May be null if payment is made to an aggregator on behalf of multiple customers.']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: DR incentive payments are administered per service territory for regulatory cost recovery filings and rate case support. PUC requires territory-level DR cost reporting; utility finance systems allocat']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event. Business justification: DR incentive payments during storm events may have special settlement rules, bonus structures, or cost allocation to storm restoration budgets. Storm attribution is essential for regulatory cost recov']
  to_dr_event_participant_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event_participant.dr_event_participant_id — Incentive payments are calculated based on participant event performance. This FK links financial records to the performance data that generated them.']
  to_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Financial settlements must reference the program for rate structure, incentive rules, and regulatory reporting.']
  actual_load_kw decimal(15,3) [note: 'The measured actual load in kilowatts during the demand response event. Used to calculate curtailment performance.']
  aggregator_share_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the payment allocated to the aggregator based on the aggregator share percentage.']
  aggregator_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the gross payment retained by the aggregator as commission for program management and customer enrollment services.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incentive payment was approved for disbursement by authorized personnel.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who approved this incentive payment for disbursement.']
  baseline_load_kw decimal(15,3) [note: 'The calculated baseline load in kilowatts representing the customers expected consumption without demand response curtailment. Used for measurement and verification (M&V) calculations.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the calculation methodology used for this payment, including baseline calculation method, M&V approach, and rate application logic.']
  capacity_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The calculated capacity payment amount in dollars based on enrolled capacity and capacity rate. Represents payment for availability.']
  capacity_rate_per_kw decimal(12,4) [note: 'The contracted capacity payment rate in dollars per kilowatt for the enrolled demand response capacity. Basis for capacity payment calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incentive payment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this payment record (e.g., USD, CAD).']
  curtailment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration in minutes that the customer maintained load curtailment during the demand response event.']
  customer_net_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net payment amount in dollars disbursed to the customer after aggregator share and penalty deductions.']
  disbursement_date date [note: 'Date on which the incentive payment was disbursed to the customer or aggregator.']
  disbursement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for the payment disbursement transaction (e.g., check number, wire confirmation, EFT trace number) for reconciliation with financial systems.']
  energy_curtailed_kwh decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total energy curtailed in kilowatt-hours during the settlement period, calculated as measured curtailment multiplied by duration. Used for energy payment calculations.']
  energy_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The calculated energy payment amount in dollars based on measured curtailment and energy rate or LMP. Represents payment for actual performance.']
  energy_rate_per_kwh decimal(12,6) [note: 'The contracted energy payment rate in dollars per kilowatt-hour for verified load curtailment. Used for retail-level energy payment calculations.']
  enrolled_capacity_kw decimal(15,3) [note: 'The committed demand response capacity in kilowatts that the customer or aggregator enrolled for this settlement period. Basis for capacity payments.']
  gross_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross incentive payment amount in dollars before aggregator share and deductions. Sum of capacity, energy, and bonus payments.']
  iso_rto_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ISO or RTO wholesale market from which this payment originates (e.g., PJM, CAISO, ERCOT, NYISO, ISO-NE, MISO, SPP). Null for retail-only programs.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incentive payment record was last updated, reflecting the most recent change to payment status, amounts, or reconciliation status.']
  lmp_price_per_mwh decimal(12,4) [note: 'The locational marginal price in dollars per megawatt-hour at the pricing node (PNode) during the demand response event. Used for wholesale market energy payment calculations.']
  measured_curtailment_kw decimal(15,3) [note: 'The verified load reduction in kilowatts achieved during the demand response event, calculated as baseline load minus actual load. Basis for energy payments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding special circumstances, adjustments, disputes, or other relevant information about this incentive payment.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the incentive payment is disbursed: bill credit applied to customer account, direct deposit to bank account, physical check, wire transfer, or settlement through aggregator.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for this incentive payment transaction, used for customer communication and payment tracking.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the incentive payment: pending calculation (awaiting M&V), pending approval (awaiting management review), approved (ready for disbursement), disbursed (payment issued), reconciled (matched with wholesale settlement), disputed (under review), or cancelled (voided). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending_calculation|pending_approval|approved|disbursed|reconciled|disputed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the incentive payment type: capacity payment for enrolled load, energy payment for actual curtailment, performance bonus for exceeding targets, penalty deduction for non-performance, reconciliation adjustment for settlement corrections, or aggregator commission for program management.']
  penalty_deduction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Penalty amount in dollars deducted from payment for non-performance, failure to curtail, or non-compliance with program requirements.']
  performance_bonus_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional incentive payment in dollars awarded for exceeding curtailment targets or achieving exceptional performance metrics.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of reconciliation between retail incentive payment and wholesale market settlement: not reconciled, reconciled (matched), variance identified (discrepancy found), under review (investigating), or resolved (variance corrected).']
  settlement_period_end timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the settlement period covered by this incentive payment. Defines the billing cycle or market settlement window.']
  settlement_period_start timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the settlement period covered by this incentive payment. Aligns with ISO/RTO settlement intervals for wholesale market payments.']
  settlement_statement_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-reference to the ISO/RTO wholesale market settlement statement from which this payment originates. Used for reconciliation with wholesale market settlements.']
  utility_net_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net cost to the utility in dollars for this demand response incentive payment after accounting for wholesale market revenue offsets and ISO/RTO settlements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of all DR financial settlements and incentive payments, serving as the single authoritative financial record for the demand domain. Covers wholesale market settlement (ISO/RTO capacity and energy payments, LMP-based calculations, performance adjustments, settlement statement references, settlement period reconciliation) and retail-level incentive disbursements (capacity payments, energy payments, performance bonuses, penalty deductions). Captures settlement period, calculation basis, enrolled capacity, measured curtailment, gross and net amounts, aggregator share, utility net cost, payment approval status, disbursement reference, reconciliation status, and ISO/RTO settlement statement cross-reference. SSOT for ALL DR financial obligations — wholesale settlement, retail incentives, aggregator payments, customer disbursements, and settlement reconciliation with wholesale markets.'
}

Business justification: Aggregator contracts and capacity commitments are structured around utility planning periods. Business justification: Aggregators are licensed to operate within specific service territories under PUC-approved agreements. The existing plain-text \'service_territory\' column is a denormalized representation; replacing wi']
  aggregation_platform varchar [note: 'Name or description of the technology platform or system used by the aggregator to manage and dispatch aggregated customer load.']

  contracted_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total contracted demand response capacity in megawatts that the aggregator has committed to provide under the service agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the aggregator record was first created in the system, capturing the initial registration date.']

  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update or modification to the aggregator record, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  maximum_event_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable duration in hours for a single demand response event under the aggregator service agreement.']
  maximum_events_per_year varchar [note: 'Maximum number of demand response events that can be called per calendar year under the aggregator service agreement.']
  measurement_verification_protocol varchar [note: 'Specific measurement and verification protocol or standard applied to validate demand response performance and calculate incentive payments.']
  minimum_notice_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum advance notice time in hours required before a demand response event can be dispatched to the aggregator.']
  next_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance review or audit of the aggregators operations and performance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the aggregator relationship and operations.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms for incentive payments and settlements with the aggregator, such as net-30 or net-60 days.']
  performance_bond_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar value of the performance bond or financial guarantee posted by the aggregator to ensure compliance with contractual capacity commitments and performance obligations.']
  performance_bond_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current performance bond or financial guarantee, after which the bond must be renewed or replaced.']
  performance_penalty_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Financial penalty rate applied per MW of underperformance when the aggregator fails to deliver committed demand response capacity during an event.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which demand response performance settlements and incentive payments are calculated and paid to the aggregator.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Federal tax identification number (EIN or TIN) for the aggregator legal entity, used for financial reporting and tax compliance.']
  telemetry_interval_minutes varchar [note: 'Frequency in minutes at which telemetry data must be transmitted from aggregated customer sites to the utility or ISO/RTO (e.g., 5-minute intervals).']
  telemetry_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time telemetry data from aggregated customer sites is required for dispatch and settlement purposes.']

  Note: 'Master record for third-party demand response aggregators and curtailment service providers (CSPs) registered to aggregate customer load for ISO/RTO wholesale DR markets. Captures aggregator legal entity, FERC/ISO registration ID, service territory, aggregation technology platform, contractual capacity commitment (MW), performance bond, compliance status, and active program associations. SSOT for aggregator identity and contractual management.'
}

Table demand.interruptible_tariff_agreement {

  billing_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_service_agreement. Business justification: An interruptible tariff agreement governs bill credits applied when a customer\'s load is curtailed. The billing service agreement is the billing contract to which those credits are posted. Utility bil']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.feeder. Business justification: Interruptible tariff agreements specify the serving feeder for interruption dispatch sequencing and SAIDI/SAIFI reliability reporting. Feeder-level interruptible load tracking is required for distribu']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter. Business justification: Interruptible tariff agreements are tied to specific meters measuring the interruptible load. Compliance testing (last_compliance_test_date), interruption verification, and billing credit calculation ']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: Interruptible tariff administration requires the meter_point for rate_schedule_code validation, TOU period alignment, and interval data collection configuration. Tariff compliance reporting and interr']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Tariff agreements must comply with approved tariff schedules filed with PUCs. Essential for tariff compliance validation, rate case support, and ensuring interruptible rate offerings match regulatory ']
  planning_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.planning_period. Business justification: Tariff agreements are structured within planning periods for capacity credit and rate design. Links interruptible load contracts to IRP planning cycles, enabling capacity value analysis and tariff cos']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise where the interruptible load is located. Identifies the physical location subject to load curtailment.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Interruptible tariff agreements require PUC approval through tariff filings and rate case proceedings for rate design, terms, and cost recovery. Links agreement to authorizing regulatory filing for ta']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: Interruptible tariff agreements are regulated per service territory; tariff schedule codes, regulatory compliance reporting, and rate case support all require service territory association. PUC-approv']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the interruptible tariff agreement. Used in customer communications, billing statements, and regulatory filings.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interruptible service agreement based on firmness and curtailment conditions. Determines the priority and conditions under which load may be interrupted.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews at expiration unless either party provides notice of termination. True for evergreen agreements, false for fixed-term agreements.']
  baseline_calculation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to calculate the customer\'s baseline load for measurement and verification (M&V) of curtailment performance. Determines how actual load reduction is measured against expected load.']
  bill_credit_rate_per_kw decimal(12,4) [note: 'The monthly bill credit rate in dollars per kilowatt ($/kW) that the customer receives for enrolling contracted interruptible capacity. Compensates the customer for maintaining load curtailment capability.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the customer with respect to their interruptible tariff obligations. Reflects whether the customer has successfully curtailed load during recent dispatch events.']
  contracted_interruptible_load_kw decimal(15,3) [note: 'The committed load capacity in kilowatts (kW) that the customer agrees to curtail upon utility request. This is the baseline interruptible capacity used for billing credits and compliance measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interruptible tariff agreement record was first created in the system. Establishes the record creation audit trail.']
  dispatch_priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for dispatching this interruptible load resource relative to other demand response resources. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for dispatch.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the interruptible tariff agreement becomes binding and the utility gains the contractual right to curtail load. Marks the start of the agreement term.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the interruptible tariff agreement expires and the utility no longer has the right to curtail load under this agreement. Nullable for evergreen agreements subject to annual renewal.']
  interruptible_tariff_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interruptible tariff agreement. Indicates whether the agreement is currently in force and eligible for dispatch.']
  interruption_notice_period_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum advance notice time in minutes that the utility must provide before initiating a load curtailment event. Defines the customer\'s preparation window.']
  iso_rto_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interruptible load capacity is enrolled in an ISO (Independent System Operator) or RTO (Regional Transmission Organization) wholesale demand response program. True if enrolled in wholesale market programs.']
  iso_rto_resource_code varchar [note: 'The unique resource identifier assigned by the ISO (Independent System Operator) or RTO (Regional Transmission Organization) for this interruptible load in wholesale market programs. Nullable if not enrolled in ISO/RTO programs.']
  last_compliance_test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance test or actual curtailment event used to verify the customer\'s ability to meet their contracted interruptible load obligation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this interruptible tariff agreement record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  maximum_annual_interruption_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'The maximum total number of hours per year that the utility may interrupt service under this agreement. Protects the customer from excessive curtailment and defines the upper bound of utility dispatch rights.']
  maximum_curtailment_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The maximum duration in hours for a single load curtailment event. Protects the customer from excessively long interruptions.']
  maximum_events_per_year varchar [note: 'The maximum number of discrete curtailment events that the utility may dispatch in a calendar year under this agreement. Limits the frequency of customer disruption.']
  minimum_curtailment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The minimum duration in minutes for which a load curtailment event must be sustained once initiated. Ensures operational effectiveness of demand response dispatch.']
  non_compliance_penalty_rate_per_kw decimal(12,4) [note: 'The penalty rate in dollars per kilowatt ($/kW) assessed when the customer fails to curtail the contracted load during a dispatch event. Enforces contractual compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional terms, conditions, special provisions, or operational notes related to this interruptible tariff agreement.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when the interruptible tariff agreement was executed by both the utility and the customer. Establishes the legal binding date of the contract.']
  tariff_agreement_to_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Interruptible tariff agreements formalize the enrollment of large C&I customers. Required to link contractual obligations to program participation.']
  tariff_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific interruptible service tariff schedule under which this agreement operates. References the PUC (Public Utility Commission) approved rate schedule.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the agreement. Defines the notice period for voluntary termination.']
  to_dr_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Interruptible tariff agreements are linked to specific DR enrollments for the customer. Required for tariff compliance tracking.']
  year_to_date_event_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of discrete curtailment events dispatched under this agreement in the current calendar year. Tracked against the maximum events per year limit.']
  year_to_date_interruption_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Cumulative total hours of load interruption dispatched under this agreement in the current calendar year. Tracked against the maximum annual interruption hours limit.']

  Note: 'Master record for interruptible service tariff agreements between the utility and large commercial/industrial customers, capturing tariff schedule reference, contracted interruptible load (kW), maximum annual interruption hours, interruption notice period, bill credit rate ($/kW), penalty for non-compliance, agreement effective and expiration dates, and current compliance status. SSOT for interruptible tariff obligations and load curtailment rights.'
}

Table demand.direct_load_control_device {

Device commissioning requires DER system interconnection approval and IEEE 1547 protection settings. Control strate']

  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: DLC devices are deployed and controlled by geographic zone for targeted load management during peak periods. Zone-level control enables localized demand reduction for distribution constraint managemen']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the electric meter serving the premise where the DLC device is installed. Used for baseline calculation and measurement and verification of load curtailment.']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Device dispatch, curtailment verificati']
  nerc_cip_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.nerc_cip_asset.
