Essential for CIP compliance, cyber security asset inventor']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical premise where the DLC device is installed. Used for geographic analysis, outage correlation, and multi-device premise management.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: DLC devices are utility-owned physical assets requiring lifecycle management: depreciation tracking, maintenance scheduling, warranty management, capital cost allocation to rate base. Asset registry i']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: DLC device program eligibility, regulatory reporting, and rate schedule compliance require service territory association. Utility DLC programs are administered per service territory under PUC-approved']
  average_curtailment_kw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Average measured load reduction in kilowatts achieved by this device during DR events. Calculated from baseline and actual load measurements for M&V reporting.']
  baseline_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate the customer baseline load for this device. Determines how curtailment is measured and verified for incentive payments.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Primary communication protocol used by the DLC device to receive control signals and report status. Determines network infrastructure requirements and latency characteristics.']
  control_strategy varchar [note: 'The control algorithm applied to this device during DR events. Defines how the device modulates load to achieve curtailment while minimizing customer comfort impact.']
  controlled_load_type varchar [note: 'Type of electrical load that the DLC device controls. Used for load forecasting, curtailment planning, and customer comfort impact assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hvac_cooling|hvac_heating|water_heater|ev_charger|pool_pump|battery_storage|general_appliance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DLC device record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the DLC device was permanently removed from service. Used for asset lifecycle tracking and program capacity reporting.']
  decommission_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the device was decommissioned. Used for failure analysis, vendor performance evaluation, and program improvement.']
  device_ownership varchar [note: 'Indicates who owns the physical DLC device. Determines maintenance responsibility, asset capitalization, and end-of-life disposition.']
  device_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the DLC device. Used for warranty tracking, field service identification, and device authentication.']
  dlc_device_to_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — DLC devices are installed at enrolled customer premises. This FK links the device to the enrollment for dispatch targeting and performance tracking.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the device was enrolled in the current DR program. Used for program participation tracking and incentive eligibility determination.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current enrollment status of the device in the DR program. Determines whether the device can be dispatched during DR events and is eligible for incentive payments.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware version installed on the DLC device. Critical for security patch management, feature availability, and protocol compatibility.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the DLC device was physically installed at the customer premise. Used for warranty period calculation and device lifecycle tracking.']
  iso_rto_resource_code varchar [note: 'Resource identifier assigned by the ISO/RTO for wholesale market participation. Used for market settlement and capacity credit tracking.']
  last_communication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful communication between the DLC device and the utility control system. Used for device health monitoring and connectivity issue detection.']
  last_control_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the device last responded to a DR control signal. Used for performance verification and device responsiveness tracking.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance or service activity performed on the device. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling and device reliability tracking.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DLC device record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  max_curtailment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum number of consecutive minutes the device can be curtailed during a single DR event. Enforces customer comfort limits and program rules.']
  network_address varchar [note: 'IP address, MAC address, or protocol-specific network identifier for the DLC device. Used for device provisioning, network troubleshooting, and security monitoring.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the next preventive maintenance activity is scheduled for the device. Used for field service planning and resource allocation.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the DLC device. Determines whether the device is available for DR event dispatch and eligible for incentive payments.']
  opt_out_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is permitted to opt out of individual DR events. Determines event dispatch logic and customer notification requirements.']
  opt_out_count_ytd varchar [note: 'Number of times the customer has opted out of DR events in the current calendar year. Used to enforce program opt-out limits and track customer engagement.']
  rated_load_kw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nameplate rated power consumption of the controlled load in kilowatts. Used for calculating total enrolled capacity and expected curtailment during DR events.']
  security_certificate_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the digital security certificate used for encrypted communication. Critical for maintaining secure command and control of DLC devices.']
  telemetry_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'Frequency in seconds at which the device reports status and operational data. Determines data granularity for M&V and real-time monitoring.']
  to_dr_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — DLC devices are installed at customer premises under a specific DR enrollment. Required for device-to-program mapping during event dispatch.']
  total_curtailment_hours_ytd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative hours of load curtailment delivered by this device in the current calendar year. Used for incentive calculation and program impact reporting.']
  total_events_dispatched_ytd varchar [note: 'Total number of DR events this device has been dispatched for in the current calendar year. Used for program performance reporting and customer communication.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty for the DLC device expires. Used for maintenance planning and replacement cost forecasting.']

  Note: 'Master record for utility-owned or utility-managed direct load control (DLC) devices installed at customer premises, including smart thermostats, load control switches, water heater controllers, and EV charger controllers. Captures device type, manufacturer, firmware version, installation date, controlled load type (HVAC, water heater, EV), rated load (kW), communication protocol (AMI, RF, cellular), operational status, and last communication timestamp.'
}

Table demand.vpp_dispatch {
  vpp_dispatch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the VPP dispatch instruction. Primary key for the VPP dispatch transaction.']
  agc_signal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.agc_signal. Business justification: VPP resources provide regulation ancillary services coordinated with AGC. The AGC signal drives VPP dispatch instructions for frequency regulation. ISO/RTO ancillary service settlement and NERC BAL-00']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party aggregator or utility entity managing the VPP portfolio and coordinating DER dispatch.']
  binding_constraint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.constraint. Business justification: VPP dispatches are triggered by binding transmission constraints in real-time operations. Grid operators and ISO/RTOs require each VPP dispatch to reference the specific constraint it was activated to']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: VPP dispatches provide regulation reserves and frequency response within specific control areas. Link required for AGC signal coordination, ACE management, and balancing authority settlement. Control ']
  coordinated_generation_dispatch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.generation_dispatch. Business justification: VPP dispatch is coordinated with conventional generation dispatch in EMS for economic dispatch optimization. Grid operators need to trace VPP demand-side dispatch alongside generation dispatch instruc']
  der_interconnection_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.der_interconnection_point. Business justification: VPP dispatch commands target specific DER interconnection points for real-time control, telemetry validation, and settlement. Critical for DERMS operations, IEEE 1547 compliance, and market participat']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: VPP dispatch instructions target specific DER assets within the aggregation for real-time control. Operations require linking dispatch commands to individual DER registry entries for SCADA/DERMS integ']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: VPP dispatch commands target specific DER systems for aggregated response. DERMS integration requires system-level interconnection specifications, telemetry configuration, and communication protocol d']
  dispatch_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to generation.dispatch_schedule. Business justification: Virtual power plant dispatches are coordinated with generation dispatch schedules by ISO/RTO for integrated grid balancing. Operators track both supply-side and demand-side dispatch instructions for r']
  dr_capacity_registration_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_capacity_registration.dr_capacity_registration_id — VPP dispatches target registered DER portfolios. This FK links the dispatch instruction to the capacity registration for performance tracking and settlement.']
  energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.energy_price. Business justification: VPP dispatch optimization uses energy price forecasts to maximize revenue and minimize costs in wholesale markets. Essential for economic dispatch decisions, market bidding strategies, and real-time d']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.feeder. Business justification: VPP dispatches target specific feeders for congestion relief and voltage support; feeder-level VPP dispatch tracking is required for ADMS/DERMS integration, post-dispatch reliability reporting, and fe']
  forecast_generation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.forecast_generation. Business justification: VPP dispatch coordinates with generation forecasts (especially renewable DERs) to balance supply-demand and optimize bidding. Critical for VPP portfolio optimization, renewable integration, and ancill']
  forecast_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.forecast_run. Business justification: VPP dispatches reference the specific forecast run for real-time optimization and post-event analysis. Critical for DERMS integration where dispatch decisions are based on 5-minute or 15-minute foreca']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: VPP dispatch decisions require real-time and short-term load forecasts to optimize DER dispatch and market participation. Essential for economic dispatch, grid services provision, and wholesale market']
  load_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.load_zone. Business justification: VPP dispatch is organized around load zones for volt-var optimization and locational capacity relief. Load zone is the primary dispatch geography for VPP operations in distribution — required for VVO-']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: VPP dispatch targets specific meter_points where DER output is measured. Dispatch performance tracking (actual_mw vs target_mw), settlement_amount calculation, and DERMS integration require linking ea']
  peak_demand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.peak_demand. Business justification: VPP dispatches during peak demand events track the forecasted peak for capacity market settlement. Links dispatch to the peak forecast used in ISO/RTO capacity performance obligations, essential for c']
  primary_vpp_dr_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event.dr_event_id — VPP dispatches occur in the context of DR events or market signals. Required to correlate VPP actions with overall DR event management.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: VPP dispatch targets specific distribution assets (substations, feeders, transformers) for locational demand response to defer capital upgrades, manage thermal constraints, and provide voltage support']
  relief_path_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.path. Business justification: VPP dispatches are executed to relieve transmission path congestion. ISO/RTO settlement and congestion cost allocation require linking each VPP dispatch to the specific transmission path it relieved, ']
  state_estimation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.state_estimation_run. Business justification: VPP dispatch targets are determined using the real-time grid state from state estimation. EMS operators require VPP dispatch records to reference the state estimation snapshot used, supporting post-di']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event. Business justification: VPP dispatches during major storms provide critical grid stabilization. Storm context is required for settlement (storm-related dispatch may have premium pricing), performance tracking, and cost recov']
  to_dr_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event.dr_event_id — VPP dispatches may be coordinated with or triggered by DR events. This FK enables correlation of VPP grid service dispatches with DR event lifecycle.']
  transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_substation. Business justification: VPP dispatches address transmission substation constraints by reducing load in specific service territories. Grid operators coordinate VPP dispatch with transmission operations to manage substation th']
  triggering_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.event. Business justification: VPP dispatches are often triggered by grid events (outages, contingencies) requiring immediate load relief or voltage support. Operational logs must reference the triggering event for DERMS coordinati']
  vpp_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the aggregated portfolio of Distributed Energy Resources (DERs) that received this dispatch instruction.']
  acknowledgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the aggregator or DERMS platform acknowledged receipt of the VPP dispatch instruction. Used to track communication latency and dispatch readiness.']
  actual_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual measured power response in megawatts (MW) delivered by the VPP portfolio during the dispatch period. Used for measurement and verification (M&V) and settlement calculations.']
  baseline_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Calculated baseline power level in megawatts (MW) representing what the VPP portfolio would have consumed or generated without the dispatch instruction. Used for measurement and verification (M&V) to calculate actual response.']
  bidirectional_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the VPP dispatch instruction utilized bidirectional DER capabilities (both charging and discharging). True for dispatches involving energy storage or vehicle-to-grid (V2G) resources; false for unidirectional load curtailment.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the VPP dispatch instruction was cancelled. Examples include grid condition change, market price change, or operational constraint. Null if the dispatch was not cancelled.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction was cancelled before completion. Null if the dispatch was not cancelled.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction was completed and DER resources returned to normal operation. Marks the end of the dispatch lifecycle.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the VPP dispatch met the minimum performance requirements for the contracted service type. True if performance ratio exceeded the compliance threshold; false otherwise. Used for capacity accreditation and penalty assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this VPP dispatch record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  der_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual DER assets (batteries, electric vehicles, smart inverters, controllable loads) that participated in this VPP dispatch instruction.']
  der_response_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of DER assets in the VPP portfolio that successfully responded to the dispatch instruction, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.92 for 92% response rate). Used to assess portfolio reliability.']
  derms_integration_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier linking this VPP dispatch to the corresponding instruction or transaction in the Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS). Enables traceability between utility dispatch systems and DER control platforms.']
  dispatch_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the VPP dispatch instruction in minutes. Calculated as the difference between dispatch end time and dispatch start time.']
  dispatch_end_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction expires and DER resources should return to normal operation.']
  dispatch_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the VPP dispatch instruction, formatted as VPP- followed by 10 digits. Used for external communication with ISO/RTO and aggregators.']
  dispatch_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of the VPP dispatch instruction. Emergency dispatches override normal operations; high priority dispatches support critical grid conditions; normal and low priority dispatches support economic optimization.']
  dispatch_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or operational reason for issuing the VPP dispatch instruction. Examples include peak demand management, renewable integration, frequency response, voltage support, or market arbitrage.']
  dispatch_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or entity that initiated the VPP dispatch instruction. Distinguishes between utility Energy Management System (EMS), ISO/RTO market dispatch, aggregator platform, Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS), or manual operator dispatch.']
  dispatch_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction becomes effective and DER resources should begin responding.']
  dispatch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the VPP dispatch instruction. Tracks progression from issuance through execution to completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|issued|acknowledged|active|completed|cancelled|failed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction was issued to the aggregated DER portfolio. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.']
  iso_rto_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the ISO or RTO market region where the VPP dispatch is being executed. Examples include CAISO, ERCOT, MISO, PJM, NYISO, ISO-NE, and SPP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CAISO|ERCOT|MISO|PJM|NYISO|ISO-NE|SPP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lmp_energy_price decimal(10,4) [note: 'Locational Marginal Price for energy at the pricing node during the dispatch interval, expressed in dollars per megawatt-hour ($/MWh). Used for settlement and revenue calculation.']
  market_product varchar [note: 'Specific wholesale market product or ancillary service that the VPP dispatch is fulfilling. Examples include Day-Ahead Market (DAM) energy, Real-Time Market (RTM) energy, regulation up/down, or operating reserves. Maps to ISO/RTO product definitions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about the VPP dispatch. Used to capture non-standard conditions or operator observations.']
  performance_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of actual MW response to target MW dispatch, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.95 for 95% performance). Used to assess VPP portfolio compliance and reliability for capacity accreditation.']
  pricing_node varchar [note: 'Locational Marginal Price (LMP) node or pricing point where the VPP portfolio is registered and settled in the wholesale market. Used for financial settlement and locational value calculation.']
  ramp_rate_mw_per_min decimal(8,3) [note: 'Required rate of change in power output in megawatts per minute (MW/min). Specifies how quickly the VPP portfolio must transition from current state to target dispatch level.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of grid service requested from the VPP portfolio. Includes energy arbitrage, capacity provision, frequency regulation, spinning reserve, non-spinning reserve, and voltage support. Determines the operational mode and response requirements for the DER fleet.']
  settlement_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total financial settlement amount in dollars for the VPP dispatch performance. Calculated based on actual MW response, LMP energy price, and applicable market product rates. Used for aggregator and customer incentive payments.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the settlement amount. Typically USD for North American energy markets.']
  settlement_interval varchar [note: 'Time granularity for measuring and settling the VPP dispatch performance. Aligns with ISO/RTO market settlement intervals (typically 5-minute, 15-minute, or hourly).']
  target_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Target power output or curtailment in megawatts (MW) requested from the VPP portfolio. Positive values indicate generation or discharge; negative values indicate load reduction or charging.']
  telemetry_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'Frequency in seconds at which real-time telemetry data was collected from the VPP portfolio during the dispatch. Typical values range from 1 to 60 seconds depending on service type and ISO/RTO requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this VPP dispatch record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a Virtual Power Plant (VPP) dispatch instruction issued to an aggregated portfolio of DERs (batteries, EVs, smart inverters, controllable loads) to provide grid services beyond traditional DR curtailment. Captures dispatch timestamp, service type (energy, capacity, frequency regulation, spinning reserve, voltage support), target MW, ramp rate, dispatch duration, ISO/RTO market product, settlement interval, actual response (MW), DERMS integration reference, and dispatch source (utility, ISO/RTO, aggregator). Distinct from standard DR events as VPP dispatches coordinate bidirectional DER resources for wholesale market products and ancillary services.'
}

  capacity_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.capacity_requirement. Business justification: DR capacity registrations must align with ISO/RTO capacity requirements for resource adequacy planning. Registrations are validated against capacity requirements to ensure sufficient installed capacit']

  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry.   regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Capacity registrations require ISO/RTO filings and PUC reporting for resource adequacy compliance and capacity market participation. Links registration to regulatory filing for capacity compliance tra']

  to_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Capacity registrations are made under specific DR programs for ISO/RTO market participation. Required for capacity reporting.']
  annual_capacity_payment_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total annual capacity payment in USD for this registration, calculated as registered_capacity_mw × capacity_payment_rate_per_mw_day × days_in_season. Used for revenue forecasting and financial reporting.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this registration was approved by the ISO/RTO or utility. Marks the transition to certified status.']
  baseline_methodology varchar [note: 'The ISO/RTO-approved baseline methodology used for measuring demand reduction performance (e.g., High 5 of 10, Mid 8 of 10, Regression-based). Determines how capacity performance is calculated during events.']
  capacity_obligation_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The firm capacity obligation in megawatts (MW) that must be delivered during performance assessment hours. May differ from registered capacity due to derating factors or performance adjustments.']
  capacity_payment_rate_per_mw_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'The capacity payment rate in dollars per megawatt per day ($/MW-day) for this registration, as determined by the ISO/RTO capacity auction clearing price for the zone and season.']
  capacity_season varchar [note: 'The capacity delivery year or season for which this registration is effective (e.g., 2024/2025, Summer 2024). Aligns with ISO/RTO capacity auction and delivery periods.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date on which the DR capacity registration was certified by the ISO/RTO as meeting all technical, metering, and performance requirements.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification status of the DR capacity registration with the ISO/RTO. Certified status is required for capacity payments and market participation.']
  compliance_test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent ISO/RTO compliance test or capacity performance verification test for this registration.']
  compliance_test_result_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Measured demand reduction in megawatts (MW) achieved during the most recent compliance test. Used to validate registered capacity and adjust capacity credits.']
  compliance_test_status varchar [note: 'Outcome status of the most recent compliance test. Failed tests may result in capacity derating or registration suspension.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this registration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  iso_rto_market varchar [note: 'The ISO/RTO market in which this DR capacity is registered. Determines market rules, capacity auction structure, and performance obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PJM|NYISO|ISO-NE|CAISO|MISO|SPP|ERCOT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  iso_rto_registration_number varchar [note: 'External registration identifier assigned by the ISO/RTO capacity market for this DR resource or portfolio. This is the business identifier used in wholesale market communications and settlements.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this registration record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this registration record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special conditions, exceptions, or operational details related to this DR capacity registration. Used for operational coordination and issue tracking.']
  performance_penalty_rate_per_mwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Penalty rate in dollars per megawatt-hour ($/MWh) applied for non-performance during ISO/RTO performance assessment hours. Varies by market and performance tier.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Performance classification tier assigned by the ISO/RTO based on historical performance, resource type, and telemetry capabilities (e.g., Tier 1, Tier 2, Emergency-Only). Affects capacity credit and payment rates.']
  portfolio_composition_json varchar [note: 'JSON structure containing detailed composition of the DR portfolio, including individual customer IDs, premise IDs, meter IDs, resource types, and individual capacity contributions. Used for portfolio management and settlement allocation.']
  portfolio_name varchar [note: 'Business name or designation for the DR capacity portfolio. May represent an aggregator portfolio, utility program portfolio, or Virtual Power Plant (VPP) resource pool.']
  puc_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this registration must be included in state Public Utility Commission (PUC) demand response program reporting and Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance filings.']
  registered_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total registered demand response capacity in megawatts (MW) committed to the ISO/RTO capacity market. This is the capacity obligation that must be available during performance periods.']
  registration_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this DR capacity registration becomes effective and capacity obligations begin. Typically aligns with the start of the capacity delivery season.']
  registration_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this DR capacity registration expires and capacity obligations end. Typically aligns with the end of the capacity delivery season.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DR capacity registration. Active status is required for capacity payments and dispatch eligibility.']
  registration_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DR capacity registration structure. Determines reporting obligations, settlement processes, and performance measurement methodologies.']
  resource_type_mix varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or JSON structure describing the mix of resource types in this registration (e.g., load_curtailment, battery_storage, solar_der, ev_charging, chp). Captures the composition of the portfolio for capacity credit calculations.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this registration was submitted to the ISO/RTO capacity market. Used for tracking registration timelines and auction participation.']
  submitted_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who submitted this registration to the ISO/RTO. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  telemetry_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'Required telemetry reporting interval in seconds (e.g., 4, 10, 60) for real-time performance monitoring. Null if telemetry is not required.']
  telemetry_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time telemetry data submission to the ISO/RTO is required for this registration. Typically required for Tier 1 performance resources and VPP registrations.']
  to_aggregator varchar [note: 'FK to demand.aggregator.aggregator_id — Many capacity registrations are submitted by aggregators. This FK links the registration to the aggregator entity for portfolio management and FERC Order 2222 compliance.']
  to_program varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Capacity registrations are made under specific DR programs for ISO/RTO market participation. This FK links the registered capacity to the program for reporting and settlement.']

  Note: 'Master record of DR capacity registered with an ISO/RTO or utility capacity market, representing individual customer resources, aggregator portfolios, and VPP resource pools. Captures registration ID, ISO/RTO market, portfolio name, resource type mix (load curtailment, DER, battery, EV), capacity season, registered MW, geographic zone, performance tier and obligation, capacity payment rate ($/MW-day), compliance test results, certification status, registration effective date, portfolio composition details, and aggregator portfolio metadata (ISO/RTO registration ID, resource type mix, total registered capacity). SSOT for wholesale DR capacity positions, aggregator portfolio registrations, VPP resource pools, and ISO/RTO capacity reporting obligations.'
}

Table outage.event {
  event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each outage event record in the OMS. Primary key for the outage_event data product. Serves as the authoritative SSOT identity key for all downstream joins, lineage, and reporting.']
  cause_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.cause. Business justification: The event table stores cause_code (STRING) and cause_description (STRING) as denormalized text fields. The cause table is the authoritative reference for outage cause classifications. Adding cause_id ']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Outages within control areas impact ACE, CPS compliance, frequency response obligations, and NERC reporting. Control center operators need to track outage impacts on balancing authority performance me']
  control_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_center. Business justification: Outage events are operationally managed by a specific control center. NERC TOP/EOP standards require knowing which control center coordinated the outage response. Domain experts expect this link for p']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Outage events must track DER involvement for root cause analysis, IEEE 1547 compliance verification, fault contribution assessment, and regulatory reporting. OMS systems routinely flag DER-related dis']
  detecting_ami_head_end_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.ami_head_end. Business justification: Outage events detected via AMI last-gasp must reference the detecting AMI head-end system. event has detection_source (plain text) but no FK. This supports AMI-OMS integration KPI reporting, head-end ']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Outage events occur within forecast zones. Utilities track zone-level outage impacts for load forecast adjustments, reliability index calculations by zone, and regulatory reporting. OMS systems routin']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Outage events must be evaluated against regulatory obligations for NERC/PUC reportability. Compliance officers determine which outages trigger mandatory reporting under specific obligations (NERC EOP,']
  reliability_index_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.reliability_index_period. Business justification: Connect each outage event to its reporting period for reliability metrics; many events belong to one reliability_index_period.']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulated service territory within which the outage occurred. Used for PUC regulatory reporting, territory-level reliability benchmarking, and rate case support.']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent storm or severe weather event record when is_storm_event is True. Links individual outage events to the storm event for aggregate storm impact reporting and mutual aid cost tracking.']
  actual_restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The Actual Time of Restoration (ATR) — the confirmed timestamp when the last affected customer on this outage event was restored to service. Used as the authoritative end time for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI duration calculations.']
  affected_customers_commercial varchar [note: 'Number of commercial customer service points affected by this outage event. Subset of customers_affected_count. Used for commercial segment reliability reporting and economic impact estimation.']
  affected_customers_industrial varchar [note: 'Number of industrial customer service points affected by this outage event. Subset of customers_affected_count. Used for industrial segment reliability reporting and large-customer impact notifications.']
  affected_customers_residential varchar [note: 'Number of residential customer service points affected by this outage event. Subset of customers_affected_count. Used for customer class-level reliability reporting and equity analysis required by some state PUCs.']
  caidi_minutes decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Customer Average Interruption Duration Index (CAIDI) for this outage event, representing the average restoration time experienced by affected customers. Calculated as total customer_minutes_interrupted divided by customers_affected_count. Stored at event level for benchmarking.']
  call_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer calls or trouble reports received and clustered to this outage event. Used to gauge customer impact severity, prioritize restoration, and assess IVR/call center load during storm events.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the outage event record was first created in the OMS or Silver layer data product. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA tracking of event creation latency.']
  crew_dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the first field crew was dispatched to the outage location. Used to measure dispatch response time (detection_timestamp to crew_dispatch_timestamp) as an operational KPI and for regulatory response time compliance.']
  crew_on_site_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the first field crew arrived on-site at the outage location. Used to measure crew travel time and assess field response performance against SLA targets.']
  critical_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outage event affects one or more critical facilities (hospitals, emergency services, water treatment, telecommunications, or other life-safety infrastructure). True triggers elevated priority and mandatory regulatory notification protocols.']
  customer_minutes_interrupted decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total customer minutes interrupted (CMI) contributed by this outage event, calculated as the sum of interruption duration (minutes) multiplied by customers affected for each restoration segment. Primary input to SAIDI (System Average Interruption Duration Index) calculation.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer service points (meters) interrupted by this outage event at peak impact. Used as the primary customer impact metric for SAIFI calculation (interruptions per customer) and regulatory reporting to PUC.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'The source or mechanism by which the outage was first detected. Supports analysis of detection method effectiveness and AMI/SCADA investment value. Values: ami_ping (AMI outage notification), scada_alarm (SCADA supervisory alarm), customer_call (inbound customer report), predictive_model (ML-based prediction), field_crew (crew observation), smart_meter_outage_notification (last gasp signal).']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world timestamp when the outage event was first detected or reported, regardless of detection source (AMI ping, SCADA alarm, customer call, or predictive model). This is the authoritative event start time used for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI duration calculations per IEEE 1366.']
  energy_not_served_mwh decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated energy not served (ENS) in MWh due to this outage event, calculated as the product of average load (MW) on the affected circuit and the outage duration (hours). Used for economic impact assessment, IRP planning, and regulatory reporting.']
  estimated_restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The Estimated Time to Restoration (ETR) communicated to customers and field crews, representing the predicted date and time by which all affected customers will be restored. Updated dynamically as restoration progresses. Distinct from actual_restoration_timestamp.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the outage event within the OMS workflow. Transitions: new (detected, unworked) → in_progress (crew dispatched or switching underway) → restored (all customers re-energized) → closed (event verified and finalized) → cancelled (duplicate or false positive confirmed).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outage event per IEEE 1366 definitions: sustained (interruption > 5 minutes), momentary (≤ 5 minutes), planned (scheduled maintenance), major_event_day (MED per IEEE 1366 beta method), or partial (affecting a subset of customers on a circuit). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sustained|momentary|planned|major_event_day|partial|rolling — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  fault_location varchar [note: 'Descriptive location of the fault or interruption point on the network (e.g., pole number, span identifier, address nearest to fault). Captured from field crew report or automated fault location indicator (FLI) in the ADMS.']
  initial_call_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the first customer call or report associated with this outage event was received by the Customer Information System (CIS) or IVR. Used for call clustering analysis and detection latency measurement.']
  is_major_event_day boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this outage event occurred on a day declared as a Major Event Day (MED) per the IEEE 1366 beta method. MED events are excluded from standard SAIDI/SAIFI calculations and reported separately to PUC. True = MED event; False = normal event.']
  is_storm_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this outage event was caused by or associated with a declared storm or severe weather event. Storm-flagged events may be subject to separate regulatory reporting thresholds and mutual aid activation criteria.']
  isolation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the fault was isolated from the network: automatic (protective device operated automatically), manual (field crew operated device manually), remote (SCADA/ADMS remote switching), or flisr_automated (ADMS FLISR automation executed switching sequence).']
  nerc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this outage event meets NERC reporting thresholds (e.g., EOP-004 disturbance reporting criteria based on MW loss, customer count, or duration). True = event must be reported to NERC/DOE via OE-417 or equivalent.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated customer outage notifications (IVR, SMS, email, or app push) have been sent for this outage event. True = at least one notification batch dispatched. Used for regulatory compliance tracking of customer communication requirements.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the first customer outage notification was dispatched for this event. Used to measure notification latency from detection and to demonstrate compliance with PUC customer communication time requirements.']
  oms_event_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known event identifier assigned by the Outage Management System (GE PowerOn ADMS). This is the operational business identifier used by field crews, dispatchers, and customer service representatives to reference the outage event across systems.']
  predicted_cause_code varchar [note: 'The cause code predicted by the OMS predictive model or ADMS fault analysis engine at the time of initial detection, before field crew investigation. Compared against final cause_code for model accuracy tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weather|equipment_failure|animal_contact|vegetation|human_error|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  predicted_customers_affected varchar [note: 'The number of customers predicted to be affected by the outage at the time of initial detection, as estimated by the OMS/ADMS predictive model. Compared against actual customers_affected_count for model accuracy and ETR calibration.']
  prediction_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence score (0.00–100.00) assigned by the predictive model or ADMS fault analysis engine to the initial outage prediction, including predicted cause and affected scope. Used to assess model reliability and calibrate automated dispatch thresholds.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Restoration priority classification assigned to the outage event, used to sequence crew dispatch and switching operations. Critical: hospitals, emergency services, critical infrastructure; High: large commercial/industrial, high customer count; Medium: standard residential; Low: single-customer or non-critical.']
  puc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this outage event meets the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) threshold for mandatory individual event reporting (e.g., exceeds customer count or duration thresholds defined in the utility\'s tariff or service quality rules).']
  reported_symptom varchar [note: 'The primary symptom reported by the first customer caller or detected by the AMI/SCADA system at the time of initial report. Used for triage, cause hypothesis, and crew dispatch guidance.']
  saidi_contribution_minutes decimal(18,4) [note: 'This event\'s contribution to the System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI), expressed in customer-minutes per total customers served. Calculated as customer_minutes_interrupted divided by total customers served in the reporting period. Stored at event level for regulatory period aggregation.']
  saifi_contribution decimal(18,6) [note: 'This event\'s contribution to the System Average Interruption Frequency Index (SAIFI), expressed as customer interruptions per total customers served. Calculated as customers_affected_count divided by total customers served. Stored at event level for regulatory period aggregation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the outage event record. Supports change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Core master record for every unplanned or planned power interruption event managed by the OMS. Captures the full lifecycle from initial detection (via AMI ping, SCADA alarm, customer call clustering, or predictive model) through final restoration. Includes cause classification, outage type (sustained, momentary, planned, major event per IEEE 1366), affected circuit/feeder, estimated vs. actual restoration time (ETR/ATR), initial prediction metadata (source, confidence, predicted scope, predicted cause, predicted affected segments), source call/report metadata (call receipt timestamp, source type, reported symptom, call disposition, duplicate flag, call clustering results), lifecycle status transitions with full audit trail (status code, transition timestamp, triggering actor, status notes), affected network segment detail (segment identifier, type, de-energization timestamps, isolation method, customer count, restoration sequence priority), SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI contribution, storm flag, Major Event Day declaration status, and OMS event identifier. This is the authoritative SSOT for outage event identity, lifecycle, initial prediction, source reporting, status history, and segment-level impact in the outage domain.'
}

Table outage.interruption {
  interruption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a single customer or premise-level service interruption record. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with the interrupted service point at the time of the interruption. Supports customer-facing outage communication and regulatory customer impact reporting.']
  ami_meter_id varchar [note: 'The AMI meter identifier associated with the service point at the time of the interruption. Used to correlate last-gasp and first-breath AMI signals from the MDM system for automated interruption detection and restoration confirmation.']
  billing_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_service_agreement. Business justification: Individual customer interruptions require service agreement context to apply correct credit calculation rules based on rate schedule, service class, and contractual SLA terms. Enables automated credit']
  cause_id varchar [note: 'FK to outage.cause']
  crew_dispatch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field crew dispatch record associated with the restoration of this interruption. Links to workforce management for crew response time analysis and restoration efficiency reporting.']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Customer interruptions at DER-equipped service points require DER system reference for islanding analysis, restoration sequencing, and regulatory reporting of DER performance during grid disturbances ']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the substation serving the affected service point at the time of the interruption. Supports substation-level reliability performance reporting and outage root cause analysis.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outage event that caused this customer-level interruption. Links the granular interruption record to the broader outage event for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI roll-up calculations.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution feeder circuit that serves the affected service point. Supports feeder-level reliability analysis, SAIDI/SAIFI benchmarking by circuit, and GIS-based outage mapping.']
  interruption_planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planned maintenance window associated with this interruption, when applicable. Null for unplanned outages. Links to the planned outage schedule for customer notification and regulatory reporting.']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: Interruptions must reference the meter_point (AMI collection point) directly for interval data gap analysis, automated outage detection via AMI, and SAIDI/SAIFI calculations at the meter_point level. ']
  outage_switching_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the switching order executed to isolate or restore the affected circuit segment. Links the customer-level interruption to the operational switching workflow in the ADMS/OMS.']
  service_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service delivery point (meter point or premise connection) that experienced the interruption. The fundamental unit of interruption tracking per IEEE 1366.']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.service_territory']
  to_planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'FK to outage.planned_outage_window.planned_outage_window_id — Customer interruptions from planned maintenance link to the planned outage window. Required for distinguishing planned vs unplanned interruptions in reliability reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this interruption record was first created in the system, either by automated OMS event processing or manual dispatcher entry. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  critical_care_type varchar [note: 'Specific category of critical care designation for the affected customer. Used to prioritize restoration sequencing and determine notification requirements. Only populated when is_critical_care is true.']
  customer_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the affected customer by service class at the time of the interruption. Used for segmented SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reporting by customer class as required by state PUC reliability standards and NERC reporting.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Number of customers affected by the parent outage event at the time this interruption record was created. Denormalized from the outage event for regulatory reporting convenience. Used in SAIFI numerator calculations and NERC/PUC customer impact reporting.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the data quality status of this interruption record as assessed during Silver layer validation. \'validated\' indicates all timestamps and durations are confirmed; \'estimated\' indicates one or more fields are estimated; \'suspect\' indicates anomalies detected; \'corrected\' indicates the record has been manually revised post-initial capture.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the service interruption in whole minutes, calculated as the difference between interruption_end_timestamp and interruption_start_timestamp. Stored as a business field to support SAIDI and CAIDI reliability index calculations without requiring real-time computation. Null if interruption is still active.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer\'s service was restored, recorded to millisecond precision. Null if the interruption is still active. Used as the end point for duration calculations and CAIDI index computation per IEEE 1366.']
  estimated_restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Estimated date and time of service restoration communicated to the customer and used for crew scheduling. Distinct from interruption_end_timestamp (actual restoration). Supports customer communication accuracy tracking and ETR (Estimated Time to Restore) performance measurement.']
  interruption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the interruption record. \'active\' indicates the customer is currently without power; \'restored\' indicates service has been re-energized; \'cancelled\' indicates the interruption was logged in error; \'pending_confirmation\' indicates restoration has been initiated but field confirmation is outstanding.']
  interruption_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interruption as planned (scheduled maintenance), unplanned (forced outage), or momentary (duration less than 5 minutes per IEEE 1366 definition). Drives separate SAIDI/SAIFI/MAIFI index tracking and regulatory reporting segmentation.']
  is_critical_care boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affected customer or premise is designated as a critical care account (medical baseline, life support equipment, or other medically vulnerable designation). Critical care customers receive priority restoration and mandatory advance notification under state PUC rules.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this interruption record was most recently modified, including status updates, restoration confirmation, or cause code revisions. Supports change tracking and Silver layer incremental processing.']
  major_event_day_exclusion boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interruption has been formally excluded from the standard SAIDI/SAIFI reliability index calculations under the IEEE 1366 Major Event Day beta method. Distinct from storm_event_flag — this is the formal regulatory exclusion determination.']
  momentary_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interruption qualifies as a momentary interruption (duration less than 5 minutes per IEEE 1366 definition). Momentary interruptions are tracked separately for MAIFI (Momentary Average Interruption Frequency Index) calculations.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel used to deliver the outage notification to the affected customer. Supports multi-channel notification analytics and customer preference compliance reporting.']
  notification_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted out of outage notifications for this service point. When true, notification_sent will be false by design. Supports customer preference management and regulatory opt-out compliance.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an outage notification has been sent to the affected customer for this interruption. Supports regulatory compliance tracking for customer notification requirements mandated by state PUCs.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the outage notification was dispatched to the customer. Used to measure notification lead time relative to interruption start and to demonstrate regulatory compliance with advance notice requirements for planned outages.']
  oms_event_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known event number assigned by the Outage Management System (GE PowerOn ADMS) to the parent outage event. Serves as the cross-system integration key for reconciliation between OMS, CIS, and MDM systems.']
  protective_device_operation varchar [note: 'Type of protective device that operated to isolate the fault causing this interruption. Supports equipment performance analysis, recloser operation tracking, and fuse-saving vs. trip-and-reclose strategy evaluation.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric reference number assigned to this interruption record, used in customer communications, regulatory filings, and OMS integration. Format: INT-YYYY-NNNNNNNN.']
  regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interruption must be included in regulatory reliability reports submitted to NERC, FERC, or state PUCs. Interruptions below minimum duration thresholds or excluded under MED rules may be flagged as non-reportable.']
  restoration_confirmation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which service restoration was confirmed. AMI ping uses Advanced Metering Infrastructure last-gasp/first-breath signals; field_crew indicates on-site verification; customer_callback indicates customer-reported restoration; scada indicates SCADA system re-energization signal; estimated indicates restoration assumed based on switching operations.']
  restoration_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether service restoration has been confirmed for this interruption, either through AMI meter ping, field crew confirmation, or customer callback. Distinguishes between estimated restoration and verified restoration for CAIDI accuracy.']
  restoration_method varchar [note: 'Method used to restore service to the affected customer or premise. Supports operational analysis of restoration efficiency and crew dispatch effectiveness. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: switching|repair|bypass|auto_reclosure|manual_transfer|temporary_generation — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  saidi_contribution_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'This interruption\'s individual contribution to the SAIDI reliability index, expressed in customer-minutes divided by total customers served. Stored as a pre-computed business field to support regulatory reporting aggregation without requiring full recalculation. Null if interruption is active or excluded from SAIDI.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this interruption record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking across OMS, CIS, and MDM source systems.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer\'s service interruption began, recorded to millisecond precision. This is the principal business event timestamp used as the start point for duration calculations and SAIDI/SAIFI index computation per IEEE 1366.']
  storm_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interruption occurred during a declared major event or storm. When true, the interruption may be excluded from IEEE 1366 Major Event Day (MED) calculations per the beta method, affecting SAIDI/SAIFI index reporting.']
  to_outage_event varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — Every customer-level interruption must link to its parent outage event. This is the foundational relationship for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI calculation — without it, reliability indices cannot be computed. Da']
  voltage_level varchar [note: 'Voltage classification of the circuit segment at which the interruption originated. Used to segment reliability reporting by voltage class and to identify transmission vs. distribution-level outage impacts for FERC and state PUC reporting.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather condition at the time and location of the interruption. Used for weather-normalized reliability analysis, storm response performance reporting, and climate resilience planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|wind|ice|snow|lightning|flood|extreme_heat|none — promote to reference product if additional values needed]']

  Note: 'Granular record of a single customer or premise-level service interruption linked to a parent outage event or planned maintenance window. Tracks the specific service point, customer account reference, interruption start and end timestamps, duration in minutes, cause code, restoration method (switching, repair, bypass), planned/unplanned flag, notification sent flag, notification channel, critical care designation (medical baseline, life support), restoration confirmation, and association to planned maintenance windows. Also serves as the customer-outage association record capturing per-customer impact including notification delivery status and opt-out preferences. This is the single SSOT for ALL customer-level outage impact — feeds SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI reliability index calculations, supports customer-facing outage communication, drives regulatory customer impact reporting, and provides the definitive record of which customers were affected by each event.'
}

Table outage.outage_switching_order {
  outage_switching_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the switching order record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  affected_feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary distribution feeder circuit affected by this switching order. Used for network topology analysis, outage impact assessment, and GIS integration with Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.interconnection_agreement. Business justification: Switching orders for DER isolation during outages must reference interconnection agreements to verify parallel operation permissions, export limitations, and required notification procedures per tarif']
  contingency_analysis_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.contingency_analysis_run. Business justification: Outage switching orders for transmission elements require a contingency analysis run to validate N-1 compliance before execution. NERC TOP-001 requires operators to verify contingency analysis before ']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Switching orders affect control area operations (state estimation, interchange schedules, AGC). EMS operators coordinate switching with field crews to maintain system reliability and balancing authori']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Switching orders require DER isolation verification before energization per IEEE 1547 and utility operating procedures. Operators must confirm DER status (online/offline/isolated) before executing swi']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the substation from which the affected feeder originates or where the primary switching devices are located. Used for geographic scoping and asset management integration.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outage event that triggered this switching order. Links the switching sequence to the originating interruption or planned maintenance event in the Outage Management System (OMS).']
  grid_switching_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.grid_switching_order. Business justification: Outage switching orders may reference or be coordinated with grid switching orders for transmission-connected substations. Linking enables cross-system switching coordination and safety compliance.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Switching orders on critical infrastructure require compliance documentation under NERC CIP or operational reliability standards. Operations must document adherence to switching protocols mandated by ']
  operating_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.operating_limit. Business justification: Switching orders must be validated against operating limits (thermal/voltage) before execution. NERC FAC-001/FAC-002 compliance requires operators to verify limits are not exceeded during planned de-e']
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: The outage_switching_order table has planned_outage_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating the switching order is for a planned outage, but no FK to the planned_outage_window record. Switching orders are the prima']
  poi_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.poi_specification. Business justification: Switching orders affecting DER interconnection points must reference POI specifications for proper isolation procedures, disconnect switch operation, metering configuration, and safe re-energization s']
  primary_switching_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Switching orders specify which physical switching device must be operated to isolate circuits. Safety clearance procedures require explicit asset identification. Operations teams need asset-specific o']
  state_estimation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.state_estimation_run. Business justification: Outage switching orders reference the state estimation snapshot used to validate network conditions at time of execution. NERC TOP-001 requires operators to use current network state before switching.']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event. Business justification: The outage_switching_order table has storm_event_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating the switching order is storm-related, but no FK to the storm_event master record. During storm response, switching orders are']
  weather_input_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.weather_input. Business justification: Switching orders executed during adverse weather require real-time weather context for crew safety protocols, hazard assessment, and operational decision-making. Weather conditions directly impact swi']
  actual_customers_affected varchar [note: 'The confirmed number of customer service points that were interrupted as a result of this switching order, validated post-restoration. Used for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability metric calculation and regulatory reporting to State PUCs.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the final switching step was completed and the switching order was closed. Used for outage duration calculation and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability performance reporting.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the field crew began executing the first step of the switching sequence. Used for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability metric calculation and post-event analysis.']
  adms_switching_order_ref varchar [note: 'The native switching order identifier assigned by the GE PowerOn Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the Silver Layer lakehouse and the operational ADMS source system.']
  approval_chain_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the approval chain for this switching order, including any conditional approvals, escalations, or deviations from standard authorization procedures. Supports NERC compliance audit trail.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the switching order received final authorization from the approving supervisor or operations manager. Part of the approval chain audit trail required for NERC compliance.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why this switching order was cancelled, if applicable. Populated only when order_status is \'cancelled\'. Supports post-event analysis and process improvement.']
  completed_steps_count varchar [note: 'The number of individual switching steps that have been successfully executed and confirmed as of the last update. Enables real-time progress monitoring during active switching operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this switching order record was first created in the system, which may precede the issued timestamp if the order was drafted in advance. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customer_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether advance customer notification was sent for this switching order (applicable to planned outages). Supports compliance tracking against State PUC advance notification requirements, typically 48-72 hours prior to planned interruption.']
  de_energization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the affected network segment was de-energized (taken out of service) as part of this switching order. Critical for outage duration measurement and SAIDI calculation start point.']
  energization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the affected network segment was re-energized (returned to service) following completion of the switching sequence. Marks the end of the customer interruption for SAIDI/CAIDI calculation.']
  estimated_customers_affected varchar [note: 'The estimated number of customer service points that will be or are interrupted as a result of this switching order. Used for customer notification prioritization, regulatory reporting, and SAIFI calculation inputs.']
  hazard_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing known hazards, special safety precautions, or environmental conditions relevant to executing this switching order. Examples include downed conductors, flooded equipment, or proximity to energized lines. Mandatory review by crew before execution.']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the dispatcher formally issued this switching order to the field crew. This is the principal business event timestamp marking the start of the switching order lifecycle. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this switching order record was most recently modified. Tracks any changes to the order after initial creation, supporting audit trail requirements.']
  nerc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this switching order is associated with an event that meets NERC reportability thresholds (e.g., EOP-004 disturbance reporting). When True, the event must be included in mandatory NERC regulatory submissions.']
  network_segment_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the distribution or transmission network segment affected by this switching order, including feeder name, circuit section, and geographic reference. Supports field crew orientation and post-event reporting.']
  oms_incident_number varchar [note: 'The incident or trouble call number from the Outage Management System (OMS) associated with this switching order. Enables traceability from the switching sequence back to the originating customer-reported outage or system-detected fault.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the switching order. Tracks progression from initial drafting through dispatcher issuance, field execution, and final completion or cancellation. Drives workflow routing in GE PowerOn ADMS.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the switching order by operational purpose. Distinguishes planned maintenance windows from emergency outage restoration, storm response, load transfer operations, and asset commissioning/decommissioning activities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned_maintenance|emergency_restoration|storm_response|load_transfer|commissioning|decommissioning — promote to reference product]']
  planned_outage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this switching order is associated with a planned (scheduled) outage as opposed to an unplanned (forced) interruption. Planned outages are tracked separately for SAIDI/SAIFI reporting and customer advance notification compliance.']
  post_event_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this switching order has been flagged for mandatory post-event review, typically triggered by NERC reportable events, major storm response, or switching errors. Drives the post-event analysis workflow.']
  post_event_review_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text findings and corrective actions documented during the post-event review of this switching order. Populated after review completion when post_event_review_flag is True. Supports continuous improvement and regulatory compliance.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to this switching order, used for restoration prioritization and crew dispatch sequencing. Critical priority is reserved for transmission-level or large-scale customer impact events.']
  restoration_method varchar [note: 'The method used to restore service as part of this switching order. Distinguishes manual field crew switching from automated ADMS-driven Fault Location, Isolation, and Service Restoration (FLISR), remote switching via SCADA, sectionalizing, or load transfer to alternate feeders.']
  safety_clearance_number varchar [note: 'The reference number of the safety clearance (hold tag or clearance order) associated with this switching order. Ensures that de-energized equipment is properly tagged and locked out before crew work begins. Mandatory for OSHA lockout/tagout compliance.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time by which the switching sequence is expected to be completed and the network restored to target configuration. Used for outage duration estimation and customer notification.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time for field crew to begin executing the switching sequence. Used for planned maintenance scheduling and crew coordination. May differ from actual execution start time.']
  storm_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this switching order was issued in response to a major storm or declared emergency event. When True, the associated outage duration may be excluded from IEEE Std 1366 SAIDI/SAIFI calculations per major event day (MED) criteria.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why this switching order was suspended mid-execution, if applicable. Populated only when order_status is \'suspended\'. Common reasons include unexpected hazards, equipment failure, or crew safety concerns.']
  switching_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the switching order, used by dispatchers, field crews, and control room operators. Format: SO-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. This is the business-facing reference number used in OMS and ADMS systems.']
  switching_purpose_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the operational reason for issuing this switching order, including the specific fault, maintenance activity, or load transfer objective. Provides context beyond the structured order_type classification.']
  switching_zone varchar [note: 'The operational control zone or dispatch territory within which this switching order is executed. Aligns with the utility\'s geographic service territory divisions used for crew dispatch and reliability performance reporting.']
  total_steps_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual switching steps defined in this switching order sequence. Used for progress tracking and completeness validation during execution.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of this switching order, incremented each time the order is revised after initial issuance. Supports audit trail requirements and ensures field crews are executing the most current approved version of the switching sequence.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'The nominal operating voltage level in kilovolts (kV) of the network segment affected by this switching order. Determines applicable safety procedures, crew qualifications, and regulatory requirements.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated maintenance or repair work order in the asset management system (Ventyx Asset Suite or SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance). Links the switching order to the underlying maintenance activity that necessitated the switching sequence.']

  Note: 'Formal switching order issued during outage restoration or planned maintenance to reconfigure the distribution network. Captures the complete switching sequence including individual step detail (step sequence number, device identifier, device type, action type — open/close/tag/verify, required safety state, completion status, actual execution timestamp, executing crew member). Also records issuing dispatcher, executing crew, affected switching devices (sectionalizers, reclosers, breakers), safety clearance references, energization/de-energization timestamps, and approval chain. Supports granular audit trail of switching sequences for NERC compliance and post-event analysis. Integrates with GE PowerOn ADMS switching management module.'
}

Table outage.crew_dispatch {
  crew_dispatch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each crew dispatch record within the outage management system. Primary key for the crew_dispatch data product.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Crew dispatches to transmission-connected substations affect control area operations. Coordination between field crews and control center operators is essential for safe switching and maintaining syst']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Crew dispatches to DER locations require system details for safety clearances, anti-islanding verification, lockout/tagout procedures, and coordination with customer-owned generation per OSHA and util']
  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.ems_node. Business justification: Crew dispatches for transmission-level outages target specific EMS network nodes. The EMS node identifies the precise substation bus or network element requiring field crew work. Domain experts expect']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outage event that triggered this crew dispatch. Links the dispatch record to the parent outage lifecycle managed in the Outage Management System (OMS).']
